# Changelog

All notable changes are tagged in git as `vX.Y.Z`.

Versioning cadence (post-v1.0.0):
- **Patch `x.y.5`** вҖ” small / incremental changes (bug fixes, small Lexicon additions, docs, housekeeping).
- **Minor `x.y.0`** вҖ” significant changes (new corpus source, new intent family, new tooling, learned component).
- **`v2.0.0`** is reserved for the "minimally thinking Kazakh LM" вҖ” a trained compact Kazakh model plugged in as `Intent::Unknown` fallback. Not more rules вҖ” actual learned generalisation.

## [4.54.5] вҖ” 2026-05-05 вҖ” Session 6 transcript-driven fixes + first autonomous crash-tests

**Driven by user dialog test session 6 (2026-05-05) + autonomous crash-tests.** Per the user's directive, this is the first release where adam runs its own real-REPL crash-tests before shipping (autonomous-test cadence captured in `docs/weekly_plan_2026-W19.md`). Three concrete gaps from session 6 closed; one extra gap surfaced and fixed by autotest B; one architectural gap (G4) deferred with explicit trace.

### Real-REPL gap closure

| # | Pre-v4.54.5 | Post-v4.54.5 |
|---|---|---|
| **G1** | В«РўР°РҪСӢСҒР°Р№СӢТӣ.В» вҶ’ В«**ТҡСғР°РҪР°-ТӣСғР°РҪР°.** РңРөРҪ вҖ” ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣСҲР° СҒУ©Р№Р»РөСҒСғРіРө Р°СҖРҪР°Р»Т“Р°РҪ NLM, Р°СӮСӢРј Р°РҙР°Рј.В» (jarringly colloquial reduplication for an introductory turn) | В«**РҳУҷ, УҷСҖРёРҪРө.** РңРөРҪ вҖ” ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣСҲР° СҒУ©Р№Р»РөСҒСғРіРө Р°СҖРҪР°Р»Т“Р°РҪ NLM, Р°СӮСӢРј Р°РҙР°Рј.В» вң“ |
| **G2** | В«РңРөРҪС–ТЈ **Р°СӮСӢРјРҙСӢ** РөСҒС–ТЈС–Р·РҙРө РјРө?В» вҶ’ grounded fact about В«Р°СӮВ» (horse) вҖ” Acc-form 1sg.poss + memory-probe was treated as Unknown with noun_hint=Р°СӮ | В«**РЎС–Р·РҙС–ТЈ Р°СӮСӢТЈСӢР· Р”УҷСғР»РөСӮ.**В» вң“ (recall fired) |
| **G3** | В«...ТӣРҫСҒТӣР°РҪРҙР° **РҪРө РұРҫР»Р°СӮСӢРҪСӢРҪ РөСҒРөРҝСӮРөРҝ**, 2-РіРө РәУ©РұРөР№СӮС–Рҝ, 4-РәРө РұУ©Р»С–ТЈС–Р·.В» вҶ’ math_refusal (the auxiliary verb В«РөСҒРөРҝСӮРөРҝВ» between operations triggered clause-has-math-verb failure semantics) | В«**РқУҷСӮРёР¶РөСҒС–: 38**В» вң“ |
| **B2** (autotest) | В«РңРөРҪС–ТЈ **Р°СӮСӢ-Р¶У©РҪС–РјРҙС–** ТұРјСӢСӮРҝР°РҙСӢТЈ РұР°?В» вҶ’ check_contradiction template (compound В«name + family-nameВ» Acc form not in detector) | В«**РЎС–Р·РҙС–ТЈ Р°СӮСӢТЈСӢР· РһР»Р¶Р°СҒ.**В» вң“ |

### Innovations

**(1) Template fix for `greeting.intro_proposal`** вҖ” replaced В«ТӣСғР°РҪР°-ТӣСғР°РҪР°. РңРөРҪ вҖ” ...В» with В«РёУҷ, УҷСҖРёРҪРө. РңРөРҪ вҖ” ...В» (line 74, `data/dialog/templates/v1.toml`). Same conversational function, neutral tone appropriate for an introduction-acceptance turn.

**(2) `detect_ask_name` extended** вҖ” handles 4 new self-recall variants combining 1sg-poss + Acc + memory-probe phrasings:
- `Р°СӮСӢРј` / `Р°СӮСӢРјРҙСӢ` / `Р°СӮСӢ-Р¶У©РҪС–Рј` / `Р°СӮСӢ-Р¶У©РҪС–РјРҙС–` Г— `РөСҒС–ТЈС–Р·РҙРө` / `РөСҒС–ТЈРҙРө` / `ТұРјСӢСӮРҝР°*`
- `РөСҒС–РјС–Рј` / `РөСҒС–РјС–РјРҙС–` Г— same probe set

The compound-name В«Р°СӮСӢ-Р¶У©РҪВ» ("name + family-name") variant was surfaced specifically by autonomous crash-test session B.

**(3) `clause_has_math_verb` вҖ” В«РөСҒРөРҝСӮРөВ» removed** (`discourse.rs`). The top-level `input_is_math_expression` gate still recognises В«РөСҒРөРҝСӮРө-В» (auxiliary "calculate") for math-mode entry, but inside the v4.42.0 multi-clause loop we no longer treat it as a real math operator. The four real operators (РәУ©РұРөР№СӮ / РұУ©Р» / ТӣРҫСҒ / Р°Р·Р°Р№СӮ) plus the closed `Р°Р»`-forms still gate failure semantics. With В«РөСҒРөРҝСӮРөВ» off the failure list, auxiliary appendages like В«РҪРө РұРҫР»Р°СӮСӢРҪСӢРҪ РөСҒРөРҝСӮРөРҝВ» fall through silently to the accumulator-carry path.

**(4) First autonomous crash-test cadence** вҖ” adam's own REPL runs as a standard pre-release gate. Session A (РҗР№РіТҜР» / РҙУҷСҖС–РіРөСҖ / Р·РөСҖСӮСӮРөР№СӮС–РҪ activity + math chain + knowledge queries) ran clean across 12 turns. Session B (name correction + math + retrieval edges + apology) found 1 detector gap (B2, fixed) and 1 architectural concern (B1: pending name-contradiction blocks math detection вҖ” deferred to a separate release).

### Deferred

**G4** вҖ” В«РҳУҷВ» after `unknown.clarify_low_confidence` produced confused В«ТҡР°Р№СҒСӢСҒСӢ РҪР°ТӣСӮСӢ вҖ” РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°СҲСӢ РјР°, СҒТұСҖР°Р№ РјР°?В». Traced to `check_contradiction` template (line 564, v1.toml) firing with malformed `{old_value}=РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°СҲСӢ / {new_value}=СҒТұСҖР°Р№`. Phantom belief conflict appears to be created from prior turn's clarify response. Architectural fix needed in belief-tracker; deferred with full trace context.

**B1** вҖ” bare arithmetic В«17 + 25 ТӣР°РҪСҲР° РұРҫР»Р°РҙСӢ?В» blocked by pending name-contradiction. Behaviour is technically correct (modal-dialog semantics for unresolved conflict) but UX-debatable. Defer with discussion needed about precedence policy.

Stripe (11) вҖ” generative AI via agglutinative composition.

## [4.54.0] вҖ” 2026-05-05 вҖ” Lexicon expansion + 10 bridge-facts (highest-ROI batch)

**Per `project_bridge_fact_leverage`:** a single `{sub_hub} IsA {top_hub}` fact multiplies derivation leverage to +47вҖ“67/fact vs +15/fact without bridge. v4.54.0 lands the largest single-batch ROI in the v4.x line: **10 bridge facts вҶ’ +934 derived facts (+93/fact)** вҖ” far above both the prior empirical bound and the spec's +300 acceptance threshold.

### Innovations

**(1) 5 new morpheme roots** in `data/tokenizer/segmentation_roots.json` (the canonical lexicon location for morpheme-coverage). Spec named 3 from `project_morpheme_coverage_baseline`:
- **Р°Р»Т“Р°СҲ** (adverb, back, voiceless consonant) вҖ” covers 625 corpus occurrences (+ derivatives like Р°Р»Т“Р°СҲТӣСӢ)
- **РјР»РҪ** (numeral, back, nasal) вҖ” 1987 occurrences across cc100 + wikipedia
- **С–СҒРәРө** вҖ” initially added but reverted in this release: it broke the segmentation eval (`seg_183` expects `С–СҒ + РәРө`, not `С–СҒРәРө` as a unit). The `С–СҒ` root (already in lexicon at 2 chars) is below the morpheme_coverage min-root-len filter; resolving that needs a separate lexicon-tooling change deferred to a later release.

Plus 3 high-frequency bonus roots needed to clear the > 86.5 % acceptance threshold:
- **РҙР°РјСӢСӮСғ** (verb, back, vowel) вҖ” develop; 1683 occurrences
- **РјР°ТЈСӢР·РҙСӢ** (adjective, back, vowel) вҖ” important; 1605
- **Р°СҲСӢТӣ** (adjective, back, voiceless consonant) вҖ” open; 1487

**(2) 10 bridge facts** in `data/world_core/role_bridges.jsonl` (ids `bridge_role_054`вҖҰ`bridge_role_063`), each linking a sub-hub noun to a top-tier IsA hub:
- РұР°Р»Р° IsA Р°РҙР°Рј, РҙРҫСҒ IsA Р°РҙР°Рј (2 вҶ’ human-hub)
- Р¶Р°СҒР°РҪРҙСӢ РёРҪСӮРөР»Р»РөРәСӮ IsA Р¶ТҜР№Рө (1 вҶ’ system-hub)
- Р¶Р°Рҙ IsA ТұТ“СӢРј (1 вҶ’ concept-hub)
- РәРёС–Рј IsA Р·Р°СӮ, Р¶РёТ»Р°Р· IsA Р·Р°СӮ (2 вҶ’ thing-hub)
- РұТұР»СҲСӢТӣРөСӮ IsA РјТҜСҲРө, Р¶ТҜР№РәРө IsA РјТҜСҲРө (2 вҶ’ organ-hub)
- РұУ©Р»РјРө IsA РҫСҖСӢРҪ (1 вҶ’ place-hub)
- РөР» IsA РјРөРјР»РөРәРөСӮ (1 вҶ’ state-hub)

Selection criterion: 122 world_core subjects currently lack any IsA edge (computed via `comm -23` of all-subjects vs has-isa-subjects). The 10 picked are the highest-frequency missing bridges вҖ” each subject already participates in many corpus / world_core relations, so the new IsA edge cascades through R1 (transitivity), R2 (Has inheritance), R5 (shared IsA target) rules.

**(3) Re-ran the full pipeline** вҖ” `extract_facts` + `run_reasoner` + `morpheme_coverage` + `validate_foundation`. Every artefact regenerated; `verify_release_version.sh 4.54.0` green; foundation validation green.

### Acceptance metrics

### Cadence

`.0` minor вҖ” Lexicon + Knowledge expansion sized to a meaningful capability bump (10 bridges + 5 roots, full pipeline regen, derivation leverage measurably amplified). Per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0`: В«minor x.y.0 = significant capability or milestoneВ». First batch since v4.51.0 to materially shift world_core scale + derivation count.

Stripe (11) вҖ” generative AI via agglutinative composition.

## [4.53.5] вҖ” 2026-05-05 вҖ” Context-aware clarify (session 5 generic-fallback complaint closed)

**Closes the user's session-5 complaint:** "РҫСӮРІРөСӮСӢ РҙРҫР»Р¶РҪСӢ РІСӢСӮРөРәР°СӮСҢ РёР· РәРҫРҪСӮРөРәСҒСӮР° РұРөСҒРөРҙСӢ, Р° РҪРө РҫРұСүРёР№ РҫСӮРІРөСӮ РҪР° РІСҒРө, СҮСӮРҫ РҪРө Р·РҪР°РөСӮ". When `Intent::Unknown` fires AND adam can't extract a noun-hint OR the topic is low-confidence, the v4.37.5 clarify path emitted a generic В«РЎТұСҖР°Т“СӢТЈСӢР·РҙСӢ СӮРҫР»СӢТӣ СӮТҜСҒС–РҪРұРөРҙС–Рј. ТҡР°РҪРҙР°Р№ СӮР°ТӣСӢСҖСӢРҝ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ СҒТұСҖР°Рҝ РҫСӮСӢСҖСҒСӢР·?В» вҖ” completely ignoring stored profile slots (name, occupation, activity, city). v4.53.5 routes those clarify cases to a new `unknown.with_session_diagnostic` family that cites the stored context and asks the user to pick a topic from what adam already knows.

### Innovations

**(1) New `unknown.with_session_diagnostic` template family** in `data/dialog/templates/v1.toml` вҖ” 7 variants gating on different slot subsets:
- All 3 primary slots (name + occupation + activity) вҖ” full context-aware diagnostic.
- Name + occupation pair.
- Name + activity pair.
- Activity only (no name).
- Occupation only (no name).
- City only вҖ” fallback when only location is known.
- Name only вҖ” minimal context, but cites the name so user sees that adam tracks who they are.

`template_is_fillable` filters the variants to those whose slot requirements match the actual session contents; the seed picks among the eligible subset.

**(2) Planner override in `plan_response_with_epistemic`** вҖ” runs AFTER the existing epistemic / known-user override block. When the chosen `key` is `unknown.clarify_no_topic` OR `unknown.clarify_low_confidence` AND the session contains any of `name` / `occupation` / `activity` / `city`, override to `unknown.with_session_diagnostic`. Defends against missing template family via the standard `!repo.get(...).is_empty()` guard (mirrors v4.0.34 epistemic-override pattern).

**(3) Trace line `clarify_diagnostic override`** вҖ” emitted when the override fires; lists which slots are present (name=true/false occupation=true/false activity=true/false city=true/false). Lets `--trace` consumers see exactly which context-aware variant the planner chose and why.

**(4) 2 new unit tests** in `planner::tests`:
- `clarify_no_topic_with_session_routes_to_diagnostic` вҖ” Intent::Unknown with no noun_hint + session with name+occupation; asserts trace contains the override AND realised text cites a stored slot AND output is NOT the bare clarify line.
- `clarify_no_topic_without_session_keeps_bare_family` вҖ” regression guard: empty session вҶ’ no override вҶ’ `unknown.clarify_no_topic` still wins.

### Real-REPL gap closure

| Pre-v4.53.5 | Post-v4.53.5 |
|---|---|
| Vague follow-up after В«РңРөРҪС–ТЈ Р°СӮСӢРј Р”УҷСғР»РөСӮ, РјРөРҪ РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°СҲСӢРјСӢРҪ ...В» вҶ’ В«РЎТұСҖР°Т“СӢТЈСӢР·РҙСӢ СӮРҫР»СӢТӣ СӮТҜСҒС–РҪРұРөРҙС–Рј. ТҡР°РҪРҙР°Р№ СӮР°ТӣСӢСҖСӢРҝ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ СҒТұСҖР°Рҝ РҫСӮСӢСҖСҒСӢР·? РҗР·РҙР°Рҝ РҪР°ТӣСӮСӢР»Р°Рҝ Р¶С–РұРөСҖС–ТЈС–Р·СҲС–.В» (generic, ignores 3 stored slots) | В«**Р”УҷСғР»РөСӮ, СҒС–Р·РҙС– РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°СҲСӢ РҙРөРҝ РұС–Р»РөРјС–РҪ. РЎТұСҖР°Т“СӢТЈСӢР· РјР°РјР°РҪРҙСӢТ“СӢТЈСӢР·Т“Р° ТӣР°СӮСӢСҒСӮСӢ РјР°, УҷР»РҙРө РұР°СҒТӣР° СӮР°ТӣСӢСҖСӢРҝСӮР° РјР°? РҗР·РҙР°Рҝ РҪР°ТӣСӮСӢР»Р°ТЈСӢР·СҲСӢ.**В» вң“ (cites name + occupation; offers concrete topic choice) |

## [4.53.0] вҖ” 2026-05-05 вҖ” Math gerund-clause parser (session 5 deferral closed)

**Closes the v4.52.0 deferral.** Real-REPL session 5 surfaced a gap in the multi-clause word-math parser: gerund/converb-form chains like В«Р•Р»СғРҙС– РөРәС–РіРө **РәУ©РұРөР№СӮРәРөРҪРҙРө** ТҜСҲРәРө **РұУ©Р»С–Рҝ**, 7-РҪС– **Р°Р·Р°Р№СӮТӣР°РҪРҙР°** РҪРө РұРҫР»Р°РҙСӢ?В» refused to evaluate, while the same operations in imperative form (В«... РәУ©РұРөР№СӮС–ТЈС–Р·, ... РұУ©Р»С–ТЈС–Р·, ... Р°Р·Р°Р№СӮСӢТЈСӢР·В») worked. v4.53.0 fixes the gap by injecting clause separators after gerund/converb math-verb forms, letting the v4.42.0 multi-clause evaluator chain operations across them.

### Innovations

**(1) `inject_gerund_clause_separators(input)`** вҖ” new pure helper in `discourse.rs`. Walks the input and inserts `__CLAUSE_SEP__` after each math-verb gerund/converb form. 8 surface forms covered: `РәУ©РұРөР№СӮРәРөРҪРҙРө` / `РәУ©РұРөР№СӮС–Рҝ` / `РұУ©Р»РіРөРҪРҙРө` / `РұУ©Р»С–Рҝ` / `ТӣРҫСҒТӣР°РҪРҙР°` / `ТӣРҫСҒСӢРҝ` / `Р°Р·Р°Р№СӮТӣР°РҪРҙР°` / `Р°Р·Р°Р№СӮСӢРҝ`. Token-boundary-safe (pads with leading/trailing spaces; only matches whole tokens).

**(2) Multi-clause gerund support** вҖ” `try_evaluate_kazakh_word_math` calls the helper before the existing `,` / `Р¶УҷРҪРө` / `СҒРҫРҙР°РҪ РәРөР№С–РҪ` / `СҒРҫТЈСӢРҪРҙР°` clause splits. Once gerund/converb forms become explicit clause boundaries, the v4.42.0 accumulator path handles the chain unchanged.

**(3) Vowel-harmony coverage** вҖ” gerund family `-Т“Р°РҪРҙР° / -РіРөРҪРҙРө / -ТӣР°РҪРҙР° / -РәРөРҪРҙРө` and converb family `-СӢРҝ / -С–Рҝ / -Рҝ` selected per stem according to standard Kazakh vowel harmony (РәУ©РұРөР№СӮ + voiceless СӮ вҶ’ -РәРөРҪРҙРө / -С–Рҝ; ТӣРҫСҒ + voiceless СҒ back-vowel вҶ’ -ТӣР°РҪРҙР° / -СӢРҝ; etc.).

**(4) 5 new unit tests** вҖ” `word_math_gerund_chain_session5_first_line` (the canonical session-5 case вҶ’ 26), `word_math_converb_only_chain` (pure -С–Рҝ chain), `word_math_gerund_only_chain` (pure -Т“Р°РҪРҙР° chain), `word_math_qosqanda_qosyp_forms` (add-form coverage), `word_math_gerund_does_not_break_simple_imperative` (regression guard).

### Real-REPL gap closure

| Pre-v4.53.0 | Post-v4.53.0 |
|---|---|
| В«Р•Р»СғРҙС– РөРәС–РіРө РәУ©РұРөР№СӮРәРөРҪРҙРө ТҜСҲРәРө РұУ©Р»С–Рҝ, 7-РҪС– Р°Р·Р°Р№СӮТӣР°РҪРҙР° РҪРө РұРҫР»Р°РҙСӢ?В» вҶ’ math_refusal | **В«Р•СҒРөРҝСӮРөРҙС–Рј: 26 (Р¶РёСӢСҖРјР° Р°Р»СӮСӢ)В»** вң“ |

### Verification

| Gate | Result |
|---|---|
| Workspace tests | **974 passing** (was 969; +5 new gerund tests) |
| Adam-dialog lib | **306 passing** (was 301; +5) |
| `bash scripts/verify_release_version.sh 4.53.0` | green |
| Live REPL session-5 line 1 | вң“ returns 26 |
| Foundation: 2089 / 2349 / 46 / 27175 | unchanged |
| `cargo fmt --all --check` | clean |
| `cargo build --release` | clean |

### Cadence

`.0` minor вҖ” adds new public function (effectively вҖ” `inject_gerund_clause_separators` is module-private but extends a public-API surface) and closes a concrete carry-forward deferral. Per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0`: В«minor x.y.0 = significant capability or milestoneВ». First arithmetic-grammar capability addition since v4.42.0.

## [4.52.5] вҖ” 2026-05-05 вҖ” Engineering hygiene + doc currency + weekly plan

**Driven by external code review (Codex, 2026-05-05).** Fixes broken release gate and stale docs that had silently drifted across ~15 minor versions. No new product behaviour; no new tests; no foundation changes. Pure hygiene.

### What was broken

- `bash scripts/verify_release_version.sh 4.52.0` failed: 7 data manifests pinned to v4.37.0 (stale since the v4.37.5 cadence). Release gate had been silently bypassed.
- README badge claimed 31 intents; the `Intent` enum has 33 variants (v4.51.0 added `StatementOfActivity` + `AskActivity`, never reflected in README).
- `data/README.md:18` listed world_core as В«874 entries / 995 facts across 30 domainsВ» (frozen at v4.4.7); actual counts: 2089 / 2349 / 46.
- `data/README.md:19` listed retrieval as В«15 521 facts / 21 415 derivationsВ»; actual: 2349 / 27175.
- `data/world_core/README.md:106,145` had В«Current scale (v4.3.0)В» and В«1625 entries (v4.11.7)В» as the canonical totals.
- `docs/foundation_scope.md:90` claimed В«822 workspace tests at v4.22.5В»; actual: 969 at v4.52.5.
- `docs/performance.md`, `docs/corpus_audit.md` headers stamped to old versions.
- `docs/kazakh_grammar/07_dialog_architecture.md:133` listed 26-intent taxonomy.
- `crates/adam-dialog/Cargo.toml` description said В«26-intent recogniser (v4.1.0)В».
- `crates/adam-dialog/src/lib.rs` docstring said В«Stage: v3.9.5 вҖ” 26-intentВ».
- 5 crates had no `description` field in Cargo.toml: adam-kernel, adam-tokenizer, adam-corpus, adam-eval, adam-retrieval.

### What this release does

**(1)** Bumps 7 data manifests v4.37.0 вҶ’ v4.52.5: `data/eval/benchmark_manifest.json`, `data/eval/kazakh_foundation_eval_dataset.json`, `data/eval/tokenizer_experiment_manifest.json`, `data/eval/tokenizer_segmentation_eval_dataset.json`, `data/tokenizer/segmentation_roots.json`, `data/tokenizer/segmentation_rules.json`, `data/training/baseline_training_manifest.json`.

**(2)** Re-stamps every numeric claim to live ground truth: 969 tests, 33 intents, 2089/2349/46/27175 foundation totals, p50 1.07 ms.

**(3)** Adds `description` to 5 missing crate Cargo.toml files (adam-kernel, adam-tokenizer, adam-corpus, adam-eval, adam-retrieval).

**(4)** Updates `data/world_core/README.md` to surface live totals at the top of the В«Current scaleВ» section and explicitly mark the per-domain breakdown as a v4.11.7 historical snapshot.

**(5)** New `docs/weekly_plan_2026-W19.md` вҖ” concrete day-by-day deliverables for the week (W19, 2026-05-05 вҶ’ 2026-05-11): math gerund-clause parser вҶ’ context-aware clarify вҶ’ live REPL session 6 вҶ’ Lexicon + bridge-fact expansion вҶ’ clippy hygiene вҶ’ metrics-currency CI gate. Aligned with `project_v4_direction` (no investors) and `feedback_real_human_testing_with_memory`.

### Verification

| Gate | Result |
|---|---|
| `cargo test --release --workspace` | **969 passing** unchanged |
| `bash scripts/verify_release_version.sh 4.52.5` | **green** (was failing) |
| `cargo fmt --all --check` | clean |
| `cargo build --release` | clean |
| Foundation: 2089 / 2349 / 46 / 27175 | unchanged |

### Cadence

`.5` patch вҖ” pure hygiene. Per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0`: В«patch = small / incremental (bug fixes, small Lexicon additions, docs, housekeeping)В». No code-path changes; CI invariant restored; doc drift erased.

Stripe (11) вҖ” generative AI via agglutinative composition.

## [4.52.0] вҖ” 2026-05-05 вҖ” Transcript-driven session 5 fixes вҖ” continuous-form activity + compound occupation + demonstrative math anaphora

**Driven by 2026-05-05 user dialog test session 5.** v4.51.5 closed the participle-form activity case; session 5 surfaced four new gaps that v4.52.0 closes:

1. **Continuous-form activity not captured** вҖ” В«...Р¶Р°СҒР°РҪРҙСӢ РёРҪСӮРөР»Р»РөРәСӮ РјРҫРҙРөР»С–РјРҙС– **Р¶Р°СҒР°Рҝ Р¶Р°СӮСӢСҖРјСӢРҪ**В» encodes the user's activity in a converb + present-progressive auxiliary (В«Р¶Р°СҒР°Рҝ Р¶Р°СӮСӢСҖРјСӢРҪВ» = "I am making"). v4.51.5's `ACTIVITY_VERBS` covered finite-1sg and aorist-participle forms but missed the continuous form. Result: activity not stored.
2. **Compound occupation in non-occupation primary intent** вҖ” В«РңРөРҪС–ТЈ Р°СӮСӢРј Р”УҷСғР»РөСӮ, РјРөРҪ РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°СҲСӢРјСӢРҪ ...В» fired `Intent::StatementOfName` (priority winner); the secondary scan rescued activity but never tried occupation. Result: `mamandДұДҹДұm qandai?` follow-up couldn't recall occupation.
3. **AskActivity self-recall too narrow** вҖ” В«РңРөРҪС–ТЈ **РҪРөРҪС– РҙР°РјСӢСӮР°СӮСӢРҪСӢРј** РөСҒС–ТЈС–Р·РҙРө РјРө?В» ("what am I developing?") used the В«РҙР°РјСӢСӮСғВ» root + 1sg-poss participle form. Pre-v4.52.0 self-recall list only covered В«С–СҒСӮРө*В» stem; missed В«РҙР°РјСӢСӮР°СӮСӢРҪВ», В«УҷР·С–СҖР»РөР№СӮС–РҪВ», В«Р¶Р°СҒР°Р№СӮСӢРҪВ», В«Р¶Р°Р·Р°СӮСӢРҪВ», В«Р·РөСҖСӮСӮРөР№СӮС–РҪВ», В«ТӣТұСҖР°СҒСӮСӢСҖР°СӮСӢРҪВ» variants.
4. **Math anaphora limited to В«Р°Р»РҙСӢТЈТ“СӢВ»** вҖ” В«Р•РҪРҙС– **РұТұР» РҪУҷСӮРёР¶РөРҪС–** 4-РәРө РұУ©Р»С–ТЈС–Р·В» used the demonstrative В«РұТұР»В» ("this result") instead of В«Р°Р»РҙСӢТЈТ“СӢВ» ("previous"). v4.51.5 anaphora resolver didn't handle it.

### Real-REPL gap closure (session 5 transcript replay)

| Pre-v4.52.0 transcript query | Pre | Post |
|---|---|---|
| РңРөРҪС–ТЈ Р°СӮСӢРј Р”УҷСғР»РөСӮ, РјРөРҪ РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°СҲСӢРјСӢРҪ Р¶УҷРҪРө У©Р·С–РјРҪС–ТЈ Р¶Р°СҒР°РҪРҙСӢ РёРҪСӮРөР»Р»РөРәСӮ РјРҫРҙРөР»С–РјРҙС– Р¶Р°СҒР°Рҝ Р¶Р°СӮСӢСҖРјСӢРҪ. | only `name` captured | name + occupation + activity all captured вң“ |
| РңРөРҪС–ТЈ РҪРөРҪС– РҙР°РјСӢСӮР°СӮСӢРҪСӢРј РөСҒС–ТЈС–Р·РҙРө РјРө? | clarify | "**Р–Р°СҒР°РҪРҙСӢ РёРҪСӮРөР»Р»РөРәСӮ РјРҫРҙРөР» вҖ” СҒС–Р·РҙС–ТЈ ТӣР°Р·С–СҖРіС– С–СҒС–ТЈС–Р· РҙРөРҝ Р¶Р°РҙСӢРјР° СӮТҜСҒС–СҖРҙС–Рј.**" вң“ |
| РңРөРҪС–ТЈ РјР°РјР°РҪРҙСӢТ“СӢРј ТӣР°РҪРҙР°Р№? | "РңРөРҪ СҒУ©Р№Р»РөСҒСғРіРө Р¶Р°СҖР°Р»Т“Р°РҪРјСӢРҪ" (no slot, fallback) | "**РңРөРҪС–ТЈ РұС–Р»СғС–РјСҲРө, РјР°РјР°РҪРҙСӢТ“СӢТЈСӢР· РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°СҲСӢ.**" вң“ |
| Р•РҪРҙС– РұТұР» РҪУҷСӮРёР¶РөРҪС– 4-РәРө РұУ©Р»С–ТЈС–Р·. | math_refusal (anaphora unresolved) | **6** вң“ (26 Г· 4 truncated) |

### Innovations

**(1) Continuous-form activity detection** вҖ” new `CONTINUOUS_CONVERBS` array (8 stems: УҷР·С–СҖР»РөРҝ / Р¶Р°СҒР°Рҝ / Р¶Р°Р·СӢРҝ / Р·РөСҖСӮСӮРөРҝ / ТӣТұСҖР°СҒСӮСӢСҖСӢРҝ / Р°Р№РҪР°Р»СӢСҒСӢРҝ / РҙР°РјСӢСӮСӢРҝ / ТӣТұСҖСӢРҝ). Bigram match: when a converb token is found AND the next token starts with В«Р¶Р°СӮСӢСҖВ» (covers В«Р¶Р°СӮСӢСҖРјСӢРҪВ» 1sg / В«Р¶Р°СӮСӢСҖРјСӢР·В» 1pl), treat the converb as the verb position; walk back to extract object.

**(2) Two new activity-verb roots** вҖ” В«РҙР°РјСӢСӮСғВ» (develop) and В«ТӣТұСҖСғВ» (build/establish). 4 surface forms added to `ACTIVITY_VERBS`: РҙР°РјСӢСӮР°РјСӢРҪ / ТӣТұСҖР°РјСӢРҪ (finite) + РҙР°РјСӢСӮР°СӮСӢРҪ / ТӣТұСҖР°СӮСӢРҪ (participle).

**(3) `detect_occupation_in_compound(input, lexicon)`** вҖ” new pure function in `semantics.rs`. Mirrors `detect_activity_in_compound`. Tokenises the lowered input, calls `strip_copula_and_lookup_noun` against the lexicon for the priority path, falls back to a 15-entry fixed-table lookup when no lexicon is provided. Returns `Some(Some(root))` when a noun + 1sg-copula form is identifiable.

**(4) `Conversation::turn` secondary occupation scan** вҖ” runs after the primary intent absorption + activity scan. When the primary intent is NOT `StatementOfOccupation` AND the session doesn't already have an `occupation` slot, calls `detect_occupation_in_compound`. Captures occupation into session + belief graph. Lets a single utterance like В«РңРөРҪС–ТЈ Р°СӮСӢРј X, РјРөРҪ Y-РјСӢРҪВ» populate both `name` AND `occupation` slots.

**(5) AskActivity self-recall extended** вҖ” `detect_ask_activity` now treats any В«РјРөРҪС–ТЈВ» + activity-participle stem as 1sg self-recall. New stems added: РҙР°РјСӢСӮР°СӮСӢРҪ / УҷР·С–СҖР»РөР№СӮС–РҪ / Р¶Р°СҒР°Р№СӮСӢРҪ / Р¶Р°Р·Р°СӮСӢРҪ / Р·РөСҖСӮСӮРөР№СӮС–РҪ / ТӣТұСҖР°СҒСӮСӢСҖР°СӮСӢРҪ. Pre-v4.52.0 only В«С–СҒСӮРөР№СӮС–РҪВ» was matched.

**(6) Demonstrative math anaphora В«РұТұР» РҪУҷСӮРёР¶РөВ»** вҖ” `Conversation::turn` math anaphora resolver gains a third anaphora family alongside В«Р°Р»РҙСӢТЈТ“СӢВ» / В«СҒРҫТЈТ“СӢВ»: В«РұТұР» (РөСҒРөРҝСӮРөСғ )?РҪУҷСӮРёР¶РөВ» with full case-suffix preservation (Acc / Dat). Verified in transcript: В«Р•РҪРҙС– РұТұР» РҪУҷСӮРёР¶РөРҪС– 4-РәРө РұУ©Р»С–ТЈС–Р·В» now resolves to the prior result.

### Verification

| Gate | Result |
|---|---|
| Workspace tests | **969 passing** unchanged |
| Adam-dialog lib | **301 passing** unchanged |
| Live REPL transcript replay | вң“ all 4 session-5 scenarios verified end-to-end |
| Foundation: 2089 / 2349 / 46 / 27175 | unchanged |
| `cargo fmt --all --check` | clean |
| `cargo build --release` | clean |

### Cadence

`.0` minor вҖ” adds new public function (`detect_occupation_in_compound`), new architectural primitive (compound-clause occupation rescue mirroring v4.51.0 activity rescue), and 4 distinct detector innovations. Per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0`: В«minor x.y.0 = significant capability or milestoneВ». Closes 4 concrete session-5 transcript gaps + materially improves dialog continuity (single-utterance multi-slot capture is the v4.x context-aware-dialog directive).

Stripe (11) вҖ” generative AI via agglutinative composition.

## [4.51.5] вҖ” 2026-05-05 вҖ” Transcript-driven session 4 fixes вҖ” participle activity + math anaphora + vowel-initial respect form

**Driven by 2026-05-04 user dialog test session 4.** v4.51.0 closed the compound-clause activity case; session 4 surfaced three new gaps that v4.51.5 closes:

1. **Activity participle form not captured** вҖ” В«РңРөРҪ У©Р·С–РјРҪС–ТЈ Р¶Р°СҒР°РҪРҙСӢ РёРҪСӮРөР»Р»РөРәСӮ РјРҫРҙРөР»С–РјРҙС– **УҷР·С–СҖР»РөР№СӮС–РҪ** РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°СҲСӢРјСӢРҪВ» encodes the user's activity in an aorist-participle modifier (-Р№СӮС–РҪ suffix) rather than a finite 1sg verb. v4.51.0's `detect_statement_of_activity` only matched 6 finite stems (В«УҷР·С–СҖР»РөР№РјС–РҪВ» etc.) and missed all participle forms. Result: activity not stored.
2. **Math anaphora unresolved** вҖ” В«Р‘РөСҒСӮС– СӮУ©СҖСӮРәРө РәУ©РұРөР№СӮС–Рҝ, РөРәС–РіРө РұУ©Р»С–ТЈС–Р·В» вҶ’ 10. Follow-up В«Р•РҪРҙС– **Р°Р»РҙСӢТЈТ“СӢ РөСҒРөРҝСӮРөСғ РҪУҷСӮРёР¶РөСҒС–РҪ** РөРәС–РіРө РәУ©РұРөР№СӮС–ТЈС–Р·В» needs the previous result substituted. Pre-v4.51.5: math evaluator received the literal string and refused.
3. **Vowel-initial respect form** вҖ” `kazakh_respectful_address` returned None for vowel-initial names like В«РҗСҖРјР°РҪВ», forcing planner fallback to the instrumental template. Per cultural rule (older addressee, vowel-initial root): take the first two letters and append В«УҷРәРөВ» вҖ” РҗСҖРјР°РҪ вҶ’ РҗСҖУҷРәРө, РҗР»РёСҸ вҶ’ РҗР»УҷРәРө, РҗР№РіТҜР» вҶ’ РҗР№УҷРәРө.

### Real-REPL gap closure (session 4 transcript replay)

| Pre-v4.51.5 transcript query | Pre | Post |
|---|---|---|
| РҳУҷ, РјРөРҪС–ТЈ Р°СӮСӢРј РЎРөСҖРіРөР№. | "РЎРөСҖРіРөР№РјРөРҪ СӮР°РҪСӢСҒТӣР°РҪСӢРјР° ТӣСғР°РҪСӢСҲСӮСӢРјСӢРҪ" (instrumental fallback) | "**РЎУҷРәРө, СӮР°РҪСӢСҒТӣР°РҪСӢРјР° ТӣСғР°РҪСӢСҲСӮСӢРјСӢРҪ.**" вң“ |
| РңРөРҪ У©Р·С–РјРҪС–ТЈ Р¶Р°СҒР°РҪРҙСӢ РёРҪСӮРөР»Р»РөРәСӮ РјРҫРҙРөР»С–РјРҙС– УҷР·С–СҖР»РөР№СӮС–РҪ РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°СҲСӢРјСӢРҪ. | only `occupation` captured | both captured: `occupation=РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°СҲСӢ` AND `activity=Р¶Р°СҒР°РҪРҙСӢ РёРҪСӮРөР»Р»РөРәСӮ РјРҫРҙРөР»` вң“ |
| УЁСҖРҪРөРәСӮС– РұРөСҒСӮС– СӮУ©СҖСӮРәРө РәУ©РұРөР№СӮС–Рҝ, РөРәС–РіРө РұУ©Р»С–ТЈС–Р·. | 10 | 10 вң“ (unchanged; baseline) |
| Р•РҪРҙС– Р°Р»РҙСӢТЈТ“СӢ РөСҒРөРҝСӮРөСғ РҪУҷСӮРёР¶РөСҒС–РҪ РөРәС–РіРө РәУ©РұРөР№СӮС–ТЈС–Р·. | math_refusal (anaphora unresolved) | **20** вң“ |
| РңРөРҪС–ТЈ РҪРө С–СҒСӮРөР№СӮС–РҪС–Рј РөСҒС–ТЈС–Р·РҙРө РјРө? | clarify | "**РңРөРҪС–ТЈ РөСҒС–РјРҙРө, Р°Т“СӢРјРҙР°Т“СӢ С–СҒС–ТЈС–Р· вҖ” Р¶Р°СҒР°РҪРҙСӢ РёРҪСӮРөР»Р»РөРәСӮ РјРҫРҙРөР».**" вң“ |

### Innovations

**(1) Activity participle detection** вҖ” extended `ACTIVITY_VERBS` array with 6 aorist-participle forms: В«УҷР·С–СҖР»РөР№СӮС–РҪВ», В«Р¶Р°СҒР°Р№СӮСӢРҪВ», В«Р¶Р°Р·Р°СӮСӢРҪВ», В«Р·РөСҖСӮСӮРөР№СӮС–РҪВ», В«Р°Р№РҪР°Р»СӢСҒР°СӮСӢРҪВ», В«ТӣТұСҖР°СҒСӮСӢСҖР°СӮСӢРҪВ». Same backward-walk object extraction; added boundary tokens В«У©Р·С–РјРҪС–ТЈВ», В«У©Р·С–ТЈРҪС–ТЈВ», В«У©Р·С–РјС–Р·РҙС–ТЈВ» to the walk-back stop list (so reflexive-possessive prefixes don't eat the activity content).

**(2) Two-pass case-suffix stripping** вҖ” `strip_object_case_suffix` now strips Accusative, then Possessive (В«-С–Рј / -СӢРј / -СҒС– / -СҒСӢВ»), so В«РјРҫРҙРөР»С–РјРҙС–В» (model-1sg.poss-acc) decomposes to В«РјРҫРҙРөР»СҢВ» (head noun, lemma form preserved as В«РјРҫРҙРөР»В» after suffix peel).

**(3) Same-clause participle path in compound parser** вҖ” v4.51.0 `detect_activity_in_compound` only fired on В«Р¶УҷРҪРөВ». v4.51.5 adds a no-В«Р¶УҷРҪРөВ» path: when activity is encoded in same-clause participle modifier (В«X-РҙСӢ Y-РөСӮС–РҪ Z-РјСӢРҪВ»), the detector runs on the full input.

**(4) `Intent::AskActivity` extended for В«РҪРө С–СҒСӮРөР№СӮС–РҪС–РјВ» / В«РҪРө С–СҒСӮРөР№СӮС–РҪС–РјРҙС–В»** вҖ” adds 1sg participle-self-recall variants alongside the existing В«РҪРө С–СҒСӮРөР№РјС–РҪВ» finite form.

**(5) `kazakh_respectful_address` vowel-initial branch** вҖ” when first character is a vowel (Р°/Уҷ/Рҫ/У©/Сғ/Тұ/СӢ/С–/Рө/СҚ/СҺ/СҸ/С‘), uses first 2 chars + В«УҷРәРөВ». Returns None only when name < 3 chars or 2nd char non-alphabetic. All 11 Kazakh vowel-initial names verified: РҗРұР°Р№вҶ’РҗРұУҷРәРө, РҗР»РёСҸвҶ’РҗР»УҷРәРө, РҗР№РіТҜР»вҶ’РҗР№УҷРәРө, РҗРјР°РҪвҶ’РҗРјУҷРәРө, УҳР»РөРјвҶ’УҳР»УҷРәРө, Р•СҖРјРөРәвҶ’Р•СҖУҷРәРө, Р«СҒТӣР°ТӣвҶ’Р«СҒУҷРәРө, РһР»Р¶Р°СҒвҶ’РһР»УҷРәРө, Т®СҒРөРҪвҶ’Т®СҒУҷРәРө.

**(6) Math anaphora resolution** вҖ” `Conversation::turn` adds a `last_math_result` session slot populated after each successful word-math evaluation. Before the next math call, the input is rewritten: В«Р°Р»РҙСӢТЈТ“СӢ (РөСҒРөРҝСӮРөСғ )?РҪУҷСӮРёР¶РөРҪС–В» / В«Р°Р»РҙСӢТЈТ“СӢ (РөСҒРөРҝСӮРөСғ )?РҪУҷСӮРёР¶РөСҒС–РҪВ» / В«Р°Р»РҙСӢТЈТ“СӢ (РөСҒРөРҝСӮРөСғ )?РҪУҷСӮРёР¶РөСҒС–РҪРөВ» / В«Р°Р»РҙСӢТЈТ“СӢ (РөСҒРөРҝСӮРөСғ )?РҪУҷСӮРёР¶РөСҒС–РҪРҙРөВ» вҶ’ digit-form with case-suffix preserved (В«10-РҪС–В» Acc / В«10-РіРөВ» Dat / В«10-РҙРөВ» Loc), so the digit-prefix-Acc parser (v4.41.7) reads it correctly.

**(7) New `statement_of_name` variant** вҖ” В«РЎС–Р·РҙС– {name_respect} РҙРөРҝ Р°СӮР°Р№РјСӢРҪ, ТӣРҫСҲ РәРөР»РҙС–ТЈС–Р·.В» Reachable when `{name_respect_distinct}` is set (now true for vowel-initial names too via innovation 5).

### Verification

| Gate | Result |
|---|---|
| Workspace tests | **969 passing** unchanged |
| Adam-dialog lib | **301 passing** unchanged |
| Live REPL transcript replay | вң“ all 5 session-4 scenarios verified |
| Foundation: 2089 / 2349 / 46 / unchanged | unchanged |
| `cargo fmt --all --check` | clean |
| `cargo build --release` | clean |

### Cadence

`.5` patch вҖ” incremental gap-closure on top of v4.51.0 architectural addition. Per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0`: В«patch = small / incrementalВ». No new Intent variants, no new template families, no new session schema; this release extends existing detectors (participle forms, vowel-initial respect, math anaphora resolver). Closes 3 concrete session-4 transcript gaps.

Stripe (11) вҖ” generative AI via agglutinative composition.

## [4.51.0] вҖ” 2026-05-04 вҖ” User-activity slot extraction (Stage A architectural addition)

**Closes the v4.50.5 carry-forward.** Adam now distinguishes the user's profession label (`occupation` вҖ” В«РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°СҲСӢВ», В«РёРҪР¶РөРҪРөСҖВ») from their current-work content (`activity` вҖ” В«Р¶Р°СҒР°РҪРҙСӢ РёРҪСӮРөР»Р»РөРәСӮВ», В«РІРөРұ-СҒР°Р№СӮ Р¶Р°СҒР°СғВ»). Pre-v4.51.0 a compound input like В«РңРөРҪ РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°СҲСӢРјСӢРҪ Р¶УҷРҪРө Р¶Р°СҒР°РҪРҙСӢ РёРҪСӮРөР»Р»РөРәСӮ УҷР·С–СҖР»РөР№РјС–РҪВ» captured only the occupation; v4.51.0 captures both via a secondary compound-clause scan. Adam can now recall activity on follow-up queries (В«РқРө С–СҒСӮРөР№СҒС–Р·?В» / В«РңРөРҪС–ТЈ РҪРө С–СҒСӮРөР№СӮС–РҪС–Рј РөСҒС–ТЈС–Р·РҙРө РјРө?В»).

### Real-REPL gap closure (user transcript replay)

| Pre-v4.51.0 transcript query | Pre | Post |
|---|---|---|
| РңРөРҪ РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°СҲСӢРјСӢРҪ Р¶УҷРҪРө Р¶Р°СҒР°РҪРҙСӢ РёРҪСӮРөР»Р»РөРәСӮ УҷР·С–СҖР»РөР№РјС–РҪ. | "Р‘Р°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°СҲСӢ РөРәРөРҪ, СӮТҜСҒС–РҪРҙС–Рј" (only occupation captured) | both captured: `occupation=РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°СҲСӢ` AND `activity=Р¶Р°СҒР°РҪРҙСӢ РёРҪСӮРөР»Р»РөРәСӮ` вң“ |
| РңРөРҪС–ТЈ РҪРө С–СҒСӮРөР№СӮС–РҪС–Рј РөСҒС–ТЈС–Р·РҙРө РјРө? | clarify ("СҒТұСҖР°Т“СӢТЈСӢР·РҙСӢ РҪР°ТӣСӮСӢР»Р°РҝвҖҰ") | "**Р–Р°СҒР°РҪРҙСӢ РёРҪСӮРөР»Р»РөРәСӮ вҖ” СҒС–Р·РҙС–ТЈ ТӣР°Р·С–СҖРіС– С–СҒС–ТЈС–Р· РҙРөРҝ Р¶Р°РҙСӢРјР° СӮТҜСҒС–СҖРҙС–Рј.**" вң“ |
| РқРө С–СҒСӮРөР№СҒС–Р·? | (didn't engage) | "**РңРөРҪС–ТЈ РөСҒС–РјРҙРө, Р°Т“СӢРјРҙР°Т“СӢ С–СҒС–ТЈС–Р· вҖ” Р¶Р°СҒР°РҪРҙСӢ РёРҪСӮРөР»Р»РөРәСӮ.**" вң“ |

### Innovations

**(1) `Intent::StatementOfActivity { activity: Option<String> }`** вҖ” new variant. Distinct from `StatementOfOccupation` (profession label) in that activity captures the CURRENT-WORK-CONTENT.

**(2) `Intent::AskActivity`** вҖ” companion. Detected by patterns В«РҪРө С–СҒСӮРөР№СҒС–Р· / РҪРө С–СҒСӮРөР№СҒС–ТЈВ» (В«what are you doing?В»), В«РҪРө УҷР·С–СҖР»РөРҝ Р¶Р°СӮСӢСҖСҒСӢР·В», В«РҪРөРјРөРҪ Р°Р№РҪР°Р»СӢСҒ*В», В«РјРөРҪС–ТЈ С–СҒС–Рј / РјРөРҪС–ТЈ РҪРө С–СҒСӮРөР№СӮС–РҪС–РјВ» (1sg self-recall).

**(3) `IntentKind::StatementOfActivity` + `IntentKind::AskActivity`** вҖ” companion enum variants for trace + history.

**(4) `detect_statement_of_activity(tokens, joined)`** вҖ” pure-function detector in `semantics.rs`. Recognizes 6 activity-verb stems (1sg-conjugated): В«УҷР·С–СҖР»РөР№РјС–РҪВ» / В«Р¶Р°СҒР°Р№РјСӢРҪВ» / В«Р¶Р°Р·Р°РјСӢРҪВ» / В«Р·РөСҖСӮСӮРөР№РјС–РҪВ» / В«Р°Р№РҪР°Р»СӢСҒР°РјСӢРҪВ» / В«ТӣТұСҖР°СҒСӮСӢСҖР°РјСӢРҪВ». Object extraction: walks backwards from the verb position, stops at clause boundaries (В«Р¶УҷРҪРөВ», В«Р°Р»В», В«СҒРҫРҙР°РҪВ», В«РұС–СҖР°ТӣВ», В«СҒРҫРҪРҙСӢТӣСӮР°РҪВ», В«РјРөРҪВ», В«РјРөРҪС–ТЈВ») or commas, joins remaining tokens. Strips Accusative (В«-РҙСӢ/-РҙС–/-РҪСӢ/-РҪС–/-СӮСӢ/-СӮС–В») / Comitative (В«-РјРөРҪ/-РҝРөРҪ/-РұРөРҪВ») case suffixes from the head noun. Falls back to В«Р¶ТұРјСӢСҒ С–СҒСӮРөР№РјС–РҪВ» bare-verb pattern (returns `Some(None)`).

**(5) `detect_ask_activity(joined)`** вҖ” pure-function ask-detector. Recognizes 2nd-person query forms (В«РҪРө С–СҒСӮРөР№*В», В«РҪРө УҷР·С–СҖР»РөРҝВ», В«РҪРө Р¶Р°СҒР°РҝВ», В«РҪРөРјРөРҪ Р°Р№РҪР°Р»СӢСҒ*В») gated against the В«РәУҷСҒС–РұВ» fragment (so В«РәУҷСҒС–РұС–ТЈС–Р· РҪРө?В» stays in `AskOccupation`). Plus 1sg self-recall: В«РјРөРҪС–ТЈ С–СҒС–РјВ» / В«РјРөРҪС–ТЈ РҪРө С–СҒСӮРөР№СӮС–РҪС–РјВ» / В«РҪРө С–СҒСӮРөР№РјС–РҪВ».

**(6) Compound-input parser `detect_activity_in_compound(input)`** вҖ” public-crate scan called by `Conversation::turn` AFTER primary intent absorption. When input contains В«Р¶УҷРҪРөВ» token, splits on it and runs `detect_statement_of_activity` on the post-В«Р¶УҷРҪРөВ» clause. Captures activity into session even when the primary detector fired `StatementOfOccupation`. Closes the В«РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°СҲСӢ AND Р¶Р°СҒР°РҪРҙСӢ РёРҪСӮРөР»Р»РөРәСӮВ» compound case.

**(7) Session slot `activity`** вҖ” populated in `absorb_entities` (direct `StatementOfActivity` path) AND in the secondary compound scan. Mirrors the `occupation` slot machinery.

**(8) Belief-graph integration** вҖ” activity facts recorded via `belief.record_user_fact(USER_SELF_KEY, "activity", &activity, turn_id)`. Enables future audit-side queries.

**(9) 2 new template families**:
- `statement_of_activity` (5 variants) вҖ” acknowledges activity statements; includes `{name_respect}` for respect-form acks.
- `ask_activity` + `ask_activity.with_known_user` (3 variants each) вҖ” ask-without-data and slot-aware-recall variants. Mirrors `ask_occupation` family pattern.

**(10) Planner integration**:
- `intent_key`: 2 new mappings (`AskActivity` вҶ’ `"ask_activity"`, `StatementOfActivity { .. }` вҶ’ `"statement_of_activity"`).
- `extract_slots`: populates `{activity}` slot from `StatementOfActivity`.
- `plan_response_with_epistemic`: known-user override `(Intent::AskActivity, _) if session.contains_key("activity") вҶ’ "ask_activity.with_known_user"`. Mirrors v4.2.5 `AskOccupation` pattern.

### Verification

| Gate | Result |
|---|---|
| Workspace tests | **969 passing** unchanged |
| Adam-dialog lib | **301 passing** unchanged |
| Live REPL transcript replay | вң“ all 4 scenarios verified (compound capture + 2 recall paths + Р”УҷРәРө-form) |
| Foundation: 2089 / 2349 / 46 / unchanged | unchanged |
| `cargo fmt --all --check` | clean |
| `cargo check --all-targets` | clean |

### Cadence

`.0` minor вҖ” Stage A architectural addition. Per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0`: В«minor x.y.0 = significant capability or milestoneВ». New Intent variant + new IntentKind + 2 detectors + compound parser + session-schema addition + 2 template families + planner overrides = kernel-signature dialog-memory expansion. Closes a concrete v4.50.5-transcript carry-forward.

Stage A status (post v4.51.0):
- v4.42.0вҖ“v4.43.6 вҖ” NLG foundation + production migration + introducer migration + 11/11 predicate coverage вң“
- v4.43.7вҖ“v4.44.5 вҖ” knowledge breadth + matcher cleanup вң“
- v4.50.5 вҖ” math truncation + respectful address + math vocabulary вң“
- **v4.51.0 вҖ” user-activity slot extraction** вҶҗ shipped

Stage B status: v4.45.0вҖ“v4.50.0 substrate complete + audit baseline shifted to trained_v0; production ranker replacement gated on eval validation (v4.50.5+ deferred).

Stripe (11) вҖ” generative AI via agglutinative composition.

## [4.50.5] вҖ” 2026-05-04 вҖ” Transcript-driven regression fixes вҖ” math truncation + respectful address + math vocabulary

**Driven by 2026-05-04 user dialog test session 3.** Three concrete gaps surfaced in the transcript:
1. Kazakh word-form math refused on simple non-integer cases (В«Р‘РөСҒСӮС– Р¶РөСӮС–РіРө РәУ©РұРөР№СӮС–Рҝ, РөРәС–РіРө РұУ©Р»С–ТЈС–Р·В» = 35/2 вҖ” should answer 17, was math_refusal).
2. Digit-form `:` operator handled but produced `None` on intermediate non-integer step (В«5+5*2:4-3В» = 5+10/4-3 вҖ” fractional 2.5 caused refusal).
3. Adam addressed the user by literal name В«Р”УҷСғР»РөСӮВ» instead of Kazakh respectful form В«Р”УҷРәРөВ» вҖ” culturally inconsistent with the user's training expectation.

Plus one missing knowledge gap: В«РҡРІР°РҙСҖР°СӮ ТҜСҲРјТҜСҲРөВ» (quadratic trinomial) had no curated fact.

### Real-REPL gap closures

| Pre-v4.50.5 transcript query | Pre | Post |
|---|---|---|
| Р‘РөСҒСӮС– Р¶РөСӮС–РіРө РәУ©РұРөР№СӮС–Рҝ, РөРәС–РіРө РұУ©Р»С–ТЈС–Р·. | math_refusal template | "**РқУҷСӮРёР¶РөСҒС–: 17 (РҫРҪ Р¶РөСӮС–)**" вң“ |
| 5+5*2:4-3 | math_refusal template | "**Р•СҒРөРҝСӮРөРҙС–Рј: 4 (СӮУ©СҖСӮ)**" вң“ |
| 5*7/2 | math_refusal template | "**Р–Р°СғР°РұСӢ вҖ” 17 (РҫРҪ Р¶РөСӮС–)**" вң“ |
| РңРөРҪС–ТЈ Р°СӮСӢРј Р”УҷСғР»РөСӮ. | "Р”УҷСғР»РөСӮ, СӮР°РҪСӢСҒТӣР°РҪСӢРјР° ТӣСғР°РҪСӢСҲСӮСӢРјСӢРҪ" (literal) | "**Р”УҷСғР»РөСӮ РҙРөРіРөРҪ Р°СӮСӢТЈСӢР·РҙСӢ РөСҒСӮРө СҒР°ТӣСӮР°Рҝ, СҒС–Р·РҙС– Р”УҷРәРө РҙРөРҝ Р°СӮР°Р№РјСӢРҪ вҖ” ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣСҲР°.**" (respect-form by default) |
| РҡРІР°РҙСҖР°СӮ ТҜСҲРјТҜСҲРө РҙРөРіРөРҪС–РјС–Р· РҪРө? | clarify | "**РҡРІР°РҙСҖР°СӮ ТҜСҲРјТҜСҲРө вҖ” axВІ + bx + c СӮТҜСҖС–РҪРҙРөРіС– РөРәС–РҪСҲС– РҙУҷСҖРөР¶РөР»С– ТҜСҲ РјТҜСҲРөРҙРөРҪ СӮТұСҖР°СӮСӢРҪ РјР°СӮРөРјР°СӮРёРәР°Р»СӢТӣ У©СҖРҪРөРә.**" вң“ |

### Innovations

**(1) Truncated integer division** in `try_evaluate_arithmetic` вҖ” pre-v4.50.5 the digit-form evaluator returned `None` for non-integer divisions (e.g., `7/2`), forcing the math_refusal template. v4.50.5 truncates to integer (`7/2 вҶ’ 3`, `5*7/2 вҶ’ 17`) matching Kazakh-conversational expectations. Division by zero remains `None` (still invalid).

**(2) Truncated integer division** in `try_evaluate_kazakh_word_math` вҖ” mirrors fix (1) for the word-form evaluator. В«Р–РөСӮС–РҪС– РөРәС–РіРө РұУ©Р»В» now returns `Some(3)` (was `None`). Multi-clause word-form queries that previously hit a non-integer intermediate step now compute through.

**(3) Respect-form-by-default `statement_of_name` template family** вҖ” replaced the v4.18.0 4-template family (3 literal-name + 1 respect-aware) with a 4-template family where 3 of 4 variants gate on `{name_respect_distinct}`. For consonant-initial names (Р”УҷСғР»РөСӮ вҶ’ Р”УҷРәРө, РңТұСҖР°СӮ вҶ’ РңУҷРәРө, РЎУҷРәРөРҪ вҶ’ РЎУҷРәРө) all 4 are admissible and the planner cycles among them; ~75 % of seeds produce a respect-form acknowledgment. For vowel-initial names where the diminutive collapses (РҗРұР°Р№, РҗР»РёСҸ), only the instrumental fallback (В«РҗРұР°Р№РјРөРҪ СӮР°РҪСӢСҒТӣР°РҪСӢРјР°вҖҰВ») remains fillable вҖ” preserving v4.18.0 behavior exactly for those cases.

**(4) Math vocabulary depth** вҖ” 3 new facts in `mathematics_basic.jsonl`:
- math_053: В«РҡРІР°РҙСҖР°СӮ ТҜСҲРјТҜСҲРө вҖ” axВІ + bx + c СӮТҜСҖС–РҪРҙРөРіС– РөРәС–РҪСҲС– РҙУҷСҖРөР¶РөР»С– ТҜСҲ РјТҜСҲРөРҙРөРҪ СӮТұСҖР°СӮСӢРҪ РјР°СӮРөРјР°СӮРёРәР°Р»СӢТӣ У©СҖРҪРөРә.В»
- math_054: В«РҡУ©РҝРјТҜСҲРө вҖ” РұС–СҖРҪРөСҲРө РјТҜСҲРөРҙРөРҪ ТӣТұСҖР°Р»Т“Р°РҪ РјР°СӮРөРјР°СӮРёРәР°Р»СӢТӣ У©СҖРҪРөРә.В»
- math_055: В«Т®СҲРјТҜСҲРө вҖ” ТҜСҲ РјТҜСҲРөРҙРөРҪ ТӣТұСҖР°Р»Т“Р°РҪ РјР°СӮРөРјР°СӮРёРәР°Р»СӢТӣ У©СҖРҪРөРә.В»

**(5) `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES` += 1** вҖ” В«РәРІР°РҙСҖР°СӮ ТҜСҲРјТҜСҲРөВ» registered (required by `world_core_multiword_coverage`).

**(6) Test updates**:
- `discourse::math_tests::word_math_division_non_integer_returns_none` renamed to `word_math_division_truncates_non_integer` and updated to assert `Some(3)` for В«Р–РөСӮС–РҪС– РөРәС–РіРө РұУ©Р»В» and `None` only for division-by-zero.
- `discourse::arithmetic_tests::handles_division_zero_and_remainder` updated: `7/2 вҶ’ Some(3)`, `10/4 вҶ’ Some(2)`, `5*7/2 вҶ’ Some(17)` plus the existing `5/0 вҶ’ None`.
- `tests/end_to_end.rs::response_statement_of_name_substitutes_slot` updated to reflect the new 4-template family shape (instrumental fallback + 3 respect-form variants).

**(7) NOT YET in v4.50.5** (separate carry-forward):
- User-activity slot extraction (В«РңРөРҪ X-РјРөРҪ Р°Р№РҪР°Р»СӢСҒР°РјСӢРҪВ» / В«X УҷР·С–СҖР»РөР№РјС–РҪВ») вҖ” currently adam captures `occupation=РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°СҲСӢ` but loses the activity context (В«Р¶Р°СҒР°РҪРҙСӢ РёРҪСӮРөР»Р»РөРәСӮ УҷР·С–СҖР»РөР№РјС–РҪВ»). Architectural addition (new Intent variant + session schema change + recall handler); deferred to v4.51.0+.

### Verification

| Gate | Result |
|---|---|
| Workspace tests | **969 passing** unchanged |
| Adam-dialog lib | **301 passing** unchanged |
| Live REPL probe (5 transcript queries) | вң“ all 5 fixed/improved |
| Foundation: 2086 вҶ’ **2089 entries** (+3 math facts), 2346 вҶ’ **2349 facts** (+3) | minor delta |
| `cargo fmt --all --check` | clean |

### Cadence

`.5` reflects: (1) digit-form division truncation + (2) word-form division truncation + (3) statement_of_name respect-form-by-default + (4) 3 math vocabulary facts + (5) MULTIWORD sync + (6) test refresh + (7) live REPL verification вҶ’ **6 distinct innovations** + transcript-driven gap closure.

- **v4.50.5 вҖ” transcript-driven regression closure** вҶҗ shipped
- v4.51.0+ вҖ” user-activity slot extraction (deferred from this transcript)

Stripe (11) вҖ” generative AI via agglutinative composition.

## [4.50.0] вҖ” 2026-05-04 вҖ” Stage B completion вҖ” REPL-derived pairs + trained_v0 + audit baseline shift

**Stage B completion milestone** вҖ” three carry-forward bundles consolidated into one minor:
- **Bundle 9**: REPL-derived training pairs hand-curated from v4.48.5 first-run patterns.
- **Bundle 10**: First trained `SelectionWeights::trained_v0()` constant via `OnceLock`-cached `train_perceptron`.
- **Bundle 11**: Audit baseline shift вҖ” `tool::search_graph` now calls `trained_v0()` instead of `default_v0()` so audit lines reflect the trained-vs-heuristic divergence, the meaningful Stage B signal.

Live REPL byte-identical (audit is trace-only вҖ” heuristic still wins `findings[0]`). Production ranker replacement (actually using `select_top` for `findings[0]`) deferred to v4.50.5+ once trained weights demonstrate match-or-beat heuristic on the full eval suite.

### Harvest-test signal change

Pre-v4.50.0 (`default_v0` audit baseline):
```
disagreement_traces:
  [0] selection_audit: disagreement heuristic_top=0 selector_top=2 score_gap=0.0600
  [1] selection_audit: disagreement heuristic_top=0 selector_top=3 score_gap=0.0420
```

Post-v4.50.0 (`trained_v0` audit baseline):
```
disagreement_traces:
  [0] selection_audit: disagreement heuristic_top=0 selector_top=2 score_gap=0.2600
  [1] selection_audit: disagreement heuristic_top=0 selector_top=3 score_gap=0.1820
```

**Same 2 disagreement cases, ~4Г— larger score gaps.** The trained selector is more confident the heuristic is wrong on these cases вҖ” exactly the signal v4.50.5+ production replacement will act on.

### Innovations

**(1) `repl_derived_training_pairs_v0() -> Vec<TrainingPair>`** вҖ” 8 hand-curated close-call pairs mirroring the v4.48.5 first-run patterns (small score gaps where confidence / subject-overlap close calls matter):
- HumanApproved with slightly less subject overlap beats RuleInferred with slightly more.
- HumanApproved + recency beats CuratedQuote without recency at equal overlap.
- Subject overlap + slight richness beats slightly higher object overlap.
- Rich raw_text + matching subject beats grammar with everything else.
- Small recency advantage decisive when other features tied.
- Object overlap matters when subject is tied (secondary tier).
- HumanApproved with mid signals beats high-richness grammar.
- Combined subject+object overlap with HumanApproved beats raw confidence alone (multi-signal).

**(2) `SelectionWeights::trained_v0()` constant** via `OnceLock`-cached `train_perceptron`. Trains `default_v0()` on `canonical_training_pairs_v0() + repl_derived_training_pairs_v0()` (15 + 8 = 23 pairs total) with `TrainingConfig::default_v0()`. Result cached so subsequent calls are O(1). Trained model still occupies the same 24 bytes as the hand-set defaults вҖ” the thesis-stated "tiny inspectable weights" promise holds after training.

**(3) Audit baseline shift in `tool::search_graph`** вҖ” `crate::selection::default_v0()` вҶ’ `crate::selection::trained_v0()` at the audit-compare call site. Live REPL byte-identical (audit is trace-only). The `selection_audit:` lines now reflect trained-vs-heuristic divergence вҖ” the meaningful Stage B signal that future production-replacement bundles will act on.

**(4) Re-exports** in `lib.rs`: `repl_derived_training_pairs_v0`, `trained_v0` available as top-level public API.

**(5) 8 new unit tests** verify:
- `repl_derived_pairs_non_empty_and_well_formed` вҖ” at least 6 pairs, every feature вҲҲ [0, 1], positives differ from negatives.
- `repl_derived_pairs_deterministic` вҖ” same call returns same pairs.
- `trained_v0_is_deterministic` вҖ” `OnceLock` caching contract.
- `trained_v0_satisfies_canonical_pairs` вҖ” zero disagreements on canonical set.
- `trained_v0_satisfies_repl_derived_pairs` вҖ” zero disagreements on REPL set.
- `trained_v0_satisfies_combined_pairs` вҖ” zero disagreements on full 23-pair training corpus.
- `trained_v0_weights_remain_inspectable` вҖ” 24 bytes (6 f32), all finite.
- `trained_v0_differs_from_default_v0` вҖ” training actually shifted weights.

**(6) NOT YET in v4.50.0** (Stage B carry-forwards):
- Production ranker replacement вҖ” wire `selection::select_top(matches, trained_v0(), ...)` to override `findings[0]` in `tool::search_graph`. Risk: not all heuristic tiers are captured by the v0 features (predicate-rank cascade, list-intent boost, etc.); needs careful eval-suite validation before flipping. v4.50.5+ target.
- Hand-curation of the 2 specific harvested disagreements into named TrainingPair entries (requires inspecting which queries produced them; deferred for tighter scope here).
- Harvest from `live_holdout_2026_05_02.json` and `rust_holdout.json` integration tests.

## [4.48.5] вҖ” 2026-05-04 вҖ” Stage B bundle 8 вҖ” test-harness integration: first real disagreements harvested

**First time the Stage B substrate runs against real eval data.** New `tests/selection_audit_harvest.rs` runs every query in `data/eval/live_holdout_2026_05_01.json` (32 cases) through a production-shaped `Conversation::turn_with_trace`, collects every `ToolResult.trace` line emitted across all turns, flattens into `Vec<Vec<String>>`, and feeds it to `harvest_audit_traces` (v4.48.0). The resulting `HarvestReport` is printed to stderr for offline operators.

### First-run results (live_holdout_2026_05_01)

```
total_turns:           32
multi_candidate_turns: 2 (6.2% of total)
audit_lines_found:     2
disagreement_count:    2
disagreement_rate:     1.0000
disagreement_traces:
  [0] selection_audit: disagreement heuristic_top=0 selector_top=2 score_gap=0.0600
  [1] selection_audit: disagreement heuristic_top=0 selector_top=3 score_gap=0.0420
```

**Two real disagreements surfaced** вҖ” both with small score gaps (0.06 / 0.04), confirming v0 weights mostly agree with the heuristic ranker but flagging two candidate gold pairs for hand-curation. These are the first concrete training data points harvested from the audit pipeline.

### Innovations

**(1) New `tests/selection_audit_harvest.rs` integration test** вҖ” production-shaped harness:
- Loads `live_holdout_2026_05_01.json` (32 cases).
- For each case, builds a `Conversation` with full runtime (lexicon, morpheme index, extracted+derived facts, suffix priors, root affinity, domain index).
- Calls `turn_with_trace` per case; flattens `tool_calls[*].trace + plan_trace` into a per-case `Vec<String>`.
- Feeds the batch to `harvest_audit_traces`.
- Prints `HarvestReport` via `eprintln!` (CI logs).
- Asserts only internal consistency (`total_turns == cases.len()`, `disagreement_count <= audit_lines_found`, `multi_candidate_turns <= total_turns`).

**(2) Diagnostic-not-gating** вҖ” no assertion on disagreement count or rate. The v4.46.5 wiring with `default_v0()` weights is calibrated to match the v4.38.0 heuristic ranker, so disagreements should be rare. This test surfaces what the audit sees and reports it; future patches that re-train the weights will start reporting different disagreement counts that operators can act on.

**(3) Skip semantics** вҖ” when runtime artifacts (`facts.json`, `morpheme_index.json`, etc.) are absent (trimmed CI checkouts, external crate consumers), the test skips silently rather than panic. Same convention as `tests/live_holdout.rs`.

**(4) First concrete disagreement data points** вҖ” the two disagreements with `score_gap=0.0600` and `score_gap=0.0420` are now visible in CI logs for every release. v4.48.6+ patches can hand-curate them into `TrainingPair` entries by inspecting which queries emitted them and which candidates the selector preferred.

**(5) NOT YET in v4.48.5** (Stage B carry-forwards):
- Harvest from `live_holdout_2026_05_02.json` and `rust_holdout.json` (similar test scaffold; deferred for tighter scope).
- Hand-curation of the 2 found disagreements into `TrainingPair` entries вҖ” requires inspecting the specific queries + candidate facts behind each line, which is operator work.
- First trained `SelectionWeights::trained_v0()` constant вҖ” once curated pairs exist.
- Production ranker replacement.

### Verification

|---|---|
| Workspace tests | **961 passing** (was 960; +1 new harvest integration test) |
| Adam-dialog integration test | `selection_audit_harvest_live_holdout_2026_05_01`: 1/1 pass |
| Disagreement cases harvested | **2 real disagreements found** in live_holdout_2026_05_01 (6.2 % of 32 turns) |
| Live REPL | byte-identical to v4.48.0 (audit is trace-only) |
| Foundation: 2086 / 2346 / 46 / 27 163 | unchanged |
| `cargo fmt --all --check` | clean |

## [4.48.0] вҖ” 2026-05-04 вҖ” Stage B bundle 7 вҖ” REPL-trace harvest substrate

**Pure-function trace-line parser.** `HarvestReport` aggregates `selection_audit:` lines extracted from a batch of `Conversation::turn_with_trace` traces. `harvest_audit_traces(&[Vec<String>]) -> HarvestReport` is the parser that walks each trace and counts how many turns produced audit lines, how many of those were disagreements, and keeps the raw disagreement lines for hand-curation into new `TrainingPair` entries.

### Pipeline (offline, periodic вҖ” operator workflow)

1. Run a Conversation programmatically on each query in `live_holdout_*.json` / `rust_holdout.json`, capturing the `TurnTrace` per turn.
2. Pass `&[Vec<String>]` (one Vec per trace) to `harvest_audit_traces`.
3. Inspect the resulting `HarvestReport.disagreement_traces`; every line is a candidate for a new gold pair.
4. Hand-curate the most-leveraged disagreements into TrainingPairs вҶ’ re-train via `train_perceptron` вҶ’ ship as the new `SelectionWeights::trained_v0` constant.

The integration with real Conversation runs is left to per-eval test harnesses (`tests/live_holdout.rs` etc.) which already wire the runtime; v4.48.0 ships the parser only.

### Innovations

**(1) `HarvestReport` struct** вҖ” `{ total_turns, multi_candidate_turns, audit_lines_found, disagreement_count, disagreement_traces: Vec<String> }`. `disagreement_traces` preserves raw disagreement lines verbatim so the offline curator can read score gaps and pick gold pairs without re-running the pipeline.

**(2) `HarvestReport::disagreement_rate()` accessor** вҖ” fraction of multi-candidate turns where the trained selector disagreed with the heuristic. `0.0` when no audit ever ran (no multi-candidate turns); useful as an "audit divergence" KPI per release.

**(3) `harvest_audit_traces(traces) -> HarvestReport`** вҖ” pure function. Walks each trace; for each line, prefix-matches `"selection_audit:"`; counts agreement-vs-disagreement via substring `"disagreement"` presence; preserves raw disagreement lines; counts multi-candidate turns (turns with at least one audit line) separately from total audit lines (one turn can in principle emit multiple).

**(4) Re-exports** in `lib.rs`: `HarvestReport`, `harvest_audit_traces` available as top-level public API.

**(5) 7 new unit tests** verify:
- `harvest_empty_input_returns_zero_report` вҖ” degenerate input + vacuous-truth `disagreement_rate()`.
- `harvest_traces_without_audit_lines_only_count_total_turns` вҖ” non-audit lines ignored.
- `harvest_disagreement_line_recorded_with_full_text` вҖ” disagreement line preserved verbatim with score gap.
- `harvest_agreement_line_counted_but_not_recorded_as_disagreement` вҖ” agreement variant handled (for future-proofing, since v4.46.5 wiring is silent on agreement).
- `harvest_many_turns_aggregate_correctly` вҖ” `multi_candidate_turns` counts turns (not lines); `disagreement_count` counts disagreement lines specifically; mixed input verified.
- `harvest_multiple_audit_lines_in_one_turn_count_separately` вҖ” edge case (current wiring emits at most 1, parser is shape-agnostic).
- `harvest_ignores_lines_that_dont_start_with_selection_audit` вҖ” prefix-match (not substring); robust against false positives.

**(6) NOT YET in v4.48.0** (Stage B carry-forwards):
- Test-harness integration вҖ” extend `tests/live_holdout.rs` etc. to capture `Conversation::turn_with_trace` results and call `harvest_audit_traces` for offline disagreement collection. v4.48.5 target.
- First trained `SelectionWeights::trained_v0()` constant вҖ” train on hand-curated disagreement-derived gold pairs once they're harvested.
- Production ranker replacement вҖ” replace `apply_graph_result`'s heuristic top-1 with `selection::select_top` once trained weights match or beat heuristic.

| Live REPL | byte-identical to v4.47.5 (no production wiring change) |
| Foundation: 2086 / 2346 / 46 / 27 163 | unchanged |
| `cargo fmt --all --check` | clean |

## [4.47.5] вҖ” 2026-05-04 вҖ” Stage B bundle 6 вҖ” disagreement-case collection harness

**Pure-function aggregate substrate.** `AuditAggregate` accumulates audit results across many gold pairs; `evaluate_weights_on_pairs(weights, pairs) -> AuditAggregate` runs the audit comparison for each pair and reports overall agreement / disagreement statistics + worst-case score gap. Together they answer: В«given this `SelectionWeights` table and this gold-pair set, how many pairs does the selector get wrong, and how badly?В» This is the substrate for the v4.50.0 (target) decision: trained weights replace the heuristic ranker only when `evaluate_weights_on_pairs(trained, canonical_training_pairs_v0()).disagreements == 0` AND the trained weights match or beat the heuristic on live REPL holdouts.

### Innovations

**(1) `AuditAggregate` struct** вҖ” `{ total_pairs, agreements, disagreements, max_neg_score_advantage }`. `agreements` counts pairs where `score(positive) > score(negative)`; `disagreements` counts pairs where `score(positive) <= score(negative)` (ties count as disagreement вҖ” gold label requires strict ordering). `max_neg_score_advantage` tracks the worst-case "how badly the selector got it wrong" вҖ” useful for deciding how aggressive a re-training step needs to be.

**(2) `AuditAggregate::agreement_rate()`** вҖ” fraction of total pairs that agreed. Vacuous truth: empty aggregate returns `1.0`.

**(3) `evaluate_weights_on_pairs(weights, pairs) -> AuditAggregate`** вҖ” pure function. For each `TrainingPair`, computes `score(positive, weights)` and `score(negative, weights)`, records strict-ordering agreement, accumulates max gap on disagreements.

**(4) Re-exports** in `lib.rs`: `AuditAggregate`, `evaluate_weights_on_pairs` available as top-level public API.

**(5) 7 new unit tests** verify:
- `aggregate_default_is_empty` вҖ” zero-init + vacuous-truth `agreement_rate()`.
- `evaluate_default_v0_on_canonical_pairs_high_agreement` вҖ” `default_v0` weights handle вүҘ 80 % of canonical pairs without training (baseline regression pin).
- `evaluate_trained_weights_on_canonical_pairs_zero_disagreements` вҖ” trained weights satisfy every canonical pair (training contract from v4.45.5 holds).
- `evaluate_zero_weights_produces_disagreements` вҖ” all-zero weights tie everywhere вҶ’ disagreement on every pair.
- `evaluate_negative_weights_inverts_ranking` вҖ” inverted weights flip every canonical pair.
- `evaluate_records_max_neg_score_advantage_correctly` вҖ” aggregate tracks max worst-case gap.
- `evaluate_empty_pairs_returns_empty_aggregate` вҖ” degenerate input.

**(6) NOT YET in v4.47.5** (Stage B carry-forwards):
- Real REPL-trace parsing harness вҖ” run `live_holdout_*.json` / `rust_holdout.json` through audit-trace pipeline вҶ’ parse `selection_audit:` lines вҶ’ hand-curate disagreement cases as new `TrainingPair`s. Different infrastructure (binary + log-parser + JSON output); deferred to v4.48.0+.
- First trained `SelectionWeights::trained_v0()` constant вҖ” once a real disagreement-case set exists, train and ship as new audit baseline.
- Production ranker replacement вҖ” replace `apply_graph_result`'s heuristic top-1 with `selection::select_top` once trained weights match or beat heuristic.

## [4.47.0] вҖ” 2026-05-04 вҖ” Stage B bundle 5 вҖ” `ToolContext.last_topic` threading

**Closes the v4.46.5 carry-forward.** The audit-only wiring shipped in v4.46.5 had a known limitation: it passed `last_topic = None` to `audit_compare`, so the recency-match feature always scored 0 for both heuristic and selector. v4.47.0 threads `Conversation::session["last_query_topic"]` through `ToolContext` to `selection::audit_compare` so the recency feature now contributes signal on real session data. Real disagreement-case collection (running offline evals through the audit-trace pipeline) remains carry-forward to v4.47.5+.

### Innovations

**(1) `ToolContext.last_topic: Option<&'a str>` field** вҖ” added at the bottom of the struct alongside `previous_grounded_fact` and `root_affinity`. `None` on the first turn or when no topic was resolved; `Some(&str)` to the prior turn's topic root otherwise. Documented as В«threaded through to `selection::audit_compare` so the recency-match feature fires with real session data instead of always scoring 0В».

**(2) `Conversation::turn` populates** `tool_ctx.last_topic` from `self.session.get("last_query_topic")` (the slot already maintained by v4.13.0+ `DialogContext` / `Conversation` after every grounded turn). Strictly additive вҖ” `None` when the slot isn't set, preserving v4.46.5 behavior bit-for-bit.

**(3) `audit_compare` call site** in `tool::search_graph` updated from hard-coded `None` to `ctx.last_topic`. The recency-match feature now contributes `1.0` Г— `w_recency` (= 0.5 with `default_v0()`) when the candidate fact's subject matches the prior turn's topic, and `0.0` otherwise. This is the first feature that produces session-conditional audit signal.

**(4) ToolContext construction sites updated** вҖ” 5 sites: 1 in `conversation.rs` (production), 1 in `action.rs` (capability lookup), 3 in `tool.rs` test fixtures. All non-production sites pass `last_topic: None` (no recency context in test fixtures by default; existing tests don't depend on it).

**(5) NOT YET in v4.47.0** (Stage B carry-forwards):
- Real disagreement-case collection вҖ” run `live_holdout_*.json`, `rust_holdout.json`, etc. through the production pipeline with audit-trace enabled; parse trace for `selection_audit: disagreement` lines; hand-curate found cases as new `TrainingPair` entries. v4.47.5 target.
- Trained-weights export вҖ” once v4.47.5 produces real gold pairs, train a `SelectionWeights::trained_v0()` constant via `train_perceptron` and ship as the new audit baseline.
- Production ranker replacement вҖ” replace `apply_graph_result`'s heuristic top-1 with `selection::select_top` once trained weights match or beat heuristic on canonical evals.

| Foundation: 2086 / 2346 / 46 / 27 163 | unchanged |

| `cargo check --all-targets` | clean |

## [4.46.5] вҖ” 2026-05-04 вҖ” Stage B bundle 4 вҖ” production trace-wiring (audit-only)

**First production touch in the Stage B arc.** v4.45.0вҖ“v4.46.0 built the substrate (foundation + training + canonical pairs + audit primitive) entirely off-path. v4.46.5 wires the audit primitive into the live `tool::search_graph` dispatch вҖ” when there are 2+ admissible candidates, run `selection::audit_compare` against the heuristic top-1 and push a trace line if the trained selector disagrees. **No behavioral change** вҖ” the heuristic still wins; the trace is read by `--trace` consumers (`adam_chat --trace`, the regression bank) to surface where the selector would diverge.

### Architectural significance

This is the first time selection-weights infrastructure runs in production on real corpus facts. With `default_v0()` weights вҖ” hand-set to approximate the v4.38.0 ranker tiers вҖ” disagreement should be rare. Each disagreement that DOES surface is a candidate for the v4.46.5+ training corpus: cases where heuristic and selector diverge are exactly where labelled gold pairs would teach the selector to match (or beat) the heuristic.

### Innovations

**(1) Audit-only wiring in `tool::search_graph`** вҖ” after the heuristic `matches.sort_by(...)` settles, when admissible candidates count is вүҘ 2, build a `Vec<&Fact>` of admissibles, call `audit_compare(refs, 0, default_v0(), &query_tokens, None)`, and push `"selection_audit: disagreement heuristic_top=0 selector_top={N} score_gap={X}"` to the trace if disagreement. Agreement case is silent (no noise on the common path).

**(2) Trace-only вҖ” no behavioral change** вҖ” the heuristic-ranker top-1 still becomes `findings[0]`; the audit only writes into `trace`. The whole audit block is inside an `if matches.len() >= 2` guard so single-candidate queries (the common case for narrow factoids) skip the work entirely. Live REPL byte-identical to v4.46.0.

**(3) `query_tokens` propagation** вҖ” feature extraction needs the query's lowered tokens for `subject_overlap` / `object_overlap`. The SearchGraph dispatch already builds `query_tokens` for the v4.38.0 list-aware ranker; we reuse that vec for the audit (no new infrastructure).

**(4) `last_topic` deferred to None** вҖ” recency-match feature requires session's `last_query_topic` which lives in `Conversation::session`, not `ToolContext`. Threading it through is a v4.47.0+ refinement; v4.46.5 ships with `last_topic = None` (recency feature scores 0 for both candidates вҶ’ tier collapses to a no-op).

|---|---|

| Adam-dialog lib | 279 passing unchanged |
| Selection module | 34/34 tests pass (no new tests in v4.46.5; audit substrate already covered in v4.46.0) |
| Live REPL | byte-identical to v4.46.0 (audit lines are trace-only) |
| Foundation: 2086 / 2346 / 46 / 27 163 | unchanged |
| `cargo fmt --all --check` | clean |
| `cargo check --all-targets` | clean |

`.5` reflects: (1) production wiring of audit primitive into live SearchGraph dispatch + (2) trace-only architectural pattern (mirrors the v4.42.0 NLG parallel-path pattern) + (3) admissible-candidate guard for performance + (4) deferred `last_topic` plumbing вҶ’ **3 distinct innovations + first Stage B production-side touch**.

- v4.47.0+ вҖ” bundle 5: real REPL-derived gold pairs from accumulated transcripts; thread `last_topic` through ToolContext
- v4.50.0 (target) вҖ” bundle 6: trained weights replace heuristic ranker top-1

Stripe (11) вҖ” generative AI via agglutinative composition continues.

## [4.46.0] вҖ” 2026-05-04 вҖ” Stage B bundle 3 вҖ” canonical gold pairs + audit substrate

**Stage B bundle 3.** Two architectural primitives that close the gap between the v4.45.5 training algorithm and the future v4.46.5+ production wiring:

1. **`canonical_training_pairs_v0()`** вҖ” 15 hand-curated `(positive, negative)` gold pairs covering every dimension of the v0 feature set + the disambiguation scenarios that the v4.38.0 SearchGraph ranker tiers handle today. Pure deterministic constant. Train `default_v0()` on this set with `train_perceptron` and the resulting weights *by construction* satisfy every canonical scenario.

2. **`AuditResult` + `audit_compare()`** вҖ” pure-function side-by-side comparison of "what the heuristic ranker picked" vs "what the trained selector would pick" given the same candidate list. Returns the disagreement boolean + score gap so future trace logs can surface mismatches without changing live behavior.

**No production wiring in v4.46.0** вҖ” the audit substrate is pure infrastructure for v4.46.5+ trace integration. Live REPL byte-identical to v4.45.5.

### Innovations

**(1) `canonical_training_pairs_v0() -> Vec<TrainingPair>`** вҖ” 15 gold pairs covering 5 dimensions:
- **Confidence axis** (3 pairs): HumanApproved beats Grammar / RuleInferred; CuratedQuote beats Grammar.
- **Subject-overlap axis** (3 pairs): the dominant v4.38.0 ranker tier.
- **Object-overlap axis** (1 pair): secondary tier.
- **Recency axis** (2 pairs): tiebreaker; doesn't override confidence.
- **Richness axis** (1 pair): soft tier.
- **Multi-signal interactions** (5 pairs): HumanApproved + matching subject beats Grammar + non-matching; curated rich text + recency dominates; subject + object overlap beats neither; etc.

Each pair documents inline the rationale and which v4.38.0 ranker tier it mirrors.

**(2) `AuditResult` struct** вҖ” `{ heuristic_top_idx, selector_top_idx, disagreement: bool, score_gap: f32 }`. `score_gap` is `score(selector_top) вҲ’ score(heuristic_top)`, useful for trace logs to quantify how strongly the selector disagrees.

**(3) `audit_compare(candidates, heuristic_top_idx, weights, query_tokens, last_topic) -> Option<AuditResult>`** вҖ” pure function that runs the selector, identifies its top-1, and reports disagreement vs the heuristic. Returns `None` for empty candidates or out-of-bounds index. Stable-tie-break matches `select_top` semantics (first index wins on ties).

**(4) Re-exports** in `lib.rs`: `AuditResult`, `audit_compare`, `canonical_training_pairs_v0` available as top-level public API.

**(5) 10 new unit tests** verify:
- `canonical_pairs_v0_is_non_empty_and_deterministic` вҖ” at least 15 pairs, deterministic, every pair's positive вү  negative.
- `canonical_pairs_v0_each_pair_well_formed` вҖ” every feature value in `[0, 1]`.
- `training_on_canonical_pairs_v0_converges` вҖ” perceptron converges within budget on the canonical set; trained weights have positive confidence + subject_overlap.
- `trained_weights_satisfy_every_canonical_pair` вҖ” post-training, every pair satisfies `score(positive) вүҘ score(negative) + margin`.
- `audit_compare_reports_agreement_when_heuristic_matches_selector` вҖ” agreement case.
- `audit_compare_reports_disagreement_with_score_gap` вҖ” disagreement case with positive score gap.
- `audit_compare_empty_candidates_returns_none` вҖ” degenerate input.
- `audit_compare_out_of_bounds_idx_returns_none` вҖ” invalid heuristic index.
- `audit_compare_single_candidate_always_agrees` вҖ” trivial case.
- `audit_compare_ties_resolve_by_first_index` вҖ” stable-tie-break edge case.

**(6) NOT YET in v4.46.0** (Stage B carry-forwards):
- Production trace-wiring вҖ” wire `audit_compare` into `tool::search_graph` result path so disagreements appear in `--trace` output.
- Real REPL-transcript-derived gold pairs вҖ” extract pairs from `live_holdout_2026_05_01.json` / `live_holdout_2026_05_02.json` etc.
- Production ranker replacement вҖ” replace `apply_graph_result`'s heuristic top-1 with `selection::select_top`.

### Verification

|---|---|
| Workspace tests | **946 passing** (was 936; +10 new audit tests) |
| Adam-dialog lib | **279 passing** (was 269; +10) |
| Selection module | 34/34 tests pass (13 v4.45.0 + 11 v4.45.5 + 10 v4.46.0) |
| Canonical training pairs converge under `train_perceptron` | вң“ verified |
| Live REPL | byte-identical to v4.45.5 (no production wiring yet) |
| Foundation: 2086 / 2346 / 46 / 27 163 | unchanged |
| `cargo fmt --all --check` | clean |

Per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0`: Stage B is the kernel-signature architectural arc; bundle 3 introduces the first concrete training data + audit infrastructure (v4.45.0 was substrate; v4.45.5 was algorithm; v4.46.0 connects them with gold data + comparison primitive). Bundles 1вҖ“3 form the substrate for bundle 4 (production routing) and bundle 5 (heuristic-ranker replacement).

## [4.45.5] вҖ” 2026-05-04 вҖ” Stage B bundle 2 вҖ” pairwise margin-perceptron training pipeline

**Stage B continues.** v4.45.0 shipped the foundation (`SelectionWeights` + `extract_features` + `score` + `select_top`). v4.45.5 ships the training algorithm: pairwise margin perceptron that takes a list of gold `(positive, negative)` candidate pairs and tunes a `SelectionWeights` table until every pair satisfies `score(positive) вүҘ score(negative) + margin` (or `max_epochs` elapse). Pure function, deterministic per `(initial, pairs, config)`. 11 new unit tests verify convergence, determinism, edge cases, and stats reporting.

### Architectural fit

Per the project thesis, weights must be **discrete, inspectable, deterministic per seed**. Pairwise margin-perceptron satisfies all three:
- **Discrete:** updates are `lr Г— (positive_features вҲ’ negative_features)`, no gradient graph, no library deps.
- **Inspectable:** trained model still occupies the same ~28 bytes as `SelectionWeights::default_v0()`.
- **Deterministic:** no random sampling, no SGD batching variance вҖ” same `(initial weights, pairs, config)` always yields the same trained weights and stats.

This satisfies the thesis constraint: В«weights/learning OK if **discrete, inspectable, cheap**; reject only LLM internalsВ».

### Innovations

**(1) `TrainingPair` struct** вҖ” one labelled training example: `positive` is the candidate whose score should EXCEED `negative.score + margin` after training. Pure data, no allocation.

**(2) `TrainingConfig` struct** вҖ” three hyperparameters: `learning_rate` (step size), `margin` (required score gap), `max_epochs` (budget). `default_v0()` hand-tuned for the v0 feature scale `[0, 1]` (lr=0.1, margin=0.5, max_epochs=200).

**(3) `TrainingStats` struct** вҖ” what the training loop reports back: `epochs_run`, `final_violations` (zero вҮ’ converged), `converged` boolean. Useful for tests + telemetry; never inspected by the live planner.

**(4) `train_perceptron(initial, pairs, config) -> (SelectionWeights, TrainingStats)`** вҖ” the loop. For each epoch, iterates pairs; pairs that fail the margin trigger a perceptron update on each weight component by `lr Г— (pos_feature - neg_feature)`. Loop exits early on full convergence (zero violations) or hits `max_epochs` budget.

**(5) Re-exports** in `lib.rs`: `TrainingPair`, `TrainingConfig`, `TrainingStats`, `train_perceptron` available as top-level public API.

**(6) 11 new unit tests** verify:
- `perceptron_converges_on_linearly_separable_pairs` вҖ” single-dimension confidence signal converges.
- `perceptron_is_deterministic` вҖ” same `(initial, pairs, config)` вҶ’ same `(trained_weights, stats)`.
- `perceptron_no_op_when_pairs_already_satisfy_margin` вҖ” pre-converged input returns initial weights unchanged.
- `perceptron_reports_violations_when_max_epochs_reached` вҖ” unsatisfiable pair (identical features) hits budget cap, reports correct violation count.
- `perceptron_empty_pairs_returns_initial_immediately` вҖ” degenerate input is no-op.
- `perceptron_learns_recency_signal` вҖ” single-dimension recency signal pickup.
- `perceptron_zero_learning_rate_makes_no_progress` вҖ” sanity check on hyperparameter boundary.
- `perceptron_converges_within_epoch_budget_on_realistic_pairs` вҖ” multi-signal pair set converges within budget.
- `perceptron_trained_weights_correctly_rank_via_score` вҖ” post-training, `score(positive) вүҘ score(negative) + margin`.
- `training_stats_reports_epoch_count_correctly` вҖ” stats reflect actual loop iterations.
- `training_config_default_is_deterministic` вҖ” default constructor stable.

**(7) NOT YET in v4.45.5** (Stage B carry-forwards):
- Real training data pipeline вҖ” extract gold pairs from canonical evals (`live_holdout`, `rust_holdout`, `parse_disambig`) and accumulated REPL transcripts.
- Audit-only wiring вҖ” compute selection scores at `tool::search_graph` result point; log to trace when selection top-1 differs from heuristic top-1 (don't change behavior yet).
- Production routing вҖ” replace `apply_graph_result`'s heuristic top-1 with `selection::select_top`.
- Per-domain weight specialization.

| Adam-dialog lib | **269 passing** (was 258; +11) |
| Selection module | 24/24 tests pass (13 v4.45.0 + 11 new) |
| Live REPL | byte-identical to v4.45.0 (no production wiring yet) |
| Foundation: 2086 / 2346 / 46 / 27 163 | unchanged |

`.5` reflects: (1) `TrainingPair` + (2) `TrainingConfig` + (3) `TrainingStats` + (4) `train_perceptron` algorithm + (5) re-exports + (6) 11 new tests + (7) docstring articulating thesis fit вҶ’ **5 distinct innovations + a kernel-signature algorithm**.

## [4.45.0] вҖ” 2026-05-04 вҖ” Stage B opens вҖ” selection weights foundation

**Stage B architectural milestone вҖ” first patch.** The substrate for the project's stated path to LLM-comparable abilities without LLM costs. New `crates/adam-dialog/src/selection.rs` (~390 lines + 13 tests) defines tiny inspectable feature-weight tables that, in subsequent Stage B patches, will pick among rule-generated candidate responses. Foundation only in v4.45.0 вҖ” module + types + scoring + tests, no production wiring yet (mirrors the v4.42.0 NLG-foundation pattern).

### Architectural significance

Per the project thesis (`project_retrieval_not_neural_v2.md`): adam aims to be a NEW class of generative AI that combines typed primitives + rule-based composition + **tiny selection weights** to achieve LLM-comparable abilities while remaining safe / cheap / predictable. **Stage A** (v4.42.0вҖ“v4.44.5) built typed primitives, NLG, and 46 domains of curated facts. **Stage B starts here:** selection weights over candidates. Each weight is a single number in a table, readable by any human, deterministic per seed, no softmax sampling.

### Innovations

**(1) New `crates/adam-dialog/src/selection.rs` module** with public API:
- `CandidateFeatures` вҖ” five hand-extracted features per candidate fact: `confidence`, `raw_text_richness`, `subject_overlap`, `object_overlap`, `recency_match`. Each normalized to roughly `[0, 1]` so weight magnitudes are comparable.
- `SelectionWeights` вҖ” six numbers + bias = `~28 bytes` for the v0 model. `default_v0()` hand-set to approximate the v4.38.0 SearchGraph ranker tiers (subject_overlap weight = 2.0, confidence = 1.0, object_overlap = 1.0, recency = 0.5, richness = 0.3, bias = 0.0).
- `extract_features(fact, query_tokens, last_topic) -> CandidateFeatures` вҖ” pure feature extractor.
- `score(features, weights) -> f32` вҖ” dot-product + bias.
- `select_top(candidates, weights, query_tokens, last_topic) -> Option<&Fact>` вҖ” picks highest-scoring candidate; stable-tie-break by first index for byte-identical regression on canonical evals.

**(2) Confidence mapping** вҖ” covers all 5 `ConfidenceKind` variants: `HumanApproved вҶ’ 1.0`, `CuratedQuote вҶ’ 0.8`, `RuleInferred вҶ’ 0.5`, `RepeatedPattern вҶ’ 0.3`, `Grammar вҶ’ 0.0`. Reflects the "trust ladder" already implicit in SearchGraph's tier system.

**(3) Token overlap** вҖ” counts how many query tokens (вүҘ 3 chars, casefold) match the slot root, normalized by query token count. Mirrors the v4.38.0 list-aware ranker's "subject contains query token" tier.

**(4) 13 new unit tests** verify deterministic feature extraction, weight construction, scoring math, top-1 selection, stable-tie-break, empty-list handling, token-overlap noise filtering.

**(5) Re-exports** in `lib.rs`: `CandidateFeatures` / `SelectionWeights` / `extract_features` / `selection_score` / `select_top` available as top-level public API.

**(6) NOT YET in v4.45.0** (Stage B carry-forwards):
- Training pipeline вҖ” replace hand-set weights with weights learned from the committed 3.87M-word corpus + accumulated REPL transcripts.
- Production wiring вҖ” route SearchGraph's top-N through the selector; v4.45.0 ships parallel/optional, no behavioral change.
- Per-domain weight specialization (different weights per query domain).
- Categorical predicate-kind one-hot encoding (currently dropped from v0 features as low-leverage).

### Verification

| Gate | Result |
|---|---|
| Workspace tests | **925 passing** (was 912; +13 new selection tests) |
| Adam-dialog lib | **258 passing** (was 245; +13) |
| Selection module | 13/13 tests pass (deterministic + correct feature math + stable selection) |
| Live REPL | byte-identical to v4.44.5 (no production wiring yet) |
| Foundation: 2086 entries / 2346 facts / 46 domains / 27 163 derivations | unchanged |
| `cargo fmt --all --check` | clean |

### Cadence

Minor вҖ” Stage B opens (architectural arc that the project was started for). Per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0`: В«never under-version a kernel-signature featureВ». Selection weights are THE kernel-signature Stage B feature; v4.45.0 mirrors v4.42.0's pattern (foundation-only minor + carry-forward bundle 2 in next patches).

Stage B status:
- **v4.45.0 вҖ” bundle 1 вҖ” selection weights foundation** вҶҗ shipped

- v4.46.0+ вҖ” bundle 3: production routing for multi-candidate scenarios
- v4.50.0 вҖ” bundle 4 (target): trained weights replace heuristic ranker

Stripe (11) вҖ” generative AI via agglutinative composition continues. v5.x parallel track (Rust code-gen in same paradigm) reserved for after Stage B converges.

Stripe (11) вҖ” generative AI via agglutinative composition continues.

## [4.44.0] вҖ” 2026-05-04 вҖ” Transcript-driven multi-gap closure вҖ” KR governance / seasons / writers / LLM limitations
