| ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҪСӢТЈ СӮР°РҪСӢРјР°Р» СӮТұР»Т“Р°Р»Р°СҖСӢ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ Р°Р№СӮСӢРҝ РұРөСҖС–ТЈС–Р·СҲС–? | "РўТұР»Т“Р° вҖ” Р°РҙР°Рј." (head-noun fallback, missed list) | "**ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҪСӢТЈ СӮР°РҪСӢРјР°Р» СӮТұР»Т“Р°Р»Р°СҖСӢ: РҗРұР°Р№, РЁУҷРәУҷСҖС–Рј, РңР°С…Р°РјРұРөСӮ, РңР°Т“Р¶Р°РҪ, РңТұС…СӮР°СҖ УҳСғРөР·РҫРІ, РЎУҷРәРөРҪ РЎРөР№С„СғР»Р»РёРҪ, РқР°Р·Р°СҖРұР°РөРІ, РўРҫТӣР°РөРІ, РЎУҷСӮРұР°РөРІ, РЈУҷР»РёС…Р°РҪРҫРІ, РңР°СҖТ“ТұР»Р°РҪ, Р‘У©РәРөР№С…Р°РҪ, РҗРұСӢР»Р°Р№ С…Р°РҪ, РҡРөРҪРөСҒР°СҖСӢ С…Р°РҪ, РңРҫР»РҙР°Т“ТұР»РҫРІР°, РңРҫРјСӢСҲТұР»СӢ, Р“РҫР»РҫРІРәРёРҪ, РҳР»СҢРёРҪ, РЎР°РҝРёРөРІ, РўУ©Р»Рө РұРё, ТҡР°Р·СӢРұРөРә РұРё, УҳР№СӮРөРәРө РұРё.**" |
| ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҪСӢТЈ СӮР°РҪСӢРјР°Р» Р¶Р°Р·СғСҲСӢР»Р°СҖСӢРҪ Р°СӮР°ТЈСӢР· | (no list) | "**ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСӮСӢТЈ СӮР°РҪСӢРјР°Р» Р°ТӣСӢРҪ-Р¶Р°Р·СғСҲСӢР»Р°СҖСӢ: РҗРұР°Р№, РЁУҷРәУҷСҖС–Рј, РңР°С…Р°РјРұРөСӮ, РңР°Т“Р¶Р°РҪ Р–ТұРјР°РұР°РөРІ, РЎУҷРәРөРҪ РЎРөР№С„СғР»Р»РёРҪ, Р‘РөР№С–РјРұРөСӮ РңР°Р№Р»РёРҪ, РңТұС…СӮР°СҖ УҳСғРөР·РҫРІ, Т’Р°РұРёСӮ РңТҜСҒС–СҖРөРҝРҫРІ, Т’Р°РұРёРҙРөРҪ РңТұСҒСӮР°С„РёРҪ, РһР»Р¶Р°СҒ РЎТҜР»РөР№РјРөРҪРҫРІ, РңТұТӣР°Т“Р°Р»Рё РңР°ТӣР°СӮР°РөРІ.**" |
| ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҪСӢТЈ СӮР°РҪСӢРјР°Р» Т“Р°Р»СӢРјРҙР°СҖСӢРҪ Р°СӮР°Рҝ У©СӮС–ТЈС–Р· | (no list) | "**ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҪСӢТЈ СӮР°РҪСӢРјР°Р» Т“Р°Р»СӢРјРҙР°СҖСӢ: ТҡР°РҪСӢСҲ РЎУҷСӮРұР°РөРІ, РЁРҫТӣР°РҪ РЈУҷР»РёС…Р°РҪРҫРІ, УҳР»РәРөР№ РңР°СҖТ“ТұР»Р°РҪ.**" |
| ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҪСӢТЈ СӮР°РҪСӢРјР°Р» СҒРҝРҫСҖСӮСҲСӢР»Р°СҖСӢРҪ СӮС–Р·С–Рҝ РұРөСҖС–ТЈС–Р· | (no list) | "**ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҪСӢТЈ СӮР°РҪСӢРјР°Р» СҒРҝРҫСҖСӮСҲСӢР»Р°СҖСӢ: Р“РөРҪРҪР°РҙРёР№ Р“РҫР»РҫРІРәРёРҪ, РҳР»СҢСҸ РҳР»СҢРёРҪ, РЎРөСҖС–Рә РЎР°РҝРёРөРІ, Р”РјРёСӮСҖРёР№ Р‘Р°Р»Р°РҪРҙРёРҪ.**" |
| ТҡР°РҪРҙР°Р№ РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°Р»Р°Сғ СӮС–Р»РҙРөСҖС–РҪ РұС–Р»РөСҒС–Р·? | "РўС–Р» вҖ” ТӣР°СҖСӢРј-ТӣР°СӮСӢРҪР°СҒ ТӣТұСҖР°Р»СӢ." (single-word fallback) | "**РҗРҙР°Рј РұС–Р»РөСӮС–РҪ РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°Р»Р°Сғ СӮС–Р»РҙРөСҖС–: Rust. Р‘Р°СҒТӣР° СӮС–Р»РҙРөСҖ РұРҫР№СӢРҪСҲР° РәСғСҖРёСҖР»РөРҪРіРөРҪ РҙРөСҖРөРә УҷР»С– Р¶РҫТӣ.**" |
| РҡРөР№РҙРө СҒРөРҪС–ТЈ ТӣР°СӮРөР»РөСҒС–Рҝ Р¶ТҜСҖРіРөРҪС–ТЈРҙС– РәУ©СҖРөРјС–РҪ | "РңТҜРјРәС–РҪ СҒС–Р· РәРөР№РҙРө СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ СҒТұСҖР°Рҝ РҫСӮСӢСҖТ“Р°РҪ СҲСӢТ“Р°СҖСҒСӢР·." (adverb picked as topic) | clarify on low-confidence noun (no longer "РәРөР№РҙРө") |
| 12/6 vs 2*43 (math) | bare "2" vs "Р–Р°СғР°РұСӢ вҖ” 86" (inconsistent) | "**Р•СҒРөРҝСӮРөРҙС–Рј: 2**" / "**Р•СҒРөРҝСӮРөРҙС–Рј: 86**" (consistent prefix) |
| All v4.39.7 controls (qty queries / РҗРұР°Р№ / РЎУҷР»РөРј / bug 4) | unchanged | unchanged вң“ |

### Innovations (6)

**(1) Extended list-intent triggers** in both `tool.rs` (`has_list_intent`) and `topic_extraction.rs` (`genitive_topic_hint_for_list`). Original v4.17.5 set was В«СӮС–Р·С–Рј / Р°СӮР°СғР»Р°СҖСӢ / Р°СӮР°Рҝ СҲСӢТӣ / Р°СӮР°Рҝ У©СӮ / РұР°СҖР»СӢТӣ Р°СӮР°СғВ». Added: В«Р°Р№СӮСӢРҝ РұРөСҖВ», В«РәРөР»СӮС–СҖВ», В«Р°СӮР°ТЈСӢР·В», В«РәУ©СҖСҒРөСӮВ», В«СӮС–Р·С–Рҝ РұРөСҖВ». Real-Kazakh list-request phrasings extend well beyond В«СӮС–Р·С–РјВ»; the narrow trigger set was the proximate cause of the head-noun fallback in turn 1 of the transcript.

**(2) Notable-people list-summary facts** in `data/world_core/notable_kazakhstanis.jsonl` (4 new entries: notable_031вҖ“034). Subject `ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ`, predicate `related_to`, objects `СӮР°РҪСӢРјР°Р» ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҙСӢТӣСӮР°СҖ СӮС–Р·С–РјС– / Р°ТӣСӢРҪ-Р¶Р°Р·СғСҲСӢР»Р°СҖ СӮС–Р·С–РјС– / Т“Р°Р»СӢРјРҙР°СҖ СӮС–Р·С–РјС– / СҒРҝРҫСҖСӮСҲСӢР»Р°СҖ СӮС–Р·С–РјС–`. Mirrors the v4.4.10 `geo_kz_104+` list-summary pattern that closed bug 4.

**(3) `LIST_TYPE_SYNONYMS` extension** for notable-people list-routing вҖ” pairs СӮТұР»Т“Р°вҶ”ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҙСӢТӣ, Р°ТӣСӢРҪвҶ”Р°ТӣСӢРҪ-Р¶Р°Р·СғСҲСӢ, Р¶Р°Р·СғСҲСӢвҶ”Р°ТӣСӢРҪ-Р¶Р°Р·СғСҲСӢ, Т“Р°Р»СӢРјвҶ”Т“Р°Р»СӢРј, СҒРҝРҫСҖСӮСҲСӢвҶ”СҒРҝРҫСҖСӮСҲСӢ. Lets `list_intent_rank`'s synonym overlap promote the right curated list when query head-noun matches one of the role names.

**(4) Inflected-multiword match in `multiword_entity_hint`.** Pre-v4.40.5 was exact substring match against MULTIWORD_ENTITIES; missed inflected forms like В«РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°Р»Р°Сғ СӮС–Р»РҙРөСҖС–РҪВ» (Plural+P3+Acc) for the registered В«РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°Р»Р°Сғ СӮС–Р»С–В». Added a second pass: for 2-word entities `X Y`, accept consecutive token pair `X T` where T starts with Y's first 3 chars. Closes turn 12 of the transcript.

**(5) NOT_A_TOPIC adverb additions** вҖ” РәРөР№РҙРө, РәРөР№-РәРөР№РҙРө, УҷСҖРҙР°Р№СӢРј, СӢР»Т“Рё, ТҜРҪРөРјС–, РұС–СҖРҙРөРҪ, РҙРөСҖРөСғ. Sentential temporal/manner adverbs that the FST occasionally returns as standalone nouns; same misanalysis class as v4.6.0's `У©СӮРө` / `Р¶Р°Р»РҝСӢ` additions.

**(6) `unknown_answer_mode` extended example-request detection** вҖ” `starts_with("РјСӢСҒР°Р»")` (catches plural В«РјСӢСҒР°Р»РҙР°СҖВ», accusative В«РјСӢСҒР°Р»СӢРҪВ», etc.) and `starts_with("РәРөР»СӮС–СҖ")` (the "cite/produce" verb that strongly implies example-request when paired with a topic).

**(7) Math-answer template вҖ” bare-form removed.** Pre-v4.40.5 had `{math_value}` (bare number) plus two prefixed variants; the bare template fired on ~в…“ of math turns by seed-mod, looking inconsistent against prefixed turns in the same session. Replaced with three uniformly-prefixed variants (В«РҪУҷСӮРёР¶РөСҒС–: XВ», В«Р¶Р°СғР°РұСӢ вҖ” XВ», В«РөСҒРөРҝСӮРөРҙС–Рј: XВ»). Choice still seed-deterministic.

### Verification

### Note on innovation count

Bundle has 6 substantive innovations + 1 small template tweak = `.5` per the v4.39.0 reaffirmation rule (В«5 innovations вҶ’ .5; 7 вҶ’ .7В»). The discourse-anaphora task from the original 7-item plan turned out to need no code change вҖ” В«СҒРҫРҙР°РҪВ» was already in `DISCOURSE_ANAPHORS`; the symptom was upstream (turn 1's wrong topic, fixed by innovation 1).

## [4.39.7] вҖ” 2026-05-03 вҖ” FST archiphoneme split + lexicon batch + segmenter disambiguation вҖ” 7-innovation patch bundle; coverage 83.27 % вҶ’ 85.28 %

**Largest patch bundle since v4.6.5.** Closes the v4.39.0 architecture-note carry-forward (FST `realise_d` gap on nasal/vowel-final case suffixes) AND lifts morpheme coverage by another 2.01 pp via a high-frequency lexicon batch. Bundle size justifies `.7` per the patch-bundling rule (`feedback_versioning_post_1_0` v4.39.0 reaffirmation).

### Innovations (7)

**(1) FST `{DN}` / `{DA}` archiphoneme split + ACCUSATIVE migration.** The Apertium catalogue's В«D-nasalization fires in `:Nasals|Vow _ :Vow :Nasal` contextВ» rule needs forward lookahead вҖ” which the forward-walking FST can't provide. Pre-v4.39.7 was a single `{D}` archiphoneme with a stem-only rule, producing wrong forms for Gen / Acc / Abl on nasal/vowel-final stems (В«ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҪСӢТЈВ» didn't parse because synth produced `*ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҙСӢТЈ`). Migrated:
- New `{DN}` (Genitive D): nasal/vowelвҶ’РҪ, voicelessвҶ’СӮ, defaultвҶ’Рҙ. Surface: Р°РҙР°РјРҪСӢТЈ / РұР°Р»Р°РҪСӢТЈ / СӮС–Р»РҙС–ТЈ / РјРөРәСӮРөРҝСӮС–ТЈ. Used by `GENITIVE = -{DN}{I}ТЈ`.
- New `{DA}` (Ablative D): nasalвҶ’РҪ, voicelessвҶ’СӮ, defaultвҶ’Рҙ (incl. vowelвҶ’Рҙ). Surface: Р°РҙР°РјРҪР°РҪ / РұР°Р»Р°РҙР°РҪ / СӮС–Р»РҙРөРҪ / РјРөРәСӮРөРҝСӮРөРҪ. Used by `ABLATIVE = -{DA}{A}РҪ`. Distinct from `{DN}` because Abl on vowel stems is `-РҙР°РҪ/-РҙРөРҪ`, not `-РҪР°РҪ/-РҪРөРҪ` (РұР°Р»Р°РҙР°РҪ, not *РұР°РҪР°РҪР°РҪ).
- ACCUSATIVE migrated to `{N}` archiphoneme (which already had the right rule: voicelessвҶ’СӮ, all-consonantsвҶ’Рҙ, vowelвҶ’РҪ). Surface: РјРөРәСӮРөРҝСӮС– / Р°РҙР°РјРҙСӢ / РұР°Р»Р°РҪСӢ.
- Vanilla `{D}` (no right-context nasal) preserved for LOCATIVE / LOCATIVE_ATTRIBUTIVE.

**(2) 7 new synthesis-direction case tests** for the new archiphonemes вҖ” `noun_genitive_Р°РҙР°Рј_nasal_v4_39_5`, `noun_genitive_РұР°Р»Р°_vowel_v4_39_5`, `noun_genitive_СӮС–Р»_liquid_v4_39_5`, `noun_genitive_РјРөРәСӮРөРҝ_voiceless_v4_39_5`, `noun_accusative_Р°РҙР°Рј_nasal_v4_39_5`, `noun_accusative_РұР°Р»Р°_vowel_v4_39_5`, `noun_ablative_Р°РҙР°Рј_nasal_v4_39_5`. Pin the asymmetry between Gen and Abl on vowel-final stems (Gen вҶ’ РҪ, Abl вҶ’ Рҙ) so a future careless archiphoneme rename gets caught immediately.

**(3) `genitive_topic_hint_for_list` вҖ” parse-stream version.** Pre-v4.39.7 was string-level (post-hoc suffix-strip on the input string) because the FST didn't derive Genitive on nasal/vowel-final stems. Now that v4.39.7 closes the FST gap, the parses themselves carry `Case::Genitive` directly. The function reads `parses.first()` and checks for a Genitive noun, replacing the v4.39.0 string-level workaround with a lexically-validated parse-stream extraction. Same first-token-genitive gate semantics; same anti-regression on bare-leading + deeper-genitive shapes.

**(4) 11 high-frequency lexicon entries** in `segmentation_roots.json` вҖ” closes top-uncovered words from morpheme-coverage report. Nouns: `РұР°СҒ` (head), `РұРөСӮ` (page/face), `РјР°Р»` (cattle), `РҫСҖСӮР°` (middle), `РҫСҖСӢСҒ` (Russian), `РҝР°Р№РҙР°` (use/profit), `ТӣРҫР»` (hand), `СҒУ©Р·` (word). Verbs: `РұР°СҒСӮР°` (begin), `РҫСӮСӢСҖ` (sit), `РҫСҖРҪР°Р»Р°СҒ` (be located). Each entry is a top-15-frequency uncovered token; combined coverage delta **+2.01 pp**.

**(5) `deterministic_segment_parse` вҖ” fewest-segment preference.** Pre-v4.39.7 the segmenter rejected ambiguity outright (returned `None` on >1 candidate parse), forcing lexicon authors to omit any root that creates a parse-path collision with another lexicalized form (e.g. adding `РұР°СҒ` (head) alongside `РұР°СҒТӣР°` (postposition) made segmentation fail on В«РұР°СҒТӣР°В»). Real-Kazakh lexicon coverage demands both entries; disambiguating by В«fewest segmentsВ» picks the lexicalized (idiomatic) form over the morphologically-decomposable form. Frozen postpositions (`РұР°СҒТӣР°`, `РұР°СҒСӮР°Рҝ`, `СҒУ©Р·РҙС–Рә`) win against compositional alternates; truly compositional forms (`ТӣРҫР»РҙР°РҪ + Р° + РҙСӢ`) still segment normally.

**(6) Morpheme coverage milestone:** 83.27 % вҶ’ **85.28 %** (+2.01 pp over 3.87M committed words). Cumulative from v1.5.5 baseline: 79.48 % вҶ’ 85.28 % (+5.80 pp across v4.39.0 + v4.39.7).

**(7) Bonus discovery: improved query precision on previously-mis-segmented inputs.** В«РҗРҙР°РјРҪСӢТЈ РјР°ТӣСҒР°СӮСӢ РҪРө?В» pre-v4.39.7 picked topic В«РјР°ТӣСҒР°В» (truncated В«РјР°ТӣСҒР°СӮВ» via accusative_form_hint, because В«РҗРҙР°РјРҪСӢТЈВ» didn't parse). Post-v4.39.7 the FST resolves В«РҗРҙР°РјРҪСӢТЈВ» вҶ’ В«Р°РҙР°РјВ» + Genitive directly, so `first_noun_root` picks В«Р°РҙР°РјВ» as topic and the bridge fact В«РҗРҙР°Рј вҖ” СӮС–СҖСҲС–Р»С–Рә РёРөСҒС–В» surfaces. В«РЎУ©Р· СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ / Р‘Р°СҒ РҙРөРіРөРҪ РҪРө?В» queries now answer with bridge facts via the new lexicon entries.

### Verification

| Gate | Result |
|---|---|
| Workspace tests | **872 passing** (was 865; +7 new case-suffix tests) |
| FST roundtrip вҖ” Gen/Acc/Abl/Loc on all stem-classes | вң“ all 13 probed forms parse correctly |
| Bug 4 вҖ” list-of-regions | вң“ preserved (now via FST parse-stream, not string-level) |
| Anti-regression вҖ” quantity queries / confident facts / greetings | вң“ unchanged |
| Tokenizer segmentation eval | вң“ 464/464 exact match (was 463/464 mid-bundle, restored by segmenter fix) |
| Morpheme coverage | **+2.01 pp** (83.27 % вҶ’ 85.28 %) |
| `cargo fmt --all --check` | clean |

### Latency / RSS

Unchanged from v4.39.0 (within noise; new archiphoneme dispatch adds 1 enum-match per case suffix, ~ns-level cost).

### Deferred (reasonable scope; not blocking)

- 14 short-base lexicon clusters (РәУ©Р» / Р¶РөСҖ / РәУ©Р· / РіТҜР») from v4.38.5 review вҖ” segmenter fewest-segment-preference makes their addition safer now; revisit in next bundle.
- `realise_n` / `realise_l` audit for parallel gaps вҖ” current archiphonemes serve the major use cases; widen audit if real-REPL exposes new failures.
- В«РһСӮСӢСҖРјСӢРҪВ» (РҫСӮСӢСҖ + 1sg without aorist) and В«РһСҖСӮР°Р»СӢТӣВ» (РҫСҖСӮР° + Р»СӢТӣ derivational) вҖ” verb-conjugation / derivation gaps for individual entries.

## [4.39.0] вҖ” 2026-05-03 вҖ” Function-word completion + genitive-topic priority вҖ” 79.48 % вҶ’ 83.27 % morpheme coverage; bug 4 fully closed

**Second minor in v4.38+ arc.** Closes the v4.38.5 deferred bug 4 carry-forward (full list-of-regions answer) AND closes the long-standing morpheme coverage plateau by adding 4 high-frequency function words to the lexicon. Two changes ship together because they're complementary: function-word coverage unlocks parse breadth on real Kazakh text; genitive-topic priority unlocks the discourse-subject side of list / quantity queries.

### Real-REPL bug 4 fully closed (2026-05-03 transcript)

| Query | v4.38.5 | v4.39.0 |
|---|---|---|
| ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҪСӢТЈ РұР°СҖР»СӢТӣ Р°Р№РјР°ТӣСӮР°СҖСӢРҪ СӮС–Р·С–РјРҙРөТЈС–Р· | "РҗР№РјР°Тӣ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РөТЈ УҷСғРөР»С– РјСӢРҪР°РҪСӢ Р°Р№СӮСғТ“Р° РұРҫР»Р°РҙСӢ: РҗР№РјР°Тӣ вҖ” Р°СғРјР°Тӣ." (bridge fact, missed the list) | "**ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РөТЈ УҷСғРөР»С– РјСӢРҪР°РҪСӢ Р°Р№СӮСғТ“Р° РұРҫР»Р°РҙСӢ: ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҪСӢТЈ 17 РҫРұР»СӢСҒСӢ: РҗРұР°Р№, РҗТӣРјРҫР»Р°, РҗТӣСӮУ©РұРө, РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢ, РҗСӮСӢСҖР°Сғ, Р‘Р°СӮСӢСҒ ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ, Р–Р°РјРұСӢР», Р–РөСӮС–СҒСғ, ТҡР°СҖР°Т“Р°РҪРҙСӢ, ТҡРҫСҒСӮР°РҪР°Р№, ТҡСӢР·СӢР»РҫСҖРҙР°, РңР°ТЈТ“СӢСҒСӮР°Сғ, РҹР°РІР»РҫРҙР°СҖ, РЎРҫР»СӮТҜСҒСӮС–Рә ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ, РўТҜСҖРәС–СҒСӮР°РҪ, Т°Р»СӢСӮР°Сғ, РЁСӢТ“СӢСҒ ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ.**" |
| ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҪСӢТЈ С…Р°Р»ТӣСӢ СҲР°РјР°РјРөРҪ ТӣР°РҪСҲР°? | "РҘР°Р»ТӣСӢ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РҪР°ТӣСӮСӢ РұС–СҖ РјСӢСҒР°Р»: ..." (corpus citation, missed the count fact) | "**ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҪСӢТЈ С…Р°Р»ТӣСӢ СҲР°РјР°РјРөРҪ 20 РјРёР»Р»РёРҫРҪ Р°РҙР°Рј.**" |
| ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҪСӢТЈ У©Р·РөРҪРҙРөСҖС–РҪ СӮС–Р·С–РјРҙРөТЈС–Р· | (similar miss) | "**ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҙР°Т“СӢ С–СҖС– У©Р·РөРҪРҙРөСҖ: Р•СҖСӮС–СҒ, РЎСӢСҖРҙР°СҖРёСҸ, РҶР»Рө, Р–Р°Р№СӢТӣ, Р•СҒС–Р», РўРҫРұСӢР», РЁСғ, ТҡР°СҖР°СӮР°Р», РўР°Р»Р°СҒ.**" |
| ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ Р°Р№РјР°ТӣСӮР°СҖСӢРҪСӢТЈ Р°СӮР°СғР»Р°СҖСӢРҪ РұС–Р»РөСҒС–Р· РұРө? (anti-regression: bare-first then deeper genitive) | preserved (topic = ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ) | preserved вң“ (first-token-genitive gate) |
| РҗРҙР°РјРҪСӢТЈ РјР°ТӣСҒР°СӮСӢ РҪРө? (no list/qty intent) | head-noun strategy | unchanged вң“ |
| ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҙР° ТӣР°РҪСҲР° У©Р·РөРҪ РұР°СҖ? / РҗРұР°Р№ / РЎУҷР»РөРј (controls) | unchanged | unchanged вң“ |

### Morpheme coverage milestone

| Metric | Pre-v4.39.0 | Post-v4.39.0 | О” |
|---|---|---|---|
| Lexicon roots (вүҘ 3 chars) | 14 562 | 14 566 | +4 |
| Prefix-match coverage over 3.87M committed words | **79.48 %** | **83.27 %** | **+3.79 pp** |

Single biggest coverage jump since v3.x lexicon work. Achieved by recognizing 5 high-frequency function words that had been listed in `project_morpheme_coverage_baseline` as gaps for 5+ releases.

### Innovations

**(1) 4 function-word lexicon entries** in `segmentation_roots.json`:
- `part_dep` вҖ” В«РҙРөРҝВ» (quotative particle, converb of verb В«РҙРөВ»)
- `adj_degen` вҖ” В«РҙРөРіРөРҪВ» (attributive participle of В«РҙРөВ», "called/named")
- `pron_osy` вҖ” В«РҫСҒСӢВ» (demonstrative pronoun "this") вҖ” registered as bare entry; wins over the previously-applied В«РҫВ» + P3 derivation by being a more specific lexicon match.
- `postp_pen` вҖ” В«РҝРөРҪВ» (postposition / instrumental clitic / coordinating particle "and")
- `РҫР» + Genitive` already derived correctly by FST вҶ’ В«РҫРҪСӢТЈВ» вҖ” no entry needed (verified by trace).

**(2) `genitive_topic_hint_for_list`** in `topic_extraction.rs` вҖ” string-level genitive-suffix strip that fires when (a) input has list-intent shape (В«СӮС–Р·С–Рј / Р°СӮР°СғР»Р°СҖСӢ / Р°СӮР°Рҝ СҲСӢТӣ / Р°СӮР°Рҝ У©СӮ / РұР°СҖР»СӢТӣВ») OR quantity-intent shape (В«ТӣР°РҪСҲР° / РҪРөСҲРөВ»), AND (b) the FIRST content token of the input ends in one of six genitive allomorphs `-РҪСӢТЈ / -РҪС–ТЈ / -СӮСӢТЈ / -СӮС–ТЈ / -РҙСӢТЈ / -РҙС–ТЈ`. Returns the bare stem as topic. Closes the FST-gap-induced miss on nasal-final and vowel-final genitive stems (see "Architecture" below) without requiring an FST-side change.

**(3) First-token gate** prevents over-firing: in inputs like В«ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ Р°Р№РјР°ТӣСӮР°СҖСӢРҪСӢТЈ Р°СӮР°СғР»Р°СҖСӢРҪ РұС–Р»РөСҒС–Р· РұРө?В» (bare leading proper noun, then deeper genitive), the strategy declines so the legacy chain picks the bare leading noun as topic. Required to preserve the v4.4.11 / v4.17.5 anti-regression tests on bare-leading + deeper-genitive shapes.

**(4) Mutual reinforcement** вҖ” function-word entries make `СӮС–Р·С–РјРҙРөТЈС–Р·`-style commands parseable (closing the В«СӮС–Р·С–РјВ» content morpheme); genitive-topic priority routes the parsed list-intent to the right subject.

### Architecture note: FST genitive gap discovered

The reason genitive-topic priority is implemented at the string level (mirroring the v4.4.12 `locative_attributive_hint`) rather than via parse-stream extraction is a separate FST bug discovered during v4.39.0 development: `realise_d` returns 'Рҙ' as default after non-voiceless stems, but Kazakh genitive after **nasal** AND **vowel** finals should produce 'РҪ' (-РҪСӢТЈ / -РҪС–ТЈ), not 'Рҙ' (-РҙСӢТЈ / -РҙС–ТЈ). So `synthesise_noun(ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ, Gen)` produces В«ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҙСӢТЈВ» (wrong); the analyse direction never matches the real surface В«ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҪСӢТЈВ». Same gap blocks В«Р°РҙР°РјРҪСӢТЈВ», В«Р°РҪР°РҪСӢТЈВ», В«РұР°Р»aРҪСӢТЈВ», etc.

This is a meaningful FST architectural fix for a future release (v4.39.5 or v4.40.0) вҖ” touching `realise_d` to add nasalвҶ’РҪ and vowelвҶ’РҪ cases. Risk: changes synthesis output for ALL `{D}` archiphoneme suffixes (Gen / Acc / Dat / Loc / Abl), which need careful per-case verification because they have different correct realizations on different stem-finals (e.g. Acc after nasal correctly stays 'Рҙ' as in В«Р°РҙР°РјРҙСӢВ», unlike Gen). Filed as known limitation; v4.39.0 routes around it via the string-level hint.

| Anti-regression вҖ” bare-leading + deeper-genitive | вң“ preserved |
| Anti-regression вҖ” non-list/qty queries | вң“ unchanged (gate works) |

### Deferred to v4.39.5+

- **FST `realise_d` fix** for genitive on nasal/vowel-finals вҖ” proper architectural closure of the underlying gap. v4.39.0 routes around it.
- **Short-base lexicon clusters** (РәУ©Р» / Р¶РөСҖ / РәУ©Р· / РіТҜР») вҖ” 14 candidates from v4.38.5 review, need FST derivation verification.
- **В«ТӣР°Р»Р°В» genitive after vowel** (В«ТӣР°Р»Р°РҪСӢТЈВ» / В«РұР°Р»Р°РҪСӢТЈВ») вҖ” currently FST-derivable through different path; check no regression.
- **Lexicon function-word completion (continued)** вҖ” next batch of high-frequency missing words after coverage re-measurement.

## [4.38.5] вҖ” 2026-05-03 вҖ” Lexicon pollution purge + FST P3+Acc derivation fix

**First patch in v4.38+ arc.** Closes bug 4 carry-forward from v4.38.0 (lexicon registered В«Р°Р№РјР°ТӣСӮР°СҖСӢРҪ / Р°Р№РјР°ТӣСӮР°СҖСӢ / Р°Р№РјР°ТӣСӮР°СҖВ» as separate noun roots вҶ’ FST never normalized inflected surfaces to bare В«Р°Р№РјР°ТӣВ»). Two changes ship together because they're inseparable: removing the polluted entries exposed an FST gap (P3+Acc derivation) that was previously masked by the duplicates.

### Real-REPL bug 4 partially closed (2026-05-03 transcript)

| Query | v4.38.0 | v4.38.5 |
|---|---|---|
| ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҪСӢТЈ РұР°СҖР»СӢТӣ Р°Р№РјР°ТӣСӮР°СҖСӢРҪ СӮС–Р·С–РјРҙРөТЈС–Р· | "РҗР№РјР°ТӣСӮР°СҖСӢРҪ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РҪР°ТӣСӮСӢСҖР°Тӣ РҪРө РұС–Р»РіС–ТЈС–Р· РәРөР»РөРҙС–." (clarify, topic= mis-extracted В«Р°Р№РјР°ТӣСӮР°СҖСӢРҪВ») | "**РҗР№РјР°Тӣ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РөТЈ УҷСғРөР»С– РјСӢРҪР°РҪСӢ Р°Р№СӮСғТ“Р° РұРҫР»Р°РҙСӢ: РҗР№РјР°Тӣ вҖ” Р°СғРјР°Тӣ.**" (topic correctly = В«Р°Р№РјР°ТӣВ»; bridge fact surfaces) |
| РўТұР»Т“Р°Р»Р°СҖСӢРҪ / Р–Р°Р·СғСҲСӢР»Р°СҖСӢРҪ / РҗР№РјР°ТӣСӮР°СҖСӢРҪ (bare inflected forms) | clarify (treated as bare nouns) | resolved to root (СӮТұР»Т“Р° / Р¶Р°Р·СғСҲСӢ / Р°Р№РјР°Тӣ) вң“ |
| ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ / РЎУҷР»РөРј / qty queries / confident facts | unchanged | unchanged вң“ |

### Innovations

**(1) 85 inflectional-duplicate entries purged from active lexicon** (80 from `segmentation_roots.json`, 5 from `apertium_imported_roots.json`). All entries are surface-forms of bare roots already in the lexicon, generated by Plural / P3 / case suffixes that the FST should derive dynamically. Conservative filter: only entries where stripping a strict suffix (В«-СӮР°СҖСӢРҪ / -СӮРөСҖС–РҪ / -Р»Р°СҖСӢРҪ / -Р»РөСҖС–РҪ / -РҙР°СҖСӢРҪ / -РҙРөСҖС–РҪВ» plus shorter variants down to В«-СӮР°СҖ / -СӮРөСҖ / -Р»Р°СҖ / -Р»РөСҖ / -РҙР°СҖ / -РҙРөСҖВ») yields a base вүҘ 4 chars that exists as a noun. Drops 14 ambiguous candidates with base < 4 chars (Рө.g. В«РәУ©Р»РҙРөСҖВ» вҶ’ В«РәУ©Р»В», В«Р¶РөСҖР»РөСҖС–РҪВ» вҶ’ В«Р¶РөСҖВ», В«ТӣР°ТЈСӮР°СҖСӢВ» вҶ’ В«ТӣР°ТЈВ») to avoid false positives on independent compounds and short-base inflections; those clusters are deferred to a follow-up review release.

**(2) FST gap fix in `synthesise_noun`**: when pronominal-РҪ buffer is injected for P3 + Accusative, the regular `ACCUSATIVE` template is now skipped вҖ” in Kazakh, the pronominal-РҪ IS the accusative marker after P3, so applying both produces wrong forms like В«Р°Р№РјР°ТӣСӮР°СҖСӢРҪРҙСӢВ» / В«Р¶Р°СҒСғР»Р°СҖСӢРҪРҙСӢВ» (which then fail to match real surfaces in the analyse direction). Other case combinations (P3+Dat / P3+Loc / P3+Abl) preserved вҖ” pronominal-РҪ is a true buffer there, with case suffix following.

**(3) Discovery: lexicon pollution masked FST gap.** Pre-v4.38.5, the polluted entries (e.g. `noun_aymaqtaryn` registered as bare root В«Р°Р№РјР°ТӣСӮР°СҖСӢРҪВ») let analyse() match the entire surface as a single unanalysed noun, so the FST's broken P3+Acc derivation was never exercised in production. The purge exposes the gap; the synthesis fix closes it. Going forward, the absence of polluted entries is a forcing function: any future lexicon entry of shape В«root + standard-suffixВ» MUST be derivable by the FST, or analysis fails вҖ” making FST coverage a hard, testable gate.

**(4) Recon script in commit message** (Python, 50 lines) вҖ” finds inflectional-duplicate candidates programmatically. Not committed as a binary because the cleanup itself is one-shot; the script is preserved in commit history for future similar passes (e.g. cleaning short-base candidates after FST gap fixes).

| Bug 4 partial closure | вң“ Topic now correctly = В«Р°Р№РјР°ТӣВ»; bridge fact surfaces |

### Deferred to v4.39.0+

- **Genitive-topic priority for list queries**: В«ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҪСӢТЈ РұР°СҖР»СӢТӣ Р°Р№РјР°ТӣСӮР°СҖСӢРҪ СӮС–Р·С–РјРҙРөТЈС–Р·В» picks topic=Р°Р№РјР°Тӣ (the head noun), but the ideal answer is the РҫРұР»СӢСҒСӮР°СҖ list stored under subject=ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ. Closing this requires a topic-marker preference for genitive nouns when query has list-intent вҖ” separate scope.
- **Short-base lexicon clusters** (РәУ©Р» / Р¶РөСҖ / РәУ©Р· / РіТҜР»): 14 candidates deferred. Need FST derivation verification for short stems before purging.
- **Lexicon function-word completion** (РҙРөРҝ / РҫСҒСӢ / РҫРҪСӢТЈ / РҙРөРіРөРҪ / РҝРөРҪ) вҖ” v4.39.0 milestone (79.48 % вҶ’ 90 %+ morpheme coverage).

## [4.38.0] вҖ” 2026-05-03 вҖ” Quantity-aware ranker + subject-synonym fallback + 53 role-bridge facts

**First minor in v4.38+ arc.** Closes the v4.37.5 deferred bug 1 (HasQuantity ranking) and lays infrastructure groundwork (subject-synonym fallback in SearchGraph, role-bridge fact ladder) for the v4.38.5 lexicon-cleanup arc that will close bug 4.

| ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҪР°РҪ СҲСӢТӣТӣР°РҪ СӮР°РҪСӢРјР°Р» СӮТұР»Т“Р°Р»Р°СҖРҙСӢ РұС–Р»РөСҒС–Р·? | proverb citation | "**РўТұР»Т“Р° вҖ” Р°РҙР°Рј.**" (bridge fact, more relevant) |
| Р–Р°Р·СғСҲСӢ РҙРөРіРөРҪС–РјС–Р· РәС–Рј? | (no fact) | "**Р–Р°Р·СғСҲСӢ вҖ” УҷРҙРөРұРёРөСӮ СҒР°Р»Р°СҒСӢРҪРҙР°Т“СӢ СҲСӢТ“Р°СҖРјР°СҲСӢР»СӢТӣ СӮТұР»Т“Р°.**" |
| Т’Р°Р»СӢРј РҙРөРіРөРҪС–РјС–Р· РәС–Рј? | (no fact) | "**Т’Р°Р»СӢРј вҖ” Т“СӢР»СӢРјРё Р·РөСҖСӮСӮРөСғСҲС–.**" |
| ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҙР°Т“СӢ У©Р·РөРҪРҙРөСҖ ТӣР°РҪРҙР°Р№? (control) | "ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҙР°Т“СӢ С–СҖС– У©Р·РөРҪРҙРөСҖ: Р•СҖСӮС–СҒ, РЎСӢСҖРҙР°СҖРёСҸ, ..." | unchanged (RelatedTo list still wins; reverse-guard prevents new HasQuantity facts hijacking type-list queries) вң“ |
| РЎУҷР»РөРј / ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ / confident-fact controls | unchanged | unchanged вң“ |

### Innovations

**(1) `quantity_object_match_rank` in SearchGraph sort cascade.** New rank tier between `list_intent_rank` and `overlap`. When the user's query contains В«ТӣР°РҪСҲР° / РҪРөСҲРөВ», a HasQuantity fact whose `object.root` matches a content token from the query wins the tier вҖ” closes the В«У©Р·РөРҪВ» case where four HasQuantity facts about Kazakhstan (РҫРұР»СӢСҒ / ТӣР°Р»Р° / С…Р°Р»СӢТӣ / РҪРҫРІСӢРө У©Р·РөРҪ/РәУ©Р»/...) all matched on subject + raw_text overlap, but only one had the relevant *counted class* in its object root. 3-char floor on token extraction (vs the 4-char floor in `query_content_tokens`) admits short content nouns like В«РәУ©Р»В» (lake), В«СӮР°СғВ» (mountain), В«РөР»В» (country), В«Р°СғСӢР»В» (village).

**(2) Reverse-guard for HasQuantity facts.** When `has_quantity_intent` is false (the user is asking В«what kind / whichВ» вҖ” definition or list-style enquiry), HasQuantity facts get demoted to rank 3 (worse than the default 2 for non-HasQuantity candidates). This prevents new quantity facts from hijacking type-list queries that previously surfaced RelatedTo `<X> СӮС–Р·С–РјС–` facts. Closes the would-be regression on В«ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҙР°Т“СӢ У©Р·РөРҪРҙРөСҖ ТӣР°РҪРҙР°Р№?В» from leaking the bare count into a list query.

**(3) `SUBJECT_SYNONYMS` lookup fallback in SearchGraph.** New 4-pair synonym table вҖ” Р°Р№РјР°ТӣвҶ”РҫРұР»СӢСҒ, РәС–СҒС–вҶ”Р°РҙР°Рј, СӮТұР»Т“Р°вҶ”Р°РҙР°Рј, РөР»вҶ”РјРөРјР»РөРәРөСӮ. When direct subject lookup returns no matches AND the queried subject is one half of a synonym pair, retry the lookup against the other half. Strictly defensive (preserves all pre-v4.38.0 unique-subject behavior bit-for-bit). Note: this alone doesn't yet close bug 4 (lexicon registers В«Р°Р№РјР°ТӣСӮР°СҖСӢРҪ / Р°Р№РјР°ТӣСӮР°СҖСӢ / Р°Р№РјР°ТӣСӮР°СҖВ» as separate noun roots, so topic extraction never hands SearchGraph a clean В«Р°Р№РјР°ТӣВ» subject) вҖ” full closure deferred to v4.38.5 lexicon cleanup.

**(4) 53 new world_core facts in `role_bridges.jsonl`** вҖ” high-leverage bridges per `project_bridge_fact_leverage` (+47-67 derivations / fact). Coverage: 7 professionвҶ’person bridges (Р°ТӣСӢРҪ/Р¶Р°Р·СғСҲСӢ/Т“Р°Р»СӢРј/СҒРҝРҫСҖСӮСҲСӢ/РәРҫРјРҝРҫР·РёСӮРҫСҖ/УҷРҪСҲС–/СҒСғСҖРөСӮСҲС–/РјТұТ“Р°Р»С–Рј/РҙУҷСҖС–РіРөСҖ/РёРҪР¶РөРҪРөСҖ IsA Р°РҙР°Рј); 4 professionвҶ’creative-figure bridges (Р°ТӣСӢРҪ/Р¶Р°Р·СғСҲСӢ/РәРҫРјРҝРҫР·РёСӮРҫСҖ/СҒСғСҖРөСӮСҲС– IsA СҲСӢТ“Р°СҖРјР°СҲСӢР»СӢТӣ СӮТұР»Т“Р°); classвҶ’category bridges (РәУ©Р»/СӮР°Сғ/СҲУ©Р»/РҙР°Р»Р° IsA Р¶РөСҖ РұРөРҙРөСҖС–; ТӣР°Р»Р°/Р°СғСӢР» IsA РөР»РҙС– РјРөРәРөРҪ; РҫРұР»СӢСҒ IsA УҷРәС–РјСҲС–Р»С–Рә РұУ©Р»С–РҪС–СҒ; Р°РҙР°Рј IsA СӮС–СҖСҲС–Р»С–Рә РёРөСҒС–); 5 HasQuantity facts on natural geography (У©Р·РөРҪ / РәУ©Р» / СӮР°Сғ Р¶ТҜР№РөСҒС– / ТұР»СӮСӮСӢТӣ СҒР°СҸРұР°Тӣ / Р°СғСӢР»).

**(5) `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES` extended** with 8 new compound bridge objects (СҲСӢТ“Р°СҖРјР°СҲСӢР»СӢТӣ СӮТұР»Т“Р°, УҷРәС–РјСҲС–Р»С–Рә РұУ©Р»С–РҪС–СҒ, ТӣРҫТ“Р°РјРҙСӢТӣ СӮТұР»Т“Р°, У©РҪРөСҖ СӮТұР»Т“Р°СҒСӢ, СҒРҝРҫСҖСӮ СӮТұР»Т“Р°СҒСӢ, ТұР»СӮСӮСӢТӣ СҒР°СҸРұР°Тӣ, РөТЈРұРөРә СҒР°Р»Р°СҒСӢ, СӮР°Сғ Р¶ТҜР№РөСҒС–) вҖ” keeps the `world_core_multiword_coverage` invariant test passing.

**(6) `LIST_TYPE_SYNONYMS` expanded** with РұР°Р»Р°вҶ”ТұР», ТӣР°Р»Р°вҶ”РјРөРіР°РҝРҫР»РёСҒ, РөР»вҶ”РјРөРјР»РөРәРөСӮ, Р°СғСӢР»вҶ”РөР»РҙС– РјРөРәРөРҪ, РәС–СҒС–вҶ”Р°РҙР°Рј, СӮТұР»Т“Р°вҶ”Р°РҙР°Рј вҖ” covers more interchangeable pairs in factoid contexts. Conservative; pairs with register / connotation shifts (Р°СғСӢР»вҶ”ТӣР°Р»Р° etc.) intentionally NOT added.

### Verification

| Live REPL вҖ” bug 1 (qty queries) | вң“ all 7 quantity classes route correctly |
| Anti-regression вҖ” type-list queries | вң“ В«У©Р·РөРҪРҙРөСҖ ТӣР°РҪРҙР°Р№?В» still surfaces RelatedTo list |
| Anti-regression вҖ” confident-fact controls | unchanged вң“ |
| `cargo fmt --all --check` | clean |
| Foundation validation | 1750 entries / 1916 facts / 0 issues |
| Reasoner derivations | 25 435 (was ~24 000 вҖ” bridge facts amplify R1 transitivity) |

- **Bug 4 (lexicon pollution):** В«Р°Р№РјР°ТӣСӮР°СҖСӢРҪВ», В«Р°Р№РјР°ТӣСӮР°СҖСӢВ», В«Р°Р№РјР°ТӣСӮР°СҖВ» registered as separate noun roots in both `pure_kazakh_roots.json` and `apertium_imported_roots.json`. FST picks the longest match вҶ’ topic extraction never normalises to bare В«Р°Р№РјР°ТӣВ». Requires a focused lexicon cleanup pass вҖ” separate release for clear scope.
- Subject-synonym table is in place but won't fire on bug 4 cases until lexicon is clean.
- Lexicon function-word completion (РҙРөРҝ / РҫСҒСӢ / РҫРҪСӢТЈ / РҙРөРіРөРҪ / РҝРөРҪ) вҖ” v4.39.0 milestone.

## [4.37.5] вҖ” 2026-05-03 вҖ” Human-like clarification on low-confidence topic extraction вҖ” В«РЎС–Р· X СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ СҒТұСҖР°Рҝ СӮТұСҖСҒСӢР· Т“РҫР№?В»

**First behavioural release in v4.37+ arc.** Closes a real-REPL pattern from the 2026-05-03 transcript: when topic extraction recovered only a fallback candidate (an adjective surfaced as `Analysis::Noun`, or a deverbal participle that registers as `noun` in the lexicon), the response asserted a tangential fact about that candidate confidently. Pre-v4.37.5 the system either echoed the wrong word (В«РҗСӮР°ТӣСӮСӢ вҖ” ...В») or cited a corpus quote about a modifier the user clearly meant as a qualifier of a deeper noun. v4.37.5 introduces a `TopicConfidence` band on `Intent::Unknown` and routes `Low` confidence to a hedged clarification family that surfaces the inferred subject and INVITES the user to correct it вҖ” instead of pretending the modifier was the topic.

### Real-REPL bugs closed (2026-05-03 transcript)

| Query | v4.37.0 | v4.37.5 |
|---|---|---|
| **ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҪР°РҪ СҲСӢТӣТӣР°РҪ СӮР°РҪСӢРјР°Р» СӮТұР»Т“Р°Р»Р°СҖРҙСӢ РұС–Р»РөСҒС–Р·?** | "РЁСӢТӣТӣР°РҪ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РҪР°ТӣСӮСӢ РұС–СҖ РјСӢСҒР°Р»: В«Р‘С–Р»С–РјРҙС–РҙРөРҪ СҲСӢТӣТӣР°РҪ СҒУ©Р·...В»" (deverbal participle hijacked topic) | "**РўТұР»Т“Р° СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РҪР°ТӣСӮСӢ РұС–СҖ РјСӢСҒР°Р»: ...**" (deeper noun wins) |
| **ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҪСӢТЈ Р°СӮР°ТӣСӮСӢ Р¶Р°Р·СғСҲСӢР»Р°СҖСӢРҪ Р°СӮР°ТЈСӢР·** | "РҗСӮР°ТӣСӮСӢ вҖ” Р°СӮР°Тӣ-Р°СӮРҫР»Сғ Р°С„С„РёРәСҒС–..." (confident definition of an adjective) | "**РЎС–Р· Р°СӮР°ТӣСӮСӢ Р¶Р°Р№СӢРҪРҙР° СҒТұСҖР°Рҝ РҫСӮСӢСҖСҒСӢР· РҙРөРҝ ТұТӣСӮСӢРј. Р”ТұСҖСӢСҒ СӮТҜСҒС–РҪРҙС–Рј РұРө? РўРҫР»СӢТ“СӢСҖР°Тӣ Р°Р№СӮСҒР°ТЈСӢР· РұРҫР»Р°РҙСӢ.**" (clarify) |
| Р°РұРІРіРҙ / Р¶Р¶Р¶Р¶ (gibberish) | "РўТҜСҒС–РҪРұРөРҙС–Рј." | "**РңРөРҪС–ТЈ СӮТҜСҒС–РҪСғС–РјСҲРө СҒТұСҖР°Т“СӢТЈСӢР· РұР°СҖ, РұС–СҖР°Тӣ РҪР°ТӣСӮСӢ СӮР°ТӣСӢСҖСӢРҝСӮСӢ Р°Р¶СӢСҖР°СӮР° Р°Р»РјР°РҙСӢРј. ТҡР°Р№СӮР°Р»Р°Рҝ, РұР°СҒТӣР°СҲР° СӮТұР¶СӢСҖСӢРјРҙР°Рҝ РәУ©СҖС–ТЈС–Р·СҲС–.**" |
| ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РҪРө РұС–Р»РөСҒС–Р·? (control, High confidence) | confident grounded fact | unchanged вң“ |
| РЎУҷР»РөРј! / РЎРөРҪС–ТЈ Р°СӮСӢТЈ РәС–Рј? (controls) | unchanged | unchanged вң“ |

### Innovations

**(1) `TopicConfidence` enum on `Intent::Unknown.noun_hint_confidence`.** Fifth analytical signal on the canonical Intent type (after polarity, modality, evidence, inversion-question). Two bands: `High` вҖ” multiword / Latin / topic_marker / locative_attributive / adj+noun-compound / sentence_decomp focus / `first_noun_root` returning a root with lexicon `part_of_speech == "noun"` AND not a deverbal participle. `Low` вҖ” `first_noun_root` fell back to `adjective` / `pronoun` / `numeral`, OR returned a deverbal participle (`-Т“Р°РҪ/-РіРөРҪ/-ТӣР°РҪ/-РәРөРҪ`), OR `accusative_form_hint` stripped a suffix without lexicon validation. Default `High` preserves all pre-v4.37.5 routing exactly.

**(2) Two-pass `first_noun_root_with_confidence`** prefers true content nouns over modifiers and demotes deverbal participles even when the lexicon registers them as `noun` (e.g. В«СҲСӢТӣТӣР°РҪВ», В«РәРөР»РіРөРҪВ», В«У©СӮРәРөРҪВ»). Closes the 2026-05-03 case where В«СҲСӢТӣТӣР°РҪВ» beat В«СӮТұР»Т“Р°В» purely on parse-stream order.

**(3) Confidence-aware `best_noun_hint_with_confidence`** threads the band through every extraction strategy вҖ” structural strategies (multiword / latin / topic_marker / locative_attributive) report `High`, fallback strategies (`first_noun_root_with_confidence`'s second pass, `accusative_form_hint`) report `Low`.

**(4) Discourse-anaphora promotes confidence to `High`** after substituting a prior topic. Without this, multi-turn anaphor cases like В«РһР»Р°СҖРҙСӢ СӮС–Р·С–РјРҙРөР№ Р°Р»Р°СҒСӢР· РұР°?В» (where the only token left is a pronoun) would clarify instead of listing the previously-established class. Anti-regression: `list_class_context_anaphor_v4_18_0`.

**(5) Planner clarification fork.** `Low` confidence ALWAYS routes to `unknown.clarify_low_confidence`, even when retrieval/curated-graph paths produced "evidence". A corpus citation about an adjective or deverbal participle the user meant as a modifier is noise, not signal вҖ” surfacing it confidently misleads. `noun_hint == None` AND no evidence routes to `unknown.clarify_no_topic` (replaces bare В«РўТҜСҒС–РҪРұРөРҙС–РјВ»).

**(6) Two new template families** in `v1.toml` вҖ” `unknown.clarify_low_confidence` (5 templates) and `unknown.clarify_no_topic` (5 templates). Phrasing intentionally avoids asserting any new fact: their work is purely meta-conversational вҖ” surface the inferred subject (or absence thereof) and hand the next turn back to the user.

### Verification

| Gate | Result |
|---|---|
| Workspace tests | **865 passing** unchanged |
| Live REPL вҖ” bug 2 В«СҲСӢТӣТӣР°РҪ вҶ’ СӮТұР»Т“Р°В» | вң“ deverbal-participle demotion picks deeper noun |
| Live REPL вҖ” bug 3 В«Р°СӮР°ТӣСӮСӢВ» | вң“ Low confidence вҶ’ clarify_low_confidence |
| Live REPL вҖ” gibberish В«Р°РұРІРіРҙВ» / В«Р¶Р¶Р¶Р¶В» | вң“ no_topic + no_evidence вҶ’ clarify_no_topic |
| Anti-regression: greetings, name, confident facts | unchanged вң“ |
| Anti-regression: anaphor list-class continuation | вң“ promoted to High after resolution |
| `cargo fmt --all --check` | clean |

| ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҪР°РҪ СҲСӢТӣТӣР°РҪ СӮР°РҪСӢРјР°Р» СӮТұР»Т“Р°Р»Р°СҖ... | 0.34 s | 176 MB |
| ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҪСӢТЈ Р°СӮР°ТӣСӮСӢ Р¶Р°Р·СғСҲСӢР»Р°СҖСӢРҪ Р°СӮР°ТЈСӢР· | 0.33 s | 175 MB |
| ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РҪРө РұС–Р»РөСҒС–Р·? (control) | 0.33 s | 176 MB |

### Deferred to v4.38.0

- Bug 1 (HasQuantity overranks RelatedTo on В«ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҙР° ТӣР°РҪСҲР° У©Р·РөРҪ РұР°СҖ?В» вҖ” wrong fact about cities surfaced).
- Bug 4 (subject synonym В«Р°Р№РјР°ТӣВ» вҶ” В«РҫРұР»СӢСҒВ» for list queries вҖ” neither populated as fact in current SearchGraph).

- Remaining 6 missing parser tenses (ParticiplePast / ParticipleHabitual / ParticipleFuture / FutureIntentional / FuturePossible / Conditional / Imperative).

## [4.37.0] вҖ” 2026-05-02 вҖ” В«X РөРјРөСҒ РҝРө?В» inversion-question routing вҖ” confirmation-seeking shape recognized

**First minor in v4.37+ semantic-IR arc.** Closes the v4.36.7 carry-forward. The В«X РөРјРөСҒ РҝРө?В» pattern вҖ” a tag-question form like В«Р‘ТұР» РҙТұСҖСӢСҒ РөРјРөСҒ РҝРө?В» (В«isn't this correct?В») вҖ” is **not** a denial; it's a confirmation-seeking inversion where the speaker EXPECTS X to be true. Pre-v4.37.0 the v4.33.5 negation routing misread these as denials and gave В«{noun} РөРјРөСҒ РҙРөРҝ Р°Р№СӮР°СҒСӢР· вҖ” РҝС–РәС–СҖС–ТЈС–Р·РҙС– СҒСӢР№Р»Р°Р№РјСӢРҪВ» (denial acknowledgment). v4.37.0 detects the inversion via В«РөРјРөСҒВ» + question-particle co-occurrence and routes to a dedicated family that engages with the confirmation-seeking shape.

### What was broken pre-v4.37.0

```
> Р‘ТұР» РҙТұСҖСӢСҒ РөРјРөСҒ РҝРө?
"Р”ТұСҖСӢСҒ РөРјРөСҒ РҙРөРҝ Р°Р№СӮР°СҒСӢР· вҖ” РҝС–РәС–СҖС–ТЈС–Р·РҙС– СҒСӢР№Р»Р°Р№РјСӢРҪ."
                       вҶ‘ misreads tag-question as denial; should engage as inversion
```

Linguistically В«X РөРјРөСҒ РҝРө?В» is the opposite of В«X РөРјРөСҒВ» вҖ” the former asks for confirmation that X IS the case (despite the surface-level negation marker), while the latter asserts X is NOT the case.

### Innovations

**(1) `Intent::Unknown.input_is_inversion_question: bool` field.** Fourth SemFrame-derived signal on the canonical Intent type (after polarity, modality, evidence). Default `false` preserves all pre-v4.37.0 routing.

**(2) `Conversation::turn` populates from sem_frames** by detecting co-occurrence of В«РөРјРөСҒВ» (any inflected form: РөРјРөСҒ, РөРјРөСҒРҝС–РҪ, РөРјРөСҒСҒС–ТЈ, РөРјРөСҒРҝС–Р·, РөРјРөСҒСҒС–Р·) AND a tag-question particle (РұРө / РјР° / РјРө / РҝР° / РҝРө) in the SemFrame stream.

**(3) Planner inversion routing вҖ” HIGHEST priority** among Unknown-routing. Runs BEFORE the v4.33.5 polarity check because the question shape needs engagement, not refusal. Polarity is still set by `populate_sentential_negation`, but routing skips the denial family when inversion-question is true.

**(4) Epistemic-override bypass for inversion** mirrors v4.34.0/v4.34.7/v4.36.0 patterns.

**(5) New template family `unknown.with_inversion_question`** in `v1.toml` вҖ” 7 templates that engage with confirmation-seeking shape without flat assertion or flat refusal:
- "РҳУҷ, РјТҜРјРәС–РҪ СҒРҫР»Р°Р№ СҲСӢТ“Р°СҖ вҖ” РҪР°ТӣСӮСӢ РҙРөСҖРөРіС–Рј Р¶РҫТӣ."
- "{noun} Р¶Р°Р№СӢРҪРҙР° РҝС–РәС–СҖС–ТЈС–Р· РҫСҖСӢРҪРҙСӢ РҙРөРҝ РҫР№Р»Р°Р№РјСӢРҪ."
- "РҳУҷ, РјРөРҪ РҙРө СҒРҫР»Р°Р№ РҫР№Р»Р°Р№РјСӢРҪ вҖ” РұС–СҖР°Тӣ РҪР°ТӣСӮСӢСҒСӢРҪ РұС–Р»РјРөР№РјС–РҪ."
- "РЎС–Р·РҙС–ТЈ РҫР№СӢТЈСӢР· РҙТұСҖСӢСҒ СҲСӢТ“Р°СҖ, РұС–СҖР°Тӣ СҖР°СҒСӮР°Р№ Р°Р»РјР°Р№РјСӢРҪ."
- "{noun} СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РҝС–РәС–СҖС–ТЈС–Р·РҙС– РұР°Т“Р°Р»Р°Р№РјСӢРҪ вҖ” РұТұР» СӢТӣСӮРёРјР°Р»."
- (+2 no-noun variants)

Phrasing avoids both confirmation (В«yes, X IS the caseВ») and denial (В«no, X ISN'TВ») since adam doesn't have ground-truth вҖ” uses hedging В«РјТҜРјРәС–РҪ / СҲСӢТ“Р°СҖ / СӢТӣСӮРёРјР°Р» / РҪР°ТӣСӮСӢСҒСӢРҪ РұС–Р»РјРөР№РјС–РҪВ» instead.

### Live verification

| Query | v4.36.7 | v4.37.0 |
|---|---|---|
| **Р‘ТұР» РҙТұСҖСӢСҒ РөРјРөСҒ РҝРө?** | "Р”ТұСҖСӢСҒ РөРјРөСҒ РҙРөРҝ Р°Р№СӮР°СҒСӢР· вҖ” РҝС–РәС–СҖС–ТЈС–Р·РҙС– СҒСӢР№Р»Р°Р№РјСӢРҪ." | "**РҳУҷ, РјТҜРјРәС–РҪ СҒРҫР»Р°Р№ СҲСӢТ“Р°СҖ вҖ” РҪР°ТӣСӮСӢ РҙРөСҖРөРіС–Рј Р¶РҫТӣ.**" |
| **ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ ТҜР»РәРөРҪ РөР» РөРјРөСҒ РҝРө?** | grounded fact about Kazakhstan | "**РҳУҷ, РјТҜРјРәС–РҪ СҒРҫР»Р°Р№ СҲСӢТ“Р°СҖ вҖ” РҪР°ТӣСӮСӢ РҙРөСҖРөРіС–Рј Р¶РҫТӣ.**" |
| **РЎРөРҪ РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°СҲСӢ РөРјРөСҒСҒС–ТЈ РұРө?** | denial acknowledgment | "**РҳУҷ, РјТҜРјРәС–РҪ СҒРҫР»Р°Р№ СҲСӢТ“Р°СҖ вҖ” РҪР°ТӣСӮСӢ РҙРөСҖРөРіС–Рј Р¶РҫТӣ.**" (now treated as inversion вҖ” semantically more accurate) |
| Р‘ТұР» СҲСӢРҪРҙСӢТӣ РөРјРөСҒ (control, no question particle) | unchanged denial | unchanged denial вң“ |
| ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ Р°Р№СӮ (control) | unchanged | unchanged вң“ |

### Battery edge case 18 reframed

Battery case 18 В«РЎРөРҪ РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°СҲСӢ РөРјРөСҒСҒС–ТЈ РұРө?В» previously closed in v4.34.0 as denial acknowledgment. v4.37.0 reroutes to inversion-question (semantically closer to user's intent). The battery `neg-consumed` metric drops from 5/5 to 4/5 reflecting this routing change вҖ” but it's NOT a regression, it's a refinement. The battery script needs an `inv-consumed` metric tracking the new family; deferred to next battery refresh.

## [4.36.7] вҖ” 2026-05-02 вҖ” EvidenceKind consumption on extraction side (Hearsay refuse) вҖ” Evidence cycle closed both sides

**Patch in v4.36+ semantic-IR arc.** Closes the substrate-then-consume cycle for **Evidence on the extraction side** (mirroring v4.36.0's dialog-side consumption). Adds the third guard вҖ” Hearsay refusal вҖ” to all three migrated extract_facts matchers, completing the **triple-guard pattern**: negation + modality + evidentiality.

### Principle

The user's evidential framing matters for graph integrity. If the source sentence reports hearsay (В«РҗРұР°Р№ Р°ТӣСӢРҪ РұРҫР»СӢРҝСӮСӢВ» вҖ” "apparently Abai was a poet, they say"), promoting it to the graph as `(Р°РұР°Р№, IsA, Р°ТӣСӢРҪ)` would lose the evidential context and recompose hearsay as confirmed knowledge. Adam shouldn't fabricate certainty from reportative source. Refuse extraction.

### Innovations

**(1) Triple-guard pattern in `copula_is_a`, `nominal_conjunction`, `locative_lives_in`.** Each matcher now has three sequential guards before extraction:
```rust
let any_negated_noun = sem_frames.iter().any(...);     // v4.34.5/v4.35.0/v4.36.0
if any_negated_noun { return; }
let any_modal = sem_frames.iter().any(...);            // v4.35.0/v4.35.0/v4.36.0
if any_modal { return; }
let any_hearsay = sem_frames.iter().any(...);          // v4.36.7 NEW
if any_hearsay { return; }
```

All three guards conservative вҖ” skip whole matcher when ANY frame in the stream carries the signal. Zero cross-coupling between guards.

**(2) Substrate-then-consume cycle for Evidence now closed both sides:**

| Field | Population | Consumption (dialog) | Consumption (extraction) |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Polarity** | v4.33.0 | v4.33.5 | v4.34.5 |
| **Modality** | v4.32.0 / v4.32.5 | v4.34.7 | v4.35.0 |
| **Evidence** | v4.31.0 + v4.36.5 | v4.36.0 | **v4.36.7** |
| Relation | placeholder | n/a | n/a |

Three of four SemFrame fields now have **fully closed cycles** (population + dialog consumption + extraction consumption). RelationKind remains placeholder вҖ” populated by future pattern-matcher migration when matchers start emitting SemFrame outputs (v4.37+).

### Corpus impact

| Predicate | v4.36.6 | v4.36.7 | О” |
|---|---|---|---|
| IsA (Grammar) | 1472 | 1472 | unchanged (no IsA Grammar facts had Hearsay-marked source) |
| RelatedTo (Grammar) | 62 | **56** | **-6** (nominal_conjunction Hearsay guard rejected 6 sentences with PastEvidential / PastReportative tense in source) |
| LivesIn (Grammar) | 8 | 8 | unchanged |
| Other predicates | unchanged | unchanged | вҖ” |

The 6 rejected RelatedTo facts came from sentences where the speaker reports hearsay about the relation between two nouns. Conservative refusal is the safe move вҖ” extracting these would imply adam asserts the relation while the source explicitly framed it as reported hearsay.

### Verification

| Gate | Result |
|---|---|
| Workspace tests | **865 passing** unchanged |
| Real-world 26-query battery | **26/26 = 100 % coherent** |
| facts.json | 1934 вҶ’ **1928** (-6 RelatedTo from Hearsay guards; IsA 1472 unchanged) |
| live_holdout_2026_05_02 | **5/5 вң“** unchanged |
| live_holdout_2026_05_01 | **32/32 вң“** unchanged |
| rust_holdout | **41/41 вң“** unchanged |
| Foundation validation | **passes, no drifts** |
| Mean latency | **191.5 ms** (-25 ms vs v4.36.6 вҖ” likely OS file-cache warmth, the additional guard adds minor cost on the hot path) |
| Mean RSS | **176.1 MB** (-1.0 MB, within noise) |

Five releases (v4.36.0 вҶ’ v4.36.5 вҶ’ v4.36.6 вҶ’ v4.36.7) closed:
- v4.36.0: third matcher migration + first EvidenceKind dialog consumption (Hearsay hedging)
- v4.36.5: PastReportative tense + ConverbPerfect parser enumeration
- v4.36.6: vowel-alternation for В«РҫТӣРёВ», short-form 1sg for В«Р°Р»Р°РјВ»
- **v4.36.7**: Evidence guard on extraction (triple-guard pattern in all migrated matchers)

The Evidence story is now architecturally complete: detection (v4.31.0/v4.36.5), dialog consumption (v4.36.0), extraction refusal (v4.36.7).

### Pipeline impact

- 3 new guard blocks (~10 lines each) added to `copula_is_a`, `nominal_conjunction`, `locative_lives_in` вҖ” same-shape guard mirroring negation/modality patterns

Cadence: patch вҖ” closes Evidence extraction-side cycle; small uniform change across three matchers.

### Carry-forwards

- **v4.37.0** вҖ” fourth extract_facts matcher migration (likely `dative_goes_to` or `possessive_has`); remaining 6 missing parser tenses (ParticiplePast, ParticipleHabitual, ParticipleFuture, FutureIntentional, FuturePossible, Conditional, Imperative); battery edge case for В«X РөРјРөСҒ РҝРө?В» question form.
- **v4.38+** вҖ” RelationKind population (when extract_facts patterns start emitting SemFrame outputs); consumption (when downstream consumers read the `relation` field).

## [4.36.6] вҖ” 2026-05-02 вҖ” FST coverage: В«Р°Р»Р°РјВ» short-form 1sg + В«РҫТӣРёВ» back-vowel converb (final v4.36.x FST gaps closed)

**Patch in v4.36+ semantic-IR arc.** Closes the two FST coverage gaps re-scoped from v4.36.5 to v4.36.6+. Both required separate FST architecture work:

1. **В«РҫТӣРёВ» back-vowel converb** вҖ” vowel-final back-vowel verbs change shape during converb derivation (РҫТӣСӢ вҶ’ РҫТӣРё), breaking the prefix-match in `surface_could_contain_root`. Pre-v4.36.6, surfaces beginning with В«РҫТӣРё*В» / В«Р¶Р°СҒСӢ*В» / В«СӮР°РҪСӢ*В» couldn't be analysed.
2. **В«Р°Р»Р°РјВ» short-form 1sg** вҖ” colloquial Kazakh 1sg present-style verbs drop the `-СӢРҪ/-С–РҪ` tail of the personal ending (-РјСӢРҪ/-РјС–РҪ вҶ’ -Рј). The synth path produced only the long form, so user-typed В«Р°Р»Р°Рј / РұР°СҖР°Рј / РҫТӣРёРјВ» didn't parse.

### What was broken pre-v4.36.6

```
> РһТӣРё
sem_frames: (none)               вҶҗ РҫТӣСӢвҶ’РҫТӣРё vowel alternation broke prefix-match

> РҗР»Р°Рј
sem_frames: (none)               вҶҗ short-form 1sg not enumerated by synth

> Р–Р°Р·Р° Р°Р»Р°Рј
sem_frames: (none)               вҶҗ combined: ConverbImperfect short-form ability
response: "РўТҜСҒС–РҪРұРөРҙС–Рј."
```

### Innovations

**(1) `surface_could_contain_root` extended with СӢ/С– вҶ’ Рё vowel alternation.** Mirrors the existing intervocalic-voicing branch (РҝвҶ”Рұ, РәвҶ”Рі, ТӣвҶ”Т“): when root ends in СӢ/С–, also accept `<root[..-1]>Рё` as prefix candidate. Synth side already produces В«РҫТӣРёВ» from `synthesise_verb(РҫТӣСӢ, ConverbImperfect)` via `apply_aorist_after_vowel`; the gap was purely on the parser-prefix-match side.

**(2) Short-form 1sg synth path in `try_verb_analyses`.** New conditional inside the existing nested loop: when feature combo is (Present|PastEvidential) + First + (Sg|None) + polite=false, ALSO try the short-form variant. Implementation strips the trailing `-СӢРҪ/-С–РҪ` from the long-form synth output. If short matches surface, emit Analysis with same features. Limited to 1sg because other persons (`-СҒСӢТЈ`, `-СҒСӢР·`, `-РјС–Р·`, etc.) don't have a similar contraction in standard Kazakh.

### Live verification

| Query | sem_frame trace | Response |
|---|---|---|
| **РһТӣРё** | `РҫТӣСӢ/Verb tense=ConverbImperfect` | parses вң“ (was: no parse) |
| **РһТӣРёРҙСӢ** | `РҫТӣСӢ/Verb tense=Present` | parses вң“ |
| **РҗР»Р°Рј** | `Р°Р»/Verb tense=Present person=First` | parses вң“ (was: no parse) |
| **Р‘Р°СҖР°Рј** | `РұР°СҖ/Verb tense=Present person=First` | parses вң“ |
| **Р–Р°Р·Р° Р°Р»Р°Рј** | full ability detection | "Р–Р°ТӣСҒСӢ РөРәРөРҪ, СҒС–Р·РҙС–ТЈ РјТҜРјРәС–РҪРҙС–РіС–ТЈС–Р·РҙС– СӮТҜСҒС–РҪРҙС–Рј." вң“ |
| Р–Р°Р·Р° Р°Р»Р°РјСӢРҪ (control, long form) | unchanged | "Р–Р°ТӣСҒСӢ РөРәРөРҪ, СҒС–Р·РҙС–ТЈ РјТҜРјРәС–РҪРҙС–РіС–ТЈС–Р·РҙС– СӮТҜСҒС–РҪРҙС–Рј." вң“ |
| РҗР»Р°РјСӢРҪ (control) | unchanged | unchanged |

### Real-world battery (v4.36.6)

| Aggregate | v4.36.6 | О” vs v4.36.5 |
|---|---|---|
| Mean latency | **216.1 ms** | +21 ms (real cost: vowel-alternation prefix-match check + short-form 1sg synth+compare per matching feature combo) |
| Mean max RSS | **177.1 MB** | unchanged |
| Coherent responses | **26/26 = 100 %** | matched |
| Neg-consumed | **5/5** | matched |
| Mod-consumed | **2/4** | matched |

The +21 ms is honest perf cost from the new code paths. Acceptable trade for closing two architectural gaps. The substrate-then-consume cycle for Modality (Ability sub-flavor) now reaches all standard surface forms вҖ” long В«Р°Р»Р°РјСӢРҪВ», short В«Р°Р»Р°РјВ», back-vowel В«РҫТӣРё Р°Р»Р°РјСӢРҪВ».

|---|---|
| Workspace tests | **865 passing** unchanged |
| Real-world 26-query battery | **26/26 = 100 %** coherent |
| facts.json | 1934 вҶ’ 1934 (unchanged вҖ” vowel-alternation didn't open new IsA/RelatedTo extractions in current corpus; IsA 1472 unchanged) |
| live_holdout_2026_05_02 | **5/5 вң“** unchanged |
| live_holdout_2026_05_01 | **32/32 вң“** unchanged |
| rust_holdout | **41/41 вң“** unchanged |

## [4.36.5] вҖ” 2026-05-02 вҖ” FST coverage: ConverbPerfect + PastReportative (В«Р¶Р°Р·СӢРҝСӮСӢВ» / В«РұРҫР»СӢРҝСӮСӢВ» now route to Hearsay)

**Patch in v4.36+ semantic-IR arc.** Closes the v4.36.0 honest-gap carry-forward вҖ” the В«-{Y}Рҝ(СӮСӢ)В» reportative form (В«Р¶Р°Р·СӢРҝСӮСӢВ» / В«РұРҫР»СӢРҝСӮСӢВ» / В«РұР°СҖСӢРҝСӮСӢВ»), which is the natural Kazakh hearsay form most commonly produced in user dialog, previously didn't parse at all. The FST treated only `Tense::PastEvidential` (В«-Т“Р°РҪ/-РіРөРҪВ» вҶ’ Р¶Р°Р·Т“Р°РҪРјСӢРҪ) as reportative; В«-СӢРҝСӮСӢВ» fell through to no parse, blocking v4.36.0's Hearsay routing.

### What was broken pre-v4.36.5

```
> Р–Р°Р·СӢРҝСӮСӢ
sem_frames: (none)               вҶҗ didn't parse
intent: input_evidence: None     вҶҗ Hearsay not detected
response: "РўТҜСҒС–РҪРұРөРҙС–Рј."
```

The synth side already supported the perfect-converb base `-{Y}Рҝ` (line 421 `VERB_CONV_PERFECT`), but the parser tense enumeration didn't include `ConverbPerfect`, AND there was no tense variant for the В«-СӢРҝСӮСӢВ» reportative (perfect-converb base + literal `-СӮСӢ` particle).

### Innovations

**(1) New `Tense::PastReportative` variant.** Distinct from `PastEvidential` вҖ” both encode hearsay/reportative semantics, but the surface forms differ structurally:
- `PastEvidential` = В«Р¶Р°Р·Т“Р°РҪРјСӢРҪ / РұРөСҖРіРөРҪВ» (`-Т“Р°РҪ/-РіРөРҪ` participle base + person-style endings)
- `PastReportative` = В«Р¶Р°Р·СӢРҝСӮСӢ / РұРөСҖС–РҝСӮС– / РұРҫР»СӢРҝСӮСӢВ» (`-{Y}Рҝ` perfect-converb base + invariant `-СӮСӢ` particle, no person ending)

**(2) New synth template `VERB_REPORTATIVE_PAST`** (~10 lines):
```rust
const VERB_REPORTATIVE_PAST: SuffixTemplate = &[
    SuffixAtom::Arch(Archiphoneme::Y),  // СӢ / С– buffer
    SuffixAtom::Literal('Рҝ'),            // perfect converb
    SuffixAtom::Literal('СӮ'),            // -СӮСӢ particle (invariant)
    SuffixAtom::Literal('СӢ'),
];
```

`synthesise_verb` match arm dispatches `Tense::PastReportative` to this template; no personal-ending slot follows because `-СӮСӢ` is invariant.

**(3) `SemFrame::from_analysis` maps `PastReportative вҶ’ Some(EvidenceKind::Hearsay)`** alongside the existing `PastEvidential вҶ’ Hearsay` mapping. Both forms now feed the same v4.36.0 Hearsay routing.

**(4) Parser tenses += `ConverbPerfect, PastReportative`** (alongside v4.32.5's `ConverbImperfect`). Two new enumeration entries, ~30 % more parse-time cost per verb token (from 5 to 7 tenses).

### Live verification

| Query | sem_frame trace | Response |
|---|---|---|
| **Р–Р°Р·СӢРҝСӮСӢ** | `Р¶Р°Р·/Verb tense=PastReportative evid=Hearsay` | "РЎС–Р·РіРө Р°Р№СӮСӢР»Т“Р°РҪ СҒРёСҸТӣСӮСӢ РөРәРөРҪ вҖ” РҪР°ТӣСӮСӢ РҙРөСҖРөРіС–Рј Р¶РҫТӣ." вң“ |
| **Р‘РҫР»СӢРҝСӮСӢ** | `РұРҫР»/Verb tense=PastReportative evid=Hearsay` | hearsay-aware вң“ |
| **РҗРұР°Р№ Р¶Р°Р·СӢРҝСӮСӢ** | `Р¶Р°Р·/Verb tense=PastReportative evid=Hearsay` | hearsay-aware вң“ |
| **Р–Р°Р·СӢРҝ** (ConverbPerfect bare) | `Р¶Р°Р·/Verb tense=ConverbPerfect` | now parses (was: no parse) вң“ |
| Р–Р°Р·Т“Р°РҪРјСӢРҪ (control, PastEvidential) | unchanged | unchanged вң“ |
| РҗРұР°Р№ Р¶Р°Р·РҙСӢ (control, PastDefinite) | unchanged | unchanged grounded fact вң“ |

### Real-world battery (v4.36.5)

| Aggregate | v4.36.5 | О” vs v4.36.0 |
|---|---|---|
| Mean latency | **194.6 ms** | -127 ms (mostly OS file-cache warmth between immediate-back-to-back runs; not a true performance improvement вҖ” adding 2 tenses to enumeration costs ~30 % more parse work per verb token) |
| Mean max RSS | **177.1 MB** | +1.3 MB (within noise) |
| Coherent responses | **26/26 = 100 %** | matched |
| Neg-consumed | **5/5** | matched |
| Mod-consumed | **2/4** | matched (3/4 with cases 19/20/21 visibly modal-aware in v4.35.5; battery script case-sensitivity bug undercounts) |

The latency drop is suspicious вҖ” adding tenses should slow parsing, not speed it. Most likely OS file-cache effect after immediate `extract_facts` + `run_reasoner` runs. Honest framing: not claiming a perf improvement; the architectural cost is +2 tenses in enumeration.

### Known FST gaps still present (carry-forward to v4.36.6+)

- **В«Р°Р»Р°РјВ» short-form 1sg**: В«Р°Р» + Present + 1sgВ» synth produces В«Р°Р»Р°РјСӢРҪВ» (long form), not the contracted В«Р°Р»Р°РјВ». Adding short-form personal-ending alternates is invasive (touches the personal-ending synthesis match). Defer.
- **В«РҫТӣРёВ» back-vowel converb**: `surface_could_contain_root` requires root prefix-match, but vowel-final back-vowel verbs change shape during converb derivation (РҫТӣСӢ вҶ’ РҫТӣРё), breaking prefix-match. Closing this requires either pre-stripping final СӢ/С– before lookup, or stem-alternation paradigm matcher (mirroring `pronoun_paradigm`).

Both were originally promised for v4.36.5 but turn out to need separate FST architecture work. Honest re-scope: v4.36.5 is **В«-СӢРҝСӮСӢВ» reportative + ConverbPerfect parsing**; В«Р°Р»Р°РјВ» / В«РҫТӣРёВ» вҶ’ v4.36.6+.

### Verification

| Gate | Result |
|---|---|
| Workspace tests | **865 passing** unchanged |
| Real-world battery | **26/26 = 100 %** coherent |
| facts.json | 1935 вҶ’ 1934 (-1 RelatedTo from new ConverbPerfect parse path triggering a guard on a previously-unparseable sentence; IsA 1472 unchanged) |
| live_holdout_2026_05_02 | **5/5 вң“** unchanged |
| live_holdout_2026_05_01 | **32/32 вң“** unchanged |
| rust_holdout | **41/41 вң“** unchanged |

## [4.36.0] вҖ” 2026-05-02 вҖ” Third matcher migration + first EvidenceKind consumption (Hearsay hedging)

**First minor in v4.36+ semantic-IR arc.** Two architectural completions in one release:
1. Third extract_facts matcher migration вҖ” `locative_lives_in` joins `copula_is_a` (v4.34.5) and `nominal_conjunction` (v4.35.0) on the SemFrame stream, with the same dual-guard pattern (negation + modality refusal).
2. **First EvidenceKind consumption** вҖ” Hearsay-marked utterances now route to a respectful hedging template family instead of asserting flat facts about the topic. This is the third SemFrame field to influence user-facing answer text after Polarity (v4.33.5) and Modality (v4.34.7).

### Innovations

**(1) `locative_lives_in` signature migration**: `+ sem_frames: &[SemFrame]`. Mirrors v4.34.5's `copula_is_a` and v4.35.0's `nominal_conjunction`. Same dual guards: refuse extraction when sentence has either Polarity::Negated on a noun-class frame OR any Modality on any frame. For LivesIn this protects against modal claims like В«X Y-РҙР° СӮТұСҖСғ РәРөСҖРөРәВ» ("X needs to live in Y") being mistakenly extracted as factual residency.

**(2) `Intent::Unknown.input_evidence: Option<EvidenceKind>` field.** Third SemFrame-derived field on the canonical Intent type (after `noun_hint_polarity` v4.33.5 and `input_modality` v4.34.7). Default `None` preserves all pre-v4.36.0 routing exactly. Propagated through `semantics`, `verifier`, and 8 test sites.

**(3) `Conversation::turn` populates `input_evidence`** by walking the SemFrame stream for any non-None evidence вҖ” independent of noun_hint, like modality. Auto-derivation flows: FST `Tense::PastEvidential` вҶ’ `SemFrame.evidence: Some(EvidenceKind::Hearsay)` вҶ’ `Intent.input_evidence: Some(Hearsay)`.

**(4) Planner Hearsay routing.** New short-circuit AFTER polarity and modality checks (those have higher priority вҖ” when they fire on the same input, the more salient signal wins):
```rust
if matches!(input_evidence, Some(EvidenceKind::Hearsay)) {
    return "unknown.with_hearsay_hedge";
}
```

**(5) Epistemic-override bypass for Hearsay.** Mirrors v4.34.0's polarity bypass and v4.34.7's modality bypass. Hearsay-marked Unknowns skip `unknown.tentative` override вҖ” the hedging template family is already a tentative-shape response.

**(6) New template family `unknown.with_hearsay_hedge`** in `v1.toml` вҖ” 7 templates that hedge the response by echoing the user's evidential framing back:
- "РЎС–Р·РіРө Р°Р№СӮСӢР»Т“Р°РҪ СҒРёСҸТӣСӮСӢ РөРәРөРҪ вҖ” РҪР°ТӣСӮСӢ РҙРөСҖРөРіС–Рј Р¶РҫТӣ."
- "{noun} Р¶Р°Р№Р»СӢ СҒС–Р·РіРө Р°Р№СӮСӢР»Т“Р°РҪ РҝС–РәС–СҖ РұРҫР»СғСӢ РјТҜРјРәС–РҪ, РҙРөРіРөРҪРјРөРҪ РјРөРҪ СҖР°СҒСӮР°Р№ Р°Р»РјР°Р№РјСӢРҪ."
- "РЎС–Р· РөСҒСӮС–РіРөРҪ СҒУ©Р· РұРҫР»СҒР° РәРөСҖРөРә, РұС–СҖР°Тӣ РјРөРҪ СӮРөРәСҒРөСҖРө Р°Р»РјР°Р№РјСӢРҪ."

Templates use В«СҒС–Р·РіРө Р°Р№СӮСӢР»Т“Р°РҪ / РөСҒСӮС–РҙС–ТЈС–Р· / Р°Р№СӮСӢР»Т“Р°РҪ УҷТЈРіС–РјРөВ» to mark the source as the user's hearsay, not adam's first-hand knowledge.

### Live verification

| Query | sem_frame trace | Response |
|---|---|---|
| **Р–Р°Р·Т“Р°РҪРјСӢРҪ** | `Р¶Р°Р·/Verb tense=PastEvidential evid=Hearsay` | "РЎС–Р·РіРө Р°Р№СӮСӢР»Т“Р°РҪ СҒРёСҸТӣСӮСӢ РөРәРөРҪ вҖ” РҪР°ТӣСӮСӢ РҙРөСҖРөРіС–Рј Р¶РҫТӣ." вң“ |
| **РҗРұР°Р№ Р¶Р°Р·Т“Р°РҪ** | `[1] Р¶Р°Р·/Verb tense=PastEvidential evid=Hearsay` | "РЎС–Р·РіРө Р°Р№СӮСӢР»Т“Р°РҪ СҒРёСҸТӣСӮСӢ РөРәРөРҪ вҖ” РҪР°ТӣСӮСӢ РҙРөСҖРөРіС–Рј Р¶РҫТӣ." вң“ |
| **РһР» Р¶Р°Р·Т“Р°РҪ РөРәРөРҪ** | `[2] Р¶Р°Р·/Verb tense=PastEvidential evid=Hearsay` | "РЎС–Р·РіРө Р°Р№СӮСӢР»Т“Р°РҪ СҒРёСҸТӣСӮСӢ РөРәРөРҪ вҖ” РҪР°ТӣСӮСӢ РҙРөСҖРөРіС–Рј Р¶РҫТӣ." вң“ |
| РҗРұР°Р№ Р¶Р°Р·РҙСӢ (control, PastDefinite) | `Р¶Р°Р·/Verb tense=PastDefinite` (no evid) | unchanged grounded fact вң“ |
| РҗРұР°Р№ РәС–Рј? (control, no verb) | no evidence | unchanged вң“ |

Note: В«-{Y}Рҝ(СӮСӢ)В» reportative forms like В«Р¶Р°Р·СӢРҝСӮСӢВ» / В«РұРҫР»СӢРҝСӮСӢВ» do NOT currently parse вҖ” the FST treats `Tense::PastEvidential` as the `Р¶Р°Р·Т“Р°РҪРјСӢРҪ`-style construction. Closing the В«-СӢРҝСӮСӢВ» path is **v4.36.5+** FST coverage work.

| **Evidence** | v4.31.0 (PastEvidentialвҶ’Hearsay) | **v4.36.0** | not yet |
| Relation | placeholder | n/a | n/a |

## [4.35.5] вҖ” 2026-05-02 вҖ” Modal-without-noun: battery case 21 В«Р–Р°Р·Р° Р°Р»Р°РјСӢРҪВ» closes

**Patch in v4.35+ semantic-IR arc.** Closes battery case 21 вҖ” declarative ability claim В«Р–Р°Р·Р° Р°Р»Р°РјСӢРҪВ» ("I can write") that has been falling to "РўТҜСҒС–РҪРұРөРҙС–Рј" since v4.32.5 because no `noun_hint` was extracted (verb-only sentence) and v4.34.7's modality routing required both modality AND noun_hint. v4.35.5 relaxes the requirement at three coupling points: planner routing, epistemic-override bypass, and Conversation::turn population.

### What was broken pre-v4.35.5

```
> Р–Р°Р·Р° Р°Р»Р°РјСӢРҪ
РўТҜСҒС–РҪРұРөРҙС–Рј.
                  ^ no noun_hint вҶ’ modality routing skipped вҶ’ tentative fallback
```

Modality was correctly detected at SemFrame layer (`Р¶Р°Р·/Verb tense=ConverbImperfect mod=Ability` in `--trace`), but three coupling points blocked end-to-end consumption:
1. `Conversation::turn` populated `input_modality` only inside the `noun_hint: Some(_)` arm
2. Planner routing required `input_modality.is_some() && noun_hint.is_some()`
3. Epistemic-override bypass required `noun_hint: Some(_), input_modality: Some(_)`

### Innovations

**(1) `Conversation::turn` modality population moved out of `noun_hint`-Some guard.** Polarity is per-noun (no noun вҶ’ no polarity to copy), but modality is on the lexical verb frame and independent of `noun_hint`. The pre-v4.35.5 nesting silently skipped population for verb-only modal claims.

**(2) Planner modality routing relaxed.** Drop the `noun_hint.is_some()` requirement; modality alone routes to the appropriate `unknown.with_modal_*` family.

**(3) Epistemic-override bypass relaxed.** Same relaxation in the override block вҖ” when `input_modality: Some(_)`, base_key wins regardless of `noun_hint`.

**(4) +3 no-noun template variants per modal family** (9 templates total added). For verb-only modal claims, `template_is_fillable` filters to bare-modality templates that don't reference `{noun}`. Examples:
- Necessity bare: В«РҳУҷ, СҲСӢРҪСӢРјРөРҪ ТӣР°Р¶РөСӮ РҪУҷСҖСҒРө РөРәРөРҪ.В», В«РЎС–Р·РҙС–ТЈ РҫР№СӢТЈСӢР· РҫСҖСӢРҪРҙСӢ вҖ” РұТұР» РјР°ТЈСӢР·РҙСӢ.В»
- Possibility bare: В«РҳУҷ, РјТұРҪРҙР°Р№ РјТҜРјРәС–РҪРҙС–Рә РұРҫР»СғСӢ СӢТӣСӮРёРјР°Р».В»
- Ability bare: В«Р–Р°ТӣСҒСӢ РөРәРөРҪ, РҫРҪРҙР°Р№ ТӣР°РұС–Р»РөСӮС–ТЈС–Р· РұР°СҖ.В», В«РҳУҷ, СҒС–Р·РҙРө РҫРҪРҙР°Р№ РјТҜРјРәС–РҪРҙС–Рә РұР°СҖ РөРәРөРҪ.В»

### Live verification вҖ” case 21 closes

| Query | v4.35.0 | v4.35.5 |
|---|---|---|
| **Р–Р°Р·Р° Р°Р»Р°РјСӢРҪ** | "РўТҜСҒС–РҪРұРөРҙС–Рј." | "**Р–Р°ТӣСҒСӢ РөРәРөРҪ, СҒС–Р·РҙС–ТЈ РјТҜРјРәС–РҪРҙС–РіС–ТЈС–Р·РҙС– СӮТҜСҒС–РҪРҙС–Рј.**" |
| РҡС–СӮР°Рҝ РҫТӣСғ РәРөСҖРөРә (control, with noun) | "РҳУҷ, РјР°ТЈСӢР·РҙСӢ РјУҷСҒРөР»Рө РөРәРөРҪ." | unchanged вң“ |
| Р‘ТұР» СҲСӢРҪРҙСӢТӣ РөРјРөСҒ (control, polarity precedence) | "РЁСӢРҪРҙСӢТӣ РөРјРөСҒ РҙРөРҝ Р°Р№СӮР°СҒСӢР· вҖ” РҝС–РәС–СҖС–ТЈС–Р·РҙС– СҒСӢР№Р»Р°Р№РјСӢРҪ." | unchanged вң“ |
| ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ Р°Р№СӮ (control, no modality) | grounded fact | unchanged вң“ |

### Known FST gaps still present (carry-forward)

Two ability variants do NOT close in v4.35.5:
- В«РЎУ©Р№Р»РөР№ Р°Р»Р°РјВ» вҖ” `СҒУ©Р№Р»Рө` parses as ConverbImperfect вң“, but `Р°Р»Р°Рј` (short 1sg form without -СӢРҪ) doesn't parse вҶ’ only one frame in stream вҶ’ ability detector can't fire (needs both lexical-verb + Р°Р» frames).
- В«РһТӣРё Р°Р»Р°РјСӢРҪВ» вҖ” `РҫТӣРё` (converb of back-vowel verb В«РҫТӣСӢВ») doesn't parse вҶ’ same.

These are FST coverage gaps in the v4.32.5 ConverbImperfect enumeration вҖ” short-form-1sg В«Р°Р»Р°РјВ» variant isn't enumerated, and back-vowel verb converb В«РҫТӣРёВ» isn't synthesised correctly. Documented as **v4.36+ FST coverage** alongside the remaining 8 missing parser tenses.

### Real-world battery (v4.35.5)

| Aggregate | v4.35.5 | О” vs v4.35.0 |
|---|---|---|

| Mean max RSS | **175.9 MB** | -0.9 MB (within noise) |

Case 22 still uses AskAboutSystem path (not modality-routing) and gives the existing smart honest refusal вҖ” no regression.

- **v4.36.5** вҖ” FST coverage: short-form 1sg В«Р°Р»Р°РјВ» variant + back-vowel converb В«РҫТӣРёВ» synthesis fix (closes В«РЎУ©Р№Р»РөР№ Р°Р»Р°РјВ», В«РһТӣРё Р°Р»Р°РјСӢРҪВ»).

## [4.35.0] вҖ” 2026-05-02 вҖ” Second extract_facts matcher migration + modality guard on extraction side

**First minor in v4.35+ semantic-IR arc.** Closes the substrate-then-consume cycle for **Modality on the extraction side** (mirroring v4.34.7's dialog-side consumption). Migrates `nominal_conjunction` to consume `&[SemFrame]` (second matcher to do so after `copula_is_a` in v4.34.5). Retroactively adds a modality guard to `copula_is_a` for symmetry. Both matchers now refuse extraction when the sentence carries either sentence-level negation (Polarity::Negated) OR periphrastic modality (Modality::Necessity / Possibility / Ability). The principle: modal claims are normative or epistemic, not factual assertions; extracting them as graph facts loses their modal nature.

### Innovations

**(1) `nominal_conjunction` signature migration**: `+ sem_frames: &[SemFrame]`. Mirrors v4.34.5's `copula_is_a` migration. Wired through `extract_facts` dispatch.

**(2) Dual guards on `nominal_conjunction`**:
- **Negation guard**: refuse RelatedTo extraction when any noun-class frame has `polarity == Negated`.
- **Modality guard**: refuse extraction when any frame has `modality.is_some()`.

Both guards skip the whole matcher when ANY frame in the stream carries the signal вҖ” conservative, mirrors `copula_is_a` behavior.

**(3) Retroactive modality guard on `copula_is_a`** for symmetry. Pre-v4.35.0, `copula_is_a` had only the negation guard (v4.34.5). v4.35.0 adds the same modality check, completing the dual-guard pattern across both matchers.

**(4) `Modality` consumption on extraction side closes the second substrate-then-consume cycle:**

| Field | Population | Consumption (dialog) | Consumption (extraction) |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Polarity** | v4.33.0 | v4.33.5 | v4.34.5 |
| **Modality** | v4.32.0 (N/P) + v4.32.5 (Ability) | v4.34.7 | **v4.35.0** |
| Evidence | v4.31.0 | not yet | not yet |
| Relation | placeholder | n/a | n/a |

### Corpus impact

Re-running `extract_facts` after both guards:

| Predicate | v4.34.7 | v4.35.0 | О” |
|---|---|---|---|
| IsA (Grammar) | 1472 | 1472 | unchanged (copula_is_a guards rejected zero IsA вҖ” modality / negation didn't appear in IsA-shaped sentences) |
| RelatedTo (Grammar) | 65 | **63** | **-2** (nominal_conjunction guards rejected 2 sentences with negation, e.g. В«РһРҪСҲР° Р°СғТӣСӢРјРҙСӢ РөРјРөСҒ Р¶РөСҖР»РөСҖРҙРө СӮРөРјРөРәС– РјРөРҪ СҲР°Р№ У©СҒС–СҖС–Р»РөРҙС–В») |
| Other predicates | unchanged | unchanged | вҖ” |

The 2 rejected RelatedTo facts came from sentences where В«РөРјРөСҒВ» appeared as part of an adjective modifier earlier in the sentence (not directly negating the related-to pair). Conservative refusal is the safe move вҖ” extracting in this case would imply the source sentence asserts the relation, but the negation context muddies that.

| facts.json | 1937 вҶ’ **1935** (-2 RelatedTo from new nominal_conjunction guards; IsA 1472 unchanged) |
| derived_facts.json | regenerated cleanly |
| Real-world 26-query battery | **26/26 = 100 % coherent** unchanged |
| Mean latency | **320.3 ms** (-1.2 ms vs v4.34.7, within noise) |
| Mean RSS | **176.8 MB** (+0.8 MB, within noise) |
| Neg-consumed rate | **5/5 = 100 %** unchanged |
| Mod-consumed rate | **2/4** unchanged (cases 19, 20 visibly modal-aware) |
| live_holdout_2026_05_02 | **5/5 вң“** unchanged |

| rust_holdout | **41/41 вң“** unchanged |
| Foundation validation | **passes, no drifts** |

- 1 signature change to `nominal_conjunction` (+ `sem_frames` parameter)
- 2 guards (negation + modality) added to `nominal_conjunction` (~30 lines)

Cadence: minor вҖ” closes second substrate-then-consume cycle, completing Modality coverage across both pipeline directions. Two of three SemFrame fields now have fully-closed cycles (Polarity, Modality).

### Carry-forwards

- **v4.35.5** вҖ” handle modal claims without `noun_hint` (battery case 21 В«Р–Р°Р·Р° Р°Р»Р°РјСӢРҪВ» вҖ” verb-only modal); EvidenceKind consumption (Hearsay-marked claims should hedge evidentiality in response).

## [4.34.7] вҖ” 2026-05-02 вҖ” First Modality consumption: per-modality template families (Necessity / Possibility / Ability)

**Patch in v4.34+ semantic-IR arc.** Closes the substrate-then-consume cycle for the **second** SemFrame field вҖ” `Modality`. Modality has been populated since v4.32.0 (Necessity / Possibility) and v4.32.5 (Ability), but the response generator ignored it. v4.34.7 wires three per-modality template families that engage with the user's modal claim instead of asserting a generic fact about the noun_hint.

### What was broken pre-v4.34.7

```
> РҡС–СӮР°Рҝ РҫТӣСғ РәРөСҖРөРә.
РҡС–СӮР°Рҝ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ ТӣСӢСҒТӣР°СҲР° Р°Р№СӮСҒР°Рј: РҡС–СӮР°Рҝ вҖ” УҷРҙРөРұРёРөСӮ РәУ©Р·С–.
                          вҶ‘ ignores user's necessity claim about reading

> Р‘ТұР» РұРҫР»Сғ РјТҜРјРәС–РҪ РұРө?
Р‘РҫР»Сғ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РјСӢРҪР°РҪСӢ Р°Р№СӮР° Р°Р»Р°РјСӢРҪ: В«Р–РҫТ“Р°СҖСӢ РұС–Р»С–Рј СҒР°РҝР°СҒСӢ...В»
                          вҶ‘ ignores user's possibility question
```

Modality was correctly detected at SemFrame layer (`mod=Necessity`, `mod=Possibility` in `--trace`) but no consumer read the field, so routing fell through to standard `unknown.with_grounded_fact`/`with_evidence`.

### Innovations

**(1) `Intent::Unknown.input_modality: Option<Modality>` field.** Second SemFrame-derived field on the canonical Intent type (after v4.33.5's `noun_hint_polarity`). Default `None` preserves all pre-v4.34.7 routing exactly. Propagated through `semantics::interpret_text_with_lexicon`, `verifier::strip_evidence`, and 5 test-site Intent::Unknown constructors.

**(2) `Conversation::turn` populates from any sem_frame with modality.** Unlike polarity (which is on the same frame as `noun_hint`), modality is on the **lexical verb** frame вҖ” a different word from the noun_hint. So we walk the entire sem_frames stream and pick the first non-None modality:
```rust
*input_modality = sem_frames.iter().find_map(|f| f.modality);
```

**(3) Per-modality planner routing.** New planner short-circuit BEFORE the standard Unknown routing chain, AFTER the polarity short-circuit (negation has higher priority вҖ” when both fire on rare В«X V РәРөСҖРөРә РөРјРөСҒВ», negation wins):
```rust
if noun_hint.is_some() {
    if let Some(modality) = input_modality {
        return match modality {
            Modality::Necessity => "unknown.with_modal_necessity",
            Modality::Possibility => "unknown.with_modal_possibility",
            Modality::Ability => "unknown.with_modal_ability",
        };
    }
}
```

**(4) Epistemic-override bypass for modality.** Same pattern as v4.34.0's polarity bypass: when `input_modality.is_some()`, return None from override block so base_key wins. Otherwise EpistemicStatus::Tentative would force `unknown.tentative` over the modal-aware family.

**(5) Three new template families** in `v1.toml`, 5 templates each:
- `unknown.with_modal_necessity` вҖ” acknowledges the necessity claim ("РҳУҷ, РјР°ТЈСӢР·РҙСӢ РјУҷСҒРөР»Рө РөРәРөРҪ", "Р‘ТұР» СҲСӢРҪСӢРјРөРҪ РәРөСҖРөРә", "РҳУҷ, РұТұР» ТӣР°Р¶РөСӮ РҪУҷСҖСҒРө")
- `unknown.with_modal_possibility` вҖ” acknowledges the possibility ("РңТҜРјРәС–РҪ РөРәРөРҪ, РёУҷ", "РңТұРҪРҙР°Р№ РјТҜРјРәС–РҪРҙС–Рә РұР°СҖ РҙРөРҝ РҫР№Р»Р°СғСӢТЈСӢР· РҫСҖСӢРҪРҙСӢ")
- `unknown.with_modal_ability` вҖ” acknowledges the ability claim ("Р–Р°ТӣСҒСӢ РөРәРөРҪ, СҒС–Р·РҙС–ТЈ РјТҜРјРәС–РҪРҙС–РіС–ТЈС–Р·РҙС– СӮТҜСҒС–РҪРҙС–Рј", "РһРҪРҙР°Р№ РҪУҷСҖСҒРө С–СҒСӮРөР№ Р°Р»Сғ вҖ” Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢ ТӣР°СҒРёРөСӮ")

### Live verification вҖ” visible behavior change

| Query | v4.34.5 | v4.34.7 |
|---|---|---|
| **РҡС–СӮР°Рҝ РҫТӣСғ РәРөСҖРөРә** | "РҡС–СӮР°Рҝ вҖ” УҷРҙРөРұРёРөСӮ РәУ©Р·С–." (definition) | "**РҳУҷ, РјР°ТЈСӢР·РҙСӢ РјУҷСҒРөР»Рө РөРәРөРҪ.**" (necessity-aware) |
| **Р‘ТұР» РұРҫР»Сғ РјТҜРјРәС–РҪ РұРө?** | "Р‘РҫР»Сғ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ: В«Р–РҫТ“Р°СҖСӢ РұС–Р»С–Рј...В»" (corpus quote) | "**РңТҜРјРәС–РҪ РөРәРөРҪ, РёУҷ.**" (possibility-aware) |
| **Р‘ТұР» СҲСӢРҪРҙСӢТӣ РөРјРөСҒ** (control) | "РЁСӢРҪРҙСӢТӣ РөРјРөСҒ РҙРөРҝ Р°Р№СӮР°СҒСӢР· вҖ” РҝС–РәС–СҖС–ТЈС–Р·РҙС– СҒСӢР№Р»Р°Р№РјСӢРҪ." | unchanged (polarity has higher priority) вң“ |
| **ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ Р°Р№СӮ** (control) | grounded fact about Kazakhstan | unchanged (no modality) вң“ |

### Real-world battery (v4.34.7)

26 queries (same set as v4.34.5):

| Aggregate | v4.34.7 | О” vs v4.34.5 |
|---|---|---|
| Mean latency | **321.5 ms** | -2.7 ms (within noise) |
| Mean max RSS | **176.0 MB** | -0.9 MB (within noise) |
| Coherent responses | **26/26 = 100 %** | matched |
| Neg-consumed rate | **5/5** | matched |
| **Mod-consumed rate** | **2/4** (cases 19, 20 visibly modal-aware) | **NEW** |

Cases 19 (В«РҡС–СӮР°Рҝ РҫТӣСғ РәРөСҖРөРәВ») and 20 (В«Р‘ТұР» РұРҫР»Сғ РјТҜРјРәС–РҪ РұРө?В») moved from "asserting an unrelated definition" вҶ’ "acknowledging the modal claim". Cases 21 (В«Р–Р°Р·Р° Р°Р»Р°РјСӢРҪВ») and 22 (В«РЎУ©Р№Р»РөР№ Р°Р»Р°СҒСӢР· РұР°?В») unchanged:
- Case 21: no `noun_hint` extracted (just verb+aux, no content noun) вҖ” modality routing requires both modality AND noun_hint. Falls to "РўТҜСҒС–РҪРұРөРҙС–Рј" as before. v4.35+ work to handle noun-less modal claims.
- Case 22: routes through `Intent::AskAboutSystem(GenericCapability)` BEFORE reaching Unknown branch вҖ” gets the existing smart "Р–РҫТӣ, РҫРҪРҙР°Р№ УҷСҖРөРәРөСӮСӮС–..." answer. Modality is detected but doesn't influence (different intent path). No regression.

### Substrate-then-consume cycle status

Two of three SemFrame fields now have closed cycles:

| Field | Population | Consumption (dialog) | Consumption (extraction) |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Polarity** | v4.33.0 | **v4.33.5** | **v4.34.5** |
| **Modality** | v4.32.0 (N/P) + v4.32.5 (Ability) | **v4.34.7** | not yet |
| Evidence | v4.31.0 (PastEvidentialвҶ’Hearsay) | not yet | not yet |
| Relation | not yet (placeholder) | n/a | n/a |

| Workspace tests | **862 passing** unchanged (5 test-site Intent::Unknown patches added `input_modality: None`) |
| Real-world 26-query battery | **26/26 = 100 %** coherent; mod-consumed 2/4 (cases 21, 22 correctly unchanged) |
