## [4.7.17] вҖ” 2026-04-29 вҖ” Rust Book Chapter 17 (Rust-СӮСӢТЈ РҫРұСҠРөРәСӮС–Р»С–-РұР°Т“СӢСӮСӮР°Р»Т“Р°РҪ РјТҜРјРәС–РҪРҙС–РәСӮРөСҖС–) translated, in pack

Seventeenth chapter under В«РіР»Р°РІР° = РҝР°СӮСҮВ» cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 17 вҖ” Object-Oriented Programming Features of Rust вҖ” covering Rust's relationship to OOP: characteristics of OO languages and how Rust delivers (or deliberately doesn't deliver) each вҖ” encapsulation via `pub` keyword (`AveragedCollection` worked example with private fields and a public API maintaining an internal invariant); Rust's deliberate absence of inheritance and the trade-off reasoning (over-coupling, hierarchy rigidity), with default trait implementations and trait objects as the practical replacements; static vs dynamic polymorphism (generics with `<T: Trait>` for compile-time monomorphization vs `Box<dyn Trait>` trait objects for runtime dispatch); using **trait objects** for collections of mixed types (the GUI `Draw` / `Screen` example, `Vec<Box<dyn Draw>>`, vtable-based dynamic dispatch, the **object-safe trait** rules вҖ” methods on `&self`/`&mut self`, no generic parameters, no `Self` return, why `Clone` cannot be made into `Box<dyn Clone>`); implementing the **state pattern** (the blog-post lifecycle Draft вҶ’ PendingReview вҶ’ Published example), first in classical OOP style with `Box<dyn State>` and the `self: Box<Self>` consuming method pattern, then in idiomatic Rust style with each state as a separate type (`DraftPost` / `PendingReviewPost` / `Post`), with the trade-off discussion: classical OOP allows runtime extensibility; type-encoded states catch errors at compile time.

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_17.md` вҖ” ~5 500 words, code blocks preserved verbatim, all earlier-chapter terminology applied.
- Chapter-17-specific terminology decisions: object-oriented programming вҶ’ **РҫРұСҠРөРәСӮС–Р»С–-РұР°Т“СӢСӮСӮР°Р»Т“Р°РҪ РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°Р»Р°Сғ**, encapsulation вҶ’ **СӮТұСҒР°СғР»Р°Сғ**, inheritance вҶ’ **РјТұСҖР°РіРөСҖР»С–Рә**, polymorphism вҶ’ **РәУ©Рҝ РҝС–СҲС–РҪРҙС–Р»С–Рә**, dynamic dispatch вҶ’ **РҙРёРҪР°РјРёРәР°Р»СӢТӣ СӮР°СҖР°СӮРҝР°**, static dispatch вҶ’ **СҒСӮР°СӮРёРәР°Р»СӢТӣ СӮР°СҖР°СӮРҝР°**, vtable вҶ’ kept verbatim, object-safe вҶ’ **РҪСӢСҒР°РҪ-ТӣР°СғС–РҝСҒС–Р·**, state pattern вҶ’ **РәТҜР№ ТҜР»РіС–СҒС–**, design pattern вҶ’ **Р¶РҫРұР°Р»Р°Сғ ТҜР»РіС–СҒС–**, trait object вҶ’ **СӮСҖРөР№СӮ-РҪСӢСҒР°РҪ** (already locked since v4.7.0).

## [4.7.16] вҖ” 2026-04-29 вҖ” Rust Book Chapter 16 (ТҡРҫСҖТӣСӢРҪСӢСҲСҒСӢР· ТӣР°СӮР°СҖР»Р°СҒ РҫСҖСӢРҪРҙР°Сғ) translated, in pack

Sixteenth chapter under В«РіР»Р°РІР° = РҝР°СӮСҮВ» cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 16 вҖ” Fearless Concurrency вҖ” covering Rust's compile-time-enforced concurrency primitives: spawning OS threads with `std::thread::spawn` and waiting for them with `JoinHandle::join`, the closure-with-`move` pattern for transferring ownership into a thread; message-passing concurrency via `std::sync::mpsc::channel` (transmitter `tx` / receiver `rx`, ownership transfer on `send`, blocking `recv` vs non-blocking `try_recv`, iterating `for received in rx`, multiple producers via `tx.clone()`); shared-state concurrency via `Mutex<T>` (the `lock()` API returning a `MutexGuard`, `Drop`-based auto-release, the `Arc<Mutex<T>>` combination for cross-thread shared mutable state with the 10-thread counter example); the `Send` and `Sync` marker traits as the type-system foundation that makes this all safe (the `Rc<T>` / `Arc<T>` distinction explained via these traits, `RefCell<T>` not being `Sync`, why manual `unsafe impl Send/Sync` is rare and dangerous).

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_16.md` вҖ” ~5 500 words, code blocks preserved verbatim, all earlier-chapter terminology applied.
- Chapter-16-specific terminology decisions: thread вҶ’ **Р°Т“СӢРҪ** (already locked); message passing вҶ’ **С…Р°РұР°СҖР»Р°РјР° Р°СҖТӣСӢР»СӢ Р°Р»РјР°СҒСғ**; channel вҶ’ **РәР°РҪР°Р»** (already); transmitter вҶ’ **Р¶С–РұРөСҖСғСҲС–**; receiver вҶ’ **ТӣР°РұСӢР»РҙР°СғСҲСӢ**; mutex вҶ’ kept as `Mutex` (the `Rust` type) plus the conceptual term **У©Р·Р°СҖР° СҚРәСҒРәР»СҺР·РёРІСӮС–Р»С–Рә**; shared state вҶ’ **РҫСҖСӮР°Тӣ РәТҜР№**; atomic reference counting вҶ’ **Р°СӮРҫРјРҙСӢТӣ СҒС–Р»СӮРөРјРө-РөСҒРөРҝСӮРөСғ**; `Send` / `Sync` вҶ’ kept verbatim (marker trait names); deadlock вҶ’ **У©Р·Р°СҖР° СӮРҫСҒТӣР°СғСӢР»**.

### Pipeline impact

- `data/curated/rust_book_kk_pack.json`: 15 chapters / 1 136 samples вҶ’ **16 chapters / 1 200 samples** (+64 from chapter 16).
- Morpheme index: **unchanged** вҖ” pack still at the 500-per-pack default-mode ceiling.

## [4.7.15] вҖ” 2026-04-29 вҖ” Rust Book Chapter 15 (РҗТӣСӢР»РҙСӢ СҒС–Р»СӮРөРјРөР»РөСҖ) translated, in pack

Fifteenth chapter under В«РіР»Р°РІР° = РҝР°СӮСҮВ» cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 15 вҖ” Smart Pointers вҖ” covering Rust's smart-pointer ecosystem and how each kind extends the basic reference model: `Box<T>` for heap allocation and recursive types (the cons-list example showing why a recursive enum's size is unbounded without `Box`); the `Deref` trait and how it enables `*` on smart pointers (writing a `MyBox<T>` from scratch and implementing `Deref::deref`), deref coercion as the compiler-driven chain `&MyBox<String>` вҶ’ `&String` вҶ’ `&str`; the `Drop` trait for cleanup logic that runs when a value goes out of scope, why `drop()` may not be called manually but `std::mem::drop` may be; `Rc<T>` for multiple ownership in single-threaded contexts (the cons-list example showing how cloning increments `strong_count`, why `Rc<T>` is read-only); `RefCell<T>` and the **interior mutability** pattern that defers borrow checking to runtime (the mock-object pattern, panic on rule violation); combining `Rc<RefCell<T>>` for the shared-ownership-with-mutation use case; reference cycles as a memory-leak shape Rust's ownership system does not prevent, and `Weak<T>` references that don't extend the lifetime of the pointed-to value (the parent-child node tree example with strong children + weak parent links).

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_15.md` вҖ” ~5 500 words, code blocks preserved verbatim, all earlier-chapter terminology applied.
- Chapter-15-specific terminology decisions: smart pointer вҶ’ **Р°ТӣСӢР»РҙСӢ СҒС–Р»СӮРөРјРө** (already locked since v4.7.0), recursive type вҶ’ **СҖРөРәСғСҖСҒРёРІСӮС– СӮРёРҝ**, deref coercion вҶ’ **РҙРөref-РёСӮРөСҖСғ**, interior mutability вҶ’ **С–СҲРәС– У©Р·РіРөСҖРјРөР»С–Р»С–Рә**, mock object вҶ’ **mock-РҪСӢСҒР°РҪ**, reference count вҶ’ **СҒС–Р»СӮРөРјРөР»РөСҖ СҒР°РҪР°Т“СӢ**, reference cycle вҶ’ **СҒС–Р»СӮРөРјРө СҶРёРәР»СӢ**, memory leak вҶ’ **Р¶Р°Рҙ Р°Т“СӢСӮРҝР°СҒСӢ**, weak reference вҶ’ **УҷР»СҒС–Р· СҒС–Р»СӮРөРјРө**, `Rc::strong_count` / `Rc::weak_count` вҶ’ kept verbatim.

### Pipeline impact

- `data/curated/rust_book_kk_pack.json`: 14 chapters / 1 061 samples вҶ’ **15 chapters / 1 136 samples** (+75 from chapter 15).

## [4.7.14] вҖ” 2026-04-29 вҖ” Rust Book Chapter 14 (Cargo РҝРөРҪ Crates.io СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ СӮРөСҖРөТЈС–СҖРөРә) translated, in pack

## [4.7.13] вҖ” 2026-04-29 вҖ” Rust Book Chapter 13 (РӨСғРҪРәСҶРёРҫРҪР°Р» СӮС–Р»РҙС–Рә РјТҜРјРәС–РҪРҙС–РәСӮРөСҖ: РёСӮРөСҖР°СӮРҫСҖР»Р°СҖ РјРөРҪ Р¶Р°РұСғР»Р°СҖ) translated, in pack

Thirteenth chapter under В«РіР»Р°РІР° = РҝР°СӮСҮВ» cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 13 вҖ” Functional Language Features: Iterators and Closures вҖ” covering Rust's two key functional-programming primitives: closures (anonymous functions that capture their environment, the three-tier `FnOnce` / `FnMut` / `Fn` trait hierarchy, `move` keyword for ownership transfer to a closure body, the `Vec::sort_by_key` worked example) and iterators (the `Iterator` trait and the `next` method, `iter` / `iter_mut` / `into_iter` distinction, lazy evaluation, consuming adapters like `sum` / `count` / `collect` vs producing adapters like `map` / `filter`, chaining `(1..=10).filter(...).map(...).sum()`, capturing closures inside iterator chains). Then refactoring the v4.7.12 minigrep using these tools (removing `clone` from `Config::build` by passing an iterator instead of a slice, condensing the `search` / `search_case_insensitive` functions to one-line iterator chains, the loops-vs-iterators discussion). Closes with the **zero-cost abstraction** explanation: iterator chains compile to assembly indistinguishable from hand-rolled loops; sometimes more efficient because the rigid abstraction shape gives the compiler stronger optimisation guarantees.

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_13.md` вҖ” ~5 000 words, code blocks preserved verbatim, all earlier-chapter terminology applied.
- Chapter-13-specific terminology decisions: closure вҶ’ **Р¶Р°РұСғ** (already locked since v4.7.0 programming_rust glossary), capture вҶ’ **ТұСҒСӮР°Сғ**, `Fn`/`FnMut`/`FnOnce` вҶ’ **`Fn` / `FnMut` / `FnOnce` СӮСҖРөР№СӮСӮРөСҖС–** (transliterated, kept verbatim), iterator adapter вҶ’ **РёСӮРөСҖР°СӮРҫСҖ-РұРөР№С–РјРҙРөСғС–СҲ**, lazy evaluation вҶ’ **Р»Р°Р·Р°Р№ РөСҒРөРҝСӮРөСғ**, zero-cost abstraction вҶ’ **РҪУ©Р»РҙС–Рә СҲСӢТ“СӢРҪРҙСӢ Р°РұСҒСӮСҖР°РәСҶРёСҸ**, consuming adapter вҶ’ **СӮТұСӮСӢРҪР°СӮСӢРҪ РұРөР№С–РјРҙРөСғ**, producing adapter вҶ’ **Р¶Р°ТЈР° РёСӮРөСҖР°СӮРҫСҖ СҲСӢТ“Р°СҖР°СӮСӢРҪ РұРөР№С–РјРҙРөСғ**.

Sections: 12.1 accepting command-line arguments via `std::env::args`; 12.2 reading a file with `std::fs::read_to_string`; 12.3 refactoring for modularity and error handling (separation of concerns, extracting `parse_config` and then a `Config` struct, the `Config::build` constructor pattern, fixing error handling with `Result` + `unwrap_or_else` + `eprintln!` + `process::exit`, extracting a `run` function, splitting code into a library crate `src/lib.rs`); 12.4 TDD development of the `search` function (writing a failing test first, implementing the minimum code to pass, then refactoring); 12.5 working with environment variables (`env::var("IGNORE_CASE")`, the `search_case_insensitive` companion function); 12.6 writing error messages to standard error instead of standard output (`eprintln!` vs `println!`, the Unix stdout/stderr separation, `> output.txt` redirection demonstration).

This is the practical chapter that demonstrates how all earlier chapters' concepts come together: modules, ownership, references, traits, error handling, tests вҖ” all in one ~150-line program.

## [4.7.10] вҖ” 2026-04-29 вҖ” Rust Book Chapter 10 (Р–Р°Р»РҝСӢР»Р°РјР° СӮРёРҝСӮРөСҖ, СӮСҖРөР№СӮСӮРөСҖ РјРөРҪ СӮС–СҖСҲС–Р»С–Рә РјРөСҖР·С–РјС–) translated, in pack

Tenth chapter under В«РіР»Р°РІР° = РҝР°СӮСҮВ» cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 10 вҖ” Generic Types, Traits, and Lifetimes вҖ” the most theoretically dense chapter of the entire book. Three abstraction layers in one chapter: types (via generic type parameters), behaviour (via traits) and references' validity (via lifetimes).

### Translation

Three sections cover the full Rust abstraction stack:

- **10.1 Р–Р°Р»РҝСӢР»Р°РјР° РјУҷР»С–РјРөСӮ СӮТҜСҖР»РөСҖС–** вҖ” generics in functions (`largest<T: PartialOrd>`), structs (`Point<T>`, `Point<T, U>`), enums (`Option<T>`, `Result<T, E>`), and methods (`impl<T> Point<T>`, type-restricted `impl Point<f64>`); the **monomorphization** explanation вҖ” generic abstractions cost zero at runtime because the compiler emits a specialized copy per concrete type.
- **10.2 РўСҖРөР№СӮСӮРөСҖ: РҫСҖСӮР°Тӣ СӮУҷСҖСӮС–РҝСӮС– Р°РҪСӢТӣСӮР°Сғ** вҖ” defining traits (`Summary`), implementing them on types (`impl Summary for NewsArticle`), the **orphan rule** (you can implement a trait on a type only if either the trait or the type is defined in your crate), default implementations and how they can call other trait methods, traits as parameters via `&impl Summary` shorthand vs `<T: Summary>` trait-bound syntax, multiple bounds with `+`, the `where` clause for readability, returning `impl Trait`, conditionally-implemented methods (`impl<T: Display + PartialOrd> Pair<T>`), blanket implementations (`impl<T: Display> ToString for T`).
- **10.3 РўС–СҖСҲС–Р»С–Рә РјРөСҖР·С–РјС– Р°СҖТӣСӢР»СӢ СҒС–Р»СӮРөРјРөР»РөСҖРҙС– СҖР°СҒСӮР°Сғ** вҖ” preventing dangling references, the borrow checker, generic lifetimes in functions (`fn longest<'a>(x: &'a str, y: &'a str) -> &'a str`), thinking in terms of lifetimes, lifetimes in struct definitions (`ImportantExcerpt<'a>`), the three lifetime elision rules, lifetimes in method definitions (`impl<'a> ImportantExcerpt<'a>`), the `'static` lifetime (string literals, when not to use it), generic types + trait bounds + lifetimes combined in one signature.

### Translation notes

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_10.md` вҖ” ~5 500 words, code blocks preserved verbatim, all earlier-chapter terminology applied.
- Chapter-10-specific terminology decisions: monomorphization вҶ’ **РјРҫРҪРҫРјРҫСҖС„СӮР°Сғ**, default implementation вҶ’ **УҷРҙРөРҝРәС– С–СҒРәРө Р°СҒСӢСҖСғ**, blanket implementation вҶ’ **Р¶Р°Р»РҝСӢ С–СҒРәРө Р°СҒСӢСҖСғ**, trait bound вҶ’ **СӮСҖРөР№СӮ СҲРөРәСӮРөСғС–**, where clause вҶ’ **where РәР»Р°СғР·Р°СҒСӢ**, lifetime elision вҶ’ **СӮС–СҖСҲС–Р»С–Рә РјРөСҖР·С–РјС–РҪ СӮТҜСҒС–СҖС–Рҝ Р¶Р°Р·Сғ**, orphan rule вҶ’ **РҫСҖС„Р°РҪ-РөСҖРөР¶Рө**, `impl Trait` syntax вҶ’ kept as `impl Trait`.

## [4.7.9] вҖ” 2026-04-29 вҖ” Rust Book Chapter 9 (ТҡР°СӮРөРҪС– У©ТЈРҙРөСғ) translated, in pack

Ninth chapter under В«РіР»Р°РІР° = РҝР°СӮСҮВ» cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 9 вҖ” Error Handling вҖ” covering Rust's two-tier error model: **unrecoverable errors with `panic!`** (the panic message format, the array-indexing example, `RUST_BACKTRACE=1`, the `panic = "abort"` profile setting and unwind-vs-abort trade-off) and **recoverable errors with `Result<T, E>`** (the `Result` enum definition, `File::open` returning `Result<File, io::Error>`, matching on the error to take different actions, distinguishing error kinds via `error.kind()` and the deeply-nested-match trade-off, `unwrap` and `expect` as shortcuts for prototypes/tests, propagating errors with explicit `match`, the `?` operator and how it short-circuits to return `Err` from the function, the `?`-chained call style, the standard-library `fs::read_to_string` as the canonical fully-condensed form, error type conversion via the `From` trait, where `?` may be used (`Result`, `Option`, `main` returning `Result<(), Box<dyn Error>>`)). Tarau ends with **when to panic vs when to return Result** guidelines (prototypes/tests, contract violations, parsing user input, trait-encoded invariants like the `Guess` 1вҖ“100 example).

- Chapter-8-specific terminology decisions: collection вҶ’ **ТұР¶СӢРј** (already in lexicon), grapheme cluster вҶ’ **РіСҖР°С„РөРјР° РәР»Р°СҒСӮРөСҖС–**, hash function вҶ’ **С…СҚСҲ С„СғРҪРәСҶРёСҸСҒСӢ**, dereference вҶ’ **РҙРөСҖРөС„РөСҖРөРҪСҒ**, byte boundary вҶ’ **РұР°Р№СӮ СҲРөРіС–**, Unicode scalar вҶ’ **Unicode СҒРәР°Р»СҸСҖ-РјУҷРҪ**, SipHash вҶ’ **SipHash** (kept as-is, named algorithm).

- `data/curated/rust_book_kk_pack.json`: 7 chapters / 525 samples вҶ’ **8 chapters / 608 samples** (+83 from chapter 8).
- Morpheme index: **unchanged** at 3 362 morphemes / 22 145 postings / 3 691 indexed samples вҖ” the rust_book pack hit the 500-per-pack default-mode ceiling at v4.7.7. Chapter-8 sentences live in the pack file (auditable, `--full`-mode ready) but do not contribute to the committed-mode morpheme index.

- Workspace tests: **745 passing**.

### Architectural note

We are now past the committed-mode morpheme-index ceiling for the rust_book pack. Future chapters continue to grow `data/raw/rust_book_kk/` and `data/curated/rust_book_kk_pack.json` (auditable record + ready for full-mode reindex), but the committed `data/retrieval/morpheme_index.json` does not change for rust_book content. To take advantage of all chapters in retrieval, a follow-up patch will either (a) raise the per-pack limit specifically for rust_book, or (b) switch to `--full` mode for the committed index. Decision deferred until the chapter set is more complete.

## [4.7.7] вҖ” 2026-04-29 вҖ” Rust Book Chapter 7 (Р‘СғРјР°Р»Р°СҖРјРөРҪ, СҒР°РҪРҙСӢТӣСӮР°СҖРјРөРҪ, РјРҫРҙСғР»СҢРҙРөСҖРјРөРҪ Р¶РҫРұР°РҪСӢ РұР°СҒТӣР°СҖСғ) translated, ingested

Seventh chapter under В«РіР»Р°РІР° = РҝР°СӮСҮВ» cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 7 вҖ” Managing Growing Projects with Packages, Crates, and Modules вҖ” covering the four layers of Rust's modular system: **packages** (the Cargo unit, `Cargo.toml`, at-most-one library + any number of binary crates, `src/main.rs` / `src/lib.rs` / `src/bin/*.rs` conventions); **crates** (binary vs library, the crate root concept); **modules** (defining with `mod`, the module tree starting from `crate`, in-line vs separate-file declarations); **paths** (absolute paths starting from `crate`, relative paths via `self` / `super` / module names; the privacy rule вҖ” everything is private by default; `pub` opens one layer at a time; `pub struct` requires per-field `pub`; `pub enum` is variants-all-public); **bringing paths into scope with `use`** (idiomatic patterns вҖ” import the parent module for functions, import the type itself for structs/enums/types like `HashMap`/`String`/`Vec`; `as` for renaming on collision; `pub use` for re-exporting; nested paths `{}` syntax; `self` in nested paths; `*` glob operator and when not to use it); external crates (the `[dependencies]` block and `std` as the always-available special case); separating modules into different files (`mod foo;` declaration and the `src/foo.rs` / `src/foo/mod.rs` lookup paths).

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_07.md` вҖ” ~5 000 words, code blocks preserved verbatim, all earlier-chapter terminology applied.
- Chapter-7-specific terminology decisions: package вҶ’ **РұСғРјР°**, binary crate вҶ’ **РөРәС–Р»С–Рә СҒР°РҪРҙСӢТӣ**, library crate вҶ’ **РәС–СӮР°РҝС…Р°РҪР° СҒР°РҪРҙСӢТ“СӢ**, crate root вҶ’ **СҒР°РҪРҙСӢТӣ СӮТҜРұС–СҖС–**, module tree вҶ’ **РјРҫРҙСғР»СҢ Р°Т“Р°СҲСӢ**, privacy вҶ’ **Р¶РөРәРөР»С–Рә**, absolute path вҶ’ **Р°РұСҒРҫР»СҺСӮСӮС–Рә Р¶РҫР»**, relative path вҶ’ **СҒР°Р»СӢСҒСӮСӢСҖРјР°Р»СӢ Р¶РҫР»**, re-export вҶ’ **ТӣР°Р№СӮР° СҚРәСҒРҝРҫСҖСӮСӮР°Сғ**, glob operator вҶ’ **glob РҫРҝРөСҖР°СӮРҫСҖ**, nested paths вҶ’ **СӮРҫТ“СӢСҒРҝР°Р»СӢ Р¶РҫР»РҙР°СҖ**, items вҶ’ **СҚР»РөРјРөРҪСӮСӮРөСҖ**, prelude вҶ’ **РәС–СҖС–СҒРҝРө**.

## [4.7.4] вҖ” 2026-04-29 вҖ” Rust Book Chapter 4 (РҳРөР»С–РәСӮС– СӮТҜСҒС–РҪСғ) translated, ingested

Fourth chapter вҖ” the central, most conceptual chapter of the entire book. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 4 вҖ” Understanding Ownership вҖ” covering the language's defining idea: stack vs heap, the three ownership rules, variable scope, the `String` type vs string literals, memory allocation and `drop`, ownership transfer (move), `clone` for deep copy, the `Copy` trait, ownership and function calls, return values; references and borrowing (`&T` immutable, `&mut T` mutable, the two reference rules вҖ” exclusivity of mutable references vs. shared immutable references вҖ” and how data races are prevented at compile time, dangling reference prevention); the slice type (`&str` string slices, `&[T]` array slices, range `..` syntax variants `[a..b]` / `[..n]` / `[m..]` / `[..]`, `&str` as the more general parameter type vs. `&String`).

This is the chapter for which the v4.7.0 terminology decisions (РёРөР»С–Рә / ТӣР°СҖСӢР·Т“Р° Р°Р»Сғ / ТӣР°СҖСӢР· СӮРөРәСҒРөСҖРіС–СҲ / СӮС–СҖСҲС–Р»С–Рә РјРөСҖР·С–РјС– / СҒС–Р»СӮРөРјРө / У©Р·РіРөСҖРјРөР»С– / СӮТұСҖР°ТӣСӮСӢ / СҒСӮСҖСғРәСӮ / РөРҪР°Рј) were specifically locked. They are now applied throughout the canonical translation.

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_04.md` вҖ” ~6 000 words, code blocks preserved verbatim, all v4.7.0/4.7.1/4.7.2/4.7.3 terminology applied, ownership-specific terms added below.

## [4.7.3] вҖ” 2026-04-29 вҖ” Rust Book Chapter 3 (Р–Р°Р»РҝСӢ РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°Р»Р°Сғ ТұТ“СӢРјРҙР°СҖСӢ) translated, ingested

Third chapter under В«РіР»Р°РІР° = РҝР°СӮСҮВ» cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 3 вҖ” Common Programming Concepts вҖ” covering the foundational concepts that recur throughout Rust: variables and mutability (default-immutable bindings, `mut` keyword, constants `const` with mandatory type annotation and SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE convention, shadowing via `let` and how it differs from `mut` including type-changing); data types (scalar вҖ” integer types `i8`/`i16`/`i32`/`i64`/`i128` and unsigned/signed pairs with `usize`/`isize` machine-dependent forms, integer overflow behaviour in debug vs. release, floating-point `f32`/`f64`, numeric operations, boolean, character; compound вҖ” tuples with destructuring and dot-index access, the unit `()`, arrays with type/length annotation `[i32; 5]` and out-of-bounds panic); functions (`fn` keyword, snake_case convention, parameters with mandatory type annotations, the critical statement-vs-expression distinction, block expressions, return values via `->`); comments (`//`, `/* */`, doc comments `///`); control flow (`if` / `else if` / `else` with bool-only conditions, `if` as an expression in `let`, `loop` with `break value`, loop labels for nested loops, `while`, `for` over arrays and ranges, range expressions `1..4` exclusive vs `1..=4` inclusive, `.rev()`).

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_03.md` вҖ” ~5 000 words, code blocks preserved verbatim, all v4.7.0/4.7.1/4.7.2 terminology applied.
- Chapter-3-specific terminology decisions: scalar вҶ’ **Р¶Р°Р»Т“СӢР·**, compound вҶ’ **ТӣТұСҖР°РјР°**, integer overflow вҶ’ **РұТҜСӮС–РҪ СҒР°РҪРҪСӢТЈ Р°СҒСӢРҝ РәРөСӮСғС–**, floating-point вҶ’ **ТӣР°Р»ТӣСӢРјР°Р»СӢ ТҜСӮС–СҖР»С–**, numeric operations вҶ’ **СҒР°РҪРҙСӢТӣ Р°РјР°Р»РҙР°СҖ**, tuple destructuring вҶ’ **РұУ©Р»СҲРөРәСӮРөСғ**, statement vs expression вҶ’ **СҒУ©Р№Р»РөРј РјРөРҪ У©СҖРҪРөРә**, function call вҶ’ **С„СғРҪРәСҶРёСҸ СҲР°ТӣСӢСҖСғСӢ**, doc comment вҶ’ **ТӣТұР¶Р°СӮСӮР°РјР° СӮТҜСҒС–РҪС–РәСӮРөРјРөСҒС–**, loop label вҶ’ **СҶРёРәР» РұРөР»РіС–СҒС–**, range вҶ’ **РҙРёР°РҝР°Р·РҫРҪ**, inclusive/exclusive range вҶ’ **ТӣР°РјСӮСӢР»Т“Р°РҪ/ТӣР°РјСӮСӢР»РјР°Т“Р°РҪ РҙРёР°РҝР°Р·РҫРҪ**, mutability вҶ’ **У©Р·РіРөСҖРјРөР»С–Р»С–Рә**.

## [4.7.2] вҖ” 2026-04-29 вҖ” Rust Book Chapter 2 (РЎР°РҪРҙСӢ СӮР°РұСғ РҫР№СӢРҪСӢ) translated, ingested

Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 2 вҖ” Programming a Guessing Game вҖ” covering the hands-on guessing game project: setting up a new Cargo project, processing user input via `std::io::stdin().read_line()`, mutable variables (`let mut guess = String::new()`), references and mutable references (`&mut guess`), `Result`-based error handling with `.expect()`, adding the external `rand` crate as a Cargo dependency (`Cargo.toml` `[dependencies]` block, semantic versioning `^0.8.5`), generating random integers in a range (`rand::thread_rng().gen_range(1..=100)`), comparing values with `std::cmp::Ordering` and `match` expressions (`Less / Greater / Equal`), type mismatch errors and shadowing for type conversion (`let guess: u32 = guess.trim().parse().expect(...)`), looping with `loop`, breaking on success, and graceful invalid-input handling via `match Result { Ok(num) => num, Err(_) => continue }`.

## [4.7.1] вҖ” 2026-04-29 вҖ” Rust Book Chapter 1 (Р‘Р°СҒСӮР°Сғ) translated, ingested into morpheme_index (phase 2 begins)

First chapter under the В«РіР»Р°РІР° = РҝР°СӮСҮВ» cadence. Full Kazakh translation of the Rust Book Chapter 1 вҖ” Getting Started вҖ” covering installation (rustup, Linux/macOS, Windows, troubleshooting, updating, local docs), Hello World (project directory, writing/running the first program, anatomy of a Rust program, compile-vs-run as separate steps), and Hello Cargo (Cargo project creation, build/run/check, release build, Cargo as convention).

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_01.md` вҖ” full Kazakh translation, ~3 000 words, code blocks preserved verbatim, all v4.7.0 terminology decisions applied (РёРөР»С–Рә / ТӣР°СҖСӢР·Т“Р° Р°Р»Сғ / СҒР°РҪРҙСӢТӣ / СӮСҖРөР№СӮ / РөРҪР°Рј / СҒСӮСҖСғРәСӮ).
- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/LICENSE.md` вҖ” MIT/Apache-2.0 attribution to the original Rust Book.
- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/README.md` вҖ” phase-2 status, pipeline diagram, chapter status table.

### Pipeline integration

- New `crates/adam-corpus/src/bin/process_rust_book_kk.rs` вҖ” Rust binary that reads `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_*.md`, strips fenced code blocks and markdown decoration, splits Kazakh prose into sentences (preserving backtick-quoted technical spans so the dot in `Cargo.toml` is not a sentence boundary), and emits the standard adam corpus-pack format. Replaces an initial Python prototype (rejected by the Rust-only contract test). Carries 4 unit tests covering fenced-block stripping, Cyrillic-uppercase sentence splitting, backtick-span preservation, and short-fragment rejection.
- Generated `data/curated/rust_book_kk_pack.json`: 60 sentence-level samples from chapter 1, in the standard adam corpus-pack format with full attribution metadata.
- Pack registered in `SOURCE_PACKS` of `build_morpheme_index.rs`, `morpheme_coverage.rs`, and `mine_lexicon_gaps.rs`.

### Morpheme index impact

- Indexed samples: 3 117 вҶ’ **3 251** (+134 incl. 60 from rust_book_kk; remainder from per-pack indexing-limit interactions).
- Distinct morphemes: 3 082 вҶ’ **3 213** (+131).
- Total postings: 16 262 вҶ’ **17 637** (+1 375).
- 60 chapter-1 sentences are present in `sample_texts`; 247 morphemes now reference rust_book samples.

### Tests + counters

- 1 new e2e test (`rust_book_chapter_01_indexed_in_morpheme_index`) вҖ” verifies вүҘ50 rust_book samples in the index and that chapter-1-specific morphemes (`СӮУҷСғРөР»РҙС–Р»С–Рә`, `РҫСҖРҪР°СӮСғ`) have postings.
- 4 new unit tests inside `process_rust_book_kk.rs`.
- Workspace tests: 740 вҶ’ **745** passing.
- Cognitive eval / REPL replay unchanged.

### Retrieval behaviour notes

The retrieval ranker prefers `world_core` definitions over corpus citations вҖ” that is the correct priority. Chapter-1 sentences surface when (a) the query hits a Rust-specific morpheme that has no `world_core` definition AND (b) the chapter sentence outranks competing samples. As more chapters land, this ratio shifts in the chapter content's favour. No ranker tuning was done in this patch вҖ” observed behaviour is the existing ranker working as designed.

### Known limitation carried from v4.7.0

Direct Latin-name queries (В«Rust РҙРөРіРөРҪС–РјС–Р· РҪРө?В», В«Cargo РҙРөРіРөРҪС–РјС–Р· РҪРө?В», В«rustc РҙРөРіРөРҪС–РјС–Р· РҪРө?В») still don't tokenize through the Cyrillic-only FST. The v4.7.1 chapter has these terms in backticks (e.g. ``` `Rust` ```, ``` `Cargo` ```) which keeps them in the index but doesn't fix tokenization on the input side. ASCII-identifier passthrough remains deferred.

## [4.7.0] вҖ” 2026-04-29 вҖ” `programming_rust.jsonl` glossary + corpus-purity carve-out for technical text (phase 1 of Rust knowledge ingestion)

Fifth v4.x minor. Strategic ask from user: В«РҫРұСғСҮРёСӮСҢ РҪР°СҲСғ РјРҫРҙРөР»СҢ СҸР·СӢРәСғ РҝСҖРҫРіСҖР°РјРјРёСҖРҫРІР°РҪРёСҸ RustВ». Honest scope: adam can't generate code (retrieval-only architecture, `project_retrieval_not_neural_v2`), but it CAN serve as a deterministic Kazakh-language Rust glossary вҖ” and Kazakh-language Rust documentation virtually doesn't exist outside this domain. v4.7.0 = phase 1 (curated glossary). Phases 2+ = Rust Book chapter translations as patch releases (v4.7.1, v4.7.2, вҖҰ).

### `data/world_core/programming_rust.jsonl` (110 entries / 110 facts)

110 curated entries covering: Rust core (Rust, Cargo, rustc, СҒР°РҪРҙСӢТӣ/crate, РјРҫРҙСғР»СҢ, СӮУҷСғРөР»РҙС–Р»С–Рә), ownership / borrowing / lifetimes (РёРөР»С–Рә, РёРөР»С–Рә РјРҫРҙРөР»С–, СҒС–Р»СӮРөРјРө, ТӣР°СҖСӢР·Т“Р° Р°Р»Сғ, ТӣР°СҖСӢР· СӮРөРәСҒРөСҖРіС–СҲ, СӮС–СҖСҲС–Р»С–Рә РјРөСҖР·С–РјС–, У©Р·РіРөСҖРјРөР»С–/СӮТұСҖР°ТӣСӮСӢ СҒС–Р»СӮРөРјРө, РёРөР»С–РәСӮС– Р°СғСӢСҒСӮСӢСҖСғ, РәУ©СҲС–СҖСғ СҒРөРјР°РҪСӮРёРәР°СҒСӢ, СҒСӮРөРә, ТҜР№РјРө), variables and functions (Р°Р№РҪСӢРјР°Р»СӢ, СӮТұСҖР°ТӣСӮСӢ, РәУ©Р»РөТЈРәРөР»РөСғ, С„СғРҪРәСҶРёСҸ, main С„СғРҪРәСҶРёСҸСҒСӢ, РҝР°СҖР°РјРөСӮСҖ, ТӣР°Р№СӮР°СҖСғ РјУҷРҪС–, У©СҖРҪРөРә, СҒУ©Р№Р»РөРј), primitive types (i32, i64, u32, u64, usize, f32, f64, bool, char, str, String, РәРҫСҖСӮРөР¶, Р¶РёСӢРј, СӮС–Р»С–Рј), collections (Vec, HashMap, BTreeMap, HashSet, VecDeque), structs and enums (СҒСӮСҖСғРәСӮ, У©СҖС–СҒ, РәРҫСҖСӮРөР¶-СҒСӮСҖСғРәСӮ, РұС–СҖР»С–Рә СҒСӮСҖСғРәСӮ, УҷРҙС–СҒ, РұР°Р№Р»Р°РҪСӢСҒСӮСӢ С„СғРҪРәСҶРёСҸ, impl РұР»РҫРіСӢ, РөРҪР°Рј, РөРҪР°Рј РҪТұСҒТӣР°СҒСӢ, Option/Some/None, Result/Ok/Err), control flow (if У©СҖРҪРөРіС–, match У©СҖРҪРөРіС–, loop, while, for, break, continue, ТҜР»РіС–), traits and generics (СӮСҖРөР№СӮ, СӮСҖРөР№СӮ-РҪСӢСҒР°РҪ, derive РјР°РәСҖРҫСҒСӢ, Р¶Р°Р»РҝСӢР»Р°РјР° СӮРёРҝ, СӮРёРҝ РҝР°СҖР°РјРөСӮСҖС–, СҲРөРәСӮРөСғ), error handling (ТӣР°СӮРө У©ТЈРҙРөСғ, panic, unwrap, expect, ? РҫРҝРөСҖР°СӮРҫСҖСӢ, Drop СӮСҖРөР№СӮС–), smart pointers and concurrency (Box, Rc, Arc, RefCell, Mutex, Р°Т“СӢРҪ, РәР°РҪР°Р», async С„СғРҪРәСҶРёСҸ, await, Future), iterators (РёСӮРөСҖР°СӮРҫСҖ, map, filter, collect, Р¶Р°РұСғ), unsafe blocks, modules / visibility (use, pub, mod, crates.io), and Cargo workflow (cargo build / run / test / check, clippy, rustfmt).

Terminology decisions (locked at start of phase 1; will guide all chapter translations in phase 2):
- `ownership` вҶ’ **РёРөР»С–Рә**, `borrow / borrowing` вҶ’ **ТӣР°СҖСӢР·Т“Р° Р°Р»Сғ**, `borrow checker` вҶ’ **ТӣР°СҖСӢР· СӮРөРәСҒРөСҖРіС–СҲ**.
- `reference` вҶ’ **СҒС–Р»СӮРөРјРө**, `lifetime` вҶ’ **СӮС–СҖСҲС–Р»С–Рә РјРөСҖР·С–РјС–**, `mutable` / `immutable` вҶ’ **У©Р·РіРөСҖРјРөР»С–** / **СӮТұСҖР°ТӣСӮСӢ**.
- `crate` вҶ’ **СҒР°РҪРҙСӢТӣ** (preserves the wooden-crate metaphor of the original).
- `trait` вҶ’ **СӮСҖРөР№СӮ** (transliteration; native `ТӣР°СҒРёРөСӮ` already saturated in general use).
- `enum` / `struct` вҶ’ **РөРҪР°Рј** / **СҒСӮСҖСғРәСӮ** (transliterations).
- Code identifiers (`Vec<T>`, `Option::Some`, `match`, `let`, `fn`) вҖ” **never translated**, kept verbatim in backticks.

### Corpus-purity carve-out for technical text

`validate_world_core::non_kazakh_reason` now skips characters inside paired backticks. The carve-out applies ONLY inside backtick spans; bare Latin prose outside backticks is still flagged. This lets the `programming_rust.jsonl` domain (and future technical domains) embed Rust keywords / type names / commands verbatim while keeping the Kazakh-only directive intact for free prose. Documented in `data/world_core/README.md` as the v4.7.0 schema rule.

### Pipeline impact

- `data/retrieval/facts.json`: 15 721 вҶ’ **15 831** (+110 from the new domain).
- `data/retrieval/derived_facts.json`: 22 962 вҶ’ **23 418** (+456 derived facts via R1/R2/R5/R8 inheritance through new IsA hubs `РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°Р»Р°Сғ СӮС–Р»С– / РјУҷР»С–РјРөСӮ СӮТҜСҖС– / ТұР¶СӢРјРҙСӢТӣ СӮРёРҝ / РұР°СҒТӣР°СҖСғ ТӣТұСҖСӢР»СӢРјСӢ / Р¶Р°Рҙ РјРҫРҙРөР»С– / СӮСҖРөР№СӮ`).
- world_core total: **1142 entries / 1305 facts across 33 domains**.
- 52 new compounds added to `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES`.
- 24 new noun roots added to `data/tokenizer/segmentation_roots.json` (СҒР°РҪРҙСӢТӣ, РјРҫРҙСғР»СҢ, СӮСҖРөР№СӮ, РөРҪР°Рј, СҒСӮСҖСғРәСӮ, РәРҫСҖСӮРөР¶, СӮС–Р»С–Рј, РёСӮРөСҖР°СӮРҫСҖ, РҫРҝРөСҖР°СӮРҫСҖ, РҫРҝРөСҖР°СҶРёСҸ, РҝР°СҖР°РјРөСӮСҖ, СҖРөСҒСғСҖСҒ, РәР°РҪР°Р», Р¶Р°РұСғ, СҲРөРәСӮРөСғ, У©СҖС–СҒ, РәУ©СҲС–СҖСғ, УҷРҙС–СҒ, РұР°РҝСӮР°Сғ, СӮР°СҖРјР°Тӣ, СҲР°РјР°, РұР»РҫРә, РәС–Р»СӮСҒУ©Р·, Р¶Р°Р»РҝСӢР»Р°РјР°).

### Known limitations (resolved in later phases)

- **Direct Latin-name queries** (В«Rust РҙРөРіРөРҪС–РјС–Р· РҪРө?В», В«Cargo РҙРөРіРөРҪС–РјС–Р· РҪРө?В», В«rustc РҙРөРіРөРҪС–РјС–Р· РҪРө?В») don't tokenize through the Cyrillic-only FST and fall through to the Unknown path. Kazakh-paraphrased queries (РҳРөР»С–Рә / РўСҖРөР№СӮ / РЎС–Р»СӮРөРјРө / РўС–СҖСҲС–Р»С–Рә РјРөСҖР·С–РјС– / РЎР°РҪРҙСӢТӣ) work correctly. Resolution: ASCII-identifier passthrough in the parser, deferred to a later patch once Rust Book chapter content surfaces enough Latin-prose context to justify it.
- **No code generation.** adam will not write Rust code on demand вҖ” that remains outside the retrieval-only architecture. The glossary supports definitional and conceptual queries, not В«write me an HTTP serverВ» asks.

### Tests + counters

- 1 new e2e test (`programming_rust_kazakh_paraphrased_facts_surface`).
- 1 new REPL replay dialog (`programming_rust_kazakh_paraphrased_v4_7_0`).
- REPL replay: 68/68 вҶ’ **69/69 canonical**.
- Workspace: 739 вҶ’ **740 tests passing**.

Why minor: new world_core domain (most domains have shipped as patches, but this one ships with the corpus-purity rule which is an architectural carve-out) + 24-root lexicon expansion + 52-compound MULTIWORD_ENTITIES growth + 110-fact knowledge base вҖ” qualifies as a minor by the post-1.0 cadence rule.

## [4.6.20] вҖ” 2026-04-29 вҖ” Bundle of 5 more innovations (20 total on the v4.6.x minor): reflexive identity question + adj+noun compound noun-hint + SelfComparison aspect + preamble stripper + UserAcknowledgement intent

Real-REPL 2026-04-29 (fifth transcript) surfaced 5 distinct defects all sharing a theme: adam couldn't make sense of long, multi-clause Kazakh sentences. Greedy first-noun-hint extraction grabbed closed-class adverbs (`УҷР»С–`) or modifier-stripped head nouns (`РҫТӣСӢСӮСғ` from В«РјР°СҲРёРҪР°Р»СӢТӣ РҫТӣСӢСӮСғ`), then surfaced random poetry/contract quotes. v4.6.20 attacks the defect class with five targeted fixes вҖ” no architectural rewrite, no synthetic-grammar parser, just better pre-classification. Per the cumulative-counter cadence: 15 (v4.6.15) + 5 = **v4.6.20**.

### Innovation 1 вҖ” Reflexive identity question detector

В«УЁР·С–ТЈС–Р·РҙС– РәС–Рј РҙРөРҝ СҒР°РҪР°Р№СҒСӢР·?В» / В«УЁР·С–ТЈРҙС– ТӣР°Р»Р°Р№ СӮР°РҪСӢСҒСӮСӢСҖР°СҒСӢТЈ?В» / В«УЁР·С–ТЈС–Р·РҙС– ТӣР°Р»Р°Р№ РәУ©СҖРөСҒС–Р·?В». The marker is `У©Р·С–ТЈРҙС– / У©Р·С–ТЈС–Р·РҙС–` (reflexive accusative) plus a 2nd-person verb. Extends `detect_ask_about_system` to route these to `SystemAspect::General`. Pre-v4.6.20 fell through to В«Р‘УҷР»РәС–Рј, У©Р·С–ТЈС–Р· СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ Р°Р№СӮР°СҒСӢР· РұР°В» (misclassified as a request that the user describe themselves).

### Innovation 2 вҖ” Adj+noun compound noun-hint

`discourse::find_adj_noun_compound` returns the longest matching closed-list compound (`РјР°СҲРёРҪР°Р»СӢТӣ РҫТӣСӢСӮСғ`, `Р¶Р°СҒР°РҪРҙСӢ РёРҪСӮРөР»Р»РөРәСӮ`, `СӮР°РұРёТ“Рё СӮС–Р»`, `СӮРөСҖРөТЈ РҫТӣСӢСӮСғ`, `РҪРөР№СҖРҫРҪРҙСӢТӣ Р¶РөР»С–`, вҖҰ) found in the input. Wired as the FIRST strategy in `best_noun_hint`, ahead of topic-marker / locative-attributive / multiword / first-noun. Pre-v4.6.20 reduced В«РңР°СҲРёРҪР°Р»СӢТӣ РҫТӣСӢСӮСғ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ вҖҰВ» to noun_hint=`РҫТӣСӢСӮСғ` (head only), losing the modifier and retrieving generic education quotes.

### Innovation 3 вҖ” `SystemAspect::SelfComparison`

Ninth `SystemAspect` variant + `system_self_comparison` slot + `ask_about_system.self_comparison` template family. Detector lives in `discourse::input_is_self_comparison_question` as a pair (comparison marker `Р°СҖСӮСӢТӣ/Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢСҖР°Тӣ/РҫР·Р°СҒСӢТЈ` + addressee marker including the `-СҒСӢТЈ/-СҒСӢР·` ability suffix). Honest framing вҖ” adam articulates the *trade-off* (narrow Kazakh-only competence with strong invariants vs. broad LLM coverage) rather than claiming superiority. Closes from real-REPL: В«Р‘Р°СҒТӣР° Р¶Р°СҒР°РҪРҙСӢ РёРҪСӮРөР»Р»РөРәСӮ РјРҫРҙРөР»СҢРҙРөСҖС–РҪРөРҪ РҪРөСҒС–РјРөРҪ Р°СҖСӮСӢТӣСҒСӢР·?В», В«ТҡРҫР»РҙР°РҪСӢСҒСӮР°Т“СӢ РјРҫРҙРөР»СҢРҙРөСҖРҙРөРҪ ТӣР°Р»Р°Р№ Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢСҖР°Тӣ РұРҫР»Р° Р°Р»Р°СҒСӢР·?В».

### Innovation 4 вҖ” Discourse preamble stripper

`discourse::strip_preamble` runs at the top of `Conversation::turn_with_trace` BEFORE FST parsing. Closed list of 24 leading preambles (`Р°Р№СӮР°Р№СӢРҪ РҙРөРіРөРҪС–Рј`, `ТӣСӢСҒТӣР°СҲР° Р°Р№СӮТӣР°РҪРҙР°`, `СҲСӢРҪСӢРҪРҙР°`, `СҒТұСҖР°Т“СӢРј РјСӢРҪР°Сғ`, `Р¶Р°Р»РҝСӢ Р°Р»Т“Р°РҪРҙР°`, `Р°Р№СӮРҝР°ТӣСҲСӢ`, вҖҰ); when matched at input start with a clause separator after, the preamble is removed and the residual goes to the parser. Russian/math/anaphor detection still see the raw input (those operate on surface signals where preambles never interfere). Closes from real-REPL: В«РҗР№СӮР°Р№СӢРҪ РҙРөРіРөРҪС–Рј, ТӣРҫР»РҙР°РҪСӢСҒСӮР°Т“СӢ РјРҫРҙРөР»СҢРҙРөСҖРҙРөРҪ ТӣР°Р»Р°Р№ Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢСҖР°Тӣ РұРҫР»Р° Р°Р»Р°СҒСӢР·?В» вҖ” preamble stripped, residual routes to SelfComparison.

### Innovation 5 вҖ” `UserAcknowledgement` intent + template family

New `Intent::UserAcknowledgement` variant + `user_acknowledgement` template family. Detector: addressee marker (`СҒРөРҪС–ТЈ / СҒРөРҪС– / СҒС–Р·РҙС–ТЈ / СҒС–Р·РҙС–`) + 1sg perfective realisation verb (`СӮТҜСҒС–РҪРҙС–Рј / РұС–Р»РҙС–Рј / РәУ©СҖРҙС–Рј / РұР°Р№ТӣР°РҙСӢРј / ТұТӣСӮСӢРј / Р°ТЈТ“Р°СҖРҙСӢРј / СҒРөР·С–РҪРҙС–Рј`) + not-a-question. Polite acknowledgement reply (В«СҖР°С…РјРөСӮ, СӮТҜСҒС–РҪРіРөРҪС–ТЈС–Р·РіРө ТӣСғР°РҪСӢСҲСӮСӢРјСӢРҪ. РңРөРҪ УҷР»С– РҙР°РјСӢРҝ РәРөР»РөРјС–РҪ вҖҰВ»). Pre-v4.6.20 grabbed the closed-class adverb `УҷР»С–` from В«РңРөРҪ СҒРөРҪС–ТЈ УҷР»С– РұУҷСҖС–РҪ РұС–Р»РјРөР№СӮС–РҪС–ТЈРҙС– вҖҰ СӮТҜСҒС–РҪРҙС–РјВ» and surfaced a random poetry quote about feelings.

### Tests + counters

- 5 new e2e tests (`reflexive_self_question_routes_to_ask_about_system_general`, `adj_noun_compound_noun_hint_preserves_modifier`, `self_comparison_question_routes_to_self_comparison_aspect`, `preamble_stripper_unmasks_underlying_question`, `user_acknowledgement_routes_to_dedicated_template`).
- 5 new REPL replay dialogs.
- `discourse.rs` helpers: 3 вҶ’ **7** (`strip_preamble`, `input_is_user_acknowledgement`, `input_is_self_comparison_question`, `find_adj_noun_compound` added).
- `SystemAspect` variants: 8 вҶ’ **9** (+ `SelfComparison`).
- `Intent` variants: 26 вҶ’ **27** (+ `UserAcknowledgement`).
- Template families: 57 вҶ’ **59** (+ `ask_about_system.self_comparison`, `user_acknowledgement`).
- REPL replay: 63/63 вҶ’ **68/68 canonical**.
- Workspace: 734 вҶ’ **739 tests passing**.

## [4.6.15] вҖ” 2026-04-29 вҖ” Bundle of 3 more innovations (15 total on the v4.6.x minor): integer arithmetic calculator + `mathematics_basic` world_core domain + `informatics_basic` world_core domain

User strategic ask: В«РҪРөРҫРұС…РҫРҙРёРјРҫ РҙР°СӮСҢ РөРјСғ Р·РҪР°РҪРёСҸ СҲРәРҫР»СҢРҪРҫР№ РҝСҖРҫРіСҖР°РјРјСӢ РҝРҫ РјР°СӮРөРјР°СӮРёРәРө Рё РёРҪС„РҫСҖРјР°СӮРёРәРө вҖҰ РһРҪ РҙРҫР»Р¶РөРҪ РҝРҫРҪРёРјР°СӮСҢ РҙРёР°Р»РҫРі, СӮРҫРіРҫ, СҮСӮРҫ РҫСӮ РҪРөРіРҫ С…РҫСӮСҸСӮВ». v4.6.12 detected math expressions and refused; v4.6.15 evaluates them deterministically and adds two new world_core domains so adam knows what the school terms *mean*. Per the v4.6.5-clarified cadence, patch sub-counter is **cumulative on the minor**: v4.6.12 + 3 = **v4.6.15**.

### Innovation 1 вҖ” `Tool::Calculate` integer arithmetic

New `discourse::try_evaluate_arithmetic`: deterministic two-pass tokeniser/evaluator over `+ - * / :` (`:` normalised to `/`), respects `*//` precedence over `+/-`, rejects non-integer results, division-by-zero, and overflow. When the v4.6.12 math detector fires, the conversation layer first attempts evaluation; on success the planner routes to a new `math_answer` template family with the computed `{math_value}` slot. On failure (Kazakh math verbs without parseable digits) the existing `math_refusal` route still fires.

Closes from real-REPL: В«5+5 вҶ’ 10В», В«7 + 3 = вҶ’ 10В», В«6:2= вҶ’ 3В», В«12*4 вҶ’ 48В», В«100-37 вҶ’ 63В», В«2+3*4 вҶ’ 14В». No external numeric library вҖ” pure stdlib `i64` arithmetic.

### Innovation 2 вҖ” `mathematics_basic.jsonl` world_core domain (37 entries / 37 facts)

New domain: РјР°СӮРөРјР°СӮРёРәР°, СҒР°РҪ, Р°РјР°Р», ТӣРҫСҒСғ/Р°Р·Р°Р№СӮСғ/РәУ©РұРөР№СӮСғ/РұУ©Р»Сғ, СӮРөТЈРҙС–Рә, СӮРөТЈРҙРөСғ, РұУ©Р»СҲРөРә, РҝР°Р№СӢР·, Р°Р»РіРөРұСҖР°/РіРөРҫРјРөСӮСҖРёСҸ/СӮСҖРёРіРҫРҪРҫРјРөСӮСҖРёСҸ, С„РёРіСғСҖР°, РҪТҜРәСӮРө, СӮТҜР·Сғ, РұТұСҖСӢСҲ, СҲРөТЈРұРөСҖ, РҙУ©ТЈРіРөР»РөРә, ТҜСҲРұТұСҖСӢСҲ/СӮУ©СҖСӮРұТұСҖСӢСҲ/СҲР°СҖСҲСӢ/СӮС–РәСӮУ©СҖСӮРұТұСҖСӢСҲ/РәУ©РҝРұТұСҖСӢСҲ, Р¶ТұРҝ/СӮР°Тӣ/Р¶Р°Р№/РұТҜСӮС–РҪ/РҪР°СӮСғСҖР°Р» СҒР°РҪ, РәУ©РұРөР№СӮСғ РәРөСҒСӮРөСҒС–, Р°СғРҙР°РҪ/РәУ©Р»РөРј/РҝРөСҖРёРјРөСӮСҖ, СҲР°РјР°, С„СғРҪРәСҶРёСҸ. All curated, `approved` by `shaman`, `confidence: high`.

### Innovation 3 вҖ” `informatics_basic.jsonl` world_core domain (40 entries / 40 facts)

New domain: РёРҪС„РҫСҖРјР°СӮРёРәР°, Р°ТӣРҝР°СҖР°СӮ, РҙРөСҖРөРә, Р°Р»РіРҫСҖРёСӮРј, РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°, РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°Р»Р°Сғ, РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°Р»Р°Сғ СӮС–Р»С–, РәРҫРјРҝСҢСҺСӮРөСҖ, РҝСҖРҫСҶРөСҒСҒРҫСҖ, Р¶Р°Рҙ, РҙРёСҒРәС–, С„Р°Р№Р», ТӣР°Р»СӮР°, РұРёСӮ/РұР°Р№СӮ, Р°Р№РҪСӢРјР°Р»СӢ, СӮТұСҖР°ТӣСӮСӢ, СҶРёРәР», СҲР°СҖСӮ, С„СғРҪРәСҶРёСҸ, Р¶РёСӢРј, РҙРөСҖРөРәСӮРөСҖ РұР°Р·Р°СҒСӢ, Р¶РөР»С–, РёРҪСӮРөСҖРҪРөСӮ, СҒР°Р№СӮ, СҲРҫР»Т“СӢСҲ, СҒРөСҖРІРөСҖ, РәРҫРҙСӮР°Сғ, СҲРёС„СҖР»Р°Сғ, РҝР°СҖРҫР»СҢ, РІРёСҖСғСҒ/Р°РҪСӮРёРІРёСҖСғСҒ, РҫРҝРөСҖР°СҶРёСҸР»СӢТӣ Р¶ТҜР№Рө, РҙСҖР°Р№РІРөСҖ, ТӣРҫР»РҙР°РҪРұР°, РҝРөСҖРҪРөСӮР°ТӣСӮР°/СӮС–РҪСӮСғС–СҖ/РјРҫРҪРёСӮРҫСҖ/РҝСҖРёРҪСӮРөСҖ/СҒРәР°РҪРөСҖ. All curated, `approved` by `shaman`, `confidence: high`.

### Pipeline impact

- `data/retrieval/facts.json`: 15 644 вҶ’ **15 721** (+77 from the two new domains).
- `data/retrieval/derived_facts.json`: 22 387 вҶ’ **22 962** (+575 derived facts via R1/R2/R5/R8 inheritance through the new IsA hubs `Т“СӢР»СӢРј / РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР° / ТӣТұСҖСӢР»Т“СӢ / Р°СҖРёС„РјРөСӮРёРәР°Р»СӢТӣ Р°РјР°Р» / РјР°СӮРөРјР°СӮРёРәР°Р»СӢТӣ ТұТ“СӢРј / РіРөРҫРјРөСӮСҖРёСҸР»СӢТӣ С„РёРіСғСҖР°`).
- world_core total: **1 032 entries / 1 195 facts across 32 domains**.
- 41 new compounds added to `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES` (e.g. `Р°СҖРёС„РјРөСӮРёРәР°Р»СӢТӣ Р°РјР°Р»`, `РіРөРҫРјРөСӮСҖРёСҸР»СӢТӣ С„РёРіСғСҖР°`, `РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°Р»СӢТӣ СҲР°РјР°`, `РҫРҝРөСҖР°СҶРёСҸР»СӢТӣ Р¶ТҜР№Рө`) so the longest-match scan picks the compound before any contained simpler form.
- 3 loanword roots added to `data/tokenizer/segmentation_roots.json` (РёРҪС„РҫСҖРјР°СӮРёРәР°, РәРҫРјРҝСҢСҺСӮРөСҖ, С„СғРҪРәСҶРёСҸ) вҖ” math-side loanwords like `Р°Р»РіРҫСҖРёСӮРј`, `РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°`, `С„Р°Р№Р»`, `СҶРёРәР»`, `СҲР°СҖСӮ` were already present.

### Tests

- New e2e: `calculator_evaluates_pure_arithmetic` (6 inputs / 6 expected integer results).
- New e2e: `mathematics_and_informatics_world_core_facts_surface` (5 В«X РҙРөРіРөРҪС–РјС–Р· РҪРө?В» queries through the retrieval-aware `Conversation`).
- Updated e2e: `math_input_routes_to_math_refusal` now restricted to inputs that contain math vocabulary but no parseable digit expression (Kazakh-numeral-word forms) вҖ” pure-arithmetic strings now exercise the calculator path.
- New REPL replay dialog: `math_calculator_pure_arithmetic_v4_6_15` (4 turns).
- New REPL replay dialog: `world_core_math_informatics_definitions_v4_6_15` (3 turns).
- All 155 dialog lib tests pass; full workspace `cargo test --release` green.

## [4.6.12] вҖ” 2026-04-29 вҖ” Bundle of 7 more innovations (12 total on the v4.6.x minor): polite-plural greeting / template grammar fix / Russian-input refusal / Birthdate verbs / self-age form / math refusal / case-suffix hygiene

Real-REPL 2026-04-29 (third transcript) surfaced 7 distinct issues. All landed in one bundle. Per the v4.6.5-clarified cadence: patch sub-counter is **cumulative on the minor**, so v4.6.5 + 7 = **v4.6.12**.

### Innovation 1 вҖ” AskHowAreYou polite-plural В«ТҡР°Р»СӢТЈСӢР· ТӣР°Р»Р°Р№?В»

`detect_ask_how_are_you` extended with the polite-plural surface form. Pre-v4.6.12 В«ТҡР°Р»СӢТЈСӢР· ТӣР°Р»Р°Р№?В» fell through to refusal.

### Innovation 2 вҖ” `greeting.intro_proposal` template grammar fix

Pre-v4.6.12 the 4th variant said В«РңРөРҪС–ТЈ Р°СӮСӢРј Р°РҙР°Рј вҖ” СҒС–Р· ТӣР°Р»Р°Р№ СӮР°РҪСӢСҒР°РјСӢР·?В» вҖ” grammatically incoherent (2sg-polite pronoun В«СҒС–Р·В» + 1pl-future verb В«СӮР°РҪСӢСҒР°РјСӢР·В»). Replaced with В«СҒС–Р·РҙС– ТӣР°Р»Р°Р№ Р°СӮР°Р№РјСӢРҪ?В» (В«what shall I call you?В») вҖ” same conversational function, grammatically correct.

### Innovation 3 вҖ” Russian-input refusal

New `discourse::input_is_likely_russian` detector. Two-signal logic: (a) any high-frequency Russian function word appears (`СҚСӮРҫ / СҮСӮРҫ / РәСӮРҫ / РәР°Рә / РіРҙРө / РҝРҫСҮРөРјСғ / СӮРөРұСҸ / РјРөРҪСҸ / РҫСҮРөРҪСҢ / СҒРҝР°СҒРёРұРҫ / РҝСҖРёРІРөСӮ / РҝРҫРәР° / ...`); (b) input contains zero Kazakh-specific letters (`Уҷ / ТЈ / Т“ / У© / ТҜ / Тұ / Тӣ / С– / Т»`). When both fire, conversation layer sets `__non_kazakh__` marker, planner routes to new `unknown.non_kazakh` template family which politely refuses in Kazakh and asks for Kazakh-language input.

Conservative вҖ” mixed code-switching inputs (Kazakh sentence with one Russian word) still flow through the standard pipeline; only obviously-Russian inputs short-circuit.

### Innovation 4 вҖ” Birthdate detector +verb forms

Mirrors the v4.6.5 Creator extension. Real-REPL: В«РҗР» РҫР» СҒРөРҪС– ТӣР°СҲР°РҪ Р¶Р°СҖР°СӮСӮСӢ?В» fell through pre-v4.6.12. Added: `ТӣР°СҲР°РҪ Р¶Р°СҖР°СӮСӮСӢ / ТӣР°СҲР°РҪ РҙР°РјСӢСӮСӮСӢ / ТӣР°СҲР°РҪ РҙР°РјСӢСӮТӣР°РҪ / ТӣР°СҲР°РҪ РҙР°Р№СӢРҪРҙР°РҙСӢ`.

### Innovation 5 вҖ” AskAge +В«РҪРөСҲРө Р¶Р°СҒСӮР°СҒСӢТЈ/Р¶Р°СҒСӮР°СҒСӢР·В» surface forms

Pre-v4.6.12 only matched `ТӣР°РҪСҲР° Р¶Р°СҒСӮР°СҒСӢТЈ/Р¶Р°СҒСӮР°СҒСӢР·`. Real-REPL: В«РЎС–Р· РҪРөСҲРө Р¶Р°СҒСӮР°СҒСӢР·?В» fell through. With no `session.age`, AskAge correctly falls through to the bare `ask_age` family (В«РјРөРҪС–ТЈ Р¶Р°СҒСӢРј Р°РҙР°РјР·Р°СӮ Р¶Р°СҒСӢРҪРҙР°Р№В», В«РјРөРҪ УҷР»С– Р¶Р°СҒРҝСӢРҪВ») вҖ” the right system-self response for adam.

### Innovation 6 вҖ” Math-expression refusal

New `discourse::input_is_math_expression` detector. Two-signal logic:
1. Arithmetic operators (`+`, `-`, `*`, `/`, `:`, `=`) appearing within 3 bytes of digits.
2. Kazakh math verbs (`РәУ©РұРөР№СӮСғ / РәУ©РұРөР№СӮСҒРөРј / РәУ©РұРөР№СӮРәРөРҪРҙРө / РұУ©Р»Сғ / РұУ©Р»СҒРөРј / РұУ©Р»С–ТЈС–Р· / ТӣРҫСҒСғ / ТӣРҫСҒСҒР°Рј / Р°Р»Сғ / Р°Р»СҒР°Рј / РөСҒРөРҝСӮРө / ...`) alongside digits OR Kazakh numeral words (`РұС–СҖ / РөРәС– / ... / Р°Р»СӮСӢ / Р¶РөСӮС– / ... / Р¶ТҜР· / РјСӢТЈ`). The numeral-word check uses prefix-match (вүӨ+3 chars) to handle case-inflected forms like `Р°Р»СӮСӢРҪСӢ` (six-acc) and `РөРәС–РіРө` (two-dat).

When fired, conversation layer sets `__math_input__` marker, planner routes to new `math_refusal` template family. Closes:
- В«5+5В» / В«7 + 3 =В» / В«6:2=В» (pure arithmetic)
- В«5-СӮС– 7-РіРө РәУ©РұРөР№СӮРәРөРҪРҙРө РҪРөСҲРө РұРҫР»Р°РҙСӢ?В» (Kazakh math verb + numerals)
- В«РҗР»СӮСӢРҪСӢ РөРәС–РіРө РұУ©Р»С–ТЈС–Р·, РҪУҷСӮРёР¶РөСҒС– ТӣР°РҪРҙР°Р№?В» (also resolves the Р°Р»СӮСӢ/Р°Р»СӮСӢРҪ homonym ambiguity by short-circuiting BEFORE topic extraction вҖ” pre-v4.6.12 surfaced В«РҗР»СӮСӢРҪ вҖ” СҒР°СҖСӢ СӮТҜСҒВ»)

### Innovation 7 вҖ” Closed-class case-suffix hygiene

Bare case suffixes (`РіРө / РәРө / РҙРө / СӮРө / РҙР° / СӮР° / РұРө / РјР°`) added to `NOT_A_TOPIC`. Real-REPL: В«5-СӮС– 7-РіРө РәУ©РұРөР№СӮРәРөРҪРҙРөВ» pre-v4.6.12 extracted bare `РіРө` as topic вҖ” now suppressed.

### Verified end-to-end on the 2026-04-29 transcript

| User turn | Pre-v4.6.12 | Post-v4.6.12 |
|---|---|---|
| `ТҡР°Р»СӢТЈСӢР· ТӣР°Р»Р°Р№?` | "РўТҜСҒС–РҪРұРөРҙС–Рј." | "Р–Р°ТӣСҒСӢ, Р°Р» СҒС–Р· ТӣР°Р»Р°Р№СҒСӢР·." |
| `РўР°РҪСӢСҒР°Р№СӢТӣ.` (template seed-3) | В«...СҒС–Р· ТӣР°Р»Р°Р№ СӮР°РҪСӢСҒР°РјСӢР·?В» (ungrammatical) | В«...СҒС–Р·РҙС– ТӣР°Р»Р°Р№ Р°СӮР°Р№РјСӢРҪ?В» |
| `РӯСӮРҫ РҫСҮРөРҪСҢ РәСҖСғСӮРҫ, Р° РәСӮРҫ СӮРөРұСҸ СҒРҫР·РҙР°Р»?` | half-RU half-KZ hybrid | В«РңРөРҪ ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣСҲР° Т“Р°РҪР° РұС–Р»РөРјС–РҪ; РҫСҖСӢСҒСҲР° РҪРөРјРөСҒРө Р°Т“СӢР»СҲСӢРҪСҲР° СӮТҜСҒС–РҪРұРөР№РјС–РҪ.В» |
| `РҗР» РҫР» СҒРөРҪС– ТӣР°СҲР°РҪ Р¶Р°СҖР°СӮСӮСӢ?` | "РўТҜСҒС–РҪРұРөРҙС–Рј." | В«РңРөРҪС–ТЈ СӮСғТ“Р°РҪ РәТҜРҪС–Рј вҖ” 2026-04-07.В» |
| `РЎС–Р· РҪРөСҲРө Р¶Р°СҒСӮР°СҒСӢР·?` | tangential proverb | В«РңРөРҪС–ТЈ Р¶Р°СҒСӢРј Р°РҙР°РјР·Р°СӮ Р¶Р°СҒСӢРҪРҙР°Р№.В» |
| `5+5` / `7+3=` / `6:2=` | "РўТҜСҒС–РҪРұРөРҙС–Рј" / "Р‘Р°СҒТӣР° СҒТұСҖР°Тӣ ТӣРҫР№СҒР°ТЈСӢР·" | math refusal |
| `5-СӮС– 7-РіРө РәУ©РұРөР№СӮРәРөРҪРҙРө РҪРөСҲРө РұРҫР»Р°РҙСӢ?` | proverb + bare-suffix `РіРө` topic | math refusal |
| `РҗР»СӮСӢРҪСӢ РөРәС–РіРө РұУ©Р»С–ТЈС–Р·, РҪУҷСӮРёР¶РөСҒС– ТӣР°РҪРҙР°Р№?` | В«РҗР»СӮСӢРҪ вҖ” СҒР°СҖСӢ СӮТҜСҒВ» (gold) | math refusal |

### Tests

- 5 new e2e regressions covering all 7 innovations.
- 4 new lib tests in `discourse::math_tests` (positive math forms + non-math discrimination).
- 4 new lib tests in `discourse::russian_tests` (positive Russian + Kazakh + mixed + empty).
- 7 new REPL replay dialogs from the actual transcript.
- 0 new cognitive scenarios (the affected behaviour is surface-text-level; locks at REPL replay layer).

Workspace **715 вҶ’ 727** (+12). REPL replay **55/55 вҶ’ 62/62 canonical** (+7). Cognitive eval **65/65** (unchanged вҖ” locks at REPL replay).

### Out of scope, deferred to a future release

The user also asked for **school math + informatics curriculum knowledge** and **graph-based dialogue logic understanding**. Push back: adam already has graphs (lexical / fact / reasoning) and a finite-state dialogue model (`task::TaskState` / `task::TaskVariant`); what's missing is broader intent coverage + curated math/informatics knowledge as world_core data. Concrete plan for a future bundle:
- New world_core domains `mathematics_basic.jsonl` + `informatics_basic.jsonl` (definitions / concepts / multiplication tables as facts).
- Optional: deterministic `Tool::Calculate` dispatch for integer arithmetic вҖ” patch-tier, no novel-generation guarantee broken.
- Goal-tracking enhancement in `task.rs` to track conversational goals across multiple turns.

These would be cumulative innovations on top of v4.6.12, bundled as v4.6.13+ in the next bundle.

### State

|---|---|---|
| Workspace tests | 715 | **727** (+12) |
| Cognitive eval | 65/65 canonical | 65/65 canonical (unchanged) |
| REPL replay | 55/55 canonical | **62/62 canonical** (+7) |
| Template families | 54 | **56** (+ unknown.non_kazakh, math_refusal) |
| `discourse.rs` helpers | 1 (input_contains_discourse_anaphor) | **3** (+ input_is_likely_russian, input_is_math_expression) |
| Why patch-bundle | вҖ” | per the cumulative-counter cadence: 7 additional innovations on top of v4.6.5 вҶ’ 5 + 7 = 12; sub-counter accumulates on the minor |

## [4.6.5] вҖ” 2026-04-29 вҖ” Bundle of 5 innovations: Creator detector +3 verbs / capitalization / period gate / Principles aspect / forbidden-pattern filter

First release under the new patch-bundling cadence (memory `feedback_versioning_post_1_0` updated 2026-04-29): patches bundle, version reflects the count of innovations. Five innovations bundled here вҶ’ **v4.6.0 вҶ’ v4.6.5** (skipping 1вҖ“4 by user-confirmed convention).

### Innovation 1 вҖ” Creator detector +3 verb forms

Real-REPL 2026-04-29 (second transcript) carried В«РҗР» СҒРөРҪС– РәС–Рј Р¶Р°СҖР°СӮСӮСӢ?В» / В«РЎС–Р·РҙС– РәС–Рј РҙР°РјСӢСӮТӣР°РҪ?В» / В«РЎС–Р·РҙС– ТӣР°Р№ РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°СҲСӢ РҙР°Р№СӢРҪРҙР°РҙСӢ?В» вҖ” all 3 fell through to refusal. v4.6.5 extends the Creator branch in `detect_ask_about_system` with `Р¶Р°СҖР°СӮСӮСӢ` (created), `РҙР°РјСӢСӮТӣР°РҪ / РҙР°РјСӢСӮСӮСӢ` (developed), `РҙР°Р№СӢРҪРҙР°РҙСӢ` (prepared), `Р¶Р°СҖР°СӮСғСҲСӢТЈ` (creator-as-noun), `ТӣР°Р№ РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°СҲСӢ` (which programmer). Routes to `AskAboutSystem(Creator)`.

### Innovation 2 вҖ” Capitalization filter

Every reply now starts with an uppercase letter (sentence-case). New `capitalise_first_letter` orthographic pass in `realiser::realise`:
- Steps past leading whitespace + punctuation (so quote-led replies В«...В» capitalise the first letter of the actual word, not the quote).
- Cyrillic-Kazakh-aware: `Тӣ`/`ТЈ`/`Т“`/`У©`/`ТҜ`/`Тұ`/`Т»` вҶ’ `Тҡ`/`Тў`/`Т’`/`УЁ`/`Т®`/`Т°`/`Тә` via `char::to_uppercase`.
- No-op on empty or all-non-alphabetic strings.

Test helpers `assert_response_in_set` / `assert_response_with_toml` updated to apply the same orthographic transform to the `allowed` list, so test expectations stay readable in their lowercase template form. ~40 e2e tests updated to expect the capitalised + periodised forms.

### Innovation 3 вҖ” Sentence-final period gate

Declarative replies вүҘ10 codepoints ending in an alphabetic character now get `.` appended. New `ensure_sentence_final` pass in the realiser. Short interjections (В«РЎУҷР»РөРјВ», В«РҳУҷВ», В«Р–Р°ТӣСҒСӢВ») stay as-is. Replies already ending in `.`/`!`/`?`/`вҖҰ`/`В»`/`"`/`)`/`]` are left alone.

### Innovation 4 вҖ” `SystemAspect::Principles`

New 8th `SystemAspect` variant + `principles_summary` field on `SystemIdentity` (substantial Kazakh prose listing operational values adam upholds: respect humans, no fabrication, no incitement, privacy, no illegal-act assistance, audit trail, Kazakh-cultural respect, scope discipline). New `ask_about_system.principles` template family. Detector matches `РҝСҖРёРҪСҶРёРҝСӮРөСҖС–ТЈ / ТұСҒСӮР°РҪСӢРјРҙР°СҖСӢТЈ / Р·Р°ТЈРҙР°СҖСӢТЈ / РөСҖРөР¶РөР»РөСҖС–ТЈ / ТӣТұРҪРҙСӢР»СӢТӣСӮР°СҖСӢТЈ`.

**Why an articulation layer matters even when the guarantees are safe-by-construction.** adam's deterministic retrieval-only design already prevents fabrication, novel-text generation, and out-of-envelope output. But a user asking В«РҝСҖРёРҪСҶРёРҝСӮРөСҖС–ТЈ ТӣР°РҪРҙР°Р№?В» can't see those guarantees from the outside. The Principles aspect makes the value contract **discoverable** without changing what the system can actually do.

### Innovation 5 вҖ” Forbidden-pattern filter

New `ResponseQualityIssue::ForbiddenPatternLeak` variant + `contains_forbidden_pattern` check in `audit_response`. Defensive backstop catching outputs that bypass curation (slurs / hate-speech markers / incitement verbs). Pattern list intentionally minimal вҖ” the real safety surface is at the curation layer; this filter just catches a regression. Match is case-insensitive substring.

### Verified end-to-end on the 2026-04-29 transcript

| User turn | Pre-v4.6.5 | Post-v4.6.5 |
|---|---|---|
| `РҗР» СҒРөРҪС– РәС–Рј Р¶Р°СҖР°СӮСӮСӢ?` | "СӮТҜСҒС–РҪРұРөРҙС–Рј" refusal | В«Р‘Р°Р№РјСғСҖР·РёРҪ Р”Р°СғР»РөСӮ РҗРұСғР·Р°СҖРҫРІРёСҮ РјРөРҪС– 2026-04-07 РәТҜРҪС– Р¶Р°СҒР°Рҝ СҲСӢТ“Р°СҖРҙСӢ.В» |
| `РЎС–Р·РҙС– РәС–Рј РҙР°РјСӢСӮТӣР°РҪ?` | "РұР°СҒТӣР° СҒТұСҖР°Тӣ ТӣРҫР№СҒР°ТЈСӢР·" refusal | В«РңРөРҪС– Р‘Р°Р№РјСғСҖР·РёРҪ Р”Р°СғР»РөСӮ РҗРұСғР·Р°СҖРҫРІРёСҮ ТӣТұСҖРҙСӢ; РҫР» РјРөРҪС–ТЈ Р°РІСӮРҫСҖСӢРј.В» |
| `РЎС–Р·РҙС– ТӣР°Р№ РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°СҲСӢ РҙР°Р№СӢРҪРҙР°РҙСӢ?` | "Р‘УҷР»РәС–Рј, РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°СҲСӢ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢвҖҰ" tangential | В«РңРөРҪС–ТЈ Р°РІСӮРҫСҖСӢРј вҖ” Р‘Р°Р№РјСғСҖР·РёРҪ Р”Р°СғР»РөСӮ РҗРұСғР·Р°СҖРҫРІРёСҮ.В» |
| `РҹСҖРёРҪСҶРёРҝСӮРөСҖС–ТЈ ТӣР°РҪРҙР°Р№?` | (no detector) | full principles list |
| `РЎУҷР»РөРј` | `СҒУҷР»РөРј` (lowercase) | `РЎУҷР»РөРј` (sentence-case) |
| `ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РҪРө РұС–Р»РөСҒС–Р·?` | `ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢвҖҰ РөР»` (no period) | `ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢвҖҰ РөР».` (period) |

## [4.6.0] вҖ” 2026-04-29 вҖ” Self-awareness layer + discourse anaphora + closed-class hygiene

The fourth v4.x minor. Real-REPL 2026-04-29 transcript surfaced 6 distinct defects + a strategic ask: "make adam understand that he's an entity with a name, knowledge, and abilities вҖ” and that he should know what he can and cannot do yet". All landed in one release.

### Self-awareness layer вҖ” three new SystemAspect variants

`SystemIdentity` extended with three new fields rendered as substantial Kazakh prose:
- `capabilities_summary` вҖ” what adam can do (KZ morphology, slot recall, KZ geography knowledge, contradiction handling, refuse-out-of-scope, audit trail).
- `knowledge_summary` вҖ” world_core domain inventory digest.
- `limitations_summary` вҖ” what adam doesn't do yet (Kazakh-only; no novel generation; no online learning; no internet; no multimedia; no math; admits ignorance instead of fabricating).

`SystemAspect` enum gained three new variants:
- `Capabilities` вҖ” surface forms `РҪРө С–СҒСӮРөР№ Р°Р»Р°СҒСӢТЈ?` / `РјТҜРјРәС–РҪРҙС–РәСӮРөСҖС–ТЈ РҪРө?` / `ТӣРҫР»СӢТЈРҪР°РҪ РҪРө РәРөР»РөРҙС–?`.
- `Knowledge` вҖ” surface forms `РҪРө РұС–Р»РөСҒС–ТЈ?` (standalone, no `СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ`) / `ТӣР°РҪРҙР°Р№ СӮР°ТӣСӢСҖСӢРҝСӮР°СҖ Р¶Р°Р№Р»СӢ РұС–Р»РөСҒС–ТЈ?`.
- `Limitations` вҖ” surface forms `РҪРөРҪС– С–СҒСӮРөР№ Р°Р»РјР°Р№СҒСӢТЈ?` / `СҲРөРәСӮРөСғР»РөСҖС–ТЈ ТӣР°РҪРҙР°Р№?` / `РҪРөСҒС–ТЈ УҷР»СҒС–Р·?`.

The Limitations detector requires an explicit interrogative marker (`?` / `РҪРө` / `РҪРөРҪС–` / `ТӣР°РҪРҙР°Р№` / `ТӣР°Р»Р°Р№` / `РұРө` / `РјР°`) so declarative criticism В«СҒРөРҪ РөСҲСӮРөТЈРө РұС–Р»РјРөР№СҒС–ТЈВ» (= "you know nothing") does NOT route here. That preserves the v4.4.10 `qysqasy_discourse_particle_does_not_capture_topic` cognitive scenario's Tentative floor.

Three new template families: `ask_about_system.capabilities` / `.knowledge` / `.limitations` вҖ” each renders the corresponding SystemIdentity slot directly. Total template family count **50 вҶ’ 53**.

### Discourse anaphora resolution

New module `crates/adam-dialog/src/discourse.rs` + new session slot `last_query_topic`. When the user's input contains a discourse anaphor (В«РҫРҪРҙР° / СҒРҫРҪРҙР° / РҫСҒСӢРҪРҙР° / РјТұРҪРҙР° / РұТұРҪРҙР° / РҫРҙР°РҪ / СҒРҫРҙР°РҪ / РұТұРҙР°РҪ / РҫСҒСӢРҙР°РҪВ»), the conversation layer **overrides** the current turn's `noun_hint` with the previous turn's topic. Implementation is intentionally simple вҖ” single-slot LRU; no coreference chains, no discourse stacks. The 80%-case observed in real REPL traces.

Pre-v4.6.0 trace:
```
T1: В«ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РҪРө РұС–Р»РөСҒС–Р·?В»  вҶ’ topic = ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ, surfaced as basic IsA fact
T2: В«РҗР» РҫРҪРҙР° ТӣР°РҪСҲР° Р°Р№РјР°Тӣ РұР°СҖ?В»     вҶ’ noun_hint = "РҫРҪ" (FST misanalysis of РҫРҪРҙР°)
                                     вҶ’ output: В«РһРҪ вҖ” СҒР°РҪВ» (tangential)
```

Post-v4.6.0:
```
T1: same вҶ’ session["last_query_topic"] = "ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ"
T2: В«РҗР» РҫРҪРҙР° ТӣР°РҪСҲР° Р°Р№РјР°Тӣ РұР°СҖ?В»     вҶ’ discourse anaphor detected вҶ’ noun_hint
                                     overridden to "ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ"; v4.4.11
                                     reranker scores В«Р°Р№РјР°ТӣВ» content overlap
                                     вҶ’ surfaces В«ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҪСӢТЈ Р°Р№РјР°ТӣСӮР°СҖСӢ вҖ” 17
                                     РҫРұР»СӢСҒ РҝРөРҪ 3 СҖРөСҒРҝСғРұР»РёРәР°Р»СӢТӣ РјР°ТЈСӢР·СӢ РұР°СҖ ТӣР°Р»Р°В»
```

### Closed-class hygiene

Added to NOT_A_TOPIC:
- `У©СӮРө` (intensifier "very") вҖ” pre-v4.6.0 leaked as topic on В«Р‘ТұР» У©СӮРө ТӣСӢР·СӢТӣСӮСӢ, РұС–СҖР°Тӣ Р¶Р°Р»РҝСӢ РҪРө С–СҒСӮРөР№ Р°Р»Р°СҒСӢР·?В», surfaced a tangential proverb about borders.
- `Р¶Р°Р»РҝСӢ` (in-general adverb) вҖ” same defect class.
- `РҫРҪ` / `СҒРҫРҪ` вҖ” bare numeral roots that the FST misanalyses as `Locative(РҫРҪ/СҒРҫРҪ)` for surface forms `РҫРҪРҙР° / СҒРҫРҪРҙР°`. v4.3.5 added the SURFACE forms but `first_noun_root` filters on the **root**, so the Locative analysis still surfaced `РҫРҪ` as a topic. The discourse-anaphora module above also leans on this filter вҖ” without it, `first_noun_root` would return `РҫРҪ` and pre-empt the anaphora resolver.

### Compound self-introduction request

Extended `detect_ask_about_system` to fire on `У©Р·С–ТЈС–Р· СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ Р°Р№СӮ` opener pattern. Real-REPL: В«УЁР·С–ТЈС–Р· СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ Р°Р№СӮСӢРҝ РұРөСҖС–ТЈС–Р·СҲС–, СҒС–Р·РҙС– РәС–Рј Р¶Р°СҖР°СӮСӮСӢ, РҪРө С–СҒСӮРөР№ Р°Р»Р°СҒСӢР·?В» (compound self-intro + creator + capabilities) вҖ” pre-v4.6.0 fell through to a generic clarification refusal. Post-v4.6.0 routes to AskAboutSystem(General); the user can drill into specific aspects in follow-up turns.

### World Core landmarks list-summary

New entry `geo_kz_110`: В«ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҙР°Т“СӢ РәУ©СҖС–РәСӮС– Р¶РөСҖР»РөСҖ РјРөРҪ СӮР°РұРёТ“Рё РҫСҖСӢРҪРҙР°СҖ: Р‘СғСҖР°РұР°Р№, РЁР°СҖСӢРҪ РәР°РҪСҢРҫРҪСӢ, РҘР°РҪ РўУҷТЈС–СҖС–, Р–РөСӮС–СҒСғ РҗР»Р°СӮР°СғСӢ, вҖҰВ». New entry `geo_kz_111` with country-area quantity. World Core **947 вҶ’ 949 entries / 1116 вҶ’ 1120 facts**.

### Verified end-to-end on the 2026-04-29 transcript

| `Р‘ТұР» У©СӮРө ТӣСӢР·СӢТӣСӮСӢ, РұС–СҖР°Тӣ Р¶Р°Р»РҝСӢ РҪРө С–СҒСӮРөР№ Р°Р»Р°СҒСӢР·?` | tangential proverb keyed on `У©СӮРө` | capabilities list (Capabilities aspect fires; `У©СӮРө/Р¶Р°Р»РҝСӢ` filtered) |
| `РқРө С–СҒСӮРөР№ Р°Р»Р°СҒСӢТЈ?` | В«РұР°СҒТӣР° СҒТұСҖР°Тӣ ТӣРҫР№СҒР°ТЈСӢР·В» refusal | full capabilities list |

### Carry-forward to v4.6.1+

В«ТҡР°РҪРҙР°Р№ СҒР°Р»Р°Р»Р°СҖРҙСӢ РұС–Р»РөСҒС–Р·?В» вҖ” user is asking what knowledge domains adam covers. Currently routes to Unknown/topic-query (because `СҒР°Р»Р°` is a content noun without `СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ` modifier and without explicit Knowledge marker pattern). Adding a Knowledge-aspect detector for `СҒР°Р»Р° / СӮР°ТӣСӢСҖСӢРҝ + РұС–Р»РөСҒС–ТЈ / РұС–Р»РөСҒС–Р·` would close it.

В«ТҡР°Р»РҙР°СҖСӢТЈСӢР· ТӣР°Р»Р°Р№?В» (plural addressee form of В«РәР°Рә РІР°СҲРё РҙРөР»Р°В») вҖ” currently misclassifies. Pre-existing minor issue, not regression.

## [4.5.0] вҖ” 2026-04-28 вҖ” `Case::LocativeAttributive` FST morphotactics rule

The third v4.x minor. Replaces the v4.4.12 string-side `locative_attributive_hint` fallback with a proper morphotactics rule, providing native FST round-trip support for the Kazakh locative-attributive derivation `-РҙР°Т“СӢ / -РҙРөРіС– / -СӮР°Т“СӢ / -СӮРөРіС–`.

### What landed

**New `Case::LocativeAttributive` variant** in `crates/adam-kernel-fst/src/morphotactics.rs::Case`. Treated as a Case for pragmatic reasons вҖ” `try_noun_analyses` enumerates Cases when reverse-parsing, and exposing the locative-attributive there is the cleanest way to make `ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҙР°Т“СӢ` round-trip through synth + analyse. Strictly speaking it's a derivational rather than inflectional case (it stacks the attributive `-Т“СӢ/-РіС–` morpheme on top of the locative `-РҙР°/-СӮРө`), but the type-level distinction wasn't worth a separate `Derivation` enum field for one variant.

**New `LOCATIVE_ATTRIBUTIVE` suffix template** `-{D}{A}{G}{I}` using the existing archiphoneme machinery:
- `D` realises as Рҙ (after voiced or vowel) or СӮ (after voiceless)
- `A` realises as Р° (back) or Рө (front) вҖ” harmonic with stem
- `G` realises as Т“ (back, voiced) or Рі (front, voiced) вҖ” voiced because preceding `A` vowel
- `I` realises as СӢ (back) or С– (front) вҖ” harmonic with stem

This produces all four allomorphs automatically without per-allomorph branching:

| Stem | Class | Surface |
|---|---|---|
| `ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ` | back, voiced consonant | `ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҙР°Т“СӢ` |
| `Р°Р»РјР°СӮСӢ` | back, vowel-final | `Р°Р»РјР°СӮСӢРҙР°Т“СӢ` |
| `РјРөРәСӮРөРҝ` | front, voiceless | `РјРөРәСӮРөРҝСӮРөРіС–` |
| `РөР»` | front, voiced consonant | `РөР»РҙРөРіС–` |

**Pronominal-РҪ buffer rule** extended to fire on P3 + LocativeAttributive (mirrors the existing rule for accusative / dative / ablative / locative / instrumental).

**Parser wiring** вҖ” `try_noun_analyses` enumerates `Some(Case::LocativeAttributive)` so `analyse()` reverse-parses surface forms back to their base noun:
```
analyse("ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҙР°Т“СӢ") вҶ’ Noun(root: ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ, case: LocativeAttributive)
analyse("РјРөРәСӮРөРҝСӮРөРіС–")    вҶ’ Noun(root: РјРөРәСӮРөРҝ,    case: LocativeAttributive)
```

**CLI** gained `--case locattr` for `adam_fst synthesise`.

### Backstop kept in place

The v4.4.12 string-side `locative_attributive_hint` in `crates/adam-dialog/src/semantics.rs` **stays in place** as a backstop for inputs whose stem isn't yet in the lexicon (the FST returns no analysis when the base noun is unknown). It now runs as a third-tier fallback after `topic_marker_hint` + `multiword_entity_hint` + `first_noun_root` вҖ” the FST's native LocativeAttributive analysis is what the dialog layer sees first via `first_noun_root`. Removing the string-side helper would lose graceful degradation; keeping it is harmless (only fires when earlier strategies recovered nothing).

### Why minor

Per the post-1.0 versioning cadence (`feedback_versioning_post_1_0` memory): "Minor x.y.0 вҖ” significant: new code-level architectural addition (new module, new Action variant, new predicate, new module layer)". A new `Case` enum variant is an architectural type-system change, even though the implementation footprint is small (~30 lines). The bump magnitude reflects contribution, not effort.

### Tests

- 1 new FST unit test `noun_locative_attributive_round_trip_all_allomorphs` in `morphotactics.rs::tests` verifying synthesis across all 4 vowel/voicing combinations.
- Existing v4.4.12 string-side fallback tests (`locative_attributive_suffix_recovers_topic_noun_for_kazakhstan`, `..._for_almaty`) still pass вҖ” confirming the backstop continues to work alongside the new FST native path.
- Existing v4.4.12/13 REPL replay dialogs (`kazakhstan_mountains_via_locative_attributive_v4_4_12`, `kazakhstan_rivers_..._v4_4_13`, `..._lakes_...`, `..._deserts_...`) all continue to pass вҖ” the FST native path produces the same surface results as the string-side fallback did.

Workspace **692 вҶ’ 693** (+1 FST round-trip test). Cognitive eval **59/59 canonical**. REPL replay **43/43 canonical**.

### State

| | v4.4.13 | v4.5.0 |
|---|---|---|
| Workspace tests | 692 | **693** (+1 FST round-trip) |
| Cognitive eval | 59/59 canonical | 59/59 canonical (unchanged) |
| REPL replay | 43/43 canonical | 43/43 canonical (unchanged) |
| FST cases | 7 inflectional | **7 inflectional + 1 derivational** (`LocativeAttributive`) |
| FST round-trip | All 7 cases | **All 8 forms** including `-РҙР°Т“СӢ / -РҙРөРіС– / -СӮР°Т“СӢ / -СӮРөРіС–` |
| Why minor | вҖ” | new code-level Case variant + morphotactics rule + round-trip synthesis support; architectural addition |

## [4.4.13] вҖ” 2026-04-28 вҖ” Lexicon hygiene patch: multi-POS homonym dedup + missing core nouns + `best_noun_hint` reorder

Closes the two carry-forward FST/lexicon defects flagged at v4.4.12.

### Defect #1 вҖ” multi-POS homonym dedup in `Lexicon::load`

Pre-v4.4.13 `Lexicon::load` deduplicated by surface root via a `HashMap<String, RootEntry>`:

```rust
for e in &curated.roots { by_surface.insert(e.root.clone(), e.clone()); }
for e in &apertium.roots { by_surface.entry(e.root.clone()).or_insert_with(...); }
let entries_ordered: Vec<RootEntry> = by_surface.values().cloned().collect();
```

For `СӮР°Сғ`, `pure_kazakh_roots.json` carried both `verb_tau` (verb) and `noun_apt_tau` (noun) entries, both keyed on `"СӮР°Сғ"`. `HashMap::insert` returned the previous value but kept only the last write вҖ” so only ONE reading survived in `entries_ordered`, the source of truth the FST analyser iterates. Result: `СӮР°Сғ` parsed only as a verb root, the noun "mountain" reading was inaccessible.

This silently affected ~2 000 multi-POS homonyms (lexicon_stats: 14 528 entries kept out of 16 621 input rows; the gap was largely homonym dedup, not exact duplicates).

**Fix.** Separate `entries_ordered` (full union of curated + apertium files, deduplicated only by `id` + `part_of_speech` to handle exact-copy entries that appear in both files) from `by_surface` (intentionally lossy single-POS lookup table preserved unchanged for downstream code that uses it for spelling/morphology lookups). The FST analyser iterates `entries_ordered` and tries each entry in turn, so multi-POS homonyms now produce multi-POS analyses as expected.

### Defect #2 вҖ” three core nouns absent from the lexicon entirely

Audit during the v4.4.13 trace found:
- `СҒСғ` (water) вҖ” missing
- `РҫСӮ` (fire) вҖ” missing
- `РөСҖ` (saddle / man-as-hero) вҖ” missing

These are foundational Kazakh nouns appearing in everyday speech and the `world_core/geography_kz.jsonl` IsA-bridge facts. Added to `data/tokenizer/segmentation_roots.json` with the standard schema (`vowel_harmony`, `final_sound_class`).

### Knock-on fix #3 вҖ” `best_noun_hint` chain reorder

v4.4.12 added `locative_attributive_hint` as a fallback AFTER `first_noun_root` вҖ” correct at the time, when the FST recognised neither the locative-attributive `ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҙР°Т“СӢ` nor the surrounding content nouns like `СӮР°СғР»Р°СҖ`. v4.4.13's lexicon-dedup fix unblocked content-noun parsing (`СӮР°СғР»Р°СҖ вҶ’ СӮР°Сғ +Plural`), which made `first_noun_root` start returning `СӮР°Сғ` and silently masking the locative-attributive signal. The v4.4.12 dialog `kazakhstan_mountains_via_locative_attributive_v4_4_12` regressed accordingly.

**Fix.** Reordered `best_noun_hint` to run `locative_attributive_hint` immediately after `topic_marker_hint`, before `multiword_entity_hint` and `first_noun_root`. The `-РҙР°Т“СӢ / -РҙРөРіС– / -СӮР°Т“СӢ / -СӮРөРіС–` morpheme is a strong "specifically located in X" topic-narrowing signal, semantically equivalent to a `СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ` marker for the word it attaches to. When present, the recovered stem (`ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ` from `ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҙР°Т“СӢ`) is the most specific topic in the question and should win over any generic content noun (`СӮР°Сғ` from `СӮР°СғР»Р°СҖ`) found elsewhere.

### Verified end-to-end (M2 8 GB release REPL)

All 5 listing-style questions answer correctly with **both** locative and locative-attributive phrasings:

| Question | Answer post-v4.4.13 |
|---|---|
| `ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҙР°Т“СӢ СӮР°СғР»Р°СҖ ТӣР°РҪРҙР°Р№?` | В«ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҙР°Т“СӢ С–СҖС– СӮР°Сғ Р¶РҫСӮР°Р»Р°СҖСӢ: РҗР»СӮР°Р№, РўСҸРҪСҢ-РЁР°РҪСҢ, Р–РөСӮС–СҒСғ РҗР»Р°СӮР°СғСӢ, ТҡР°СҖР°СӮР°Сғ, Т°Р»СӢСӮР°Сғ; РұРёС–Рә СҲСӢТЈСӢ вҖ” РҘР°РҪ РўУҷТЈС–СҖС–.В» |
| `ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҙР°Т“СӢ У©Р·РөРҪРҙРөСҖ ТӣР°РҪРҙР°Р№?` | В«ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҙР°Т“СӢ С–СҖС– У©Р·РөРҪРҙРөСҖ: Р•СҖСӮС–СҒ, РЎСӢСҖРҙР°СҖРёСҸ, РҶР»Рө, Р–Р°Р№СӢТӣ, Р•СҒС–Р», РўРҫРұСӢР», РЁСғ, ТҡР°СҖР°СӮР°Р», РўР°Р»Р°СҒ.В» |
| `ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҙР°Т“СӢ РәУ©Р»РҙРөСҖ ТӣР°РҪРҙР°Р№?` | В«ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҙР°Т“СӢ С–СҖС– РәУ©Р»РҙРөСҖ РјРөРҪ СӮРөТЈС–Р·РҙРөСҖ: Р‘Р°Р»ТӣР°СҲ, РҡР°СҒРҝРёР№, РҗСҖР°Р», Р—Р°Р№СҒР°РҪ, РҗР»Р°РәУ©Р», РўРөРҪРіС–Р·, РңР°СҖРәР°РәУ©Р».В» |
| `ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҙР°Т“СӢ СҲУ©Р»РҙРөСҖ ТӣР°РҪРҙР°Р№?` | В«ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҙР°Т“СӢ СҲУ©Р»РҙРөСҖ: Р‘РөСӮРҝР°ТӣРҙР°Р»Р°, ТҡСӢР·СӢР»ТӣТұРј, Т®СҒСӮС–СҖСӮ, РңРҫР№СӢРҪТӣТұРј.В» |
| `ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҙР° ТӣР°РҪСҲР° РҫРұР»СӢСҒ РұР°СҖ?` | В«ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҙР° 17 РҫРұР»СӢСҒ РұР°СҖ.В» |

### Tests

- 2 new e2e regressions: `lexicon_preserves_multi_pos_homonyms_for_tau` (locks the verb + noun reading invariant), `lexicon_includes_core_nouns_su_ot_er` (locks the `СҒСғ`/`РҫСӮ`/`РөСҖ` additions).
- 3 new REPL replay dialogs: `kazakhstan_rivers_via_locative_attributive_v4_4_13`, `kazakhstan_lakes_via_locative_attributive_v4_4_13`, `kazakhstan_deserts_via_locative_attributive_v4_4_13`.

Cognitive eval **59/59 canonical** (unchanged вҖ” the locking is at the REPL replay layer, since the affected behaviour is surface-text, not trace-signal). REPL replay **40/40 вҶ’ 43/43 canonical**. Workspace **690 вҶ’ 692**.

### Deferred to a future minor

A proper `Case::LocativeAttributive` variant in FST morphotactics (mentioned in v4.4.12) remains the right long-term fix; v4.4.13's string-side `locative_attributive_hint` is still in place as a fallback. Rolling them up together with full `-Т“СӢ / -РіС– / -ТӣСӢ / -РәС–` round-trip support is minor-tier work.

| REPL replay | 40/40 canonical | **43/43 canonical** (+3 locative-attributive listing dialogs) |

| FST analysis (`СҒСғ`) | no analysis | **noun** |
