## [4.0.6] вҖ” 2026-04-23 вҖ” Narrow attributive blocklist in `is_closed_class`

Continuing the noise-elimination axis from v4.0.5. That patch shipped the **rightmost-subject** fix in `temporal_after`; spot-check then surfaced a distinct noise class the rightmost scan couldn't catch: attributive `-Р»СӢТӣ / -Р»С–Рә / -Рё` adjective-derivations that the FST tags as bare nouns. When the real NP head got consumed in the ablative slot, the attributive modifier was the *only* remaining nominative candidate before the postposition вҖ” so both left-to-right and right-to-left scans picked it.

### Fix

Narrow blocklist added directly to `is_closed_class`. Nine roots, each spotted on the committed v4.0.5 runtime:

| root | gloss | v4.0.5 After-fact count |
|---|---|---:|
| `РҙТҜРҪРёРөР¶ТҜР·С–Р»С–Рә` | worldwide | 41 |
| `ТұР·Р°Тӣ` | long (duration) | 9 |
| `УҷСҒРәРөСҖРё` | military | 6 |
| `СҸРҙСҖРҫР»СӢТӣ` | nuclear | 3 |
| `СӮСҖРҫРҝРёРәР°Р»СӢТӣ` | tropical | 2 |
| `Р¶СӢРҪСӢСҒСӮСӢТӣ` | sexual / gender | 2 |
| `Р¶Р°СҖСӮ` | truncated stem of В«Р¶Р°СҖСӮСӢВ» (half) | 3 |
| `Р°СҖР°СҒСӢРҪРҙР°Т“` | possessive-locative fragment | 4 |
| `СӮР°Т“СӢ` | "again / also" (adverb tagged as noun) | 3 |

Applies globally via `is_closed_class`, not just to `temporal_after`. Every pattern matcher that consults the helper (all 11) now rejects these as subjects *and* as head-noun objects in the few places where head-nouns are scanned.

### Important non-inclusions

Three roots deliberately **excluded** from the blocklist:

- `ТұР»СӮ-Р°Р·Р°СӮСӮСӢТӣ` (national-liberation) вҖ” real compound noun; legitimate subject in some world_core / IsA contexts.
- `РұРөР»РіС–` (sign), `СҒР°РҪ` (number), `Р¶ТұСҖСӮ` (folk) вҖ” all legitimate nouns.

The regression test `is_closed_class_covers_v4_0_6_attributives` asserts both: the 9 blocked roots fail, and the 4 legitimate-noun roots pass through.

### Measured effect

Re-ran extract + reasoner pipeline on the committed 200 k-sample runtime. All 9 attributive / fragment roots verified absent from `facts.json` as subjects (spot-checked per root: 0 occurrences each).

| | v4.0.5 | v4.0.6 | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| facts.json total | 13 887 | **13 703** | **вҲ’184** |
| After facts | 269 | **219** | **вҲ’50** (primary target вҖ” attributive adjectives) |
| DoesTo facts | 9 289 | 9 192 | **вҲ’97** (cross-matcher cleanup) |
| GoesTo facts | 1 617 | 1 595 | **вҲ’22** |
| LivesIn facts | 292 | 289 | вҲ’3 |
| RelatedTo facts | 1 467 | 1 458 | вҲ’9 |
| IsA facts | 525 | 524 | вҲ’1 |
| PartOf facts | 116 | 115 | вҲ’1 |
| Has facts | 226 | 225 | вҲ’1 |
| HasQuantity / InDomain / Causes | 40 / 24 / 22 | 40 / 24 / 22 | unchanged |

The blocklist applies globally via `is_closed_class`, so gains span every matcher that consults the helper вҖ” not just `temporal_after`. The DoesTo `вҲ’97` and GoesTo `вҲ’22` drops are the attributive-as-agent cases that the Codex review didn't surface on the After side: e.g. В«РҙТҜРҪРёРөР¶ТҜР·С–Р»С–Рә ТҜСҖРҙС–СҒ X-РҪС– СӮСғРҙСӢСҖР°РҙСӢВ» вҶ’ pre-v4.0.6 extracted as `(РҙТҜРҪРёРөР¶ТҜР·С–Р»С–Рә, DoesTo, X)`.

Per-rule derivation deltas:

| rule | v4.0.5 | v4.0.6 | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| R1_is_a_transitivity | 361 | 361 | 0 |
| R2_has_inheritance | 422 | 417 | вҲ’5 |
| R3_has_inheritance_via_part_of | 26 | 26 | 0 |
| R5_shared_is_a_target | 5 437 | 5 437 | 0 |
| R6_lives_in_via_part_of | 36 | 36 | 0 |
| R7_goes_to_via_part_of | 297 | 300 | +3 |
| R8_after_transitivity | 714 | 734 | +20 |
| **total derivations** | **7 293** | **7 311** | **+18** |

Small R7 and R8 *increases* are structural: with fewer attributive-subjected base facts, the reasoner's `seen_triples` dedup set is smaller, so a few chains that were previously short-circuited now fire freely. The new derivations use clean content-noun subjects where the noisy attributive ones were blocked.

Graph: 3 287 вҶ’ **3 284** nodes (вҲ’3), 12 439 вҶ’ **12 308** edges (вҲ’131). Most-connected content nouns: **Р°РҙР°Рј (288), Р¶РөСҖ (218), РҙТҜРҪРёРө (207), ТӣР°Р·Р°Тӣ (201), Р¶СӢР» (151)**.

### Tests

**463 passing** (+1 from v4.0.5): `is_closed_class_covers_v4_0_6_attributives`.

### Scope discipline

One helper, nine new entries, one regression test. No rule changes, no world_core changes, no extractor-logic changes. Sequential 1вҶ’9 cadence preserved (v4.0.5 вҶ’ v4.0.6 вҶ’ v4.0.7).

### What's next (v4.0.7)

Axes continue to rotate per `project_v4_direction`:
- **World Core** expansion in an existing / new domain
- **New reasoning rule** R9 candidate
- More **noise elimination** if new classes surface
- **Corpus** вҖ” long-horizon FST-synthetic data generation

---

## [4.0.5] вҖ” 2026-04-23 вҖ” Noise elimination in `temporal_after` subject selector

Continuing the v4.0.x curriculum вҖ” one axis per patch, this one is **noise elimination**. Rotating axes keep new rule leverage (v4.0.4 R8) from compounding existing matcher precision gaps.

### Root cause

v4.0.4 spot-check showed R8 producing derivations like `(СӮСҖРҫРҝРёРәР°Р»СӢТӣ, After, Р°Р№СӢРҝ)` вҖ” the chain was mathematically sound but inherited a noisy base fact `(СӮСҖРҫРҝРёРәР°Р»СӢТӣ, After, Р¶СӢР»)` from `temporal_after`. Source: В«Р•РіРөСҖ **СӮСҖРҫРҝРёРәР°Р»СӢТӣ** РҫСҖРјР°РҪРҙР°СҖ РҫСҒСӢРҪРҙР°Р№ ТӣР°СҖТӣСӢРҪРјРөРҪ Р¶РҫР№СӢР»Р° РұРөСҖСҒРө, 80-40 **Р¶СӢР»РҙР°РҪ** СҒРҫТЈ Р¶РөСҖ РұРөСӮС–РҪРҙРө РјТұРҪРҙР°Р№ РҫСҖРјР°РҪРҙР°СҖ ТӣР°Р»РјР°Р№РҙСӢВ». The matcher scanned left-to-right and grabbed the first bare-nominative noun (`СӮСҖРҫРҝРёРәР°Р»СӢТӣ`, an attributive modifier) as the subject, when Kazakh SOV structure places the NP head (`РҫСҖРјР°РҪРҙР°СҖ`) closer to the verb.

### Fix

Two tiny guards in `temporal_after`:

1. **Rightmost subject, not leftmost** (`(0..post_idx-1).rev().find_map(...)` instead of `(0..post_idx-1).find_map(...)`). In Kazakh SOV the subject-NP head sits closer to the ablative / verb, so the rightmost bare-nominative candidate before the postposition is the real subject.
2. **3-char minimum root length** (mirrors the guards already present in `locative_lives_in` and `dative_goes_to`). Blocks any truncated FST stems that might leak through.

### Measured effect

Re-ran extract + reasoner pipeline on the same committed 200 k-sample runtime:

| | v4.0.4 | v4.0.5 | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| facts.json total | 13 889 | **13 887** | вҲ’2 |
| After facts | 269 | 269 | 0 (net) |
| R8_after_transitivity | 789 | **714** | **вҲ’75 (вҲ’9.5 %)** |
| total derivations | 7 368 | **7 293** | **вҲ’75** |
| graph nodes | 3 286 | 3 287 | +1 |
| graph edges | 12 447 | 12 439 | вҲ’8 |

The rightmost-subject fix correctly narrowed the `(СӮСҖРҫРҝРёРәР°Р»СӢТӣ, After, *)` class (from 2 вҶ’ 1 base facts, with R8 transitive multiplication eliminated). Most of the 75 blocked R8 derivations came from that transitive multiplication.

### Honest observation вҖ” adjacent noise class identified

The spot-check surfaced a **different** noise class still active at v4.0.5: attributive `-Р»СӢТӣ / -Р»С–Рә / -Рё` adjective-derivations that the FST tags as nouns. Top offender: **В«РҙТҜРҪРёРөР¶ТҜР·С–Р»С–РәВ»** (worldwide) вҖ” 41 `After` facts in the committed runtime, typically from patterns like В«Р‘С–СҖС–РҪСҲС– РҙТҜРҪРёРөР¶ТҜР·С–Р»С–Рә СҒРҫТ“СӢСҒСӮР°РҪ РәРөР№С–РҪ...В» where the REAL subject is elided (implicit event) and the grab-the-attributive heuristic still wins even with rightmost-scan because the head noun (`СҒРҫТ“СӢСҒ`) sits in the ablative slot, consumed as the object.

Also seen: `СҸРҙСҖРҫР»СӢТӣ` (nuclear, Г—3), `УҷСҒРәРөСҖРё` (military, Г—6), `ТұР»СӮ-Р°Р·Р°СӮСӮСӢТӣ` (national-liberation, Г—3), `Р¶СӢРҪСӢСҒСӮСӢТӣ` (sexual / gender, Г—2), `ТұР·Р°Тӣ` (long, Г—9).

Fixing this requires a different tool: a narrow **attributive blocklist** for known -Р»СӢТӣ/-Рё adjective-acting roots. Queued for the next noise-elimination patch to keep v4.0.5 single-concern per the cadence rule.

### Curated temporal chains preserved

The 6 clean seasonal / daytime R8 closures from v4.0.4 are invariant under the rightmost-scan change вҖ” they pass through a single-subject-candidate path where left-to-right and right-to-left identify the same token:

| subject | After | object |
|---|---|---|
| РәТҜР· | After | РәУ©РәСӮРөРј |
| ТӣСӢСҒ | After | Р¶Р°Р· |
| ТӣСӢСҒ | After | РәУ©РәСӮРөРј |
| СӮТҜРҪ | After | СӮТҜСҒ |
| СӮТҜРҪ | After | СӮР°ТЈ |
| РәРөСҲ | After | СӮР°ТЈ |

### Tests

**462 passing** (+1 from v4.0.4): new `temporal_after_picks_rightmost_subject_not_attributive` uses `ТӣР°Р·Р°Тӣ С…Р°Р»СӢТӣ Р¶СӢР»РҙР°РҪ СҒРҫТЈ У©Р·РіРөСҖРөРҙС–` to verify that:
- The matcher picks `С…Р°Р»СӢТӣ` (head of the NP), not `ТӣР°Р·Р°Тӣ` (attributive).
- Object stays `Р¶СӢР»` (ablative reference point).

Existing `temporal_after_extracts_noon_after_morning` continues to pass вҖ” the single-subject-candidate case is invariant under direction change.

### Scope discipline

One concern per patch. Only `temporal_after` subject selector touched, no rule changes, no world_core changes. Sequential 1вҶ’9 cadence preserved (v4.0.4 вҶ’ v4.0.5 вҶ’ v4.0.6).

### What's next

Axes continue to rotate:
- **noise elimination**: narrow attributive-adjectival blocklist (`РҙТҜРҪРёРөР¶ТҜР·С–Р»С–Рә`, `СҸРҙСҖРҫР»СӢТӣ`, `УҷСҒРәРөСҖРё`, `ТұР»СӮ-Р°Р·Р°СӮСӮСӢТӣ`, `Р¶СӢРҪСӢСҒСӮСӢТӣ`, `ТұР·Р°Тӣ`) вҖ” would knock out ~58 base After facts + their transitive R8 multiplications. Targeted v4.0.6.
- **reasoning rules**: R9 candidate ideas вҖ” After anti-symmetry curator warning (R4-style), or Causes-transitivity with type guards.
- **world_core / Lexicon**: gap `РҫСҖРјР°РҪ` (forest) surfaced by this patch's test authoring вҖ” new entries for nature domain.
- **corpus**: FST-synthetic clean data generation remains the long-horizon axis.

---

## [4.0.4] вҖ” 2026-04-23 вҖ” R8 After-transitivity rule (new reasoning rule)

One concern per patch вҖ” this one adds a new rule to the forward-chaining reasoner: **`R8_after_transitivity`**.

### Motivation

`After` is a strict partial order вҖ” mathematically the cleanest predicate to make transitive. The rule:

> `A After B вҲ§ B After C вҹ№ A After C`

mirrors `R1_is_a_transitivity` in structure but applies to temporal ordering instead of taxonomic subsumption. No semantic overreach risk вҖ” unlike Has-transitivity (mixes ownership with composition) or LivesIn-transitivity (mixes residence with physical inclusion), temporal order is a mathematical relation that transits cleanly.

This aligns with the v4.x direction captured in memory `project_v4_direction`: **intelligent thinking via simple math** вҖ” add rules with clear mathematical structure, not heuristics.

### Curated temporal chains now close automatically

`data/world_core/time.jsonl` asserts the primitive links:

```
time_011  СӮТҜСҒ After СӮР°ТЈ
time_012  РәРөСҲ After СӮТҜСҒ
time_013  СӮТҜРҪ After РәРөСҲ
time_015  Р¶Р°Р· After РәУ©РәСӮРөРј
time_016  РәТҜР· After Р¶Р°Р·
time_017  ТӣСӢСҒ After РәТҜР·
```

R8 closes these into their full transitive closure. Measured on the live runtime (re-run of `run_reasoner` over the v4.0.3 `facts.json`, which is byte-identical вҖ” only derivations change):

```
R1_is_a_transitivity:           361 вҶ’ 361   unchanged
R2_has_inheritance:             422 вҶ’ 422   unchanged
R3_has_inheritance_via_part_of:  26 вҶ’  26   unchanged
R5_shared_is_a_target:        5 437 вҶ’ 5 437 unchanged
R6_lives_in_via_part_of:         36 вҶ’  36   unchanged
R7_goes_to_via_part_of:         297 вҶ’ 297   unchanged
R8_after_transitivity:            вҖ” вҶ’  789  NEW
в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
total derivations:            6 579 вҶ’ 7 368 (+789, +12 %)
```

Curated-only R8 output (world_core-to-world_core chains) вҖ” 6 clean temporal derivations:

| subject | `After` | object |
|---|---|---|
| РәТҜР· | After | РәУ©РәСӮРөРј |
| ТӣСӢСҒ | After | Р¶Р°Р· |
| ТӣСӢСҒ | After | РәУ©РәСӮРөРј |
| СӮТҜРҪ | After | СӮТҜСҒ |
| СӮТҜРҪ | After | СӮР°ТЈ |
| РәРөСҲ | After | СӮР°ТЈ |

Every step independently verifiable: e.g. В«ТӣСӢСҒ after РәУ©РәСӮРөРјВ» вҶ’ chain `[time_017, time_016, time_015]` via `(ТӣСӢСҒ, After, РәТҜР·) вҲ§ (РәТҜР·, After, Р¶Р°Р·) вҲ§ (Р¶Р°Р·, After, РәУ©РәСӮРөРј)`.

### Known upstream noise observation

The remaining 783 R8 derivations inherit the precision profile of the **existing** text-source After extractor вҖ” which pulls noisy subject roots like `СӮСҖРҫРҝРёРәР°Р»СӢТӣ` (adjective surface mis-parsed) from `kazakh_textbooks_pack.json` and `wikipedia_kz_pack.json`. R8 transitively multiplies that noise.

Impact on users: **zero** вҖ” both `adam_chat --safe` (v4.0.3) and `adam_demo` Part 4 default (v4.0.2) already filter to fully-curated source chains, so a text-source R8 derivation can never reach the dialog path. The noisy rows only exist in raw `data/retrieval/derived_facts.json` for audit.

The upstream cause вҖ” `temporal_after` pattern matcher's subject selection lacking the content-noun / type-guard logic that `locative_lives_in` / `dative_goes_to` already have вҖ” is a known target for a subsequent patch under the "noise elimination" axis.

### Tests

**461 passing** (+5 from v4.0.3): five new reasoner unit tests вҖ”
- `r8_derives_after_transitivity` (single-chain positive)
- `r8_respects_tautology_guard`
- `r8_does_not_fire_without_chain`
- `r8_dedupes_against_existing_fact`
- `r8_chains_across_iterations` вҖ” four-season full closure: РәУ©РәСӮРөРј вҶ’ Р¶Р°Р· вҶ’ РәТҜР· вҶ’ ТӣСӢСҒ produces (РәТҜР·, РәУ©РәСӮРөРј), (ТӣСӢСҒ, Р¶Р°Р·), (ТӣСӢСҒ, РәУ©РәСӮРөРј).

### Scope discipline

One rule, one patch. No pattern-matcher changes, no world_core changes, no extraction changes. Sequential 1вҶ’9 per-integer versioning preserved (v4.0.3 вҶ’ v4.0.4 вҶ’ v4.0.5).

### What's next

The four knowledge-enrichment axes continue:
- **reasoning rules**: R8 landed. Future candidates вҖ” R9 After-anti-symmetry curator warning, R-style rules over other predicates.
- **world_core**: expansion and new domains remain the main scaling axis.
- **noise elimination**: `temporal_after` subject guards as a dedicated patch (Codex-style precision audit).
- **corpus**: clean synthetic-data generation via FST is the direction per `project_v4_direction`.

Each patch is one step. Nine steps per major keeps the pace measured.

---

## [4.0.3] вҖ” 2026-04-23 вҖ” `adam_chat --safe` investor REPL mode

Continuing the Codex v4.0.0 hand-off. v4.0.2 landed the curated-only filter
in `adam_demo` Part 4; v4.0.3 extends the same guarantee to the live
`adam_chat` REPL via an opt-in `--safe` flag. Same design philosophy:
filter is a **view**, not an extract-time change.

### API additions

- **New pub fn** [`adam_reasoning::reasoner::derivation_is_fully_curated`](crates/adam-reasoning/src/reasoner.rs): the classifier moves out of `adam_demo` and into the reasoning crate so any dialog / inspection path can share it. `adam_demo` now re-exports via `use` вҖ” zero duplication.
- **New field** `Conversation.curated_only_reasoning: bool` + builder `with_curated_only_reasoning(enabled: bool)`.
- **`inject_reasoning_chain` change**: when the flag is on, candidate derivations must pass `derivation_is_fully_curated` before the subject-first / object-fallback match. Fails through to retrieval (or plain Unknown) otherwise. Backwards-compatible when the flag is `false` (default).

### CLI

- `adam_chat --safe` (alias `--curated-only`) flips the flag at startup and logs `adam-chat: --safe mode вҖ” reasoning chains filtered to fully-curated (world_core-only) source chains`.

### Measured вҖ” real REPL output

```
$ adam_chat --once "Р°РұР°Р№ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РұС–СҖРҙРөТЈРө Р°Р№СӮ"
# Default (v4.0.2 baseline вҖ” cites text-chain derivation):
Р°РұР°Р№ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РјСӢРҪР°РҙР°Р№ РұР°Р№Р»Р°РҪСӢСҒ Р°РҪСӢТӣСӮР°РҙСӢРј: ТӣРҫСҖСӢСӮСӢРҪРҙСӢ: Р°РұР°Р№ вҖ” С…Р°Р»СӢТӣ
# "Abai is a people." Text-extracted chain, Codex-flagged.

$ adam_chat --safe --once "Р°РұР°Р№ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РұС–СҖРҙРөТЈРө Р°Р№СӮ"
# v4.0.3 safe mode (cites world_core-only R1 transitivity):
Р°РұР°Р№ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РјСӢРҪР°РҙР°Р№ РұР°Р№Р»Р°РҪСӢСҒ Р°РҪСӢТӣСӮР°РҙСӢРј: ТӣРҫСҖСӢСӮСӢРҪРҙСӢ: Р°РұР°Р№ вҖ” РјР°РјР°РҪ
# "Abai is a specialist." Derived from world_core/kz_literature:
#   lit_001  (Р°РұР°Р№ IsA Р°ТӣСӢРҪ)
#   lit_029  (Р°ТӣСӢРҪ IsA РјР°РјР°РҪ)
# R1_is_a_transitivity. Fully human-reviewed source chain.
```

This is the exact shape of an investor-safe pitch: every derivation goes through named reviewer + named rule, and a text-corpus chain that *might* be true never reaches the user.

### Tests

**456 passing** (+7 from v4.0.2):
- 5 new unit tests in `adam_reasoning::reasoner` covering the moved helper (curated / mixed / text-only / empty / prefix-boundary).
- 2 new e2e tests in `adam-dialog/tests/end_to_end.rs`:
  - `safe_mode_rejects_text_source_chain_derivations` вҖ” default chats on text-chain; `--safe` refuses.
  - `safe_mode_still_cites_fully_curated_derivations` вҖ” `--safe` continues firing on world_core chains (guards against overreach).

### Scope discipline

Exactly one feature вҖ” the `--safe` chat flag + shared helper. No matcher changes, no extraction changes, no docs migration beyond the directly-affected files. v4.0.x cadence preserved at single-integer patch steps (v4.0.2 вҶ’ v4.0.3 вҶ’ v4.0.4).

### What's next (v4.0.4)

- Surface `--safe` mode in a refreshed README demo transcript alongside the default mode, so investors see both sides from one page.
- Continue the Codex precision-hygiene hand-off with the next small, single-concern patch.

---

## [4.0.2] вҖ” 2026-04-23 вҖ” Investor-safe demo mode: curated-source-chain filter in `adam_demo`

Small, focused patch continuing the Codex v4.0.0 hand-off. Same design philosophy as v4.0.1: a surgical fix at the right layer, not a broad architecture change.

### The problem

Codex flagged three specific chains in `adam_demo` Part 4 as public-demo-unsafe:

- `Р°РұР°Р№ is_a С…Р°Р»СӢТӣ`  вҖ” R1 transitivity via `wikipedia_kz_pack.json`
- `РөТЈРұРөРә вҖ” У©Р·РөРҪ`  вҖ” R1 transitivity chaining through metaphorical corpus text
- `СӮРҫРҝСӢСҖР°Тӣ goes_to РҙРөРҪРө`  вҖ” R7 chain with cross-domain semantic collision

Each was technically correct вҖ” FST-typed, fully `rule_id`-tagged, `source_chain` non-empty вҖ” but the **text-extracted** facts feeding the chains had weaker semantic integrity than the hand-reviewed World Core entries they competed with. An investor looking at the demo would read "adam: Abai is a people" and stop listening.

### The fix

A demo-layer filter, not an extract-layer filter (recall preserved for downstream consumers):

- New helper [`derivation_is_fully_curated`](crates/adam-dialog/src/bin/adam_demo.rs): returns `true` iff **every** `FactSource` in the derivation's `source_chain` has a `pack` starting with `"world_core/"`. Empty chains fail closed.
- `adam_demo` Part 4 per-rule-representative picker now requires this predicate by default.
- `--all-derivations` dev flag bypasses the filter for audit / debugging.

### Measured effect

| | before | after | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| Total derivations loaded | 6 579 | 6 579 | unchanged (filter is a view) |
| **Fully-curated chains** | 4 753 | **4 753 (72.2 %)** | reported in Part 4 header |
| Rules represented in Part 4 default | all 4 firing | 4 curated rules (R1, R2, R3, R5) | R6/R7 have вүҘ1 curated chain but subject-uniqueness guard filters in current artefact |
| Codex-flagged bad chains | shown as R1/R7 examples | **eliminated** | all three had text-pack `source_chain` entries |

Post-v4.0.2 Part 4 per-rule-representative output (real):

```
  [R1_is_a_transitivity]    СӮТҜР»РәС– --is_a--> Р¶Р°РҪСғР°СҖ           (world_core/animals)
  [R2_has_inheritance]      ТӣТұСҒ Р¶РҫР»СӢ --has--> Р¶ТұР»РҙСӢР·        (world_core/astronomy)
  [R3_has_inheritance_via_part_of]  ТӣР°Р·Р°Тӣ --has--> РұР°СҒ      (world_core/colors + body_parts)
  [R5_shared_is_a_target]   РұУ©Р»Сғ --related_to--> РәУ©РұРөР№СӮСғ    (world_core/numbers)
```

The R5 sample вҖ” В«division related to multiplicationВ» via shared-math-operation IsA target вҖ” is an unusually clean investor pitch for math-driven reasoning. Every claim traceable to a named reviewer (`shaman` at this stage).

### Tests

**449 passing** (+5 from v4.0.1): 5 unit tests for the `derivation_is_fully_curated` helper covering curated / mixed / text-only / empty / prefix-boundary cases.

### Scope discipline

Part 4 `adam_demo` only. `adam_chat --safe` equivalent is deferred to v4.0.3 вҖ” keeping each patch single-concern.

### What's next (v4.0.3)

- Wire the same curated-only filter into `adam_chat` behind a `--safe` / `--curated-only` flag. Dialog's `inject_reasoning_chain` currently scans *all* derivations; the filter belongs there too for investor-safe chat mode.

---

## [4.0.1] вҖ” 2026-04-23 вҖ” В«РқРөР»С–РәСӮРөРҪ?В» REPL path fix (Codex v4.0.0 review follow-up)

Small, focused patch closing the bug Codex caught in the v4.0.0 audit:
**В«РқРөР»С–РәСӮРөРҪ?В» was still routed through `StatementOfLocation { city: "РқРөР»С–Рә" }`**
in the real `adam_chat` REPL despite the v3.9.5 `NOT_A_TOPIC` work. The
unit-level test `not_a_topic_covers_v3_9_5_additions` was passing, but it
exercised `first_noun_root` / `content_roots` вҖ” not the ablative-scan path
in `detect_statement_of_location`. Two separate code paths, one covered,
one not.

### Root cause

FST analysis of "РҪРөР»С–РәСӮРөРҪ" returns three parses (in deterministic order):

```
noun: РҪРөР»С–Рә +Ablative
noun: РҪРөР»С–Рә +Singular +Ablative
noun: РҪРөР»С–РәСӮРөРҪ
```

`detect_statement_of_location` scans parses for the first `Case::Ablative`
noun and returns its root as a city. Before v4.0.1 that was the stripped
stem `РҪРөР»С–Рә`, and `NOT_A_TOPIC` only contained the full surface form
`РҪРөР»С–РәСӮРөРҪ` вҖ” so the skip-check matched nothing. Result: REPL reply
"РқРөР»С–РәСӮРө СӮТұСҖР°СҒСӢР· РұР°" ("Do you live in РқРөР»С–Рә?") to "РқРөР»С–РәСӮРөРҪ?".

### Fix

1. Add `РҪРөР»С–Рә` (the FST-stripped stem) to `NOT_A_TOPIC` in
   `adam_dialog::semantics`.
2. `detect_statement_of_location` now **skips any noun whose root is in
   `NOT_A_TOPIC`** at the case-scan step вҖ” same filter the content-root
   path already uses. Covers ablative, locative, and `Locative+P1Sg`
   branches uniformly.
3. Mirror `РҪРөР»С–Рә` in `adam_reasoning::patterns::is_closed_class` for
   cross-layer consistency.

### Regression test вҖ” REPL path, not unit filter

New e2e test `nelikten_is_not_absorbed_as_city` in
`crates/adam-dialog/tests/end_to_end.rs` exercises the exact
`Conversation::turn(...)` path Codex reproduced:

- Turn 1: "РјРөРҪ ТҡРҫСҒСӮР°РҪР°Р№РҙР°РјСӢРҪ" вҶ’ `session.city = "ТҡРҫСҒСӮР°РҪР°Р№"` (baseline
  works).
- Turn 2: "РқРөР»С–РәСӮРөРҪ?" вҖ” `session.city` MUST remain "ТҡРҫСҒСӮР°РҪР°Р№" (not be
  overwritten with "РқРөР»С–Рә").
- Turn 3: fresh `Conversation`, bare "РқРөР»С–РәСӮРөРҪ?" вҖ” `session.city` MUST
  stay absent.

Pre-v4.0.1 Turn 2 failed the assertion. Post-v4.0.1 it passes.

### Verified in REPL

```
$ cargo run -p adam-dialog --bin adam_chat -- --once "РқРөР»С–РәСӮРөРҪ?"
СӮТҜСҒС–РҪРұРөРҙС–Рј
```

(Previously: В«РқРөР»С–РәСӮРө СӮТұСҖР°СҒСӢР· РұР°В».)

### Tests

**444 passing** (+1 from v4.0.0): the new e2e regression.

### Housekeeping

- `cargo clean` reclaimed **37.4 GiB** of `target/` artefacts (9.7 GiB
  вҶ’ 42 GiB free). Standing procedure вҖ” run before every release when
  free space drops below ~15 GiB.
- Project direction captured in `project_v4_direction` memory: develop
  on M2 8 GB without investors; synthetic FST-generated data + strict
  Kazakh grammar rules as the path to "intelligent reasoning via simple
  math"; sequential 1вҶ’9 per-integer versioning (v4.0.1 вҶ’ v4.0.2 вҶ’
  v4.0.3 вҖҰ), no half-step jumps.

### What's next

- v4.0.2: reasoning-demo precision polish вҖ” filter out the remaining
  Codex-flagged noise chains (`Р°РұР°Р№ is_a С…Р°Р»СӢТӣ`, `РөТЈРұРөРә вҖ” У©Р·РөРҪ`,
  `СӮРҫРҝСӢСҖР°Тӣ goes_to РҙРөРҪРө`) at the demo layer, not at extraction.
- v4.0.3+: continued patch-level hardening ahead of the next minor
  (v4.1.0) which will carry architectural additions.

---

## [4.0.0] вҖ” 2026-04-23 вҖ” World Core 500+ expansion + contradiction immune system + Codex-review response

**Major release.** Codex's v3.9.5 review correctly flagged that reasoning was scaling faster than precision вҖ” В«РұР°Р»Р° lives_in РәТҜРҪ Р¶ТҜР№РөСҒС–В», В«(РөРіРөСҖ, DoesTo, РіР°Р·)В», В«(Р¶Р°Р»Т“Р°, GoesTo, Р¶РөСҖ)В», В«РөТЈРұРөРә вҖ” У©Р·РөРҪВ» were real chains in `facts.json`, not hypothetical. v4.0.0 addresses both ends of the problem: (1) **expand curated knowledge** to outweigh extracted noise via sheer IsA density, and (2) **add a contradiction immune system** that categorically refuses the classes of false derivations Codex exhibited.

### 1. World Core expansion вҖ” 200 вҶ’ 507 entries / 270 вҶ’ 601 facts

Seven new domains authored by `shaman` at `approved` status:

| new domain | entries | facts | content |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| `colors.jsonl` | 37 | 38 | primary colors, Kazakh traditional (Р°Р»СӮСӢРҪ / РәТҜРјС–СҒ / РәУ©РәСҒРҫТЈСӢ / РұРҫР· / ТӣТұР»Р°), nature-color associations, kemperqosaq (rainbow), vision-domain |
| `numbers.jsonl` | 45 | 54 | digits 1вҖ“10, tens 20вҖ“90, 100 / 1000 / million / billion, basic operations (ТӣРҫСҒСғ, Р°Р»Сғ, РәУ©РұРөР№СӮСғ, РұУ©Р»Сғ), even/odd, time units |
| `kz_literature.jsonl` | 60 | 69 | 18 authors (РҗРұР°Р№, РңР°С…Р°РјРұРөСӮ, Р–Р°РјРұСӢР», РңР°Т“Р¶Р°РҪ, РЁУҷРәУҷСҖС–Рј, Р«РұСӢСҖР°Р№, РңТұС…СӮР°СҖ, РһР»Р¶Р°СҒ, РңТұТӣР°Т“Р°Р»Рё, вҖҰ), 7 works (ТҡР°СҖР° СҒУ©Р·РҙРөСҖ, РҗРұР°Р№ Р¶РҫР»СӢ, ТҡРҫРұСӢР»Р°РҪРҙСӢ, ТҡСӢР· Р–С–РұРөРә, РҗР»РҝР°РјСӢСҒ, ТҡРҫР·СӢ РҡУ©СҖРҝРөСҲ, вҖҰ), 12 genres (У©Р»РөТЈ, РҝРҫСҚРјР°, СҖРҫРјР°РҪ, УҷТЈРіС–РјРө, РөСҖСӮРөРіС–, РҙР°СҒСӮР°РҪ, Р¶СӢСҖ, вҖҰ), structure (СҲСғРјР°Тӣ, СӮР°СҖРјР°Тӣ, ТұР№ТӣР°СҒ, РҝРҫСҚР·РёСҸ, РҝСҖРҫР·Р°) |
| `food.jsonl` | 50 | 50 | bread (РҪР°РҪ, РұР°СғСӢСҖСҒР°Тӣ, СҲРөР»РҝРөРә), meat (ТӣРҫР№/СҒРёСӢСҖ/Р¶СӢР»ТӣСӢ/СӮТҜР№Рө РөСӮС–), Kazakh traditional dishes (РұРөСҲРұР°СҖРјР°Тӣ, РәСғСӢСҖРҙР°Рә, СҒРҫСҖРҝР°), dairy (СҒТҜСӮ, ТӣСӢРјСӢР·, СҲТұРұР°СӮ, Р°Р№СҖР°РҪ, ТӣР°Р№РјР°Тӣ, С–СҖС–РјСҲС–Рә, ТӣТұСҖСӮ, СҒР°СҖСӢ РјР°Р№), fruits, vegetables, grains, beverages |
| `clothing.jsonl` | 35 | 35 | general (РәУ©Р№Р»РөРә, СҲР°Р»РұР°СҖ, СӮРҫРҪ, С–СҲС–Рә), Kazakh traditional (СҲР°РҝР°РҪ, РәР°РјР·РҫР», СҒУҷСғРәРөР»Рө, РәРёРјРөСҲРөРә), headwear (СӮР°ТӣРёСҸ, ТӣР°Р»РҝР°Тӣ, РұУ©СҖС–Рә, РҫСҖР°РјР°Р»), footwear (РјУҷСҒС–, РәРөРұС–СҒ, РөСӮС–Рә, РұУҷСӮРөТЈРәРө), fabrics, jewellery |
| `proverbs.jsonl` | 40 | 43 | curated mР°ТӣР°Р» with embedded IsA / Causes / RelatedTo facts вҖ” В«Р•ТЈРұРөРә СӮТҜРұС– вҖ” РұРөСҖРөРәРөВ», В«Р‘С–Р»С–Рј вҖ” ТӣСғР°СӮВ», В«РўС–Р» вҖ” ТұР»СӮ РұРөР»РіС–СҒС–В», В«Р‘С–СҖР»С–Рә вҖ” РұР°Р№Р»СӢТӣВ», В«РҗРҪР° вҖ” РұР°Р»Р°РҪСӢТЈ Р°Р»Т“Р°СҲТӣСӢ ТұСҒСӮР°Р·СӢВ» |
| `animals.jsonl` | 40 | 42 | predators (ТӣР°СҒТӣСӢСҖ, СӮТҜР»РәС–, Р°СҖСӢСҒСӮР°РҪ, Р¶РҫР»РұР°СҖСӢСҒ, Р°СҺ), game (ТӣРҫСҸРҪ, СӮРёС–РҪ, РұТұТ“СӢ, РәРёС–Рә, Р°СҖТӣР°СҖ), birds (РұТҜСҖРәС–СӮ, СӮСӢСҖРҪР°, Р°ТӣТӣСғ, ТҜРәС–, СӮР°СғСӢТӣ, ТӣР°Р·, ТҜР№СҖРөРә, СӮРҫСҖТ“Р°Р№, ТӣР°СҖТ“Р°, СҒР°СғСӢСҒТӣР°РҪ), domestic (СҒРёСӢСҖ, ТӣРҫР№, Р¶СӢР»ТӣСӢ, СӮТҜР№Рө, РёСӮ, РјСӢСҒСӢТӣ), insects, taxonomy (СҒТҜСӮТӣРҫСҖРөРәСӮС–, Р¶СӢСҖСӮТӣСӢСҲ, Р¶УҷРҪРҙС–Рә, Р·РҫРҫР»РҫРіРёСҸ, РҫСҖРҪРёСӮРҫР»РҫРіРёСҸ) |

Plus existing 6 domains carried forward. **Total: 507 entries / 601 facts across 13 domains.** All 0-rejection on `validate_world_core`.

### 2. Contradiction immune system

Codex's v3.9.5 review surfaced specific false chains in `facts.json`. Each fix is targeted and test-enforced:

- **R6/R7 astronomical-target guard** (new in reasoner.rs): `is_astronomical_object` helper (РәТҜРҪ / Р°Р№ / Р¶РөСҖ / РјР°СҖСҒ / СҲРҫР»РҝР°РҪ / РјРөСҖРәСғСҖРёР№ / СҺРҝРёСӮРөСҖ / СҒР°СӮСғСҖРҪ / СғСҖР°РҪ / РҪРөРҝСӮСғРҪ / РәТҜРҪ Р¶ТҜР№РөСҒС– / РіР°Р»Р°РәСӮРёРәР° / ТӣТұСҒ Р¶РҫР»СӢ / Т“Р°Р»Р°РјСҲР°СҖ / Р¶ТұР»РҙСӢР· / Р°СҒРҝР°РҪ РҙРөРҪРөСҒС– / РјРөСӮРөРҫСҖ / Р°СӮРјРҫСҒС„РөСҖР° / РҫСҖРұРёСӮР°). R6 and R7 now refuse derivations where the target `C` is astronomical-scale. Closes `(РұР°Р»Р°, LivesIn, Р¶РөСҖ) + (Р¶РөСҖ, PartOf, РәТҜРҪ Р¶ТҜР№РөСҒС–) вҹ№ (РұР°Р»Р°, LivesIn, РәТҜРҪ Р¶ТҜР№РөСҒС–)` вҖ” the homonymous В«Р¶РөСҖВ» (both "ground" and "Earth") cross-domain leak.
- **Object-side 3-char minimum** (locative_lives_in, dative_goes_to): mirrors the subject-side guard from v3.8.5. Closes `(РұР°Р»Р°, LivesIn, Т“Р°РҪ)` where the FST emitted a `-Т“Р°РҪ` participle tail as a standalone root, and analogous `-СӢРҪ / -С–РҪ / -ТӣР°РҪ / -СҒСӢРҪ` fragments.
- **`is_closed_class` expansion** (patterns.rs, 20+ new entries):
  - conjunctions: `РөРіРөСҖ` / `Р°Р»Р°Р№РҙР°` / `РұС–СҖР°Тӣ` / `РҙРөРіРөРҪРјРөРҪ` / `СҒРҫРҪРҙСӢТӣСӮР°РҪ` / `РҙРөРјРөРә` / `СҸТ“РҪРё` / `УҷР№СӮСҒРө` / `У©Р№СӮРәРөРҪС–` / `СҒРҫРҪРҙР°` / `СҒРҫРҪСӢРјРөРҪ` вҖ” closes `(РөРіРөСҖ, DoesTo, РіР°Р·)` ("if" as subject);
  - adverbial oblique stems: `Р¶Р°Р»Т“Р°` / `СӮРөРә` / `ТӣР°РҪР°` / `Т“Р°РҪР°` вҖ” closes `(Р¶Р°Р»Т“Р°, GoesTo, Р¶РөСҖ)`;
  - fragment-suffix standalones: `Т“Р°РҪ` / `РіРөРҪ` / `ТӣР°РҪ` / `РәРөРҪ` / `СӢРҪ` / `С–РҪ` / `СҒСӢРҪ` / `СҒС–РҪ` вҖ” defence in depth alongside the 3-char minimum above.

Three new regression tests: `r6_refuses_astronomical_derived_target`, `r6_still_fires_for_country_target`, `r7_refuses_astronomical_derived_target`. The "still fires for country" test is the key one вҖ” the guard must NOT block legitimate `(person, LivesIn, city) + (city, PartOf, country) вҹ№ (person, LivesIn, country)` chains.

### 3. Measured impact at T4_200k

| | v3.9.5 | v4.0.0 | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| facts.json (total) | 13 771 | **13 889** | **+118** |
| curated (HumanApproved) | 270 | **601** | **+331** (Г—2.2) |
| extracted (Grammar) | 13 501 | 13 288 | вҲ’213 (filter noise removal) |
| graph nodes | 3 151 | **3 286** | **+135** |
| graph edges | 12 317 | **12 447** | **+130** |
| **derivations** | **2 058** | **6 579** | **+4 521 (Г—3.2)** |
| predicate coverage | 11/11 | 11/11 | preserved |

Per-predicate fact counts вҖ” World Core drives structural gains, filters trim noise:

| predicate | v3.9.5 | v4.0.0 | delta | driver |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---|
| **IsA** | 294 | **525** | **+231** | world_core breadth (13 domains вҶ’ many IsA) |
| RelatedTo | 1 446 | 1 467 | +21 | |
| **Has** | 207 | 226 | +19 | world_core body_parts/society |
| **PartOf** | 105 | 116 | +11 | |
| **HasQuantity** | 29 | 40 | +11 | numbers.jsonl (year has 12 months etc) |
| **Causes** | 6 | **22** | **+16** | proverbs + biology entries |
| **InDomain** | 5 | **24** | **+19** | kz_literature genres + sciences |
| After | 269 | 265 | вҲ’4 | |
| LivesIn | 313 | 292 | **вҲ’21** | 3-char object filter + fragment-suffix closed-class |
| GoesTo | 1 692 | 1 617 | **вҲ’75** | same filters |
| DoesTo | 9 399 | 9 295 | **вҲ’104** | same filters |

Per-rule derivation counts вҖ” **R5 and R1 jump from denser IsA graph; R6/R7 shrink as astronomical-guard blocks false chains**:

| rule | v3.9.5 | v4.0.0 | delta | reason |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---|
| R1_is_a_transitivity | 114 | **361** | **+247 (Г—3.2)** | more IsA chains (world_core 507 entries вҶ’ 525 IsA facts вҶ’ dense A-IsA-B-IsA-C paths) |
| R2_has_inheritance | 253 | **422** | **+169** | denser IsA base |
| R3_has_inheritance_via_part_of | 15 | **26** | **+11** | body_parts PartOf chains |
| **R5_shared_is_a_target** | 933 | **5 437** | **+4 504 (Г—5.8)** | 525 IsA facts form exponentially more sibling pairs sharing a target |
| **R6_lives_in_via_part_of** | 103 | **36** | **вҲ’67** | **astronomical-target guard** blocked 67 false chains (`РұР°Р»Р° lives_in РәТҜРҪ Р¶ТҜР№РөСҒС–` class) |
| **R7_goes_to_via_part_of** | 640 | **297** | **вҲ’343** | **same guard** вҖ” biggest precision win |

R6/R7 shrinkage is a **net precision gain**: the 67+343 = 410 blocked derivations were chains where the target was an astronomical-scale object (homonymous В«Р¶РөСҖВ» bridging "ground" to "Earth", or adverbial "Р¶Р°Р»Т“Р°" chaining through "Р¶РөСҖ" to "РәТҜРҪ Р¶ТҜР№РөСҒС–"). Genuine `(person, LivesIn, city) + (city, PartOf, country) вҹ№ (person, LivesIn, country)` chains still fire, as verified by `r6_still_fires_for_country_target` test.

Most-connected graph nodes (content-noun focus preserved): **Р°РҙР°Рј (289), Р¶РөСҖ (219), РҙТҜРҪРёРө (211), ТӣР°Р·Р°Тӣ (201), Р°СӮ (150)**.

### Tests

**443 passing** (+3 from v3.9.5): `r6_refuses_astronomical_derived_target`, `r6_still_fires_for_country_target`, `r7_refuses_astronomical_derived_target`.

### Architectural stance вҖ” Codex response

Codex's full recommendation included a Typed World Model with EntityType ontology, Fact Promotion Pipeline with `Candidate`/`Verified`/`HumanApproved` tiers, and a dedicated Contradiction/Absurdity Filter. v4.0.0 ships **targeted** fixes вҖ” the filters are hand-coded blocklists rather than type-derived constraints вҖ” because every hand-coded filter is test-enforceable today without gating on a larger ontology design. The Typed World Model is a v5.x target; v4.x adds incremental curated-knowledge breadth and domain-specific blocklists as new noise classes surface.

The architectural stance stays: **adam is not competing with ChatGPT on breadth.** v4.0.0's 507 curated entries + 600+ typed facts, each with a named reviewer, are measured against GPT-4's "correct answer" baseline: correct per-claim, traceable per-source, zero hallucination by construction. This is a substrate for sovereign-AI / education / government domains, not a general-purpose Q&A model.

### What's next (v4.5+)

- v4.5: investor-ready MVP вҖ” scripted 3-minute `adam_demo_v4` narrative pulling **exclusively** from `HumanApproved` tier; screencast recording; one-page pitch deck.
- `validate_world_core` integrated into `scripts/validate_foundation.sh` as a CI gate (currently standalone).
- v5.x: Typed World Model вҖ” EntityType ontology per root, type-constrained rule firing, Fact Promotion Pipeline with `Candidate`/`Verified` tiers that require evidence from multiple sources before promotion.

---

## [3.9.5] вҖ” 2026-04-23 вҖ” World Core expansion + R6/R7 rules + dialog closed-class sync

**Continuation of the v3.9.0 architectural direction.** Three independent improvements, each a small and contained delta:

### 1. World Core expansion вҖ” 80 вҶ’ 200 entries / 126 вҶ’ 270 facts

Three new domains added by `shaman` at `approved` review status:

| domain | entries | facts |
|---|---:|---:|
| astronomy | 30 | 41 |
| time | 20 | 38 |
| geography_kz | 30 | 47 |
| **biology_basic** (v3.9.5) | **40** | **41** |
| **body_parts** (v3.9.5) | **40** | **55** |
| **society** (v3.9.5) | **40** | **48** |
| **TOTAL** | **200** | **270** |

Content:
- `biology_basic.jsonl` вҖ” human, mammals, common animals (РёСӮ, РјСӢСҒСӢТӣ, Р¶СӢР»ТӣСӢ, ТӣРҫР№, СҒРёСӢСҖ, СӮТҜР№Рө, РөСҲРәС–, ТӣТұСҒ, РұР°Р»СӢТӣ), plants (Р°Т“Р°СҲ, СҲУ©Рҝ, РіТҜР», РұРёРҙР°Р№), terrain (РҫСҖРјР°РҪ, РҙР°Р»Р°, СҲУ©Р», СӮР°Р№РіР°, СӮР°Сғ, У©Р·РөРҪ, РәУ©Р», СӮРөТЈС–Р·, РјТұС…РёСӮ), cell / organism, biology + ecology as sciences. 41 typed facts.
- `body_parts.jsonl` вҖ” head parts (РұР°СҒ, РұРөСӮ, РәУ©Р·, ТӣТұР»Р°Тӣ, РјТұСҖСӢРҪ, Р°СғСӢР·, СӮС–Р», СӮС–СҒ, СҲР°СҲ), limbs (РјРҫР№СӢРҪ, РёСӢТӣ, ТӣРҫР», СҒР°СғСҒР°Тӣ, Р°Р»Р°ТӣР°РҪ, СӮС–Р·Рө, Р°СҸТӣ, СӮР°РұР°РҪ), internal organs (Р¶ТҜСҖРөРә, У©РәРҝРө, РұР°СғСӢСҖ, РұТҜР№СҖРөРә, Р°СҒТӣР°Р·Р°РҪ, РјРё, ТӣР°РҪ), structural (СҒТҜР№РөРә, РөСӮ, СӮРөСҖС–, Р¶ТҜР№РәРө), 6 quantified claims (В«Р°РҙР°РјРҙР° РөРәС– РәУ©Р· РұР°СҖВ» etc), anatomy as a science. 55 typed facts.
- `society.jsonl` вҖ” state / law / constitution / parliament / president / courts, family (Р°РҪР°, УҷРәРө, РұР°Р»Р°), education (РјРөРәСӮРөРҝ, СғРҪРёРІРөСҖСҒРёСӮРөСӮ, РҫТӣСғСҲСӢ, РјТұТ“Р°Р»С–Рј, СҒСӮСғРҙРөРҪСӮ, РәС–СӮР°Рҝ, РәС–СӮР°РҝС…Р°РҪР°), sciences (РјР°СӮРөРјР°СӮРёРәР°, С„РёР·РёРәР°, С…РёРјРёСҸ, СӮР°СҖРёС…), economy (Р°ТӣСҲР°, СӮРөТЈРіРө, РөТЈРұРөРә), professions (РҙУҷСҖС–РіРөСҖ, РјТұТ“Р°Р»С–Рј, РёРҪР¶РөРҪРөСҖ, Р·Р°ТЈРіРөСҖ), dimension (РҙС–РҪ, РјУҷРҙРөРҪРёРөСӮ, СӮС–Р», ТӣР°Р·Р°Тӣ СӮС–Р»С–, С…Р°Р»СӢТӣ). 48 typed facts.

All 200 entries pass `validate_world_core` with 0 rejections / 0 Kazakh-purity warnings.

### 2. R6 + R7 вҖ” spatial + directional transitivity rules

Two new forward-chaining rules, activated now that v3.8.0's verb-root fix gave `LivesIn` / `GoesTo` real data AND v3.9.0's `geography_kz.jsonl` curated a `city PartOf country` chain:

| rule | formula | example |
|---|---|---|
| **R6_lives_in_via_part_of** | `A LivesIn B вҲ§ B PartOf C вҹ№ A LivesIn C` | (Р”УҷСғР»РөСӮ, LivesIn, ТҡРҫСҒСӮР°РҪР°Р№) вҲ§ (ТҡРҫСҒСӮР°РҪР°Р№, PartOf, ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ) вҹ№ (Р”УҷСғР»РөСӮ, LivesIn, ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ) |
| **R7_goes_to_via_part_of** | `A GoesTo B вҲ§ B PartOf C вҹ№ A GoesTo C` | (РҫР», GoesTo, РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢ) вҲ§ (РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢ, PartOf, ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ) вҹ№ (РҫР», GoesTo, ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ) |

Both emit `ConfidenceKind::RuleInferred` with a 2-source chain. Tautology-guarded (A вү  C). 6 new unit tests: `r6_derives_lives_in_via_part_of`, `r6_respects_tautology_guard`, `r6_does_not_fire_without_part_of_edge`, `r6_dedupes_against_existing_fact`, `r7_derives_goes_to_via_part_of`, `r7_respects_tautology_guard`.

Reasoner roster is now 5 active rules: R1 / R2 / R3 / R5 / R6 / R7 (6 total). R4 remains curator-warning only.

### 3. Dialog `NOT_A_TOPIC` sync вҖ” closes В«РқРөР»С–РәСӮРөРҪ вҶ’ РқРөР»С–РәСӮРө СӮТұСҖР°СҒСӢР· РұР°В» bug

v3.8.5 free-form REPL testing surfaced: user typing В«РқРөР»С–РәСӮРөРҪ?В» (В«why?В» вҖ” an interrogative) got reply В«РқРөР»С–РәСӮРө СӮТұСҖР°СҒСӢР· РұР°В» (В«Do you live in РқРөР»С–Рә?В»). The FST analysed В«РқРөР»С–РәСӮРөРҪВ» as `РқРөР»С–Рә` + ablative suffix (a valid morphological parse), and `adam-dialog::semantics::NOT_A_TOPIC` lacked the interrogative entries that `adam-reasoning::patterns::is_closed_class` had gained in v3.5.0+.

Fix: expanded `NOT_A_TOPIC` to mirror `is_closed_class` вҖ” added interrogatives (`РҪРөР»С–РәСӮРөРҪ`, `РҪРөРіРө`, `ТӣР°СҲР°РҪ`, `ТӣР°Р№РҙР°`, `ТӣР°РҪРҙР°Р№`, `РәС–Рј`, `РҪРө`, `ТӣР°Р№`, `ТӣР°РҪСҲР°`), demonstrative qualifiers (`РјТұРҪРҙР°Р№`, `СҒРҫРҪРҙР°Р№`, `РҫРҪРҙР°Р№`, `РјСӢРҪР°РҙР°Р№`, `СҒРҫРҪРҙР°Р№-Р°Тӣ`, `РәРөР№РұС–СҖ`, `У©Р·`, `У©Р·С–`, `РұС–СҖРҪРөСҲРө`, `РұР°СҖСҲР°`, `УҷСҖРұС–СҖ`, `УҷСҖ`, `РұС–СҖ`, `РәРөР№`), plus the comparison particle `СҒРёСҸТӣ` missing since v3.5.0.

Regression test `not_a_topic_covers_v3_9_5_additions` asserts every newly-added word is present AND that content nouns (РұР°Р»Р°, РәС–СӮР°Рҝ, РјРөРәСӮРөРҝ, ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ, Р¶РөСҖ) still pass through.

### Committed runtime delta

Measured on T4_200k after v3.9.5 extraction (`--bench-order --max-total 200000`):

| | v3.9.0 | v3.9.5 | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| facts.json (total) | 13 627 | **13 771** | **+144** |
| curated (world_core, HumanApproved) | 126 | **270** | **+144** (new domains) |
| extracted (text, Grammar) | 13 501 | 13 501 | 0 (text corpus unchanged) |
| graph nodes | 3 100 | **3 151** | **+51** |
| graph edges | 12 175 | **12 317** | **+142** |
| **derivations** | **704** | **2 058** | **+1 354 (Г—2.9)** |
| predicate coverage | 11/11 (100 %) | 11/11 (100 %) | preserved |

Per-predicate fact counts after v3.9.5:

| predicate | v3.9.0 | v3.9.5 | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| DoesTo | 9 399 | 9 399 | 0 |
| GoesTo | 1 692 | 1 692 | 0 |
| RelatedTo | 1 446 | 1 446 | 0 |
| LivesIn | 313 | 313 | 0 |
| **IsA** | 219 | **294** | **+75** (world_core biology/society/body_parts) |
| After | 269 | 269 | 0 |
| **Has** | 190 | **207** | **+17** |
| **PartOf** | 65 | **105** | **+40** (body_parts / biology / society chains) |
| **HasQuantity** | 29 | **35** | **+6** |
| **Causes** | 3 | **6** | **+3** (biology water/air entries) |
| **InDomain** | 2 | **5** | **+3** (biology/anatomy sciences) |

Per-rule derivation counts вҖ” **R6 and R7 fire for the first time**:

| rule | v3.9.0 | v3.9.5 | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| R1_is_a_transitivity | 42 | **114** | +72 |
| R2_has_inheritance | 173 | **253** | +80 |
| **R3_has_inheritance_via_part_of** | 0 | **15** | +15 (first real fire on curated chains) |
| R5_shared_is_a_target | 489 | **933** | +444 (Г—1.9) |
| **R6_lives_in_via_part_of** | 0 | **103** | +103 (NEW вҖ” v3.9.5) |
| **R7_goes_to_via_part_of** | 0 | **640** | +640 (NEW вҖ” v3.9.5) |

**R7 is the biggest single-rule contribution**: every one of the 1 692 extracted `GoesTo` facts whose destination is a city that curated `geography_kz` identifies as part of ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ (or РһСҖСӮР°Р»СӢТӣ РҗР·РёСҸ / Р•СғСҖР°Р·РёСҸ) now produces a derivation at the country level. This is exactly the "city-level facts + curated chain вҶ’ country-level conclusions" leverage the v3.9.5 plan targeted.

Most-connected graph nodes (content-noun focus preserved): **Р°РҙР°Рј (290), Р¶РөСҖ (221), РҙТҜРҪРёРө (210), ТӣР°Р·Р°Тӣ (200), Р°СӮ (156)**. В«Р°РҙР°РјВ» (human) remains central вҖ” a stronger semantic signal than any raw corpus statistic would give.

### Tests

**440 passing** (+7 from v3.9.0): 4 R6 regressions + 2 R7 regressions + 1 NOT_A_TOPIC sync test.

### Architectural status

At v3.9.5 adam has:
- **200 curated knowledge entries** вҶ’ 270 facts with full reviewer provenance
- **5 active forward-chaining rules** (R1, R2, R3, R5, R6, R7) вҖ” R6/R7 turn the clean v3.8.5-hardened LivesIn/GoesTo predicates into new derivations
- **11/11 predicate coverage** preserved from v3.9.0
- **Dialog intent layer synced with reasoning closed-class** вҖ” one single source of truth for В«what is a content nounВ»

Every curated fact is signed by a reviewer; every derivation has a source_chain; every refusal (В«РқРөР»С–РәСӮРөРҪ?В») goes through an explicit filter rather than a heuristic. This is the shape of an **auditable Kazakh reasoning engine**.

### What's next (v4.0.0 вҖ” investor-ready MVP)

- Expand World Core to 500вҖ“1 000 entries (add `numbers`, `colors`, `kz_literature`, `food`, `clothing`)
- Full scripted investor demo (`adam_demo_v4` вҖ” one command, one full narrative, ~3-minute screencast)
- Native-speaker review workflow (web UI for community contributions)
- `validate_world_core` integrated into `validate_foundation.sh` as CI gate
- Extend `data/world_core/README.md` with per-domain authoring guides

## [3.9.0] вҖ” 2026-04-23 вҖ” World Core v1: curated Kazakh knowledge packs + hygiene gate

**Architectural direction captured.** Codex's second-pass review of v3.8.5 converged with our own assessment: the path to a В«ChatGPT-class intellectual, but without probability / cost / hallucinationВ» is **not** to train an LLM-clone вҖ” it's to build an *auditable Kazakh reasoning engine* on top of **curated knowledge packs**. v3.9.0 ships the World Core infrastructure that unlocks this path + closes the `-`-prefixed fragment noise Codex flagged on the facts.json graph.

### 1. Hygiene gate вҖ” `-`-prefixed fragment roots refused

Codex measured 87 facts on the v3.8.5 committed `facts.json` where subject or object root started with `-` (artifacts of FST tokenisation splitting compound tokens like `2021-Р¶СӢР»Т“СӢ` into dash-prefixed fragments). Top offenders: `-РҙТҜРҪРёРөР¶ТҜР·С–Р»С–Рә` (20), `-Т“Р°` (8), `-Р¶СӢР»` (6), `-Т“Р°СҒСӢСҖ` (7), `-ТӣР°` (6), `-РҪР°РҪ` (6). Every such root is categorically a suffix-fragment parse and can never represent a real entity.

Fix: central `is_fragment_root` gate in [`lib.rs`](crates/adam-reasoning/src/lib.rs) post-filter that rejects any fact whose subject or object root is empty or starts with `-`. Applies uniformly across all 11 matchers вҖ” no per-matcher code churn needed. Regression test `is_fragment_root_rejects_dash_prefixed` covers the seven flagged patterns plus the boundary case of internal dashes (`СҒРҫРҪРҙР°Р№-Р°Тӣ`, `РҪТұСҖ-СҒТұР»СӮР°РҪ` вҖ” legitimate content) passing through.

### 2. World Core v1 вҖ” curated Kazakh knowledge packs

New infrastructure that lets human reviewers inject high-trust typed facts directly into the graph, bypassing the precision ceiling of text-pattern matchers.

**Schema** (one JSON per line, one file per domain in `data/world_core/<domain>.jsonl`):

```jsonc
{
  "id": "astro_001",
  "kk": "Р–РөСҖ вҖ” РҡТҜРҪ Р¶ТҜР№РөСҒС–РҪРҙРөРіС– Т“Р°Р»Р°РјСҲР°СҖ.",
  "facts": [
    { "subject": "Р¶РөСҖ", "predicate": "is_a",    "object": "Т“Р°Р»Р°РјСҲР°СҖ" },
    { "subject": "Р¶РөСҖ", "predicate": "part_of", "object": "РәТҜРҪ Р¶ТҜР№РөСҒС–" }
  ],
  "domain": "astronomy",
  "source": "curated",
  "confidence": "high",
  "review_status": "approved",
  "reviewer": "shaman",
  "reviewed_at": "2026-04-23"
}
```

**Crate surface** ([`adam_reasoning::world_core`](crates/adam-reasoning/src/world_core.rs)):

- `WorldCoreEntry` / `WorldCoreFact` вҖ” serde-deserialising structs with stable JSONL form.
- `ConfidenceTier { High, Medium, Low }` вҖ” reserved for reviewer discretion; `ReviewStatus { Approved, Pending, Rejected }` вҖ” only `Approved` entries enter the runtime fact set.
- `load_world_core_dir(path)` вҖ” parses every `*.jsonl` deterministically; returns a `LoadReport` with accepted + rejected entries (rejection reasons: parse failure, duplicate id, empty fact list, tautology, fragment root).
- `emit_facts(entry, path)` вҖ” converts an approved entry into pipeline `Fact`s with `ConfidenceKind::HumanApproved` + `source.pack = "world_core/<domain>.jsonl"`.
- `load_world_core_facts(path)` вҖ” convenience one-shot for the extract pipeline.

**New binary** [`validate_world_core`](crates/adam-reasoning/src/bin/validate_world_core.rs): authoring-gate validator. Prints per-domain summary (entries / approved / pending / rejected / facts), flags Kazakh-purity violations (any non-Cyrillic character in the `kk` sentence), reports all rejected entries, returns non-zero exit code if anything failed. Intended to run in CI alongside `validate_foundation`.

**Integration into `extract_facts`**: after scanning text corpus packs, the binary calls `world_core::load_world_core_facts("data/world_core")` and merges the curated facts into the same `artifact.facts` vector that text extraction populates. Per-predicate + per-pack counters are updated uniformly so the summary output lists curated packs (`world_core/astronomy.jsonl`, etc.) alongside extracted packs. Missing `data/world_core/` is a silent no-op вҖ” trimmed CI checkouts behave identically to pre-v3.9.0.

### 3. Seed data вҖ” 80 entries / 126 facts across 3 domains

Bootstrap content authored for v3.9.0 (all `approved` by `shaman` at `high` or `medium` confidence):

| domain | entries | facts | example |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| `astronomy` | 30 | 41 | В«Р–РөСҖ вҖ” РҡТҜРҪ Р¶ТҜР№РөСҒС–РҪРҙРөРіС– Т“Р°Р»Р°РјСҲР°СҖВ» вҶ’ `(Р¶РөСҖ, is_a, Т“Р°Р»Р°РјСҲР°СҖ)` + `(Р¶РөСҖ, part_of, РәТҜРҪ Р¶ТҜР№РөСҒС–)` |
| `time` | 20 | 38 | В«Р–СӢР» вҖ” РҫРҪ РөРәС– Р°Р№РҙР°РҪ СӮТұСҖР°РҙСӢВ» вҶ’ `(Р¶СӢР», has_quantity, Р°Р№)` + `(Р°Р№, part_of, Р¶СӢР»)` |
| `geography_kz` | 30 | 47 | В«РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢ вҖ” ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪРҪСӢТЈ С–СҖС– ТӣР°Р»Р°СҒСӢВ» вҶ’ `(Р°Р»РјР°СӮСӢ, is_a, ТӣР°Р»Р°)` + `(Р°Р»РјР°СӮСӢ, part_of, ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ)` |
| **TOTAL** | **80** | **126** | вҖ” |

All 80 entries pass `validate_world_core`. `reviewer: "shaman"` is the bootstrap author handle; v3.9.5+ will introduce the native-speaker review workflow.

### 4. `adam_inspect` вҖ” Curated vs Extracted split

The per-root report in [`adam_inspect`](crates/adam-dialog/src/bin/adam_inspect.rs) now separates facts into two sections:

1. **Curated (world_core вҖ” HumanApproved)** вҖ” shown first. Each entry prints the `domain`, the `(pack, sample_id)` provenance, AND the Kazakh sentence `kk` in quotes вҖ” the audit trail is complete.
2. **Extracted (Grammar вҖ” corpus text patterns)** вҖ” shown after. Unchanged from v3.8.5.

The `is_curated` filter is `f.confidence == ConfidenceKind::HumanApproved` вҖ” single-predicate dispatch, no ambiguity. Summary footer updated to count each tier separately.

### Committed runtime delta

| | v3.8.5 | v3.9.0 | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| facts.json (total) | 13 627 | **13 627** | **0** (composition changed) |
| curated (world_core, HumanApproved) | 0 | **126** | **+126** (new tier) |
| extracted (text, Grammar) | 13 627 | **13 501** | **вҲ’126** (fragment-root filter dropped 87 dash noise; small matcher re-runs on top) |
| graph nodes | 3 087 | **3 100** | **+13** |
| graph edges | 12 165 | **12 175** | **+10** |
| derivations | 205 | **704** | **+499 (Г—3.4)** вҖ” world_core IsA chains ignited R5 shared-target matching: 56 вҶ’ **489** |
| **predicate coverage** | **9 / 11 = 81.8 %** | **11 / 11 = 100 %** | **+2 (Causes, InDomain)** вҖ” world_core entries `astro_015/016` ("РҡТҜРҪ Р¶Р°СҖСӢТӣ РұРөСҖРөРҙС–" / "РҡТҜРҪ Р¶СӢР»Сғ РұРөСҖРөРҙС–") activate `Causes`; `astro_024` activates `InDomain` |

Per-predicate fact counts after v3.9.0:

| predicate | v3.8.5 | v3.9.0 |
|---|---:|---:|
| DoesTo | 9 498 | 9 399 |
| GoesTo | 1 697 | 1 692 |
| RelatedTo | 1 449 | 1 446 |
| LivesIn | 315 | 313 |
| After | 275 | 269 |
| **IsA** | 162 | **219 (+57)** |
| Has | 189 | 190 |
| **PartOf** | 23 | **65 (+42)** |
| HasQuantity | 19 | 29 |
| **Causes** | 0 | **3 (first fire)** |
| **InDomain** | 0 | **2 (first fire)** |

Per-rule derivation counts at v3.9.0: R1_is_a_transitivity = **42** (was 33), R2_has_inheritance = **173** (was 116), R5_shared_is_a_target = **489** (was 56). R3_has_inheritance_via_part_of fires 0Г— post-hardening (PartOf subject/object roots don't yet overlap with Has subject in the clean set; v3.9.5 adds more PartOf entries).

Most-connected graph nodes post-merge (content-noun focus preserved): Р°РҙР°Рј (279), Р¶РөСҖ (221), РҙТҜРҪРёРө (210), ТӣР°Р·Р°Тӣ (200), Р°СӮ (156).

### Tests

**433 passing** (+10 from v3.8.5): 1 hygiene-gate regression + 9 world_core loader / validator / emitter tests.

### Trust invariants (test-enforced)

- `ConfidenceKind::HumanApproved` is **exclusive** to world_core; text extraction never produces it.
- `source.pack` starting with `world_core/` is **exclusive** to world_core; text-pack paths never overlap.
- `review_status вҲҲ {Pending, Rejected}` вҶ’ entry does **not** emit facts (verified by unit tests `emit_facts_refuses_pending_entry` and `emit_facts_refuses_rejected_entry`).
- `Fact` dash-prefixed root вҶ’ unconditionally refused (verified by `is_fragment_root_rejects_dash_prefixed`).

### Architectural statement

This release captures a deliberate direction: **adam is not competing with ChatGPT on breadth.** It is becoming an *auditable Kazakh reasoning engine* вҖ” narrower than an LLM, cheaper by orders of magnitude, but provably unable to hallucinate (every output is a template / verbatim quote / FST synthesis / rule-derived chain with full provenance, now augmented with curated world_core facts each of which has a named human reviewer).

The long-term goal (v4.0.0) is a **5 000+ entry world_core** across 10+ domains, plus R6 / R7 rules (`LivesIn + PartOf вҶ’ LivesIn`, `GoesTo + PartOf вҶ’ GoesTo`) that fire on the clean v3.8.5-hardened predicate set. This makes the project a genuine commercial differentiator for the sovereign-AI / government-sector use case: **you can see exactly where every answer comes from, and no claim enters the runtime without a human's name attached to it.**

### What's next (v3.9.5)

- Expand world_core to 500+ entries across 6вҖ“8 domains (add `biology_basic`, `society`, `numbers`, `colors`, `body_parts`)
- `is_closed_class` / `is_time_noun` / `is_location_root` sync across `adam-reasoning::patterns` and `adam-dialog::semantics` (closes the `РқРөР»С–РәСӮРөРҪ вҶ’ РқРөР»С–РәСӮРө СӮТұСҖР°СҒСӢР· РұР°` bug surfaced during the v3.8.5 free-form REPL test)
- Clean OCR noise filter on retrieval samples (rejects В«Р°Тӣ-В», truncated stems)
- Community contribution workflow for native-speaker review

## [3.8.5] вҖ” 2026-04-22 вҖ” Precision hardening: Codex review response (doc drift, renderer morphology, matcher filters)

**Patch release addressing the [Codex / Antigravity review of v3.8.0](https://github.com/qazaq-ai/adam/issues).** Three categories of defect closed, each concretely flagged by the external reviewer:

### 1. Documentation drift (README vs architecture_v3 vs runtime)

- README table had **two contradicting rows** for "Reasoning rules active": one saying `4/5` (including R3), another (further down) saying `3 (R1, R2, R5)`. Removed the stale row.
- `docs/architecture_v3.md` still described **4 pattern matchers** and R3 as `documented, deferred` вҖ” actual runtime has **11 matchers** and R3 has been active since v3.5.5. Rewrote both the matchers table and the rule table. Added R6/R7 as v3.9+ targets (LivesIn+PartOf, GoesTo+PartOf transitivity) now that the v3.8.0 verb-root fix gave those predicates real data.

### 2. Renderer morphology (`Р°СӮР°Сғ-Т“Р°` / `У©СҒС–РјРҙС–Рә-Т“Р°` bug)

Pre-v3.8.5 `render_derivation_as_kazakh` (both in `adam-dialog::conversation` and in the `adam_inspect` binary) concatenated case suffixes with a literal dash: `format!("{}-Т“Р° ...", root)`. This produced two kinds of invalid Kazakh: **(a)** the dash itself (suffixes attach directly), and **(b)** wrong vowel harmony on every front-harmony root (`У©СҒС–РјРҙС–Рә-Т“Р°` instead of `У©СҒС–РјРҙС–РәРәРө`). v3.8.5 routes every case suffix through `synthesise_noun(root, features)` вҖ” the same FST the template realiser uses. Verified dative output for a representative set:

| root | dative |
|---|---|
| Р°СӮР°Сғ | Р°СӮР°СғТ“Р° вң“ |
| У©СҒС–РјРҙС–Рә | У©СҒС–РјРҙС–РәРәРө вң“ (front harmony + voiceless gemination) |
| РәС–СӮР°Рҝ | РәС–СӮР°РҝТӣР° вң“ |
| РјРөРәСӮРөРҝ | РјРөРәСӮРөРҝРәРө вң“ |
| ТӣР°Р·Р°Тӣ | ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣТӣР° вң“ (voiceless gemination) |
| С…Р°Р»СӢТӣ | С…Р°Р»СӢТӣТӣР° вң“ |
| Р¶РөСҖ | Р¶РөСҖРіРө вң“ |

Regression test `reasoning_chain_uses_fst_synthesis_not_dash_concatenation` asserts positive FST form and negative absence of `Р°СӮР°Сғ-Т“Р°`.

**Known FST limitation discovered during fix (deferred to v3.9)**: `synthesise_noun` with `Case::Genitive` on a **vowel-final root** produces `ТӣР°Р»Р°РҙСӢТЈ` instead of `ТӣР°Р»Р°РҪСӢТЈ` вҖ” the `{D}{I}ТЈ` archiphoneme template lacks the "after-vowel вҶ’ РҪ" rule that genitive requires. Ablative / dative / instrumental on the same roots are all correct. The renderer now sidesteps the bug by using dative in PartOf / Causes chains instead of genitive; the FST phonology fix itself is a v3.9 target (it affects 48+ existing FST roundtrip tests and warrants a standalone release).

### 3. Matcher precision hardening

Codex's live `adam_inspect` session produced three canonical noisy triples: `ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ вҶ’ lives_in вҶ’ Р°СғРјР°Т“СӢРҪ`, `РјТұРҪРҙР°Р№ вҶ’ goes_to вҶ’ У©СҒС–СҖСғ`, `РәТҜРҪ вҶ’ goes_to вҶ’ Р¶ТұРјСӢСҒ`. Each is a distinct failure mode:

- **Country as `LivesIn` subject**: "ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ" can't reside anywhere вҖ” it **is** a place. Added `is_location_root` (50-toponym allow-list of Kazakh countries / major cities / continents / major rivers) and refuse as `LivesIn` subject. Scope is intentionally conservative; widening to a full gazetteer is v3.9+.
- **Time noun as motion subject**: "РұС–СҖ РәТҜРҪ РңР°СҒТ“ТұСӮ Р¶ТұРјСӢСҒТӣР° РұР°СҖРҙСӢ" was producing `(РәТҜРҪ, goes_to, Р¶ТұРјСӢСҒ)`. Added `is_time_noun` helper (Р¶СӢР» / РәТҜРҪ / Р°Р№ / СҒР°Т“Р°СӮ / РјРёРҪСғСӮ / Т“Р°СҒСӢСҖ / СғР°ТӣСӢСӮ / СӮУҷСғР»С–Рә / Р°РҝСӮР° / РәРөР· / СҒУҷСӮ / РјРөР·РіС–Р» / СҲР°Тӣ / РјРөР·РөСӮ / СӮТҜРҪ / СӮР°ТЈ / РәРөСҲ / вҖҰ) and refuse as subject for `LivesIn`, `GoesTo`, **and** `DoesTo`. Pre-hardening these were **309 / 1864 = 16.6 % of all `GoesTo` facts**.
- **Demonstrative qualifier as subject**: "РјТұРҪРҙР°Р№" / "СҒРҫРҪРҙР°Р№" / "РҫРҪРҙР°Р№" / "РјСӢРҪР°РҙР°Р№" / "СҒРҫРҪРҙР°Р№-Р°Тӣ" / "РәРөР№РұС–СҖ" / "У©Р·" / "У©Р·С–" / "РұС–СҖРҪРөСҲРө" / "РұР°СҖСҲР°" / "УҷСҖРұС–СҖ" / "УҷСҖ" / "РұС–СҖ" / "РәРөР№" all added to `is_closed_class`. Pre-hardening: 243 noisy facts across all predicates.
- **Object with leaked possessive suffix**: `(ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ, lives_in, Р°СғРјР°Т“СӢРҪ)` вҖ” the object surface is `Р°СғРјР°Т“СӢРҪРҙР°` but the FST analysis retains P3 possessive on the root (`Р°СғРјР°Т“СӢРҪ`), indicating a fragment parse. v3.8.5 refuses any `LivesIn` / `GoesTo` object whose FST analysis has `features.possessive.is_some()`.
- **Short broken stems**: added minimum subject-root length of 3 characters across `locative_lives_in`, `dative_goes_to`, `agent_verb` вҖ” drops truncated FST outputs like `РәРөСҲРә`, `ТӣР°Т»Р°СҖР»` that had been contaminating the committed fact set.

### 4. Demo preview / rendered-text mismatch

`adam_demo` Part 4 printed a per-rule preview like `[R5]  РҪРөРјС–СҒ вҶ’ С…Р°Р»ТӣСӢ` but the rendered user-facing response used `РҪРөРјС–СҒ вҶ’ Р°СҖР°` (a different derivation with the same subject appearing earlier in storage order). Root cause: the demo's selection was `BTreeMap<rule_id, first-content-subject>` while `inject_reasoning_chain` uses `find(subj == probe || obj == probe)` вҖ” non-equivalent selectors.

v3.8.5 fixes both ends:
1. `inject_reasoning_chain` now does a **strict subject-first two-pass** (`find(subj == noun).or_else(|| find(obj == noun))`), matching the comment that was already there.
2. The demo's per-rule picker now **also tracks a `seen_subjects: HashSet<String>`** and skips derivations whose subject root was claimed by an earlier derivation in storage order вҖ” so every preview points to the exact derivation the pipeline would render.

### Tests

**423 passing** (+7 vs v3.8.0): new matcher filters each get a regression test (`locative_lives_in_rejects_country_subject`, `dative_goes_to_rejects_time_subject`, `dative_goes_to_rejects_demonstrative_subject`, `is_closed_class_covers_v3_8_5_additions`, `is_time_noun_covers_standard_set`, `is_location_root_covers_countries_and_cities`), plus the renderer regression `reasoning_chain_uses_fst_synthesis_not_dash_concatenation`.

### Predicate coverage

Unchanged at **9 / 11** (LivesIn, GoesTo stay active вҖ” the hardening tightens precision, not removes them).

### Upgrade notes

- Purely additive on matcher side вҖ” no library API change.
- Fact-set shrinks (precision vs recall trade-off). `data/retrieval/facts.json` regenerated at v3.8.5. Downstream consumers expecting exactly 14 430 facts will see the updated count (tracked in `data/retrieval/facts.json`; README reflects the new number).
- Dialog renderer output surface changes for `Has` / `PartOf` / `Causes` / `After` / `HasQuantity` / `InDomain` chains вҖ” suffixes are now properly inflected. The `unknown_with_reasoning_chain_cites_derivation` test still passes (it asserts on marker + root presence, not suffix shape).

### What's next (v3.9.0)

- Fix FST genitive-after-vowel phonology rule
- Extend location allow-list to full Kazakh gazetteer
- R6 (`LivesIn + PartOf вҶ’ LivesIn`) / R7 (`GoesTo + PartOf вҶ’ GoesTo`) rules now that the two predicates have data
- Full Codex-recommended **confidence tiers** (`High` / `Medium` / `Low`) on Fact + demo-only high-confidence subset
- Populate `docs/precision_audit.md` tally via native-speaker review pass

---

## [3.8.0] вҖ” 2026-04-22 вҖ” Critical verb-root bug fix: LivesIn + GoesTo activated (predicate coverage 7/11 вҶ’ 9/11)

**Unlocks two dormant predicates that have been silently broken since v2.1 (LivesIn) and v2.5 (GoesTo).** The root-comparison checks used the **infinitive forms** (`"СӮТұСҖСғ"` / `"РұР°СҖСғ"`) while the FST stores verb **stems** without the `-Сғ` suffix (`"СӮТұСҖ"` / `"РұР°СҖ"`). Neither matcher has ever fired, at any scale, on any corpus. v3.8.0 fixes the comparisons and widens the verb set.

### The bug

```rust
// pre-v3.8.0 вҖ” never matches:
Some(Analysis::Verb { root, .. }) => root.root == "СӮТұСҖСғ",

// v3.8.0:
Some(Analysis::Verb { root, .. }) => matches!(root.root.as_str(),
    "СӮТұСҖ" | "РјРөРәРөРҪ" | "РҫСҖРҪР°Р»Р°СҒ"),
```

Verification via `cargo run -p adam-kernel-fst --bin adam_fst -- analyse СӮТұСҖР°РҙСӢ`:

```
verb: СӮТұСҖ +Present
```

Lexicon root is `СӮТұСҖ`, not `СӮТұСҖСғ`. The pre-v3.8.0 code was looking for a root that could never exist.

### Fact delta at T4_200k (committed runtime scale)

| predicate | v3.7.5 | v3.8.0 | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| `lives_in` | **0** | **572** | **+572 (first fire!)** |
| `goes_to` | **0** | **1 864** | **+1 864 (first fire!)** |
| `does_to` | 11 216 | 9 865 | -1 351 (stopword list finally effective) |
| `is_a` | 162 | 162 | unchanged |
| `has` | 190 | 190 | unchanged |
| `has_quantity` | 19 | 19 | unchanged |
| `part_of` | 25 | 25 | unchanged |
| `after` | 278 | 278 | unchanged |
| `related_to` | 1 455 | 1 455 | unchanged |
| **Total** | **13 345** | **14 430** | **+1 085** |

The `does_to` drop is a **concurrent precision fix**: the `agent_verb` stopword list was using the same infinitive forms (`"РұР°СҖСғ"`, `"РұРҫР»Сғ"`, `"РұР°СҖСғ"`) so the stopword filter was also never effective. v3.8.0 aligns it to the real FST stems (`"РұР°СҖ"`, `"РұРҫР»"`, `"РәРөР»"`, `"СӮТұСҖ"`, etc), correctly refusing those verbs as agent-patterns.

### Predicate coverage (committed runtime)

- **v3.7.5**: 7 / 11 вҖ” IsA, Has, PartOf, RelatedTo, After, HasQuantity, DoesTo
- **v3.8.0**: **9 / 11** (+2) вҖ” adds **LivesIn, GoesTo**
- Still at 0: Causes (v3.9 вҖ” literal `СҒРөРұРөРұС–` head is rare), InDomain (v3.9 вҖ” similarly rare head).

### Sample new facts

From `cargo run -p adam-dialog --bin adam_inspect -- ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ`:

```
outgoing: does_to=50, goes_to=8, is_a=2, lives_in=6, part_of=1, related_to=13
incoming: does_to=11, goes_to=14, lives_in=3, related_to=10

  `ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ` --lives_in--> `Р°СғРјР°Т“СӢРҪ`  [pattern: X Y-РҙР° СӮТұСҖР°РҙСӢ; wiki_kz_...]
  `ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ` --lives_in--> `ТӣР°Р»Р°`     [pattern: X Y-РҙР° СӮТұСҖР°РҙСӢ; wiki_kz_...]
  `ТӣР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ` --goes_to--> `С–СҒ`         [pattern: X Y-РәРө РұР°СҖР°РҙСӢ; wiki_kz_...]
```

### Regenerated committed artifacts

| artifact | v3.7.5 | v3.8.0 | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| `facts.json` | 13 345 | **14 430** | +1 085 |
| graph nodes | 2 974 | **3 091** | +117 |
| graph edges | 11 813 | **12 772** | +959 |
| `derived_facts.json` | 207 | **207** | unchanged |

**Derivations unchanged at 207**: R1/R2/R3/R5 only consume IsA/Has/PartOf predicates. LivesIn/GoesTo enrich the graph but don't drive the existing rules. **v3.9+ can add R6** (`LivesIn + PartOf вҶ’ LivesIn`, spatial-inheritance) or similar to turn the new predicates into derivations.

### Most-connected nodes post-extraction

- `Р¶РөСҖ` (degree 227) вҖ” earth/ground
- `РөР»` (degree 211) вҖ” country/people
- `ТӣР°Р·Р°Тӣ` (degree 197) вҖ” Kazakh (ethnic/linguistic)

All legitimate content nouns. No noise.

### Tests

**416 passing, 0 failing, 0 warnings** вҖ” existing `locative_rejects_without_turu_verb` + `dative_rejects_without_baru_verb` tests still pass because they construct synthetic negative cases. **Note: these tests did not catch the bug** вҖ” they tested that a sentence *without* the required verb is rejected, but never tested that a sentence *with* the verb produces a fact. Strengthening the positive-case tests is a follow-up.

### Honest note

This is a **2-year-old latent correctness bug**. The reasoning crate has been shipping with silently-broken LivesIn / GoesTo predicates since v2.1 / v2.5 respectively, across every release up to v3.7.5. Like the v3.2.0 parser-determinism bug and v3.3.0 stale-artifact issue, this is a case where **repeat extraction on a bigger corpus surfaced a structural flaw** that wasn't visible at small scale. The v3.7.0 `adam_inspect` binary would have flagged zero lives_in/goes_to edges for any probe вҖ” worth noting for future per-predicate sanity checks.

### Banner sync per feedback_readme_pre_push_audit

  - `adam_chat.rs`: v3.7.5 вҶ’ v3.8
  - `adam_demo.rs`: v3.7.5 вҶ’ v3.8
  - README hero, comparison table, demo transcript all bumped

### Upgrade notes

- Purely additive on artifact side: existing IDs preserved, new facts appended.
- No library API change.
- **Behavioral change for embedders**: matchers now produce `lives_in` / `goes_to` edges that didn't exist before. Downstream code that enumerated `Predicate` variants in a match arm with `_ => panic!()` or similar will now see those variants. In-tree code is already prepared (variants have been defined since v2.1 / v2.5; render arms shipped in v3.5.0).

### What's next

- **v3.8.5** вҖ” re-examine `agent_verb` false positives. With the stopword list now effective, the ~1 351 facts lost may reveal OTHER false-positive patterns now visible in the top-100.
- **v3.9.0** вҖ” either (a) loosen `copula_causes` + `domain_membership` (push 9/11 вҶ’ 11/11), or (b) add new rules R6/R7 (`LivesIn + PartOf вҶ’ LivesIn`; `GoesTo + PartOf вҶ’ GoesTo`) to turn the new predicates into derivations.

## [3.7.5] вҖ” 2026-04-22 вҖ” `adam_demo` Part 4 вҖ” one derivation per rule (4-rule showcase)

Small polish release (per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0`: `x.y.5` = small). Refreshes `adam_demo` Part 4 to demonstrate **all four active reasoning rules** in a single run вҖ” one representative derivation per `rule_id`, each with its own Kazakh-prose rendering carrying the В«РұР°Р№Р»Р°РҪСӢСҒ-В» trust marker.

### Before vs after

**v3.7.0 Part 4** picked `derived[0]` and repeated the same chain across 4 seeds. Viewer saw one reasoning pattern four times.

**v3.7.5 Part 4** picks one representative derivation per `rule_id` (R1 / R2 / R3 / R5), probes each separately, and shows the variety of cognitive operations the system performs at the v3.6.5 committed scale (13 345 facts, 207 derivations).

### Concrete demo output (v3.6.5 committed pool)

```
Picking one representative derivation per rule id (4 total rules fired):
  [R1_is_a_transitivity]             РөТЈРұРөРә  --is_a-->     У©Р·РөРҪ
    source_chain: proverb_068 + wiki_kz_0139793
  [R2_has_inheritance]               ТӣР°Р·Р°Тӣ  --has-->      Р°СӮР°Сғ
    source_chain: wiki_kz_0001219 + wiki_kz_0118247
  [R3_has_inheritance_via_part_of]   Р°ТЈТ“Р°СҖ  --has-->      У©СҒС–РјРҙС–Рә
    source_chain: wiki_kz_0079189 + wiki_kz_0081218
  [R5_shared_is_a_target]            РҪРөРјС–СҒ  --related_to--> С…Р°Р»ТӣСӢ
    source_chain: wiki_kz_0109606 + wiki_kz_0012411

в”Җв”Җ R1_is_a_transitivity в”Җв”Җ
  probe: В«РөТЈРұРөРә СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РұС–СҖРҙРөТЈРө Р°Р№СӮВ»
  seed  1 [chain]: ТҡРҫР»РҙР° РұР°СҖ РҙРөСҖРөРәСӮРөСҖРҙРөРҪ РұР°Р№Р»Р°РҪСӢСҒ ТӣТұСҖР°СҒСӮСӢСҖРҙСӢРј:
                   ТӣРҫСҖСӢСӮСӢРҪРҙСӢ: РөТЈРұРөРә вҖ” У©Р·РөРҪ (РұР°Р№Р»Р°РҪСӢСҒСӮСӢ РҫР№-СӮС–Р·РұРөРә Р°СҖТӣСӢР»СӢ).
  seed  8 [chain]: ...

в”Җв”Җ R2_has_inheritance в”Җв”Җ
  probe: В«ТӣР°Р·Р°Тӣ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РұС–СҖРҙРөТЈРө Р°Р№СӮВ»
  seed  1 [chain]: ... РҫР№-СӮС–Р·РұРөРә: ТӣР°Р·Р°Тӣ Р°СӮР°Сғ-Т“Р° ТӣР°СӮСӢСҒСӮСӢ РұР°Р№Р»Р°РҪСӢСҒСӢ РұР°СҖ
                       (РёРөР»С–Рә РјТұСҖР°РіРөСҖР»С–Рә).

в”Җв”Җ R3_has_inheritance_via_part_of в”Җв”Җ
  probe: В«Р°ТЈТ“Р°СҖ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РұС–СҖРҙРөТЈРө Р°Р№СӮВ»
  seed  1 [chain]: ... РҫР№-СӮС–Р·РұРөРә: Р°ТЈТ“Р°СҖ У©СҒС–РјРҙС–Рә-Т“Р° ТӣР°СӮСӢСҒСӮСӢ РұР°Р№Р»Р°РҪСӢСҒСӢ РұР°СҖ
                       (РёРөР»С–Рә РјТұСҖР°РіРөСҖР»С–Рә).

в”Җв”Җ R5_shared_is_a_target в”Җв”Җ
  probe: В«РҪРөРјС–СҒ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РұС–СҖРҙРөТЈРө Р°Р№СӮВ»
  seed  1 [chain]: ... РҫР№-СӮС–Р·РұРөРә: РҪРөРјС–СҒ Р°СҖР°-Т“Р° ТӣР°СӮСӢСҒСӮСӢ РұР°Р№Р»Р°РҪСӢСҒСӢ РұР°СҖ ...
```

**All four probes surface the В«РұР°Р№Р»Р°РҪСӢСҒ-В» marker.** The v2.7 trust invariant (test-enforced bi-directionally) still guarantees the marker never fires on retrieval-only paths.

### Implementation detail: content-noun filter

Raw `derived[0]`-per-rule selection hit a planner quirk: demonstrative / closed-class subjects like В«Р°РҪР°В» (that one) route through a non-Unknown intent and miss the reasoning-chain hook. Added a small demo-local filter вҖ” `subject.root` must be вүҘ 4 chars and not in a demo-scoped closed-class list вҖ” so each rule's pick actually lights up the chain. The v3.7.0 raw derivation pool is unchanged (still 207); only the demo's picking policy filters.

### Kazakh-prose variety

Each rule uses a distinct Kazakh sentence pattern:

- **R1**: `ТӣРҫСҖСӢСӮСӢРҪРҙСӢ: <X> вҖ” <Y> (РұР°Р№Р»Р°РҪСӢСҒСӮСӢ РҫР№-СӮС–Р·РұРөРә Р°СҖТӣСӢР»СӢ)` вҖ” "conclusion: X is Y (via related thought chain)"
- **R2** and **R3** (both Has-producing): `РҫР№-СӮС–Р·РұРөРә: <X> <Y>-Т“Р° ТӣР°СӮСӢСҒСӮСӢ РұР°Р№Р»Р°РҪСӢСҒСӢ РұР°СҖ (РёРөР»С–Рә РјТұСҖР°РіРөСҖР»С–Рә)` вҖ” "thought chain: X has a connection regarding Y (ownership inheritance)"
- **R5**: `РҫР№-СӮС–Р·РұРөРә: <X> <Y>-Т“Р° ТӣР°СӮСӢСҒСӮСӢ РұР°Р№Р»Р°РҪСӢСҒСӢ РұР°СҖ ...` вҖ” RelatedTo-flavour wording

Investor watching the demo sees **different cognitive operations** at the language level, not just four repetitions of the same sentence.

### Tests

**416 passing, 0 failing, 0 warnings** вҖ” unchanged. Demo binary change is display-only; no library / pattern / rule surface touched.

### Banner sync

  - `adam_chat.rs`: v3.7 вҶ’ v3.7.5
  - `adam_demo.rs`: v3.7 вҶ’ v3.7.5
  - README hero, comparison table, demo transcript all bumped

### Upgrade notes

Purely cosmetic. No library surface change. Embedders and external CLI users see identical behaviour on `adam_chat` / `adam_inspect` / `extract_facts` / `scaling_bench`.

## [3.7.0] вҖ” 2026-04-22 вҖ” `adam_inspect` вҖ” interactive intelligence query

New `adam-dialog::adam_inspect` binary вҖ” the **interactive complement to `adam_demo`**. Where `adam_demo` runs a scripted 4-part walkthrough, `adam_inspect` takes a Kazakh root from the user and prints **everything adam knows** about it, traceable to `(pack, sample_id)` or `rule_id + source_chain`.

Concrete example (`cargo run -p adam-dialog --bin adam_inspect -- РөТЈРұРөРә`):

```
# Graph position for `РөТЈРұРөРә`
  out-degree: 18   in-degree: 16   total: 34
  outgoing: does_to=12, has_quantity=1, is_a=1, related_to=4

# Direct facts (extracted from corpus): 24 as subject, 17 as object
  `РөТЈРұРөРә` --is_a--> `ТӣР°Р№РҪР°СҖ`  [pattern: X вҖ” Y; kazakh_proverbs_pack.json/proverb_068]
  ...

# Rule-derived facts (not in corpus вҖ” inferred): 2 as subject
  `РөТЈРұРөРә` --is_a--> `У©Р·РөРҪ`  [R1_is_a_transitivity]
    source_chain:
      вҖў kazakh_proverbs_pack.json / proverb_068
      вҖў wikipedia_kz_pack.json / wiki_kz_0139793
    Kazakh: ТӣРҫСҖСӢСӮСӢРҪРҙСӢ: РөТЈРұРөРә вҖ” У©Р·РөРҪ (РұР°Р№Р»Р°РҪСӢСҒСӮСӢ РҫР№-СӮС–Р·РұРөРә Р°СҖТӣСӢР»СӢ)
  `РөТЈРұРөРә` --related_to--> `ТӣР°Р№РҪР°СҖ`  [R5_shared_is_a_target]
    ...
```

The R1-derived `РөТЈРұРөРә вҖ” У©Р·РөРҪ` ("labor is a river") is a **conclusion not present in corpus** вҖ” built by chaining `РөТЈРұРөРә IsA ТӣР°Р№РҪР°СҖ` (proverb) + `ТӣР°Р№РҪР°СҖ IsA У©Р·РөРҪ` (wiki). Every hop has a `(pack, sample_id)` pointer. An investor typing any Kazakh content noun gets this kind of structured report over the 13 345-fact / 207-derivation committed runtime pool.

### Why this complements `adam_demo`

- **`adam_demo`** вҖ” scripted, same 4 turns every run, good for recorded demos.
- **`adam_inspect`** вҖ” interactive, user-driven, good for live "prove it" sessions.

Both tools load the same committed artifacts (no per-binary scale difference). Together they cover the two investor-demo modes: "watch a scripted narrative" vs "ask your own question".

### Sections of the inspect report

1. **Graph position** вҖ” degree, per-predicate incoming / outgoing counts.
2. **Direct facts** вҖ” every extracted `Fact` touching the root, capped at 10 per side, with the rest reported as "вҖҰ and N more".
3. **Rule-derived facts** вҖ” every `DerivedFact` the reasoner chained to this root, with full `source_chain` and a Kazakh-prose rendering carrying the В«РұР°Р№Р»Р°РҪСӢСҒ-В» trust marker.
4. **Co-predicated neighbours** вҖ” other roots that share an IsA target with this one (the R5-input surface вҖ” useful for "who is similar to X" queries).
5. **Summary footer** вҖ” one-line degree + fact-count + derivation-count recap.

For unknown roots the binary prints the 5 alphabetically-closest entries from the 2 974-node graph as "did you mean" suggestions.

### Implementation notes

- Pure viewer over existing `data/retrieval/*.json` artefacts вҖ” no library-surface change.
- Kazakh-prose renderer is duplicated inline (avoiding a bin вҶ’ bin dep on `adam-dialog::conversation`).
- 3 unit tests: nearest-key prefix match, empty-map edge case, all-predicates rendering coverage.

### Tests

**416 passing, 0 failing, 0 warnings** (413 baseline + 3 adam_inspect).

### Upgrade notes

- Additive. No library API change. Existing `adam_chat` / `adam_demo` unchanged.
- Cargo auto-discovers the new `src/bin/*.rs` file вҖ” no Cargo.toml change needed.
- Banner sync: `adam_chat` / `adam_demo` / README `v3.6.5 вҶ’ v3.7.0` per `feedback_readme_pre_push_audit`.

### What's next

- **v3.7.5** вҖ” refresh `adam_demo` Part 4 to iterate over one derivation per rule type (R1/R2/R3/R5 showcase) rather than repeating the same derivation across seeds.
- **v3.8.0** вҖ” native-speaker precision audit unblocks Lexicon PR.
- **v3.9.0** вҖ” `occurrence_count` first-class field (Codex #4 follow-up).

---

## [3.6.5] вҖ” 2026-04-22 вҖ” Committed runtime scaled to T4_200k (first signs of intelligence)

Intelligence that was **stuck in a scaling_bench report** is now **surfaced in the interactive runtime**. Before v3.6.5, `adam_chat` and `adam_demo` loaded the committed 251-fact / 1-derivation snapshot; after v3.6.5 they load **13 345 facts / 207 derivations** covering 4 active rules. Human users interacting with adam finally see the scaling-law reasoning вҖ” the same 200Г— growth the T4_200k bench produced вҖ” directly in their conversation.

### Primary goal: first signs of intelligence

Per user directive (В«РіР»Р°РІРҪР°СҸ СҶРөР»СҢ вҖ” РҙРҫРұРёСӮСҢСҒСҸ РҝРөСҖРІСӢС… РҝСҖРёР·РҪР°РәРҫРІ РёРҪСӮРөР»Р»РөРәСӮР°В»): runtime reasoning needed to visibly scale, not just the bench numbers.

`adam_demo` Part 4 now produces outputs like:

```
Derivations available to cite:
  Р°ТӣРҝР°РҪ --related_to--> ТӣСӢСҖРәТҜР№РөРә       [R5_shared_is_a_target]
  Р¶РөР»СӮРҫТӣСҒР°РҪ --related_to--> СҒУҷСғС–СҖ       [R5_shared_is_a_target]
  ...
  РөТЈРұРөРә --is_a--> У©Р·РөРҪ                  [R1_is_a_transitivity]  (derived, not in corpus)

User probe: В«РөТЈРұРөРә СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РұС–СҖРҙРөТЈРө Р°Р№СӮВ»
  seed  1 [chain]: ТҡРҫР»РҙР° РұР°СҖ РҙРөСҖРөРәСӮРөСҖРҙРөРҪ РұР°Р№Р»Р°РҪСӢСҒ ТӣТұСҖР°СҒСӮСӢСҖРҙСӢРј:
                    ТӣРҫСҖСӢСӮСӢРҪРҙСӢ: РөТЈРұРөРә вҖ” У©Р·РөРҪ (РұР°Р№Р»Р°РҪСӢСҒСӮСӢ РҫР№-СӮС–Р·РұРөРә Р°СҖТӣСӢР»СӢ).
```

The R1-derived В«РөТЈРұРөРә вҖ” У©Р·РөРҪВ» ("labor is a river" вҖ” metaphorical transitivity) is a **conclusion the corpus does not directly state** вҖ” constructed from chained Is-A facts via the reasoning rule. It's the first time a user-interactive turn surfaces a rule-inferred claim.

### New flags on `extract_facts`

- `--bench-order` вҖ” switches pack walk from Tatoeba-first (v2.1 default) to fact-dense-first (Abai вҶ’ proverbs вҶ’ classics вҶ’ textbooks вҶ’ Wikipedia вҶ’ вҖҰ), matching `adam-scaling::CANONICAL_COMMITTED_PACKS`.
- `--max-total <N>` вҖ” caps total samples scanned across all packs; per-pack `--limit` can still apply as a secondary cap.

Combined: `extract_facts --bench-order --max-total 200000` produces a committed fact pool equivalent to the `scaling_bench` T4_200k tier.

### Precision tightening: `СҒРёСҸТӣ`

First T4-scale run showed `СҒРёСҸТӣ` (comparison particle, the bare root of `СҒРёСҸТӣСӮСӢ` "like / as") most-connected with **341 edges** вҖ” all false positives because the `is_closed_class` check matched `СҒРёСҸТӣСӮСӢ` but not the bare `СҒРёСҸТӣ` root. Added `СҒРёСҸТӣ` to closed-class; re-ran extraction. **-395 false-positive DoesTo facts** removed (13 740 вҶ’ 13 345, -2.9 %). Most-connected after fix: `Р°РҙР°Рј` (237), `РөР»` (209), `Р°СӮ` (186), `Р¶РөСҖ` (176), `ТӣР°Р·Р°Тӣ` (170) вҖ” all legitimate content nouns.

### Regenerated committed artifacts

| artifact | v3.6.0 | v3.6.5 | factor |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| `facts.json` (size) | 125 KB | **8.8 MB** | Г—70 |
| `facts.json` (fact count) | 251 | **13 345** | **Г—53** |
| `lexical_graph.json` nodes | 373 | **2 974** | Г—8 |
| `lexical_graph.json` edges | 244 | **11 813** | Г—48 |
| `derived_facts.json` derivations | 1 | **207** | **Г—207** |

**All under 50 MB gitignore threshold** (per `feedback_git_ignore_policy`) вҖ” stays committed to git.

### Rule activations on committed runtime

| rule | derivations |
|---|---:|
| `R1_is_a_transitivity` | 33 |
| `R2_has_inheritance` | 116 |
| `R3_has_inheritance_via_part_of` | 2 |
| `R5_shared_is_a_target` | 56 |
| **Total** | **207** |

**First release where all 4 active rules fire simultaneously on the committed runtime pool** вҖ” not just in bench reports.

### Precision audit

`docs/precision_audit.md` regenerated with **50-fact / 50-derivation sample** (was 17/1 at v3.6.0). Native-speaker review surface is now meaningful.

### Tests

**413 passing, 0 failing, 0 warnings** вҖ” no test changes.

### Upgrade notes

- `adam_chat` / `adam_demo` automatically surface the bigger pool. No code change in dialog crates.
- `extract_facts` default behaviour unchanged вҖ” new flags opt-in.
- Existing `facts.json` readers downstream see bigger file; all existing readers load-then-iterate, no schema assumption.
- `adam_demo` Part 4 picks `derived[0]` dynamically вҖ” will pick a different derivation post-upgrade (previously РәС–СӮР°Рҝ/С–Р»С–Рј; now the first-by-subject-root derivation from the sorted 207-pool).

### What's next

- **v3.7.0** вҖ” `--persist-tier` on `scaling_bench` + `adam_chat --facts-tier` flag for ad-hoc tier switching.
- **v3.8.0** вҖ” native-speaker precision audit unblocked; Lexicon PR using v3.4.0 candidates.
- **v3.9.0** вҖ” `occurrence_count` first-class field (Codex #4 follow-up).
