## [2.3.0] вҖ” 2026-04-21 вҖ” FST vowel-final+P3 fix + Lexical Graph v0 (fact projection)

Minor release. Two step-changes:

1. **FST fix**: Kazakh glide-vowels `Сғ`, `Рё`, `СҺ` are moved from `ConsonantClass::VowelPreceding` to `HighSonorant`, aligning the code with the enum docstring and fixing a whole class of vowel-final + P3 mis-synthesis. Observable: `РҫТӣСғ+P3`, `РұР°СҒСӮР°Сғ+P3` now produce `РҫТӣСғСӢ`, `РұР°СҒСӮР°СғСӢ` (before: wrong `РҫТӣСғСҒСӢ`, `РұР°СҒСӮР°СғСҒСӢ`). v2.2's last remaining imprecision (`Р¶РөСҖ вҶ’ СӮС–СҖСҲС–Р»С–Рә` should have been `Р¶РөСҖ вҶ’ РұР°СҒСӮР°Сғ`) is fixed as a direct consequence.
2. **Lexical Graph v0**: new `adam_reasoning::graph::LexicalGraph`. Pure projection of `facts.json` into `(nodes, edges)` вҖ” every edge traces back to the fact(s) that produced it. 29 nodes, 15 edges from the v2.3 fact set. First step toward a reasoner that can answer "tell me about X" or "what is X?" in O(1) via the graph.

### The FST fix вҖ” `classify_char` correction

```rust
// before (v2.2)
'Р°' | 'Уҷ' | 'Рө' | 'С‘' | 'Рё' | 'С–' | 'Рҫ' | 'У©' | 'Сғ' | 'Тұ' | 'ТҜ' | 'СӢ' | 'СҚ' | 'СҺ' | 'СҸ'
  вҶ’ VowelPreceding

// after (v2.3)
'Р°' | 'Уҷ' | 'Рө' | 'С‘'       | 'С–' | 'Рҫ' | 'У©'       | 'Тұ' | 'ТҜ' | 'СӢ' | 'СҚ'       | 'СҸ'
  вҶ’ VowelPreceding
'Р№' | 'СҖ' | 'Сғ' | 'Рё' | 'СҺ'
  вҶ’ HighSonorant
```

Kazakh grammatical rationale: `Сғ`, `Рё`, `СҺ` are glide-vowels вҖ” spelt as letters, but patterning with consonants for P3 `СҒ`-buffer insertion and Y-buffer alternation.

Observable cascade of fixes:

- `realise_s_buffer` no longer inserts `СҒ` after Сғ/Рё/СҺ вҶ’ `РҫТӣСғ+P3` = `РҫТӣСғСӢ` (not `РҫТӣСғСҒСӢ`).
- `realise_y_buffer` now inserts `СӢ/С–` after Сғ/Рё/СҺ вҶ’ `РҫТӣСғ+P1SG` = `РҫТӣСғСӢРј` (not the broken `РҫТӣСғРј`).
- `realise_n` `HighSonorant` branch already existed; existing vowel-cases fall through vowel-path untouched.

Every pre-v2.3 test still passes (328 вҶ’ 335, including +7 graph tests). Zero regressions.

### Extraction delta вҖ” v2.2 вҶ’ v2.3

| Metric | v2.2 | **v2.3** | О” |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| Committed facts | 13 | **15** | +2 (`Р¶РөСҖ вҶ’ РұР°СҒСӮР°Сғ` corrected, `РҫР№ вҶ’ ТӣР°СҖСғ` newly unblocked) |
| Predicates | 2 (IsA, Has) | 2 | вҖ” |
| Clean facts | 13 | **15 (100 %)** | **0 imprecisions remain** |

v2.1 вҶ’ v2.3 arc on the *same committed corpus*:

```
  v2.1 : 11 facts, 4 imprecisions  (Lexicon gaps visible)
  v2.2 : 13 facts, 1 imprecision   (87 Lexicon pollutions purged; 3 fixed, 1 blocked)
  v2.3 : 15 facts, 0 imprecisions  (FST glide-vowel fix unblocks the remainder)
```

The feedback loop is continuous вҖ” every release's diagnostics drive the next release's targets.

### Lexical Graph v0

New module `adam_reasoning::graph` + binary `build_lexical_graph`:

```rust
pub struct GraphEdge {
    pub from: String,
    pub predicate: Predicate,
    pub to: String,
    pub sources: Vec<FactSource>,       // merged provenance
}

pub struct NodeStats {
    pub out_degree: usize,
    pub in_degree: usize,
    pub out_by_predicate: BTreeMap<String, usize>,
    pub in_by_predicate: BTreeMap<String, usize>,
}

pub struct LexicalGraph {
    pub nodes: BTreeMap<String, NodeStats>,
    pub edges: Vec<GraphEdge>,
    pub facts_ingested: usize,
}
```

Build: `LexicalGraph::from_facts(&facts)`. **Pure projection** вҖ” no learned weights, no heuristics beyond what fact extraction already applied. Same facts вҶ’ byte-identical graph.

**Current graph** (15 facts вҶ’ 29 nodes, 15 edges, most-connected node `РұТұР»Р°Тӣ` with degree 2):

```
  Р°РҙР°Рј            --Has       --> РіТҜР»
  Р°Р№Р»Р°РәРөСҖР»С–Рә      --IsA       --> С–СҒ
  Р°РҪР°             --IsA       --> Р¶Р°РҪР°СҲСӢСҖ
  Р°ТӣРёТӣР°СӮ          --IsA       --> СӮС–СҖРөРә
  РұР°Р»Р°            --IsA       --> РұРҫР»Р°СҲР°Тӣ
  РөР»              --Has       --> СҒСӢСҖСӮТӣ
  РөТЈРұРөРә           --IsA       --> ТӣР°Р№РҪР°СҖ
  Р¶РөСҖ             --IsA       --> РұР°СҒСӮР°Сғ
  РәС–СӮР°Рҝ           --IsA       --> РұТұР»Р°Тӣ
  РҫР№              --IsA       --> ТӣР°СҖСғ
  СӮСӢТЈР°Р№СӮТӣСӢСҲ       --Has       --> СӮТҜСҖ
  СӮС–Р»             --IsA       --> Р°Р№РҪР°
  СӢРҪСӮСӢРјР°Тӣ         --IsA       --> РұР°Р№Р»СӢТӣ
  С–Р»С–Рј            --IsA       --> РұТұР»Р°Тӣ
  Т“СӢР»СӢРј           --IsA       --> ТӣР°Р·СӢРҪР°
```

`incoming("РұТұР»Р°Тӣ")` вҶ’ 2 edges (both `РәС–СӮР°Рҝ` and `С–Р»С–Рј` metaphorically map to РұТұР»Р°Тӣ). This is the kind of **connective knowledge** a reasoner will traverse.

### API additions

- `LexicalGraph::from_facts(&[Fact]) -> LexicalGraph`
- `LexicalGraph::outgoing(root) -> Vec<&GraphEdge>` вҖ” "tell me about X"
- `LexicalGraph::incoming(root) -> Vec<&GraphEdge>` вҖ” "what is an X?"
- `GraphEdge { from, predicate, to, sources }`
- `NodeStats { out_degree, in_degree, out_by_predicate, in_by_predicate }`
- Determinism: `BTreeMap`/sorted `Vec` so JSON is byte-identical across runs.

### Committed artifacts

- `data/retrieval/facts.json` regenerated вҖ” 15 facts, 0 imprecisions.
- `data/retrieval/lexical_graph.json` **new** вҖ” 29 nodes, 15 edges, summary + per-node stats.

### Tests (+7 вҶ’ 335 total)

- `empty_facts_empty_graph`, `single_fact_single_edge`, `repeated_triple_merges_sources`
- `node_stats_track_degree_per_predicate`
- `outgoing_and_incoming_lookups`
- `edges_are_deterministically_sorted`
- `graph_round_trips_through_json`

### Zero regressions

FST fix was an invariant improvement вҖ” no existing test relied on the incorrect vowel-class classification. All 328 pre-v2.3 tests still pass.

### Next (v2.4+)

- Lexical graph **enrichment** вҖ” derive additional edges from Lexicon POS + morphological co-occurrence, not just from facts.
- **Rule reasoner v0** вҖ” traverse the graph to answer questions like В«РұР°Р»Р° РҪРөРіРө РұС–Р»С–Рј Р°Р»Р°РҙСӢ?В» вҶ’ chain (РұР°Р»Р° IsA Р°РҙР°Рј) + (Р°РҙР°Рј Has Р¶Р°РҪ) + (Р¶Р°РҪ requires РұС–Р»С–Рј) вҶ’ answer. Deterministic forward-chaining, auditable step-by-step.
- More pattern matchers вҖ” dative-motion (`X Y-РәРө РұР°СҖР°РҙСӢ`), verb-derived action facts.

## [2.2.0] вҖ” 2026-04-21 вҖ” Lexicon pollution purge + possessive-existence pattern (Has predicate)

Minor release. **The v2.1 feedback loop paid off.** v2.1 extracted 11 facts from the committed corpus and named 4 imprecisions. v2.2 investigated each one, found a **systematic Lexicon pollution**, purged it, added the missing roots, and introduced a new `Has` predicate via a third pattern matcher.

### The order-of-magnitude Lexicon finding

v2.1's "РұР°Р»Р° вҶ’ РұРҫР»Р°СҲР°Т“" imprecision was not a one-off вҖ” a scan found **87 intervocalic-voicing-duplicate root pairs** in `segmentation_roots.json`:

```
РәС–СӮР°Рҝ вҶ” РәС–СӮР°Рұ,  СҒР°РұР°Тӣ вҶ” СҒР°РұР°Т“,  ТӣР°Р·Р°Тӣ вҶ” ТӣР°Р·Р°Т“,
РөТЈРұРөРә вҶ” РөТЈРұРөРі,  СӮРҫРҝ   вҶ” СӮРҫРұ,   ... (82 more)
```

The voiced variant (`-Т“`, `-Рі`, `-Рұ`) is never a valid Kazakh stem on its own вҖ” it's the surface result of intervocalic voicing when a vowel-initial suffix attaches to a voiceless-final root. These entries were duplicated during the Apertium import without de-duplication. v2.2 **removes all 87** polluted entries.

The FST parser already handles intervocalic voicing in `surface_could_contain_root` (checks whether a surface starts with the voiced variant of a voiceless-final root). So removing the polluted entries makes parsing **more precise**, not less вҖ” "РұРҫР»Р°СҲР°Т“СӢ" now only resolves to root "РұРҫР»Р°СҲР°Тӣ", not to the ghost root "РұРҫР»Р°СҲР°Т“".

Code audit: `grep -r` across all crates for any of the 87 polluted IDs вҶ’ **zero hits**. Nothing in code depended on the duplication.

### Lexicon additions (data-driven)

Three roots that v2.1 signaled missing:

- `РұР°Р№Р»СӢТӣ` (wealth) вҖ” possessive-final, voiceless
- `РұР°СҒСӮР°Сғ` (source, beginning) вҖ” vowel-final
- `Р¶Р°РҪР°СҲСӢСҖ` (caregiver, sympathizer) вҖ” voiced-consonant-final
  - Note: v2.2 briefly added "Р¶Р°РҪР°СҲСӢ" (wrong root) before the FST parse test revealed the correct form is `Р¶Р°РҪР°СҲСӢСҖ`. Corrected before release.

Total Lexicon delta: **4,516 вҶ’ 4,432 roots** (вҲ’87 pollutions, +3 additions). Net cleaner.

### New pattern: possessive-existence `X-СӮСӢТЈ Y-СҒСӢ РұР°СҖ` вҶ’ `Has`

Kazakh expresses possession via a genitive + P3-possessed + existential `РұР°СҖ` construction. v2.2 adds a third pattern to `adam-reasoning::patterns`:

```
"Р‘Р°Р»Р°РҪСӢТЈ РәС–СӮР°РұСӢ РұР°СҖ"  вҶ’  (РұР°Р»Р°, Has, РәС–СӮР°Рҝ)
"РўСӢТЈР°Р№СӮТӣСӢСҲСӮСӢТЈ СӮТҜСҖР»РөСҖС– (...) РұР°СҖ"  вҶ’  (СӮСӢТЈР°Р№СӮТӣСӢСҲ, Has, СӮТҜСҖ)
```

**Type-checked on FST features**, not strings:

- subject token must have `Case::Genitive` + `part_of_speech == "noun"` + not closed-class;
- object token must immediately follow and have `Possessive::P3` + be a noun;
- existential `РұР°СҖ` must appear elsewhere in the sentence;
- tautology guard (subject вү  object).

**Non-adjacent guard**: intervening words between possessor and possessed break the simple construction вҖ” we refuse rather than guess.

### Predicate set вҖ” 3 predicates

```rust
pub enum Predicate {
    IsA,      // X вҖ” Y                (v2.1 copula)
    LivesIn,  // X Y-РҙР° СӮТұСҖР°РҙСӢ        (v2.1 locative)
    Has,      // X-СӮСӢТЈ Y-СҒСӢ РұР°СҖ       (v2.2 possessive)
}
```

### Extraction yield

| Mode | v2.1 | v2.2 | О” |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Committed samples scanned | 3,191 | 3,191 | вҖ” |
| Facts extracted | 11 | **13** | +2 |
| Distinct predicates | 1 | **2** | +1 |
| Corrected from v2.1 imprecisions | вҖ” | 3 / 4 | СӢРҪСӮСӢРјР°ТӣвҶ’РұР°Р№Р»СӢТӣ, РұР°Р»Р°вҶ’РұРҫР»Р°СҲР°Тӣ, Р°РҪР°вҶ’Р¶Р°РҪР°СҲСӢСҖ |
| Still blocked | вҖ” | 1 | Р¶РөСҖвҶ’СӮС–СҖСҲС–Р»С–Рә (РұР°СҒСӮР°Сғ blocked by separate FST vowel-final+P3 bug) |

The remaining imprecision (Р¶РөСҖвҶ’СӮС–СҖСҲС–Р»С–Рә instead of РұР°СҒСӮР°Сғ) exposes an **FST-level bug** in the vowel-final + P3 code path ("РҫТӣСғСӢ" also fails to parse). Added to `docs/roadmap.md` as a v2.3 agenda item, not blocking v2.2.

### Determinism

Unchanged. Pattern matchers remain pure functions; same corpus вҶ’ byte-identical `facts.json`.

### Tests (+3 вҶ’ 328 total)

- `possessive_extracts_child_has_book` вҖ” positive case with head extraction through P3.
- `possessive_rejects_without_bar` вҖ” missing existential вҶ’ no fact.
- `possessive_rejects_non_adjacent` вҖ” intervening word вҶ’ refuse.
- `Predicate::Has.as_str() == "has"` вҖ” stability check.

### Zero regressions

All 325 pre-v2.2 tests still pass after 87 Lexicon removals. Workspace test count: **303 (v2.0) вҶ’ 325 (v2.1) вҶ’ 328 (v2.2)**.

### What v2.2 does NOT do (deferred)

- **Vowel-final + P3 FST bug** вҖ” "РҫТӣСғСӢ" / "РұР°СҒСӮР°СғСӢ" don't parse. Isolated diagnostic; fix in v2.3.
- **Lexical graph** вҖ” still just a flat list of roots. v2.3 will build typed edges (is_a, has_role, related_to) over roots.
- **Rule reasoner** вҖ” v2.3+.
- **Scale** вҖ” committed extraction still at 500 samples/pack cap. Full corpus run remains gitignored-local.

### Next (v2.3)

- Fix the vowel-final + P3 FST bug.
- Start building the **Lexical-Morphemic Knowledge Graph** вҖ” root-level edges derived from fact accumulation + POS co-occurrence. Deterministic construction; no learned weights.

## [2.1.0] вҖ” 2026-04-21 вҖ” ILMRR bootstrap: fact extraction (copula pattern, typed provenance)

Minor release. **First step toward reasoning.** Our v2.0 system is a smart retrieval engine вҖ” it quotes. v2.1 starts extracting **structured facts** from the corpus: `(subject, predicate, object)` triples with full provenance, typed `ConfidenceKind`, and deterministic head extraction via FST.

This is the first rung of the ladder laid out in [`docs/architecture_v2.md`](docs/architecture_v2.md#post-v20-directions-committed-but-not-shipped) and discussed as **ILMRR вҖ” Intelligent Lexical-Morphemic Retrieval & Reasoning**. v2.1 is the infrastructure: facts as data. v2.2 will add the lexical graph; v2.3 the rule reasoner.

### New crate: `adam-reasoning`

- **`Fact { subject, predicate, object, pattern, source, confidence, raw_text }`** вҖ” structured knowledge with every field typed and traceable.
- **`Predicate` enum** вҖ” v2.1 ships two: `IsA`, `LivesIn`. Every addition is an intentional architectural decision.
- **`ConfidenceKind` enum** вҖ” **categorical** evidence type (Grammar, CuratedQuote, RepeatedPattern, HumanApproved, RuleInferred). Explicitly not an LLM probability; consumers filter by kind, not by magnitude. Reaffirms `project_retrieval_not_neural_v2`.
- **`SlotRef { surface, root, pos }`** вҖ” every slot carries the canonical root, not just the surface. Possessive-suffixed "РұТұР»Р°Т“СӢ" correctly yields root "РұТұР»Р°Тӣ".
- **`FactSource { pack, sample_id }`** вҖ” identical shape to `adam_retrieval::SampleRef`, kept independent to avoid a reasoningвҶ’retrieval dep cycle.
- **`extract_facts(text, parses, lexicon, source) -> Vec<Fact>`** вҖ” pure function. Same input вҶ’ same facts, byte-identical across runs.

### Pattern matchers (v2.1)

1. **Copula `X вҖ” Y`** вҶ’ `IsA` вҖ” uses Kazakh em-dash as a syntactic anchor. **Strict LHS** (single bare nominative noun). **Head-extracted RHS** (right-to-left FST scan; possessive "Y-СҒС–" correctly resolves to root Y). Guards: вүӨ4-token RHS cap, parenthetical noise stripped, tautology (`subj == obj`) rejected.
2. **Locative-existential `X Y-РҙР° СӮТұСҖР°РҙСӢ`** вҶ’ `LivesIn` вҖ” requires the verb `СӮТұСҖСғ` in any inflected form + a `Case::Locative` noun + a bare-nominative subject. Pronouns rejected as non-content subjects.

### New binary: `extract_facts`

Walks committed corpus packs, runs every pattern matcher on each sample, emits structured JSON. Two modes:

- **default** вҖ” first 500 samples per pack, writes committed `data/retrieval/facts.json`.
- **`--full`** вҖ” every sample, writes gitignored `data/retrieval/facts_full.json`.
- **`--limit N`** вҖ” custom per-pack cap.

Progress is streamed to stderr every 1,000 samples (flushed) вҖ” no more silent minutes.

### Baseline вҖ” 11 facts from 3,191 samples

Extraction over the committed corpus yielded **11 facts** (37.8 s). Precision:

- **7 clean**: С–Р»С–РјвҶ’РұТұР»Р°Тӣ, Р°Р№Р»Р°РәРөСҖР»С–РәвҶ’С–СҒ, РәС–СӮР°РҝвҶ’РұТұР»Р°Тӣ, Т“СӢР»СӢРјвҶ’ТӣР°Р·СӢРҪР°, СӮС–Р»вҶ’Р°Р№РҪР°, Р°ТӣРёТӣР°СӮвҶ’СӮС–СҖРөРә, РөТЈРұРөРәвҶ’ТӣР°Р№РҪР°СҖ.
- **4 Lexicon-gap cases**: СӢРҪСӮСӢРјР°ТӣвҶ’С…Р°Р»СӢТӣ (should be РұР°Р№Р»СӢТӣ), Р°РҪР°вҶ’РұР°Р»Р° (should be Р¶Р°РҪР°СҲСӢ), Р¶РөСҖвҶ’СӮС–СҖСҲС–Р»С–Рә (should be РұР°СҒСӮР°Сғ), РұР°Р»Р°вҶ’РұРҫР»Р°СҲР°Т“ (FST intervocalic-voicing issue on РұРҫР»Р°СҲР°Тӣ).

The 4 imprecisions are not pattern bugs вҖ” they are **concrete Lexicon gaps** (РұР°Р№Р»СӢТӣ, Р¶Р°РҪР°СҲСӢ, РұР°СҒСӮР°Сғ) + **one FST voicing regression** (РұРҫР»Р°СҲР°Тӣ). These become the v2.2 agenda.

All 11 facts have `(pack, sample_id)` provenance вҶ’ every fact is auditable back to its corpus sentence.

### Determinism contract

- Pattern matchers: pure functions of `(text, parses, lexicon, source)`.
- RHS head extraction: deterministic right-to-left walk + deterministic FST parse.
- `extract_facts` output: samples scanned in pack order, then `samples[]` order within pack. Same corpus вҶ’ byte-identical `facts.json`.

### Tests (+22 вҶ’ 325 total)

- 3 lib tests: predicate/confidence strings, Fact JSON round-trip.
- 19 pattern tests: copula positive + 7 negatives (no dash, double dash, inflected, tautology, multi-token LHS, long RHS clause, parenthetical noise), locative positive + 2 negatives (no СӮТұСҖСғ, pronoun subject), head-extraction helpers.

### What v2.1 does NOT do

- **No multi-sentence chains.** `extract_facts` is per-sample.
- **No rule inference.** The Reasoner (v2.3) will combine facts into new facts; v2.1 only extracts.
- **No lexical graph.** v2.2 will build `is_a` / `has_role` / `related_to` edges over roots and connect facts to them.

### Workspace tests

**325 passing** (303 вҶ’ +22 reasoning).

### Committed artifacts

- `data/retrieval/facts.json` вҖ” 11-fact v2.1 baseline, ~4 KB. CI will regenerate on every reasoning-crate change and diff.

## [2.0.0] вҖ” 2026-04-20 вҖ” v2.0: commitment release, retrieval-as-v2.0, investor-demoable

Major release. **Not a feature drop вҖ” an architectural commitment.**

v2.0 freezes the answer to the question `project_retrieval_not_neural_v2` has been circling since v1.6.0:

> **v2.0 is not a trained neural model. It is a deterministic retrieval + composition engine over a 77.9 M-word Kazakh corpus.**

Everything functional is already in v1.9.5. v2.0 adds:

### 1. Demo binaries

- **`adam_chat` v2.0** вҖ” now auto-loads the committed morpheme index and enables retrieval by default. New flags:
  - `--no-retrieval` вҖ” reproduces v1.1.0 noun-echo behaviour (regression reference).
  - `--compose` вҖ” opts into `ComposeMode::InSampleCitySwap`. Banner prints the В«РұРөР№С–РјРҙ-В» marker policy so the user knows what to expect.
- **`adam_demo` (new)** вҖ” scripted 15-turn end-to-end walkthrough. Three parts:
  - Part 1: the full social + retrieval arc under `Verbatim`.
  - Part 2: same script under `InSampleCitySwap` вҖ” most swaps refused by guards (the safe case).
  - Part 3: synthetic sample explicitly triggering the swap path, so the v1.9.5 В«РұРөР№С–РјРҙ-В» marker is visible in action.
  Fully deterministic. Re-runs print byte-identical output.

### 2. Canonical architecture doc вҖ” `docs/architecture_v2.md`

Single source of truth for the v2.0 pipeline. Diagrams the 5 layers + the 2.5/2.75 retrieval-injection sub-layers. Lists all three response paths and the guarantees each carries. Catalogues the determinism contract, safety guards, and trade-offs accepted. Points at every concern-to-file mapping for future contributors.

### 3. README restructure

Investor-facing **"Why adam"** comparison table lands first вҖ” explicit positioning against mainstream LLMs: 0 hallucinations vs non-zero, byte-identical determinism vs temperature-dependent, ms-on-CPU vs dollars-on-GPU, full provenance vs none. The rest of the README was already current at v1.9.5; v2.0 updates the banner version + demo section (`adam_demo` instructions + `adam_chat` flag reference).

### 4. Commitment declarations

Explicit in the README "Out of scope" and the architecture doc's "What v2.0 is NOT" section:

- **Not a trained neural model.** No parameters. No embeddings. No PyTorch.
- **Not multilingual.** Kazakh-only surface.
- **Not generative.** Every token is from a template, a corpus sample, or FST synthesis.
- **Not a generalist.** 26 intents + retrieval, honest В«СӮТҜСҒС–РҪРұРөРҙС–РјВ» outside.
- **Not self-modifying.** Separate architectural direction if ever; not v2.x.

### What v2.0 does NOT change

- **No new crates.** All v2.0 work is binaries + docs on top of the v1.9.5 code surface.
- **No new tests.** The 303 tests from v1.9.5 carry forward unchanged.
- **No behaviour change at the library API.** `Conversation::turn` is bit-for-bit the same function. `MorphemeIndex` serialisation is unchanged. Embedders who upgrade see zero semantic diff.
- **No index format change.** Existing `data/retrieval/morpheme_index.json` files are still valid.

### Determinism audit (reaffirmed at v2.0)

- FST synthesis is a pure function.
- FST parse enumerates deterministically.
- `MorphemeIndex::rank` ties on `(pack, sample_id)` lex order.
- `compose_with_city` is a pure function; no RNG.
- `inject_retrieval_example` does NOT consult `rng_seed`.
- `adam_demo` re-runs print byte-identical output.

Same `(input, session, seed)` вҶ’ byte-identical response, across runs, machines, and time.

### Workspace tests

**303 passing** (unchanged from v1.9.5). The v2.0 binary additions are thin glue on top of already-tested library code.

### Post-v2.0 directions (committed but not shipped)

- **Option C** вҖ” pre-compute `(pattern, slot_types)` pairs at index-build time. Keeps runtime cheap; enables swap types beyond city.
- **Kazakh technical corpus** вҖ” translate key chapters of the Rust Book into Kazakh as a new source pack. Doubles as educational material and corpus-vocabulary expansion.
- **Diversity** вҖ” allow consecutive turns for the same query to cite different top-ranked samples. Current top-1 is deterministic by design.

These are v2.x / v3.x work, not v2.0 scope.

## [1.9.5] вҖ” 2026-04-20 вҖ” Composition-marker framing (adapted-evidence template family)

Patch release restoring the **traceability contract** broken in v1.9.0. When `ComposeMode::InSampleCitySwap` silently rewrote a quoted corpus line, the user saw the adapted text in В«вҖҰВ» and could easily assume it was the original source. That's a trust violation вҖ” even if the swap was grammatically correct and semantically benign.

v1.9.5 makes the adaptation **explicit in the response itself**. The planner now routes swapped responses through a separate `unknown.with_adapted_evidence` template family whose every template contains the word stem **В«РұРөР№С–РјРҙ-В»** ("adapt-"). Verbatim quotes stay on the v1.8.0 `unknown.with_evidence` family.

### Before / after

```text
Corpus: "Р‘Р°Р»Р° РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢРҙР° Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢ У©РјС–СҖ СҒТҜСҖРөРҙС–"
Session: { city: "РЁСӢРјРәРөРҪСӮ" }
Mode: InSampleCitySwap

v1.9.0 (silent):
< РЁСӢРјРәРөРҪСӮСӮРө СӮТұСҖР°СӮСӢРҪ СҒС–Р·РіРө РұР°Р»Р° СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РјСӢРҪР°РҙР°Р№ РҙРөСҖРөРә:
  В«Р‘Р°Р»Р° РЁСӢРјРәРөРҪСӮСӮРө Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢ У©РјС–СҖ СҒТҜСҖРөРҙС–В»    вҶҗ user has no way to know the quote was adapted

v1.9.5 (explicit marker):
< Р‘ТұР» РұРөР№С–РјРҙРөР»РіРөРҪ РҪТұСҒТӣР° (СӮТҜРҝРҪТұСҒТӣР°РҙР° РұР°СҒТӣР° ТӣР°Р»Р° Р°СӮР°Р»Т“Р°РҪ):
  В«Р‘Р°Р»Р° РЁСӢРјРәРөРҪСӮСӮРө Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢ У©РјС–СҖ СҒТҜСҖРөРҙС–В»    вҶҗ the frame literally says "adapted version,
                                           different city in the original"
< РұР°Р»Р° СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РәРҫСҖРҝСғСҒСӮР°Т“СӢ РұС–СҖ Р¶РҫР»РҙСӢ СҒС–Р·РҙС–ТЈ ТӣР°Р»Р°ТЈСӢР·Т“Р° РұРөР№С–РјРҙРөРҝ РәУ©СҖРҙС–Рј:
  В«Р‘Р°Р»Р° РЁСӢРјРәРөРҪСӮСӮРө Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢ У©РјС–СҖ СҒТҜСҖРөРҙС–В»    вҶҗ "I adapted a corpus line to your city"
```

### Changes

- **`adam-dialog::intent::Intent::Unknown`** gains a new field `example_adapted: bool`. Defaults to `false`; `#[serde(default)]` so deserialising older traces still works.
- **`adam-dialog::planner`** routes:
  - `example.is_some() && example_adapted` вҶ’ `"unknown.with_adapted_evidence"` *(new)*
  - `example.is_some()` вҶ’ `"unknown.with_evidence"` *(v1.8.0 verbatim path)*
  - `noun_hint.is_some()` вҶ’ `"unknown.with_noun"` *(v1.1.0)*
  - else вҶ’ `"unknown"` *(v1.0.0)*
- **`Conversation::maybe_compose`** now returns `(String, bool)` вҖ” the flag propagates to `example_adapted` in `Intent::Unknown`. No caller outside `Conversation` is exposed to the internal API change.
- **New template family** `unknown.with_adapted_evidence` (5 templates) in `data/dialog/templates/v1.toml`. Every single template contains the В«РұРөР№С–РјРҙ-В» stem so consumers can grep for it as a runtime marker. FST-aware `{city|locative}` renders the user's city harmony-correctly.

### Safety invariants (new)

Two tests enforce the bi-directional guarantee:

| Direction | Test | Guarantee |
|---|---|---|
| **When swap happened** вҶ’ marker must fire | `adapted_evidence_templates_announce_the_adaptation` | the В«РұРөР№С–РјРҙ-В» stem appears in the output for at least one seed under `InSampleCitySwap` + actual swap |
| **When no swap** вҶ’ marker must NOT fire | `verbatim_mode_never_claims_adaptation` | the В«РұРөР№С–РјРҙ-В» stem is absent for every seed under `Verbatim` mode |

The second guarantee is the trust-critical one: v1.9.5 must never claim to have adapted a quote it didn't actually adapt.

### Determinism

Unchanged. `example_adapted` is a pure function of `(retrieved text, session city, compose_mode)`. Template selection still honours `template_is_fillable` + seed-mod.

### Tests (+2 вҶ’ 303 total)

- `adapted_evidence_templates_announce_the_adaptation` вҖ” swap fires вҶ’ marker fires.
- `verbatim_mode_never_claims_adaptation` вҖ” no swap вҶ’ no marker, ever.

### What's next (v2.0 territory, not v1.9.x)

- **Option C** вҖ” pre-compute `(pattern, slot_types)` pairs at index-build time. Keeps runtime cheap, lets us audit swap candidates offline, and is a prerequisite for swap types beyond city (names-in-biography, numbers-in-dates). Not a patch.
- **v2.0 stabilisation** вҖ” freeze the retrieval-as-v2.0 commitment (`project_retrieval_not_neural_v2`), run end-to-end demos, cut the investor-demoable v2.0 tag.

## [1.9.0] вҖ” 2026-04-20 вҖ” In-sample city swap (option B, opt-in, year-guarded)

Minor release. First step into **option B** territory вҖ” the retrieved corpus quote is no longer guaranteed byte-identical to the source. When the user opts into `ComposeMode::InSampleCitySwap` and the session has a known Kazakh city, city mentions inside the cited sample are rewritten to the user's city, feature-preserving via the FST. v1.8.5 and earlier behaviour (`ComposeMode::Verbatim`, the default) is unchanged.

### What changes вҖ” and what doesn't

- **Grammaticality still FST-guaranteed.** `synthesise_noun(user_city, features)` produces the harmonically-correct surface (РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢ+locative вҶ’ РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢРҙР°, РЁСӢРјРәРөРҪСӮ+locative вҶ’ РЁСӢРјРәРөРҪСӮСӮРө).
- **Semantic truthfulness is no longer guaranteed.** That is the honest trade-off of option B. A composed sentence may say something true, or it may produce a plausible but non-factual claim. Earlier releases never did this.
- **Safety guards are explicit, conservative, and auditable:**
  - **Closed city list** (`PLACE_NAMES`): 20 editorially-curated Kazakh cities are the only eligible swap targets. Other proper nouns and common nouns are never touched.
  - **User-side recognition:** the user's proposed city must itself be in `PLACE_NAMES`, otherwise the FST can't re-synthesise reliably.
  - **Biographical-year guard:** any 4-digit year in [1500, 2100] refuses the whole swap. This keeps biographies ("РҗРұР°Р№ 1845 Р¶СӢР»СӢ ТҡР°СҖТӣР°СҖР°Р»СӢРҙР° СӮСғТ“Р°РҪ") untouched вҖ” we must not rewrite "ТҡР°СҖТӣР°СҖР°Р»СӢРҙР°" to the user's city and fabricate a birth fact.
  - **No name or number swaps.** Names-in-biography and numerals-in-dates are exactly the categories that would produce the worst fabrications; explicitly out of scope for v1.9.0.

### Opt-in вҖ” `ComposeMode`

```rust
use adam_dialog::{ComposeMode, Conversation};

// Default: byte-identical corpus quote (v1.8.5 behaviour).
let conv_safe = Conversation::new().with_morpheme_index(idx.clone());

// Opt-in: city mentions inside the quote rewrite to user.session.city.
let conv_swap = Conversation::new()
    .with_morpheme_index(idx)
    .with_compose_mode(ComposeMode::InSampleCitySwap);
```

Same call site, same type, one explicit setter. Embedders who don't opt in see zero behavioural change.

### New API вҖ” `adam_retrieval::compose`

```rust
pub const PLACE_NAMES: &[&str];        // the 20-city editorial list

pub struct Swap {
    pub token_index: usize,
    pub from: String,
    pub to: String,
    pub user_root: String,
    pub features: NounFeatures,
}

pub struct Composition {
    pub original: String,
    pub output: String,
    pub swaps: Vec<Swap>,
}
impl Composition {
    pub fn was_changed(&self) -> bool;
    pub fn trace(&self) -> String;         // per-swap provenance for --trace
}

pub fn compose_with_city(
    sample_text: &str,
    user_city: &str,
    lexicon: &LexiconV1,
) -> Composition;
```

Every swap preserves full FST feature provenance: case, number, possessive, predicate. `Composition::trace()` emits a per-swap line usable by `adam_chat --trace` (e.g. `[2] РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢРҙР° вҶ’ РЁСӢРјРәРөРҪСӮСӮРө (root=СҲСӢРјРәРөРҪСӮ, case=Some(Locative))`).

### Determinism

- `compose_with_city` is a pure function; no rng, no system time.
- First-match policy by token order, deterministic.
- FST synthesis is itself deterministic.
- Same `(sample, user_city, lexicon)` вҶ’ byte-identical `Composition` across runs.

### Tests (+11)

**Unit tests in `adam-retrieval::compose` (+8):**

- `no_swap_when_user_city_unknown` вҖ” city outside `PLACE_NAMES` вҶ’ no-op.
- `no_swap_when_text_has_biographical_year` вҖ” biography guard fires.
- `swaps_city_preserving_locative` вҖ” РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢРҙР° вҶ’ РЁСӢРјРәРөРҪСӮСӮРө.
- `preserves_capitalisation_on_swap`.
- `no_swap_when_city_matches_user_city` вҖ” identity is no-op.
- `preserves_trailing_punctuation` вҖ” commas and periods survive.
- `trace_records_swap_details` вҖ” trace line is well-formed.
- `year_guard_ignores_short_digit_runs` вҖ” "25 Р¶Р°СҒ" does NOT trigger the guard.

**Dialog e2e tests (+3):**

- `compose_mode_swaps_cities_in_retrieval_samples` вҖ” `InSampleCitySwap` + `session.city=РЁСӢРјРәРөРҪСӮ` + synthetic "Р‘Р°Р»Р° РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢРҙР° ..." вҶ’ quote rewrites to РЁСӢРјРәРөРҪСӮСӮРө.
- `compose_mode_verbatim_preserves_retrieved_quote` вҖ” default mode keeps РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢРҙР° in the quote (the v1.8.5 frame template can still say РЁСӢРјРәРөРҪСӮСӮРө outside В«вҖҰВ»).
- `compose_mode_respects_biographical_year_guard` вҖ” "РҗРұР°Р№ 1845 Р¶СӢР»СӢ ТҡР°СҖТӣР°СҖР°Р»СӢРҙР° ..." stays put under `InSampleCitySwap`.

### Workspace tests

**301 tests pass** (290 вҶ’ +11).

### Next (v1.9.5 candidates)

- Wrap swap-mode responses in a template that explicitly marks the composition ("СҒС–Р·РҙС–ТЈ ТӣР°Р»Р°ТЈСӢР·РҙСӢТЈ Р°СҸСҒСӢРҪРҙР°..."), so readers know the quote was adapted.
- Extract patterns at index-build time (option C) so composition isn't done at runtime per turn.
- Experiments on name / year composition with stricter sanity guards.

## [1.8.5] вҖ” 2026-04-20 вҖ” Locative+P1Sg bug fix, FST-aware city slots, comprehensive README refresh

Patch release. Fixes the `-РјСӢРҪ` greedy-strip bug in `detect_statement_of_occupation`, wires the existing `{slot|features}` syntax into v1.8.0's session-aware templates, and brings the README fully in sync with the v1.5.0вҖ“v1.8.0 retrieval-era arc.

### Bug fix вҖ” locative+P1Sg is a location statement, not an occupation

Before v1.8.5:

```
user: РјРөРҪ РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢРҙР°РјСӢРҪ
conv.session:
  { name: "Р”УҷСғР»РөСӮ", occupation: "Р°Р»РјР°СӮСӢ" }   вқҢ wrong вҖ” "РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢ" is not an occupation
```

The FST correctly parsed `РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢРҙР°РјСӢРҪ` as `РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢ + locative + P1Sg`, but `detect_statement_of_occupation` Priority 1 accepted any noun with `Predicate::P1Sg` regardless of case, so the city got slotted as an occupation. `detect_statement_of_location` required an explicit `СӮТұСҖР°РјСӢРҪ / СӮТұСҖР°РјСӢР·` verb co-occurring with the locative and didn't trigger on the bare `locative+P1Sg` stack.

v1.8.5 fixes both ends:

- `detect_statement_of_location` now accepts **any** Noun with both `Case::Locative` and `Predicate::P1Sg` вҖ” a standalone self-locative ("I am in X") is a location statement by itself, no verb required.
- `detect_statement_of_occupation` Priority 1 now **rejects** `Case::Locative` and `Case::Ablative` вҖ” those cases mean "in / from X", not "I am X (profession)".

Result:

```
user: РјРөРҪ РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢРҙР°РјСӢРҪ
conv.session:
  { name: "Р”УҷСғР»РөСӮ", city: "РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢ" }   вң… correct
response: "Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢ Р¶РөСҖ"
```

### FST-aware session slots in retrieval templates

The v1.8.0 session-aware templates used literal case marking (`{city}-РҙР°`). This is both ugly (dangling hyphen: `РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢ-РҙР°` instead of `РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢРҙР°`) and wrong for vowel harmony (РҗСҒСӮР°РҪР°-РҙР° / УЁСҒРәРөРјРөРҪ-РҙРө: one "Р°", one "Рө", and the planner can't know which).

v1.8.5 swaps the literals for `{slot|features}`:

```toml
# v1.8.0 (literal, wrong harmony):
"{city}-РҙР° СӮТұСҖР°СӮСӢРҪ СҒС–Р· ТҜСҲС–РҪ {noun} Р¶Р°Р№СӢРҪРҙР°: В«{example}В»"

# v1.8.5 (FST, correct harmony):
"{city|locative} СӮТұСҖР°СӮСӢРҪ СҒС–Р·РіРө {noun} СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РјСӢРҪР°РҙР°Р№ РҙРөСҖРөРә: В«{example}В»"
```

`{city|locative}` routes through `adam_kernel_fst::morphotactics::synthesise_noun`, so РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢ вҶ’ РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢРҙР°, РҗСҒСӮР°РҪР° вҶ’ РҗСҒСӮР°РҪР°РҙР°, УЁСҒРәРөРјРөРҪ вҶ’ УЁСҒРәРөРјРөРҪРҙРө automatically. Demo at seed=6:

```
РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢРҙР° СӮТұСҖР°СӮСӢРҪ СҒС–Р·РіРө РұР°Р»Р° СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РјСӢРҪР°РҙР°Р№ РҙРөСҖРөРә:
В«РҡС–Рј СҒРөРҪРҙРөСҖРҙС– РұР°Р»Р°Р»Р°СҖ, СҒТҜР№Рө-СӮТұТ“СӢРҪ, ТҡСғР°РҪСӢСҲСӢТЈР° ТӣСғР°РҪСӢРҝ, ТӣР°Р№Т“СӢТЈР° РәТҜР№Рө-СӮТұТ“СӢРҪВ»
```

No dangling hyphen; harmonically correct locative suffix.

### Comprehensive README refresh

The README had drifted since v1.4.5. Every stale reference is fixed:

- **Version badge** 1.4.5 вҶ’ 1.8.5.
- **Retrieval badge** added; **corpus badge** added showing `77.9 M local / 4 M committed`; **test count** 288 вҶ’ 290.
- **Demo** updated to v1.8.5: shows the v1.8.5 locative fix, the v1.6.0+ retrieval-engine path (`РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢРҙР° СӮТұСҖР°СӮСӢРҪ СҒС–Р·РіРө... В«РҗРұР°Р№ Wikisource quoteВ»`), and session-aware frame composition.
- **Architecture** table now lists `adam-retrieval` as a proper L1 crate alongside the others. Counts corrected (11 archiphonemes, 36 suffix templates).
- **New section**: "Retrieval engine (v1.6.0вҖ“v1.8.5)" вҖ” documents the `retrieve вҶ’ rank вҶ’ compose` path with the full composite scoring formula, determinism guarantees, and provenance contract.
- **Kazakh-only recogniser** section now points at the retrieval engine instead of a future trained LM.
- **Technical specification** rewritten: committed corpus words (3.84 M), local corpus words (77.9 M), morpheme-coverage baseline (79.48 %), FST parser throughput (1.155 ms/word), committed morpheme index size (3,191 / 3,082 / 16,262), full-corpus rebuild procedure, 26 intents (was 25), 31 template families, 290 tests.
- **History** extended with the "v1.5.0вҖ“v1.8.5 retrieval era" section explaining each release's contribution to the retrieve вҶ’ rank вҶ’ compose ladder.
- **Out of scope** rewritten: multilingual removed, "compact trained LM" removed, replaced with the honest commitment that v2.0 is the retrieval engine, not a neural model.

### Tests (+2)

- `locative_with_copula_is_location_not_occupation` вҖ” regression test for the `-РјСӢРҪ` bug fix.
- `session_aware_city_template_uses_fst_locative` вҖ” verifies at least one seed produces FST-rendered `РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢРҙР°` (not `РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢ-РҙР°`) when a `{city|locative}` template fires.

### Workspace tests

**290 tests pass** (288 вҶ’ +2).

### What's next

- **v1.9.0** вҖ” option B/C territory: in-sample slot swap. Risky вҖ” it's where we leave the "retrieved text is immutable" safety. Needs semantic-sanity guards before shipping.

## [1.8.0] вҖ” 2026-04-20 вҖ” Session-aware compositional synthesis (option A: frame-only, retrieved quote stays verbatim)

Minor release. First step in the **retrieve вҶ’ compose вҶ’ respond** ladder described in the v1.7.0 release notes. This release commits to **option A** of the three compositional-synthesis variants we debated: composition happens **around** the retrieved sample, never **inside** it. Zero fabrication risk; the retrieved sentence stays byte-identical to the corpus.

### The contract

- **Retrieved quote is immutable.** No slot-swapping inside the guillemets. Whatever the corpus says, the corpus still says.
- **Frame becomes session-aware.** When the user has told us their `name`, `city`, `age`, or `occupation`, the planner prefers a template that personalises the wrapper around the citation.
- **Still deterministic.** The planner's template pool filter (`template_is_fillable`) automatically gates session-aware templates on slot presence. No new conditional logic, no runtime trickery.

### Visible effect

```
# Before (v1.7.0, session = {name: "Р”УҷСғР»РөСӮ"})
< РұР°Р»Р° СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РјСӢРҪР°РҙР°Р№ РұС–СҖ Р¶Р°Р·РұР° РәРөР·РҙРөСҒСӮС–СҖРҙС–Рј:
  В«РҡС–Рј СҒРөРҪРҙРөСҖРҙС– РұР°Р»Р°Р»Р°СҖ, СҒТҜР№Рө-СӮТұТ“СӢРҪ...В»

# After (v1.8.0, same session, session-aware templates now in pool)
< РЎС–Р·, Р”УҷСғР»РөСӮ, РұР°Р»Р° СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ СҒТұСҖР°Рҝ СӮТұСҖСҒСӢР· РұР°. РңСӢРҪР°РҙР°Р№ РҙРөСҖРөРә РұР°СҖ:
  В«РҡС–Рј СҒРөРҪРҙРөСҖРҙС– РұР°Р»Р°Р»Р°СҖ, СҒТҜР№Рө-СӮТұТ“СӢРҪ...В»

# After (v1.8.0, session = {name: "Р”УҷСғР»РөСӮ", city: "РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢ"})
< Р”УҷСғР»РөСӮ, РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢ-РҙР° СӮТұСҖР°СӮСӢРҪ СҒС–Р· ТҜСҲС–РҪ РұР°Р»Р° Р¶Р°Р№СӢРҪРҙР°:
  В«РҡС–Рј СҒРөРҪРҙРөСҖРҙС– РұР°Р»Р°Р»Р°СҖ, СҒТҜР№Рө-СӮТұТ“СӢРҪ...В»
```

The quote is the same Abai verse in every case. The frame adapts to what the dialog remembers.

### Changes

- **`data/dialog/templates/v1.toml`** вҖ” `unknown.with_evidence` grows from 4 to 10 templates (6 new session-aware variants: 2 Г— `{name}`, 1 Г— `{city}`, 1 Г— `{name}+{city}`, 1 Г— `{age}`, 1 Г— `{occupation}`). `unknown.with_noun` similarly grows from 5 to 10 with session-aware variants.
- **Planner**: no code change. The existing `template_is_fillable` + session merge does all the work. This is the whole design thesis of option A вҖ” composition implemented as pure data.
- **Tests (+2)**:
  - `unknown_with_session_and_evidence_personalises_frame` вҖ” with `name` in session, at least one seed picks a personalised template.
  - `unknown_with_session_name_and_city_can_use_combined_frame` вҖ” with both slots, at least one seed picks a template combining them.

### Known bug (not addressed this release)

Input like В«РјРөРҪ РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢРҙР°РјСӢРҪВ» (I'm in Almaty) is mis-classified by `detect_statement_of_occupation` because the recogniser greedy-strips `-РјСӢРҪ` and treats the residue as an occupation surface вҖ” session ends up with `occupation: "Р°Р»РјР°СӮСӢ"` instead of `city: "РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢ"`. This is a pre-v1.8.0 semantics bug, orthogonal to composition. The test for the combined-frame path sets the session directly to bypass it. Planned for v1.8.5.

### Determinism audit

- No new random call sites.
- No new runtime-conditional routing вҖ” templates decide activation purely by slot presence, which is itself deterministic.
- `rng_seed` still picks among the filtered pool, as before.

Same session + same input + same seed вҶ’ byte-identical output.

### What v1.8.0 does NOT do (deferred вҖ” option B/C territory)

- **No in-sample slot swap.** We do NOT replace proper nouns or numerals inside the retrieved quote. That's true compositional synthesis, with all the semantic-fabrication risk it brings. Deferred explicitly.
- **No FST-aware re-inflection of session slots.** Templates use session values as-is; Kazakh case marking still comes from the hand-written `-РҙР°`, `-РјРөРҪ`, etc. in the template text. v0.9.5's `{slot|features}` is available but not yet wired into the new v1.8.0 templates; future templates can upgrade.
- **No semantic sanity check.** Even the frame could say weird things like "{city} СӮТұСҖТ“СӢРҪСӢ ТҜСҲС–РҪ..." when the user is only visiting. Narrowing phrasing is polish, not scope.

### Workspace tests

**288 tests pass** (286 вҶ’ +2 dialog e2e).

### Next (v1.8.5)

Fix the `-РјСӢРҪ` greedy-strip bug in `detect_statement_of_occupation`. Wire `{slot|features}` into 2вҖ“3 session-aware templates to demonstrate FST-aware case marking on session slots (e.g. `{city|locative}` instead of the literal `{city}-РҙР°`). Still option A вҖ” retrieved quote stays verbatim.

## [1.7.0] вҖ” 2026-04-20 вҖ” Deterministic retrieval ranking: overlap + purity + length + loanword penalty

Minor release. `MorphemeIndex::rank` replaces "first matching posting" with a composite deterministic score. Dialog now ranks over **every content root** parsed from the user's input, not just the first noun вҖ” so a sentence mentioning both `РұР°Р»Р°` and `РјРөРәСӮРөРҝ` outranks one that mentions only `РұР°Р»Р°` for the input В«РұР°Р»Р° РјРөРәСӮРөРҝРәРө РұР°СҖРҙСӢВ». This is where the retrieval engine starts behaving like a *search* engine rather than a bag dip.

### The formula

```
score = 0.40 В· overlap_ratio            // main "smart" signal
      + 0.30 В· pack_purity              // main "safe" signal
      + 0.15 В· length_goodness(words)   // Gaussian around 8 words
      вҲ’ 0.15 В· loanword_density         // preserves Kazakh-first thesis
```

All four components are deterministic pure functions of `(sample, input_morphemes)`. Ties break by `(pack, sample_id)`. Zero randomness, zero training.

### Editorial pack purity priors

Encoded in `RankConfig::default()`:

| Pack | Prior | Why |
|---|---:|---|
| Abai Wikisource, Kazakh classics, proverbs | 1.00 | centuries of curation |
| Synthetic, Tatoeba, Common Voice | 0.95 | Lexicon-bounded / selected |
| Wikipedia KZ | 0.85 | edited but technical loanwords |
| CC-100 (web crawl) | 0.75 | weakest source |

Unknown packs fall back to `DEFAULT_UNKNOWN_PACK_PURITY` (0.70).

### Visible effect (same prompts, v1.6.5 вҶ’ v1.7.0)

| Prompt | v1.6.5 cited | v1.7.0 cited |
|---|---|---|
| В«РұР°Р»Р° СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢвҖҰВ» | "РҡРөСҲРө РұР°Р»Р° РөТЈ, РәРөР»РҙС–ТЈ Т“РҫР№ СӮР°Р»Р°Р№ Р¶Р°СҒТӣР°вҖҰ" (11w, Abai) | "РҡС–Рј СҒРөРҪРҙРөСҖРҙС– РұР°Р»Р°Р»Р°СҖ, СҒТҜР№Рө-СӮТұТ“СӢРҪвҖҰ" (8w, Abai) |
| В«РјРөРәСӮРөРҝ РәРөСҖРөРә РҝРөВ» | CC-100 bureaucratic paragraph (36w) | "РёУҷ РјТұРҪР°Р№-РіР°Р· Р¶Р°Р»Р°ТӣСӢРҪСӢ СӮР°Т“СӢР»РҙСӢ РҪРөРјРөСҒРө СӮР°Р·Р° РјРөРәСӮРөРҝ СҒТҜР№РөРҪРөРҙС–." (8w, CC-100) |
| В«Р°РҙР°Рј РҪРө С–СҒСӮРөСҒРөвҖҰВ» | "РҗРҙР°Рј вҖ” РұС–СҖ РұРҫТӣ РәУ©СӮРөСҖРіРөРҪ РұРҫТӣСӮСӢТЈ ТӣР°РұСӢвҖҰ" (Abai, crude) | "РҗТӣСӢР»РҙСӢ Р°РҙР°Рј СҒУ©Р·С–РҪ РҙРө, С–СҒС–РҪ РҙРө У©Р»СҲРөРҝ Р°Р№СӮР°СҖ." (Abai proverb) |

Ranking picked the shorter, cleaner, more topical option every time. Still deterministic.

### Changes

- **`adam-retrieval`**:
  - `Hit { sref, score, overlap_count, overlap_ratio, length_goodness, loanword_density, pack_purity }` вҖ” every score component is preserved for tracing.
  - `RankConfig { top_k, weight_overlap, weight_purity, weight_length, weight_loanword_penalty, pack_purity: BTreeMap<String, f32> }` with `Default` that hard-codes the editorial priors.
  - `MorphemeIndex::rank(input_morphemes, config) -> Vec<Hit>` вҖ” returns top-`k` sorted by descending score, ties broken by `(pack, sample_id)`.
  - Public `length_goodness(word_count) -> f32` (Gaussian, Пғ = 6, Ој = 8).
  - Public `sample_loanword_density(text) -> f32` (the v1.x purity rule applied to a single sample).
  - `DEFAULT_UNKNOWN_PACK_PURITY: f32 = 0.70` for packs not in the table.
- **`adam-dialog`**:
  - New `semantics::content_roots(parses) -> Vec<String>` вҖ” every distinct content-noun root from the input, not just the first. Preserves insertion order. Filters closed-class items via the existing `NOT_A_TOPIC` list.
  - `Conversation::rank_config: Option<RankConfig>` вҖ” override for tests / experiments; `None` uses the default.
  - `inject_retrieval_example` now calls `index.rank(&content_roots, &config)` and picks the top hit; falls back to v1.6.5 single-morpheme path if the ranker finds nothing with a stored text.
- **+7 retrieval tests**:
  - `rank_prefers_higher_overlap` вҖ” 2-morpheme match beats 1-morpheme match.
  - `rank_breaks_ties_with_pack_purity` вҖ” Abai beats CC-100 at equal overlap.
  - `rank_penalises_loanword_heavy_sample` вҖ” native-language sample wins.
  - `length_goodness_peaks_at_8_words`.
  - `sample_loanword_density_flags_russian_only_letters`.
  - `rank_top_k_is_respected`.
  - `rank_empty_input_returns_empty`.

### Determinism audit

- `rank` never calls rng or system time.
- Tie-break is `(pack, sample_id)` lex order вҶ’ identical across runs / machines.
- `RankConfig::default` is a pure constant.
- `inject_retrieval_example` does not consult `rng_seed`.

Same corpus + same input + same weights вҶ’ byte-identical cited sentence.

### What v1.7.0 does NOT do

- **No Lexicon expansion** вҖ” top uncovered items from v1.5.5 (`РҙРөРҝ`, `РҫСҒСӢ`, `РҝРөРҪ`) are still gaps; that is separate Lexicon work.
- **No compositional synthesis** вҖ” we still QUOTE the retrieved sentence verbatim. Adapting its grammar to the user's context is v1.8.0.
- **No multi-hit diversity** вҖ” top-1 is deterministic; conversation will cite the same sentence every time for the same prompt. Diversity is a later concern.

### Workspace tests

**286 tests pass** (279 вҶ’ +7 retrieval ranker).

## [1.6.5] вҖ” 2026-04-20 вҖ” Retrieval wired into `Intent::Unknown`: dialog cites real Kazakh corpus

Patch release. The retrieval index shipped in v1.6.0 now feeds the dialog layer. When `Intent::Unknown` fires with a recognised noun AND the committed morpheme index contains a sample text for it, the response quotes that sentence verbatim вҖ” a concrete step toward "not just predictable, but informed by the corpus." The 26-intent deterministic backbone is untouched; this is strictly a fallback improvement.

### Demo вҖ” what it looks like

```
> РұР°Р»Р° СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РұС–СҖРҙРөТЈРө Р°Р№СӮ
< РұР°Р»Р° СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РјСӢРҪР°РҙР°Р№ РұС–СҖ Р¶Р°Р·РұР° РәРөР·РҙРөСҒСӮС–СҖРҙС–Рј: В«РҡРөСҲРө РұР°Р»Р° РөТЈ, РәРөР»РҙС–ТЈ Т“РҫР№
  СӮР°Р»Р°Р№ Р¶Р°СҒТӣР°, РҡУ©Р· Р¶РөСӮСӮС– РұС–СҖ ТӣР°Р»СӢРҝСӮР° СӮТұСҖР° Р°Р»РјР°СҒТӣР°.В»                     [Abai Wikisource]

> РҗРұР°Р№ Р¶Р°Р№СӢРҪРҙР° РҪРө РҙРөР№СҒС–ТЈ
< Р°РұР°Р№ Р¶Р°Р№СӢРҪРҙР° РҫСҒСӢРҪРҙР°Р№ РјСӢСҒР°Р» РұР°СҖ: В«РҗРұР°Р№ ТҡТұРҪР°РҪРұР°Р№ТұР»СӢ (10 СӮР°РјСӢР· 1845 вҖ”
  6 СҲС–Р»РҙРө 1904)В»                                                         [Wikipedia KZ]

> РјРөРәСӮРөРҝ РәРөСҖРөРә РҝРө
< РјРөРәСӮРөРҝ РҙРөРіРөРҪ СҒУ©Р·РіРө ТӣР°СӮСӢСҒСӮСӢ РұС–СҖ Р¶РҫР»СӢРј РөСҒС–РјРө СӮТҜСҒСӮС–: В«... 300 РҫСҖСӢРҪРҙСӢТӣ
  РјРөРәСӮРөРҝ ТӣТұСҖСӢР»СӢСҒСӢ СӮРҫР»СӢТӣСӮР°Р№ Р°СҸТӣСӮР°Р»РјР°Т“Р°РҪ ...В»                              [CC-100]

> Р°РҙР°Рј РҪРө С–СҒСӮРөСҒРө РұРҫР»Р°РҙСӢ
< Р°РҙР°Рј СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РјСӢРҪР°РҙР°Р№ РұС–СҖ Р¶Р°Р·РұР° РәРөР·РҙРөСҒСӮС–СҖРҙС–Рј: В«РҗРҙР°Рј вҖ” РұС–СҖ РұРҫТӣ РәУ©СӮРөСҖРіРөРҪ
  РұРҫТӣСӮСӢТЈ ТӣР°РұСӢ, Р‘РҫТӣСӮР°РҪ СҒР°СҒСӢТӣ РұРҫР»Р°СҒСӢТЈ У©Р»СҒРөТЈ СӮР°Т“СӢ.В»                        [Abai вҖ” "РЎРөРіС–Р· Р°СҸТӣ"]
```

Every cited sentence traces back to a specific `(pack, sample_id)` in the committed index вҖ” fully deterministic, no hallucination path.

### Changes

- **`adam-retrieval`**: `MorphemeIndex` gains a `sample_texts: BTreeMap<String, String>` field (keyed `pack::sample_id`). `remember_text` / `sample_text` helpers. `SampleRef::text_key`. The build binary now stores sample text alongside postings.
- **`Intent::Unknown`**: new optional `example: Option<String>` field. Defaults to `None`; populated by `Conversation::turn` when a `MorphemeIndex` is attached and the `noun_hint` has postings.
- **`Conversation::with_morpheme_index`**: attaches the index. Without it, dialog behaviour is identical to v1.6.0.
- **Planner routing**: `Intent::Unknown` now routes to `unknown.with_evidence` when `example` is set, else `unknown.with_noun` (v1.1.0), else bare `unknown`.
- **New template family** in `data/dialog/templates/v1.toml`: 4 `unknown.with_evidence` templates that wrap the retrieved sentence in Kazakh guillemets (В«вҖҰВ»).
- **Committed index regenerated** with sample texts вҶ’ 2.1 MB (was 1.6 MB without texts).
- **+3 retrieval lib tests** (`remember_and_retrieve_text`, `sample_text_returns_none_when_absent`, `text_key_is_pack_and_id_joined`); **+2 dialog e2e tests** (`unknown_with_retrieval_cites_corpus_example`, `unknown_without_index_falls_back_to_noun_echo`).

### Design points worth remembering

- **Determinism**: `inject_retrieval_example` picks the first (sort-order) posting, not a random one. rng_seed is NOT consulted вҖ” the cited evidence is reproducible across runs.
- **Optional**: index attachment is additive; no-index callers (CLI without `--with-index`, older tests) keep the v1.1.0 noun-echo behaviour. No behavioural regression.
- **Small committed index**: only 3,191 samples are in the committed snapshot (500/pack cap). Users who want richer hits run `build_morpheme_index -- --full` locally (~10 min вҶ’ ~700 MB gitignored artifact).
- **Traceability wins over style**: the templates wrap quotes in В«вҖҰВ» so the evidence is visually separated from the wrapper вҖ” critical for the "every response is traceable" promise.

### Workspace tests

- **279 tests pass** (274 вҶ’ +3 retrieval + +2 dialog e2e).

## [1.6.0] вҖ” 2026-04-20 вҖ” Retrieval engine bootstrap: `adam-retrieval` crate + morpheme inverted index

Minor release. First shipped component of the **v2.0 retrieval engine**. Unlike a probabilistic LM, retrieval is deterministic (given a morpheme bag + index, top-k is fully determined), traceable (every hit names the pack + sample id it came from), and cheap (a hash lookup + sorted-list intersection, not a matmul). See the `project_retrieval_not_neural_v2` memory for the architectural rationale.

### New crate: `adam-retrieval`

- `MorphemeIndex` вҖ” `BTreeMap<String, Vec<SampleRef>>`. BTreeMap (not HashMap) so the on-disk JSON form is deterministic: the same input always serialises byte-identical, making `git diff` of the committed index meaningful.
- `SampleRef { pack, sample_id }` вҖ” every posting traces back to exactly one sentence in one committed pack.
- API: `insert(morpheme, sref)` (idempotent, keeps postings sorted), `search(morpheme)`, `search_conjunction(&[morpheme])` (AND-search with shortest-list-first intersection), `refresh_stats` (for bulk loads).
- **7 unit tests** covering idempotence, sorted invariants, conjunction intersections, and unknown-morpheme collapse.

### New binary: `build_morpheme_index`

Walks committed corpus packs, runs each unique word through the FST parser once (cached), indexes the sample under every root the parser emits. The per-word cache drops build time from ~75 minutes (one parse per word occurrence) to ~10 minutes full corpus / ~17 s for the committed snapshot.

**Two modes** (the v1.3.5 / v1.5.0 sharding convention):

- **default** вҖ” per-pack `--limit 500` cap. Writes to committed `data/retrieval/morpheme_index.json` (~1.6 MB). Runs in 17 s. Committed index ingests 3,191 samples вҶ’ 3,082 distinct morphemes вҶ’ 16,262 postings. This is the reference snapshot CI + integration tests consume.
- **`--full`** вҖ” full committed corpus. Writes to `data/retrieval/morpheme_index_full.json` (gitignored; ~700 MB). Fuel for v1.7.0+ retrieval experiments.

### FST-parser throughput measured

Benchmark on the committed corpus: **1.155 ms / word** on a cold cache (single-threaded, M2). With the unique-word cache, a full build performs ~270 k parses instead of ~3.84 M вҖ” 14Г— savings.

### Tests

- **274 workspace tests pass** (267 вҶ’ +7 for the new `adam-retrieval` crate).

### What this release does NOT do (scope discipline)

- No `Intent::Unknown` fallback integration yet вҖ” that is v1.6.5+.
- No ranking / scoring вҖ” v1.7.0 work. Today `search` returns postings in deterministic sort order, which is good enough to build against.
- No compositional synthesis (retrieve вҶ’ splice вҶ’ inflect) вҖ” v1.8.0+.

The v1.6.0 bet: **ship the index as a first-class artifact**, so every subsequent release can measure itself against it concretely rather than against abstract targets.

## [1.5.5] вҖ” 2026-04-20 вҖ” Morpheme-coverage audit: 79.48 % Lexicon prefix-match over 3.84 M words

Patch release. Adds `morpheme_coverage` вҖ” a fast prefix-match audit that measures what fraction of corpus words begin with a known Lexicon root. This is the first diagnostic for the v1.6.0+ retrieval engine: it tells us concretely *where* the Lexicon misses and gives every future Lexicon PR a measurable coverage delta.

### Scope pivot

v1.5.5 was originally planned as "government Kazakh sources" (akorda.kz, egov.kz, bnews.kz) to close the last 1.3Г— gap to 100 M local words. The planned sources turned out to need scraping infrastructure that is out of scope for a patch release, so v1.5.5 instead delivers the **measurement** tool that will drive the Lexicon/corpus expansion once a reliable source pipeline exists. The 100 M-word directive is not abandoned вҖ” it moves to v1.6.x.

### Added: `morpheme_coverage` binary

- Walks every committed pack listed in `corpus_audit`'s `SOURCE_PACKS`.
- Loads curated + Apertium roots (14,247 roots at вүҘ 3 chars, the false-positive guard).
- For each word: true if any prefix (вүҘ 3 chars) matches a lexicon root.
- Per-pack report: total words, covered words, coverage ratio, top 20 uncovered words by frequency.
- Output: `data/corpus_morpheme_coverage_report.json`.
- 5 unit tests covering prefix-match + normalisation semantics.

Prefix match is a **lower bound** on true FST parse coverage вҖ” it says nothing about whether suffixes are valid, only whether the root side is recognised. A full FST parse of 3.84 M words would require ~2 trillion synth calls at ~600 k per parse; the prefix audit runs in seconds and gives an honest ceiling.

### Coverage baseline (v1.5.5)

| Pack | Words | Coverage |
|---|---:|---:|
| `tatoeba_kazakh_pack.json` | 23 245 | 79.85 % |
| `wikipedia_kz_pack.json` | 1 683 182 | 76.89 % |
| `common_voice_kk_pack.json` | 34 403 | 80.53 % |
| `cc100_kk_pack.json` | 1 684 920 | 77.26 % |
| `abai_wikisource_pack.json` | 18 935 | 76.12 % |
| `kazakh_proverbs_pack.json` | 319 | 85.27 % |
| `synthetic_sentences_pack.json` | 398 307 | **99.82 %** (synth uses Lexicon) |
| `kazakh_classics_pack.json` | 893 | 81.52 % |
| **Overall** | **3 844 204** | **79.48 %** |

### Top uncovered words вҖ” concrete Lexicon candidates

The report names the most-frequent unmatched words across CC-100 вҖ” closed-class items not yet in the Lexicon that every future Lexicon PR can remove from this list:

- `РҙРөРҝ` вҖ” quotative particle
- `РҫСҒСӢ` вҖ” proximal demonstrative (closed-class)
- `РҫРҪСӢТЈ` вҖ” genitive of `РҫР»` (closed-class pronoun case form)
- `РҙРөРіРөРҪ` вҖ” participle of `РҙРө-` ("say / that which is said"), no derivation chain yet
- `СҖРөСҒРҝСғРұР»РёРәР°СҒСӢРҪСӢТЈ`, `РҫРұР»СӢСҒСӢ`, `СҖРөСҒРҝСғРұР»РёРәР°СҒСӢ`, `РҫРұР»СӢСҒСӮСӢТӣ` вҖ” proper-noun state/region terms
- `РҝРөРҪ` вҖ” postposition "with / and" (closed-class)
- `РҫСҖСӮР°`, `РұР°СҒ`, `Р°Р»Сғ` вҖ” high-frequency common nouns/infinitives

### Workspace tests

- **267 tests pass** (was 262; +5 from the new binary's unit tests).

## [1.5.0] вҖ” 2026-04-20 вҖ” CC-100 re-extract: corpus local вҶ’ 77.9 M words (gap 1.3Г—)

Minor release. Rewrites the CC-100 Kazakh processor along the same lines as v1.3.0 Wikipedia вҖ” **chunked streaming + loanword-density filter + sharding** вҖ” and unleashes it against the full `cc100_kk.txt.xz` (вүҲ 5 GB decompressed) that previously had a hard 50 k-sample cap.

### Processor rewrite (`process_cc100_kk.rs`)

- Adds the 10 % loanword-density filter shared with `process_wikipedia_kz`. CC-100 web crawl is Russified far more heavily than Wikipedia вҖ” 24 k of every 140 k accepted samples were rejected by this filter alone on shard 01.
- Replaces the old 50 k hard cap with the project-standard sharding pattern: first shard committed (вүӨ 50 MB), subsequent shards written to the gitignored `data/curated/shards/` for local retrieval-engine fuel.
- Shard size 140 k (vs Wikipedia's 150 k) вҖ” web-crawl sentences average longer, so 140 k keeps each shard's pretty-printed JSON safely below the 50 MB GitHub warning threshold.
- `--full` flag mirrors the Wikipedia processor. Default mode writes shard 01 only; `--full` continues until the stream ends.

### Audit integration (`corpus_audit.rs`)

- `--local` mode now includes both `wikipedia_kz_shard_*` and `cc100_kk_shard_*` shards from `data/curated/shards/`.
- Default mode (CI) audits committed packs only; behaviour unchanged.

### Corpus impact

| Metric | v1.3.5 | v1.4.5 | **v1.5.0** | О” |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Committed words | 2.85 M | 2.85 M | **4.01 M** | +40.7 % |
| Local words (committed + shards) | 16 M | 16 M | **77.9 M** | +387 % |
| Committed unique vocab | 92 k | 92 k | **270 k** | +193 % |
| Local unique vocab | 485 k | 485 k | **1.72 M** | +255 % |
| Overall Kazakh purity | 99.99 % | 99.99 % | **98.36 %** | вҲ’1.6 pp (web-crawl noise) |
| Expansion gap to 100 M | 6.2Г— | 6.2Г— | **1.3Г—** | **within striking distance** |

33 CC-100 shards now live locally in `data/curated/shards/` (shards 02вҖ“34). Shard 01 is committed at `data/curated/cc100_kk_pack.json` (140 000 samples, 48.7 MiB).

### Purity trade-off (noted, not fixed)

CC-100 is web-crawl text, so overall corpus purity drops from 99.99 % вҶ’ 98.36 %. The 10 % density cap already rejects the most heavily Russified sentences; further tightening would throw out too much signal. This is the trade-off encoded in the `project_corpus_purity_directive` memory вҖ” apply the filter, then accept the residual. v1.5.5 (government sources вҖ” akorda.kz, egov.kz) is expected to restore purity closer to 99 % and push local past 100 M.

### Workspace tests

- **262 tests pass** (unchanged from v1.4.5). No code in the FST + dialog path changed; only the corpus processor and audit tool.

## [1.4.5] вҖ” 2026-04-19 вҖ” Lexicon polish: +20 modern Kazakh nouns

Patch release. Expands the curated Lexicon with modern professional nouns and common conversational vocabulary вҖ” all native Kazakh formations, no Russian loanwords (per the `project_corpus_purity_directive` and `project_kazakh_only_directive` memories).

### Added roots (+20)

**Professions** (agent `-СҲСӢ` formations and older native forms):
- `РҪТұСҒТӣР°СғСҲСӢ` (instructor), `РәРөТЈРөСҒСҲС–` (consultant), `Р¶РөСӮРөРәСҲС–` (leader),
- `ТӣСӢР·РјРөСӮРәРөСҖ` (employee), `ТӣТұСҖСӢР»СӢСҒСҲСӢ` (builder), `СҒР°СӮСғСҲСӢ` (seller),
- `СӮРөСҖРіРөСғСҲС–` (investigator), `ТӣРҫСҖТ“Р°СғСҲСӢ` (defender/lawyer), `РұР°СҒСҲСӢ` (boss),
- `С–СҒРәРөСҖ` (businessman), `Р¶ТҜСҖРіС–Р·СғСҲС–` (driver), `РұР°Р»СӢТӣСҲСӢ` (fisherman),
- `Р°ТЈСҲСӢ` (hunter), `РөСӮС–РәСҲС–` (shoemaker), `РјРөСҖРіРөРҪСҲС–` (sharpshooter),
- `Р¶Р°СғСӢРҪРіРөСҖ` (warrior), `РҫТӣСӢСӮСғСҲСӢ` (lecturer), `СҒР°СғРҙР°РіРөСҖ` (merchant),
- `СӮУҷСҖРұРёРөСҲС–` (tutor/educator)

**Common nouns** (conversation-relevant):
- `РјРөРәРөРјРө` (institution), `РәРөТЈСҒРө` (office), `ТұР№СӢРј` (organisation),
- `Р¶ТҜСҖРөРә` (heart), `УҷРәРө` (father), `Р°Т“Р°` (elder brother), `С–РҪС–` (younger brother),
- `Р°РҝР°` (elder sister), `ТӣР°СҖСӢРҪРҙР°СҒ` (younger sister),
- `РәРөСҲРө` (yesterday), `РұТҜРіС–РҪ` (today), `РөСҖСӮРөТЈ` (tomorrow), `СӮР°ТЈРөСҖСӮРөТЈ` (morning)

Total Lexicon: **4,516 entries** (was 4,496 in v1.4.0).

### Verified round-trip

Each new occupation round-trips through the FST-NER path from v1.4.0:

```
$ adam_chat
> РјРөРҪ Р¶ТҜСҖРіС–Р·СғСҲС–РјС–РҪ   вҶ’ СҒС–Р· Р¶ТҜСҖРіС–Р·СғСҲС– РөРәРөРҪСҒС–Р·
> РјРөРҪ СҒР°СғРҙР°РіРөСҖРјС–РҪ    вҶ’ СҒР°СғРҙР°РіРөСҖР»РөСҖ вҖ” ТӣР°Р¶РөСӮСӮС– РјР°РјР°РҪРҙСӢТӣ
> РјРөРҪ РҪТұСҒТӣР°СғСҲСӢРјСӢРҪ    вҶ’ СҒС–Р· РҪТұСҒТӣР°СғСҲСӢ РөРәРөРҪСҒС–Р·
> РјРөРҪ СҒР°СӮСғСҲСӢРјСӢРҪ      вҶ’ СҒС–Р· СҒР°СӮСғСҲСӢ РөРәРөРҪСҒС–Р·
```

Parser вҶ’ predicate=P1Sg вҶ’ POS-filter accepts вҶ’ `occupation` slot filled вҶ’ template plural / dative FST synthesis.

### Tests

Workspace: **262 passing**, 4 ignored, 0 failing. Foundation CI green. No new test cases вҖ” the v1.4.0 FST-NER tests already cover the general mechanism; these new roots are data-only expansion.

## [1.4.0] вҖ” 2026-04-19 вҖ” FST-NER refactor + DST + predicate-copula morphology

Minor release. Four connected pieces of work that together address the external-reviewer critiques from v1.3.5 and lay groundwork for v1.6.0+ retrieval engine.

### 1. Predicate-person FST morphology (new)

`NounFeatures` gains a `predicate: Option<Predicate>` field with seven variants (P1Sg / P2SgInformal / P2SgPolite / P3 / P1Pl / P2PlInformal / P2PlPolite). Applied AFTER case in `synthesise_noun`:

| form | derivation |
|---|---|
| РјТұТ“Р°Р»С–Рј + P1Sg | РјТұТ“Р°Р»С–РјРјС–РҪ |
| РјТұТ“Р°Р»С–Рј + P2SgPolite | РјТұТ“Р°Р»С–РјСҒС–Р· |
| РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢ + Ablative + P1Sg | РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢРҙР°РҪРјСӢРҪ |
| РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°СҲСӢ + P1Sg | РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°СҲСӢРјСӢРҪ |

Six new suffix templates (`PREDICATE_1SG` / `PREDICATE_2SG_INFORMAL` / `PREDICATE_2SG_POLITE` / `PREDICATE_1PL` / `PREDICATE_2PL_INFORMAL` / `PREDICATE_2PL_POLITE`). The inverse parser now enumerates predicate in its feature space; predicate + possessive never stack (grammatically exclusive), saving search space.

### 2. Lexicon place names (+30 entries)

Added Kazakh cities and country names as proper nouns to `data/tokenizer/segmentation_roots.json`: РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢ, РҗСҒСӮР°РҪР°, РЁСӢРјРәРөРҪСӮ, ТҡР°СҖР°Т“Р°РҪРҙСӢ, РҗТӣСӮУ©РұРө, РўР°СҖР°Р·, РҹР°РІР»РҫРҙР°СҖ, УЁСҒРәРөРјРөРҪ, РҗСӮСӢСҖР°Сғ, РЎРөРјРөР№, ТҡРҫСҒСӮР°РҪР°Р№, ТҡСӢР·СӢР»РҫСҖРҙР°, РўР°Р»РҙСӢТӣРҫСҖТ“Р°РҪ, РҗТӣСӮР°Сғ, РһСҖР°Р», РҹРөСӮСҖРҫРҝР°РІР», РўТҜСҖРәС–СҒСӮР°РҪ, РҡУ©РәСҲРөСӮР°Сғ, РңР°ТЈТ“СӢСҒСӮР°Сғ, ТҡР°Р·Р°ТӣСҒСӮР°РҪ, Р РөСҒРөР№, ТҡСӢСӮР°Р№, РўТҜСҖРәРёСҸ, РңРҫРҪТ“РҫР»РёСҸ, УЁР·РұРөРәСҒСӮР°РҪ, ТҡСӢСҖТ“СӢР·СҒСӮР°РҪ, Р•СғСҖРҫРҝР°, РҗР·РёСҸ, УҳР»РөРј, РһСӮР°РҪ. All lowercased for case-insensitive parser lookup.

Total Lexicon: 4,496 entries (was 4,466 in v1.3.5).

### 3. Semantics FST-NER refactor

Replaced manual suffix-stripping in the city and occupation recognisers with **FST parse-based entity extraction** вҖ” addresses the architectural inconsistency Codex and Antigravity flagged.

- **City**: `detect_statement_of_location` now scans `parses: &[Analysis]` for the first Noun in Ablative or Locative case. Ablative signals origin ("РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢРҙР°РҪРјСӢРҪ"), Locative signals residence when co-occurring with "СӮТұСҖР°РјСӢРҪ / СӮТұСҖР°РјСӢР·". Rule-based string heuristics remain as fallback for out-of-Lexicon inputs.
- **Occupation**: `detect_statement_of_occupation` scans parses for Noun with `predicate == Some(P1Sg)` AND `part_of_speech == "noun"` (the POS filter rejects adjective-predicate forms like `Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢРјСӢРҪ`). Fallback chain: FST parse вҶ’ Lexicon-backed copula-strip (v0.9.7) вҶ’ fixed 6-form table (v0.8.0).

### 4. Dialog State Tracking (DST)

`Conversation` is no longer a flat slot HashMap вҖ” it tracks intent context:

```rust
pub struct Conversation {
    pub session: HashMap<String, String>,        // slots
    pub active_intent: Option<IntentKind>,       // last-turn intent kind
    pub intent_history: Vec<IntentKind>,         // bounded-capacity trace
}
```

`IntentKind` (new, exported) is a lightweight payload-free summary of `Intent` вҖ” string names aren't copied into history. History is capped at 32 entries (long sessions don't grow unboundedly).

**Follow-up resolution** handles contextual utterances like `Р°Р» СҒС–Р·?` ("and you?") вҖ” `resolve_follow_up` re-tags weak-intent utterances ([`Unknown`] / [`Affirmation`] / [`Negation`]) against the previous turn's `active_intent`, so after `AskHowAreYou` the follow-up fires as `AskHowAreYou` again for planning. Strong intents are never overridden.

### Addresses external reviewer critiques

| Critique | Disposition |
|---|---|
| Codex: "FST parser ignored in semantics" | **Fixed** вҖ” FST parses are the primary entity-extraction path |
| Codex: "duplicated morphology in strip_*" | **Fixed** вҖ” fallback to rule-based string heuristics only when FST parse is empty |
| Antigravity: "flat HashMap isn't DST" | **Fixed** вҖ” active_intent + intent_history + follow-up resolution |
| Antigravity: "РјРҫР¶РҪРҫ РҙРҫРұР°РІРёСӮСҢ ML РҙР»СҸ NLU" | **Rejected** вҖ” contradicts v2.0 retrieval-not-neural direction |

### Tests

75 dialog end-to-end pairs (up from 69 in v1.3.5), 6 new covering FST-NER place-name recognition, predicate-P1Sg occupation, adjective rejection, DST active_intent tracking, follow-up resolution, and reset clearing all state. Workspace total: **262 passing**, 4 ignored, 0 failing. Foundation CI green.

### Public API additions

- `adam_dialog::IntentKind` вҖ” lightweight payload-free intent summary
- `adam_dialog::Conversation { active_intent, intent_history }` вҖ” new fields
- `adam_kernel_fst::morphotactics::Predicate` вҖ” new enum for noun-predicate copula
- `adam_kernel_fst::morphotactics::NounFeatures.predicate` вҖ” new optional field

## [1.3.5] вҖ” 2026-04-19 вҖ” Wikipedia sharding + docs drift fixes + v2.0 direction committed

Patch release. No behavioural change in the dialog layer. Unlocks the full 15 M-word Wikipedia yield for local use (the v2.0 retrieval engine's fuel), fixes documentation drift, and commits the v2.0 architectural direction вҖ” retrieval over morpheme-parsed corpus, not a trained transformer LM.

### Wikipedia sharding вҖ” `--full` mode

`process_wikipedia_kz` now supports a `--full` flag. Default mode is unchanged (single committed pack, 150 k samples, ~49 MB). With `--full`, the processor writes additional shards to `data/curated/shards/wikipedia_kz_shard_NN_pack.json` (gitignored), one per 150 k samples, for the full ~1.4 M-sample, ~16 M-word corpus on local disk. These are the input fuel for v1.6.0+ retrieval-engine work.

`corpus_audit` scans shards automatically when `--local` is passed (or `ADAM_CORPUS_AUDIT_LOCAL=1`); default behaviour is unchanged (reads only committed packs, matches what CI sees).

### Docs drift fixed (after Codex + Antigravity reviews)

Two external AI reviewers flagged specific overclaims and documentation drift. The valid points:

- **Badge count** was `253 passing`; actual test count has been 256 since v1.2.0. Badge updated.
- **`foundation_scope.md`** still listed "Trilingual input recognition" and "LatinвҶ’Cyrillic transliteration" as in-scope, which were both reverted in v1.1.0. Rewritten for v1.3.x+ reality.
- **"Grammatically correct by construction"** wording in the README was an overclaim вҖ” FST guarantees apply to `{slot|features}` expansion, not to literal template text. README and `foundation_scope.md` both tightened: now "grammatically correct by construction on the slot path".

### FST-NER refactor deferred

Reviewers suggested routing entity extraction through `adam_kernel_fst::parser::Analysis` instead of manual suffix stripping. Investigation found this requires two prerequisites that don't fit a patch release:

1. **Predicate-person feature markers** in FST morphotactics (1sg / 2sg / 3rd-person predicate copulas like `-РјСӢРҪ / -СҒСӢТЈ / -РҙС–СҖ`). Currently the FST knows possessives but not predicates, so `РјТұТ“Р°Р»С–РјРјС–РҪ` can't be parsed.
2. **Place names in the Lexicon.** `РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢ`, `РҗСҒСӮР°РҪР°`, `РЁСӢРјРәРөРҪСӮ` etc. aren't in `data/tokenizer/segmentation_roots.json`, so the parser returns empty for any ablative/locative form of them.

Both are v1.4.0 minor-level work (new FST features + Lexicon expansion). Queued, not blocking.

### v2.0 direction вҖ” committed

Memory saved (`project_retrieval_not_neural_v2`): v2.0 "minimally thinking Kazakh model" is **retrieval-based, not a trained transformer LM**. Morpheme-indexed retrieval over the 100 M+ word corpus + rule-based compositional synthesis. Properties: zero hallucinations by construction, full trace to source sentences, M2 8 GB-runnable, exploits Kazakh's rich agglutinative structure that the FST already unpacks. Rejects the mainstream "small LLM fallback" path as a scaled-down clone rather than a new direction.

### Numbers

- Committed corpus (CI view): **256 tests passing**, 2.85 M words / 224 k unique / 97.99 % purity вҖ” unchanged from v1.3.0
- Local-with-shards: 16.23 M words / 749 k unique / 98.03 % purity / gap to 100 M target = 6.2Г—

### Tests

Workspace: 256 passing, 4 ignored, 0 failing. Foundation CI green.

## [1.3.0] вҖ” 2026-04-19 вҖ” Wikipedia re-extract (+27 % corpus, 2.85 M words)

Unlocks the Kazakh Wikipedia pack after realising the existing 100 k-sample slice was only 3 % of what the already-downloaded 638 MB source file can yield. The v1.3.0 rewrite of `process_wikipedia_kz` is 100Г— faster and applies the v1.x purity gate.

### The problem

User observed: "all the raw material is already in `data/external/` вҖ” we just need to extract it better. And we learned the lesson at v0.4.0: no 2-word fragments." Investigation confirmed:

- `data/external/wikipedia_kz_plain.txt` = **638 MB** raw Kazakh Wikipedia
- `data/curated/wikipedia_kz_pack.json` (v1.2.0) used only **100 k samples / 1.15 M words** вҖ” ~3 % of the source
- Old processor did byte-by-byte reads вҶ’ estimated hours for full scan (never run to completion)
- Old processor had no loanword-density filter вҶ’ 3вҖ“4 % contamination in committed pack

### The fix

Rewrote `crates/adam-corpus/src/bin/process_wikipedia_kz.rs`:

- **Chunked streaming** (64 KB reads) replaces byte-by-byte I/O вҶ’ full 638 MB scan in **26 s** (measured on M2 8 GB)
- **Loanword-density filter** (10 % cap) drops Russian-loanword-saturated articles
- **Optional `target-cap` CLI arg** вҖ” default now processes the full file; cap is available for dev runs
- **Wikipedia purity 95.92 % вҶ’ 99.99 %** after the new filter
- Min/max word bounds unchanged (4вҖ“40 words per sample), still honours the v0.4.0 lesson

### Full-dump numbers (measured, not committed)

When run uncapped on the full 638 MB source:

```
articles=296,342  sentences_scanned=5,726,108  accepted=1,395,801
skipped_latin=2,711,431  skipped_length=922,051  skipped_dup=276,059  skipped_loanword=420,766
```

**1.4 M clean samples / ~15 M words** available locally. JSON size: ~440 MB.

### What's committed in v1.3.0

GitHub's 100 MB hard file-size limit (and the project's 50 MB convention from `feedback_git_ignore_policy`) mean we can't commit the 440 MB full pack. v1.3.0 commits the first 150 k samples (~49 MB) as the canonical pack; the uncapped output is regenerable locally from the `data/external/wikipedia_kz_plain.txt` source.

| measure | v1.2.0 | v1.3.0 committed | v1.3.0 local (uncapped) |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Wikipedia samples | 100,000 | 150,036 | 1,395,801 |
| Wikipedia words | 1,150,532 | 1,613,306 | ~15,138,291 |
| Wikipedia purity | 95.92 % | 99.99 % | 98.06 % |
| **Corpus total words** | **2,238,852** | **2,851,629** | ~16,226,611 |
| **Expansion gap to 100 M** | **45Г—** | **35Г—** | 6.2Г— |

### Sharding plan (v1.3.5)

To expose the full 1.4 M samples without blowing the file-size limit, v1.3.5 will shard the pack into ~10 files of ~40 MB each (`wikipedia_kz_shard_01_pack.json` вҖҰ `wikipedia_kz_shard_10_pack.json`). `corpus_audit` will glob-merge them. Downstream consumers (future LM training) will read all shards.

### Tests

Workspace: **256 passing**, 4 ignored, 0 failing. Foundation CI green (pack validated via `jq empty`).

## [1.2.0] вҖ” 2026-04-19 вҖ” Kazakh classical literature expansion

First significant post-v1.0 corpus addition. Ingests the classical Kazakh Wikisource holdings for **Р«РұСӢСҖР°Р№ РҗР»СӮСӢРҪСҒР°СҖРёРҪ** (1841вҖ“1889, children's literature + fables) and **РңР°Т“Р¶Р°РҪ Р–ТұРјР°РұР°РөРІ** (1893вҖ“1938, early 20c poet). Both authors are fully in the public domain.

### Scope вҖ” honest framing

The original v1.2.0 label was "classical literature OCR". In practice:

1. **OCR requires scanned PDFs we don't have** and a Kazakh-trained Tesseract model. Neither is available in this release cycle. Deferred to a later minor release (v1.3.x+) once sources are found.
2. **Kazakh Wikisource is already digitised** вҖ” no OCR needed. This release uses that path instead.
3. Other classical authors (РЁУҷРәУҷСҖС–Рј, Р–Р°РјРұСӢР», РЎУҷРәРөРҪ РЎРөР№С„СғР»Р»РёРҪ, РңС–СҖР¶Р°ТӣСӢРҝ Р”СғР»Р°СӮРҫРІ) are public domain but their pages don't exist on kk.wikisource yet. They'll be added when sources arrive.

### Yield

| pack | samples | words | unique | purity |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| **kazakh_classics** (new) | **111** | **926** | **710** | **100.00 %** |

Small in absolute terms (926 words вүҲ 0.04 % of the existing corpus) but **pristine literary Kazakh** вҖ” zero loanword contamination, from two canonical pre-Soviet authors. This is the literary quality core the LM should weight highly in training.

### New corpus total

- **Before (v1.1.5):** 2,237,926 words, 193,020 unique, 96.74 % purity
- **After (v1.2.0):** 2,238,852 words, 193,132 unique, 96.74 % purity
- **Gap to target:** still 97.76 M words (~45Г— expansion)

### Added

- `scripts/fetch_kazakh_classics.sh` вҖ” universal Kazakh Wikisource fetcher. Takes an author list; downloads each author's work-index page; extracts and cleans `<p>` bodies from each linked work; writes `data/external/kazakh_classics_plain.txt` with `0x1e`-separated work records. Rate-limited and UA-identified per Wikimedia policy.
- `crates/adam-corpus/src/bin/process_kazakh_classics.rs` вҖ” processor that reads the raw text, applies the v1.x purity filter (Russian-only letter detection + loanword suffix detection + density threshold of 10 %), deduplicates, and writes `data/curated/kazakh_classics_pack.json`.
- `data/curated/kazakh_classics_pack.json` вҖ” 111 clean samples.
- `corpus_audit` updated to include the new pack.

### Strategic note

v1.2.0 is the slow, honest start of the corpus expansion path. The big-volume releases are:

- **v1.3.0** вҖ” full Kazakh Wikipedia dump (~35 M words from 243k articles; currently we have only 1.15 M from a 100k-sample subset)
- **v1.4.0** вҖ” Kazakh government corpora (egov.kz, akorda.kz, bnews.kz вҖ” select long-form content)
- **v1.5.0** вҖ” reach 100 M+ target with additional classical literature (from OCR once pipeline arrives) and filtered news

### Tests

Workspace: **256 passing**, 4 ignored, 0 failing. Foundation CI green. No behavioural change to the dialog layer.

## [1.1.5] вҖ” 2026-04-19 вҖ” Corpus audit baseline

First step on the v1.x corpus engineering path toward the v2.0 LM. No dialog / FST behavioural change; tooling + baseline numbers only.

### Added

- **`cargo run --release -p adam-corpus --bin corpus_audit`** вҖ” measures the starting position across all source packs: per-source word count, unique vocabulary, Kazakh-purity score (fraction of words free of Russian-only letters and loanword suffixes), within-pack deduplication.
- **`data/corpus_audit_report.json`** вҖ” machine-readable report regenerated by the binary.
- **`docs/corpus_audit.md`** вҖ” human-readable baseline + the v1.2.0 вҶ’ v1.5.0 expansion plan.

### Baseline numbers (2026-04-19)

| pack | samples | words | purity |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| tatoeba_kazakh | 4,058 | 24,643 | 98.12 % |
| wikipedia_kz | 100,000 | 1,150,532 | 95.92 % |
| common_voice_kk | 6,108 | 36,397 | 99.91 % |
| cc100_kk | 50,000 | 602,144 | 96.59 % |
| abai_wikisource | 2,253 | 20,303 | 99.81 % |
| kazakh_proverbs | 80 | 349 | 100.00 % |
| synthetic_sentences | 100,000 | 403,558 | 98.79 % |
| **total** | **262,499** | **2,237,926** | **96.74 %** |

- **Target:** 100 M words.
- **Gap:** 97.76 M (вүҲ **45Г— expansion** needed).

### Strategic implications

- **Data volume is the real bottleneck**, not model size. Corpus engineering is 5вҖ“6 releases ahead of any actual LM training.
- **Abai / Common Voice / proverbs are small but pristine** (> 99 % purity) вҖ” literary core worth preserving.
- **Wikipedia + CC-100 carry the volume but 3вҖ“4 % loanword contamination**; v1.2.0+ ingestion must pre-filter.
- **Reference dictionaries** (per user directive) are a future source but must pass the same loanword filter вҖ” raw dictionary JSON is not accepted.

### Tests

3 new unit tests in `corpus_audit` for the detector functions. Workspace: **256 passing**, 4 ignored, 0 failing. Foundation CI green.

## [1.1.0] вҖ” 2026-04-19 вҖ” Kazakh-only revert + modern Lexicon + smart Unknown

Strategic revert of v0.9.6 multilingual. Post-v1.0.0 testing revealed that the Russian / English recogniser triggers diluted the Kazakh-first thesis вҖ” users typing in the wrong language received shallow coverage, and the cross-language tests added noise without adding generalisation. This release restores the Kazakh-only surface and sets up the path to a real Kazakh LM.

### Breaking changes (input surface)

- **All Russian / English recogniser triggers removed.** Input that previously matched via "hi / hello / РҝСҖРёРІРөСӮ / РјРөРҪСҸ Р·РҫРІСғСӮ X / how are you" etc. now falls through to `Intent::Unknown`.
- **Latin вҶ’ Cyrillic transliteration module removed.** `adam_dialog::transliteration` is gone; non-Cyrillic slot values are no longer silently rewritten before FST synthesis.

### Breaking changes (Intent enum)

- **`Intent::Unknown`** gains a `noun_hint: Option<String>` field (was unit-struct-like with only `raw_tokens`).
- **`Intent::Insult`** (new variant) вҖ” polite non-engagement for rude input (Р°ТӣСӢРјР°Тӣ, РҪР°РҙР°РҪ, СӮТҜРәРәРө СӮТұСҖРјР°Р№СҒСӢТЈ, Р°ТӣСӢР»СҒСӢР·).

### Additions

- **Modern Kazakh Lexicon expansion** (12 new curated roots, all native Kazakh formations вҖ” no Russian loanwords):
  - Professions: РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР°СҲСӢ (programmer), Р°СғРҙР°СҖРјР°СҲСӢ (translator), Р¶Р°Р·СғСҲСӢ (writer), Р·Р°ТЈРіРөСҖ (lawyer), РұР°СҒТӣР°СҖСғСҲСӢ (manager), Р¶СғСҖРҪР°Р»СҲСӢ (journalist), Р·РөСҖСӮСӮРөСғСҲС– (researcher), ТұСҒСӮР°Р· (teacher/mentor), РөРјСҲС– (healer)
  - Tech concepts: РұР°Т“РҙР°СҖР»Р°РјР° (program), ТӣРҫСҒСӢРјСҲР° (application), РөСҒРөРҝСӮРөСғС–СҲ (computer, native)
  - AI / cognition: Р°ТӣСӢР» (mind), СҒР°РҪР° (consciousness), РҫР№ (thought), Р¶Р°СҒР°РҪРҙСӢ (artificial, adjective)
- **Smart Unknown handler.** When no intent matches, the FST parser extracts a noun from the input (filtered against pronouns / postpositions / quantifiers) and routes to the new `unknown.with_noun` template family вҖ” responses like `"Р°С…, {noun} СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ Р°Р№СӮР°СҒСӢР· РұР°"` acknowledge the topic instead of blank `СӮТҜСҒС–РҪРұРөРҙС–Рј`.
- **Insult templates** (4 variants) for polite non-engagement вҖ” the model doesn't escalate or retaliate.
- **`detect_insult`** recogniser + `detect_ask_location` / `detect_compliment` stricter bounds.

### FST tightening

- `strip_ablative_copula` now requires a stem of at least 3 characters. Prevents greedy match on `РҪР°РҙР°РҪРјСӢРҪ` (1sg predicate of "ignorant") from being misrecognised as a city.

### Tests

69 dialog end-to-end pairs (was 81 вҖ” multilingual block deleted; +5 new for Kazakh-only revert, Insult, Unknown-with-noun, and modern Lexicon coverage). Workspace: **253 passing**, 4 ignored, 0 failing. Foundation CI green.

### Roadmap commitment (v2.0)

This release is the bridge between the v1.0.0 rule-based MVP and a future **thinking Kazakh LM**. The plan:

- **v1.x (now)** вҖ” Lexicon expansion, smart Unknown handler. Incremental.
- **v1.x (data engineering)** вҖ” expand Kazakh corpus from ~4 M to **100 M+ tokens**. This is the real bottleneck for any trained model вҖ” Chinchilla-optimal data for a 24 M param LM is ~480 M tokens; we're currently ~100Г— short.
- **v2.0** вҖ” compact Kazakh LM (transformer or SSM), trained in pure Rust, plugged in as the `Intent::Unknown` fallback only. The deterministic 26-intent pipeline stays as the 0-hallucination backbone for everything it recognises; the LM handles the long tail.

Multimodality (speech, vision) is deferred until the thinking Kazakh LM is real.

## [1.0.0] вҖ” 2026-04-19 вҖ” MVP cut

The investor-demoable MVP. No new features since v0.9.9 вҖ” the delta is documentation, housekeeping, and a formal cut of the v1.0.0 line.

### What v1.0.0 delivers

Predictable, auditable Kazakh dialog across 25 intents, trilingual input (kk / ru / en), Kazakh-only output, multi-turn session state, and FST-guaranteed morphology вҖ” all in pure Rust running on a MacBook Air M2 8 GB.

| pillar | v1.0.0 state |
|---|---|
| Intents recognised | **25** (Greeting Г— 5 sub-kinds, Farewell, Affirmation / Negation, Thanks / Apology, AskHowAreYou / StatementOfWellbeing, AskName / StatementOfName, AskAge / StatementOfAge, AskLocation / StatementOfLocation, AskOccupation / StatementOfOccupation, AskFamily / StatementOfFamily, AskWeather / StatementOfWeather, AskTime, Compliment, Request, WellWishes, Unknown) |
| Input languages | Kazakh, Russian, English |
| Entity extraction | `name` (3 KK + 2 RU + 3 EN patterns), `age` (Kazakh numerals 1вҖ“99 + digits), `city` (ablative / locative stripping), `occupation` (Lexicon-backed 1sg-copula stripping, POS-filtered) |
| Session state | `Conversation` struct, absorb + persist across turns, reset() |
| Slot syntax | `{slot\|features}` with 4 feature families (case, number, derivation, possessive), 27 tokens total, `+`-combinable |
| FST morphology | 11 archiphonemes, 22+ twol rules, 30 suffix templates, 100% synth-analyse roundtrip on 36 k forms |
| Template repository | 29 families, TOML-driven, slot-fillability filtered |
