| Latin name support | transliteration module (digraphs + single-letter map) feeds FST when template requests morphology on a Latin root |
| Foundation CI | validates every layer; `validate_foundation.sh` green |

### Documentation refresh

- **README.md** fully rewritten as the v1.0.0 MVP story. The pre-v0.4.5 transformer narrative is compressed into a single "History" section; transformer-era sample generations, training pipeline, and PPL stats removed from the forward-looking story.
- **docs/roadmap.md** capped with a v1.0.0 final entry; earlier phases condensed to a lifecycle view.
- **docs/repository_layout.md** updated with the full current crate list (was missing `adam-kernel-fst` and `adam-dialog`).
- **docs/kazakh_grammar/07_dialog_architecture.md** status flipped from "design document, pre-implementation" to "shipped in v1.0.0".
- **docs/foundation_scope.md** aligned with the v1.0.0 deliverable.
- **docs/training_baseline.md** and **docs/eval_baseline.md** marked as legacy context (transformer phase v0.1вҖ“v0.4).
- Per-subdirectory READMEs under `data/` (dialog, curated, lexicon_v1, training) were added in v0.8.5's cleanup pass and still accurately reflect the v1.0.0 state.

### Tests

Unchanged from v0.9.9: **271 passing**, 4 ignored, 0 failing. Foundation CI green.

### Post-v1.0.0

The MVP is the release surface. Future work candidates (not promised, not scheduled):

- Native-speaker review of the template set вҖ” a real, human review pass.
- Lexicon expansion beyond the 14 k curated roots (proper nouns, modern vocabulary under a separate "loanword-allowed" tier).
- Polished Latin-to-Cyrillic transliteration (silent-h handling for English names).
- Verb slot expansion (`{root|verb_features}` with a different synthesiser dispatch).
- Additional intents beyond the 25-intent surface.

Any of these would ship as v1.1.0+ and are explicitly out of scope for the v1.0.0 cut.

## [0.9.9] вҖ” 2026-04-19

Morphology correctness pass + template phrasing polish. The last stretch before the v1.0.0 MVP cut.

### FST Instrumental fix (two bugs, one mechanism)

The `INSTRUMENTAL` suffix template previously used the harmony-alternating archiphoneme `{E}`, but Kazakh Instrumental is actually invariant in vowel вҖ” always `-РјРөРҪ/-РұРөРҪ/-РҝРөРҪ`, never `-РјР°РҪ/-РұР°РҪ/-РҝР°РҪ`. Replaced with a literal `Рө`:

```diff
- const INSTRUMENTAL: SuffixTemplate = &[Arch(M), Arch(E), Literal('РҪ')];
+ const INSTRUMENTAL: SuffixTemplate = &[Arch(M), Literal('Рө'), Literal('РҪ')];
```

Separately, `realise_m` flipped `Nasal вҶ’ 'Рұ'` which produced `РјТұТ“Р°Р»С–РјРұРөРҪ`. Fixed to `Nasal вҶ’ 'Рј'`, giving standard `РјТұТ“Р°Р»С–РјРјРөРҪ`. Voiced obstruent вҶ’ `Рұ` (rare but preserved).

Before / after samples:

| root | pre-v0.9.9 | v0.9.9 |
|---|---|---|
| РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢ | РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢРјР°РҪ вқҢ | РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢРјРөРҪ вң“ |
| РҗСҒСӮР°РҪР° | РҗСҒСӮР°РҪР°РјР°РҪ вқҢ | РҗСҒСӮР°РҪР°РјРөРҪ вң“ |
| РјТұТ“Р°Р»С–Рј | РјТұТ“Р°Р»С–РјРұРөРҪ вқҢ | РјТұТ“Р°Р»С–РјРјРөРҪ вң“ |
| Р”Р¶РҫС…РҪ | Р”Р¶РҫС…РҪРұР°РҪ вқҢ | Р”Р¶РҫС…РҪРјРөРҪ вң“ |
| Р”УҷСғР»РөСӮ | Р”УҷСғР»РөСӮРҝРөРҪ вң“ | Р”УҷСғР»РөСӮРҝРөРҪ вң“ |
| РјРөРәСӮРөРҝ | РјРөРәСӮРөРҝРҝРөРҪ вң“ | РјРөРәСӮРөРҝРҝРөРҪ вң“ |

### Cleanup

`Archiphoneme::E` and `realise_e` were only used by the Instrumental template and are now dead. Removed both per YAGNI.

### FST regression tests

Added 6 unit tests to `morphotactics` covering every consonant-class path of the new Instrumental + the back/front vowel invariance. These lock in the fix so future archiphoneme refactors can't re-break it.

- `noun_instrumental_front_consonant_final` (Р”УҷСғР»РөСӮ вҶ’ РҝРөРҪ)
- `noun_instrumental_back_consonant_final` (Р”Р¶РҫС…РҪ вҶ’ РјРөРҪ)
- `noun_instrumental_back_vowel_stem_stays_Рө_not_Р°` (РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢ вҶ’ РјРөРҪ, regression)
- `noun_instrumental_vowel_final_stem` (РұР°Р»Р°, СӮР°Сғ вҶ’ РјРөРҪ)
- `noun_instrumental_voiceless_final_gives_РҝРөРҪ` (РјРөРәСӮРөРҝ вҶ’ РҝРөРҪ)
- `noun_instrumental_nasal_final_gives_РјРөРҪ_not_РұРөРҪ` (РјТұТ“Р°Р»С–Рј вҶ’ РјРөРҪ, regression)

### Template polish pass

Dropped awkward / filler templates and replaced with context-specific acknowledgements:

| key | removed | added |
|---|---|---|
| `statement_of_age` | `СӮТҜСҒС–РҪРҙС–Рј`, `Р¶Р°СҒСӢТЈСӢР· РәРөР»С–СҒСӮС–` | `ТӣСғР°СӮСӮСӢ РәРөР·РөТЈС–ТЈС–Р·` |
| `statement_of_location` | `СӮТҜСҒС–РҪРҙС–Рј` | `СӮР°РјР°СҲР° У©Р»РәРө` |
| `statement_of_occupation` | `РјР°ТӣСӮР°РҪСӢСҲРҝРөРҪ` (solo) | `РјР°ТӣСӮР°РҪР°СӮСӢРҪ Р¶ТұРјСӢСҒ` |
| `statement_of_weather` | `СӮТҜСҒС–РҪРҙС–Рј` | `СӮР°РұРёТ“Р°СӮ РјРөР·РіС–Р»С–РҪРө Р»Р°Р№СӢТӣ` |

"СӮТҜСҒС–РҪРҙС–Рј" as a solo response felt generic/repetitive. Replaced with phrases that match the topic of the user's statement.

### Numbers

- **FST unit tests:** 84 (was 78) вҖ” 6 new Instrumental regressions
- **Dialog end-to-end tests:** 81 (unchanged, assertion sets updated)
- **Workspace tests:** **271 passing**, 4 ignored, 0 failing
- **Foundation CI:** passing

### Known v0.9.9 limitations

- Silent English `h` still not special-cased in transliteration (`John вҶ’ Р”Р¶РҫС…РҪ` rather than the conventional `Р”Р¶РҫРҪ`). Cosmetic; FST synthesis works fine on either.
- Native-speaker review has NOT been conducted вҖ” the polish was a phrasing pass by inspection, not formal review. A real review is queued for post-v1.0.0 refinement.

## [0.9.8] вҖ” 2026-04-19

Slot syntax completes the noun-feature surface (Derivation + Possessive), Latin names get transliterated before FST synthesis, and templates gain a layer of cross-slot personalisation that uses multiple remembered entities in a single response.

### Slot syntax: full noun-feature coverage

Adds 11 derivation tokens and 7 possessive tokens to `parse_noun_features`:

```text
{root|agent}           вҶ’ Agent (-СҲСӢ/-СҲС–)
{root|abstract}        вҶ’ Abstract (-Р»СӢТӣ/-Р»С–Рә)
{root|privative}       вҶ’ Privative (-СҒСӢР·/-СҒС–Р·)
{root|endowed}         вҶ’ Endowed (-Р»СӢ/-Р»С–)
{root|similative}      вҶ’ Similative (-РҙР°Р№/-РҙРөР№)
{root|comparative}     вҶ’ Comparative (-СҖР°Тӣ/-СҖРөРә)
{root|verbalnoun}      вҶ’ VerbalNoun (-Сғ)
{root|actionnoun}      вҶ’ ActionNoun (-СӢРј/-С–Рј)
{root|diminutive}      вҶ’ Diminutive (-СҲСӢТӣ/-СҲС–Рә)
{root|ordinal}         вҶ’ Ordinal (-РҪСҲСӢ/-РҪСҲС–)
{root|collective}      вҶ’ Collective (-РөСғ/-Р°Сғ)

{root|p1sg}            вҶ’ P1Sg (my)
{root|p2sg}            вҶ’ P2SgPolite (your, polite default)
{root|p2sg_inf}        вҶ’ P2SgInformal
{root|p3}              вҶ’ P3 (his/her)
{root|p1pl}            вҶ’ P1Pl (our)
{root|p2pl}            вҶ’ P2PlPolite
{root|p2pl_inf}        вҶ’ P2PlInformal
```

Combinations work as you'd expect: `{name|agent+p1sg+dative}` yields root вҶ’ Agent derivation вҶ’ P1Sg possessive вҶ’ Dative case, all in one synthesis pass.

### Latin вҶ’ Cyrillic transliteration

New module `adam_dialog::transliteration` converts Latin proper names to Kazakh Cyrillic BEFORE `synthesise_noun` when a template requests morphology on a non-Cyrillic slot value. v0.9.6 guarded against garbled output by falling back to plain substitution; v0.9.8 replaces that fallback with real transliteration so FST inflection actually runs on foreign names.

| Latin input | transliterated |
|---|---|
| `Anna` | РҗРҪРҪР° |
| `Tom` | РўРҫРј |
| `John` | Р”Р¶РҫС…РҪ |
| `Zhanna` | Р–Р°РҪРҪР° |
| `Sharon` | РЁР°СҖРҫРҪ |
| `Charlie` | Р§Р°СҖР»РёРө |

Conservative single-letter + digraph mapping: `sh/ch/zh/kh/gh/ph/th/ts/yo/ya/yu/ye` as digraphs, rest letter-by-letter (`j вҶ’ РҙР¶`, `c вҶ’ Рә`, `x вҶ’ РәСҒ`, `y вҶ’ Р№`). Silent `h` in English is **not** special-cased (`John вҶ’ Р”Р¶РҫС…РҪ`, not `Р”Р¶РҫРҪ`) вҖ” intentionally conservative.

**Policy:** plain `{name}` substitution still keeps the user's original spelling ("СҒУҷР»РөРј John"). Only `{name|features}` triggers transliteration вҶ’ synthesis.

### Cross-slot templates

New templates reference multiple session entities in one response. Eligible only when every slot is fillable; plain variants stay available otherwise.

| key | new templates |
|---|---|
| `ask_how_are_you` | `"Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢРјСӢРҪ {name}, Р°Р» СҒС–Р· ТӣР°Р»Р°Р№СҒСӢР·"`, `"Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢРјСӢРҪ, СҖР°С…РјРөСӮ {name}"` |
| `statement_of_age` | `"{name}, {age} Р¶Р°СҒСӮР°СҒСӢР·, СӮР°РјР°СҲР°"`, `"{name}, {age} Р¶Р°СҒ вҖ” РәРөСҖРөРјРөСӮ РәРөР·РөТЈ"` |
| `statement_of_occupation` | `"{name}, {occupation} вҖ” ТӣТұСҖРјРөСӮСӮС– РәУҷСҒС–Рҝ"`, `"{name}, СҒС–Р· {city|locative} {occupation} РөРәРөРҪСҒС–Р·"` |
| `compliment` | `"СҖР°С…РјРөСӮ {name}"`, `"СҖР°С…РјРөСӮ {name}, СҒС–Р· РҙРө СӮР°РјР°СҲР°СҒСӢР·"` |

The triple-slot `"{name}, СҒС–Р· {city|locative} {occupation} РөРәРөРҪСҒС–Р·"` only fires after the user has stated all three entities вҖ” "Р”УҷСғР»РөСӮ, СҒС–Р· РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢРҙР° УҷРҪСҲС– РөРәРөРҪСҒС–Р·".

### Tests

81 dialog end-to-end pairs (up from 78), 3 new cross-slot tests covering (name+ask_how_are_you), (name+age), and (name+city+occupation triple). 23 lib-level unit tests (13 slot_syntax + 6 transliteration + 4 planner).

Workspace: **265 passing**, 4 ignored, 0 failing. Foundation CI green.

### Known v0.9.8 limitations

- Silent `h` in English is not special-cased (`John вҶ’ Р”Р¶РҫС…РҪ` rather than the standard spelling `Р”Р¶РҫРҪ`). Good enough for FST synthesis; a more polished transliterator is v1.0.0+ work.
- Back-vowel instrumental harmony (`РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢ` вҶ’ `РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢРјР°РҪ` rather than `РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢРјРөРҪ`) is a pre-existing FST quirk вҖ” check `INSTRUMENTAL` template archiphoneme `E` resolution. Out of scope for the dialog layer.
- Derivation + Possessive tokens are fully parseable; the current template set uses only a handful of the 18 feature tokens. Template authors have the full surface available when needed.

## [0.9.7] вҖ” 2026-04-19

Lexicon-backed occupation recognition. The fixed 6-form table (`РјТұТ“Р°Р»С–РјРјС–РҪ вҶ’ РјТұТ“Р°Р»С–Рј` and five others) is replaced with generic 1sg-copula stripping + noun lookup against the 14 k-entry Lexicon. Any noun in the Lexicon ending in a 1sg predicate suffix (`-РјСӢРҪ/-РјС–РҪ/-РҝСӢРҪ/-РҝС–РҪ/-РұСӢРҪ/-РұС–РҪ`) is now recognised.

### What now works

```
$ adam_chat
> РјРөРҪ Р°ТӣСӢРҪРјСӢРҪ           вҶ’ СҒС–Р· Р°ТӣСӢРҪ РөРәРөРҪСҒС–Р·            (new: Р°ТӣСӢРҪ is in Lexicon, not in the old table)
> РјРөРҪ УҷРҪСҲС–РјС–РҪ           вҶ’ УҷРҪСҲС–Р»РөСҖ вҖ” ТӣР°Р¶РөСӮСӮС– РјР°РјР°РҪРҙСӢТӣ  (FST plural on the new extract)
> РјРөРҪ Т“Р°Р»СӢРјРјСӢРҪ          вҶ’ СҒС–Р· Т“Р°Р»СӢРј РөРәРөРҪСҒС–Р·
> РјРөРҪ СҒСғСҖРөСӮСҲС–РјС–РҪ        вҶ’ СҒС–Р· СҒСғСҖРөСӮСҲС– РөРәРөРҪСҒС–Р·
> Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢРјСӢРҪ              вҶ’ Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢ РөРәРөРҪ                  (POS filter вҶ’ wellbeing, not occupation)
```

### Public API additions

- `adam_dialog::interpret_text_with_lexicon(input, parses, Option<&LexiconV1>) -> Intent`
- `adam_dialog::semantics::interpret_text_with_lexicon` (module-level)

The original `interpret_text(input, parses)` is now a thin wrapper that calls the lexicon-aware variant with `None` вҖ” existing callers keep working.

### Implementation detail

```rust
fn strip_copula_and_lookup_noun(tokens: &[String], lex: &LexiconV1) -> Option<String> {
    const COPULA_SUFFIXES: &[&str] = &["РјСӢРҪ", "РјС–РҪ", "РҝСӢРҪ", "РҝС–РҪ", "РұСӢРҪ", "РұС–РҪ"];
    for t in tokens {
        for suffix in COPULA_SUFFIXES {
            let Some(root) = strip_suffix_chars(t, suffix) else { continue };
            if root.chars().count() < 2 { continue; }
            if let Some(entry) = lex.get(&root) {
                if entry.part_of_speech == "noun" {
                    return Some(root);
                }
            }
        }
    }
    None
}
```

- **POS filter** rejects adjectives (`Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢ`, `Р¶Р°РјР°РҪ`) so "Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢРјСӢРҪ" still routes to StatementOfWellbeing.
- **Min-length 2** guards against stripping into short function words.
- **Char-count indexing** keeps UTF-8 boundaries safe.

`respond`, `respond_with_repo`, and `Conversation::turn` all pass the lexicon into the new recogniser automatically.

### Tests

78 dialog end-to-end pairs (up from 73), 5 new:
- 1 positive case covering `Р°ТӣСӢРҪ` (out-of-table noun)
- 1 bulk test for `УҷРҪСҲС– / Т“Р°Р»СӢРј / СҒСғСҖРөСӮСҲС–`
- 1 adjective-negative-case ensuring `Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢРјСӢРҪ` stays wellbeing
- 1 unknown-root case (`xyzzyРјСӢРҪ` вҶ’ must not become occupation)
- 1 multi-turn absorption test (lexicon-derived occupation persists to session)

Workspace: **251 passing**, 4 ignored, 0 failing. Foundation CI green.

### Known v0.9.7 limitations

- LatinвҶ’Cyrillic transliteration is intentionally NOT shipped. Latin names ("John") continue to bypass `{name|features}` FST synthesis via the v0.9.6 safety guard. Transliteration lands in v0.9.8 alongside broader slot-syntax enrichment.
- Lexicon coverage is the cap вҖ” occupations not in the 14 k-entry Lexicon (`С„РёР»РҫСҒРҫС„`, `РҝСҖРҫРіСҖР°РјРјРёСҒСӮ`) still don't extract. Data-layer expansion is orthogonal work.

## [0.9.6] вҖ” 2026-04-19

Multilingual recogniser surface. The model now reads Kazakh, Russian, and English input across all 25 intents and replies exclusively in Kazakh. This is NOT translation вҖ” the core pipeline stays deterministic Kazakh-only. The expansion is purely at the recogniser layer: more surface forms map to the same Intent taxonomy.

### Triggers added (per intent)

- **Greeting** (casual/polite/time-of-day): `hi/hello/hey`, `РҝСҖРёРІРөСӮ`, `Р·РҙСҖР°РІСҒСӮРІСғР№СӮРө`, `РҙРҫРұСҖРҫРө СғСӮСҖРҫ/РҙРөРҪСҢ/РІРөСҮРөСҖ`, `good morning/afternoon/evening/day`
- **Farewell**: `bye/goodbye/see you`, `РҙРҫ СҒРІРёРҙР°РҪРёСҸ/РҝРҫРәР°`
- **Affirmation**: `yes/yeah/yep/sure/ok`, `РҙР°/РәРҫРҪРөСҮРҪРҫ/Р°РіР°`
- **Negation**: `no/nope/nah`, `РҪРөСӮ`
- **Thanks**: `thanks/thank you`, `СҒРҝР°СҒРёРұРҫ/РұРҫР»СҢСҲРҫРө СҒРҝР°СҒРёРұРҫ`
- **Apology**: `sorry/excuse me`, `РёР·РІРёРҪРё/РёР·РІРёРҪРёСӮРө/РҝСҖРҫСҒСӮРё`
- **AskHowAreYou**: `how are you/how's it`, `РәР°Рә РҙРөР»Р°/РәР°Рә СӮСӢ/РәР°Рә РІСӢ`
- **StatementOfWellbeing**: `fine/great/i'm good/i'm fine`, `С…РҫСҖРҫСҲРҫ/РҪРҫСҖРјР°Р»СҢРҪРҫ/РҫСӮР»РёСҮРҪРҫ`
- **AskName**: `what is/what's your name`, `РәР°Рә СӮРөРұСҸ/РІР°СҒ Р·РҫРІСғСӮ`
- **StatementOfName**: four new patterns вҖ”
  - `meРҪСҸ Р·РҫРІСғСӮ <N>`, `РјРҫС‘ РёРјСҸ <N>` (Russian)
  - `my name is <N>`, `call me <N>`, `hi i am <N>` (English; bare "I am X" is ambiguous so requires a leading greet token)
- **AskAge**: `how old are you`, `СҒРәРҫР»СҢРәРҫ СӮРөРұРө/РІР°Рј Р»РөСӮ`
- **AskLocation**: `where are you from / where do you live`, `РҫСӮРәСғРҙР° СӮСӢ/РІСӢ`
- **AskOccupation**: `what do you do / what's your job`, `РәРөРј СҖР°РұРҫСӮР°РөСҲСҢ/Р·Р°РҪРёРјР°РөСҲСҢСҒСҸ`
- **AskWeather**: `how's / what's the weather`, `РәР°РәР°СҸ РҝРҫРіРҫРҙР°`
- **AskTime**: `what time is it / what's the time`, `СҒРәРҫР»СҢРәРҫ РІСҖРөРјРөРҪРё/РәРҫСӮРҫСҖСӢР№ СҮР°СҒ`
- **Compliment**: `great/awesome/wonderful/excellent/well done`, `РјРҫР»РҫРҙРөСҶ/РҫСӮР»РёСҮРҪРҫ/Р·РҙРҫСҖРҫРІРҫ`
- **Request**: `please/need help/can you help`, `РҝРҫР¶Р°Р»СғР№СҒСӮР°/РҝРҫРјРҫРіРёСӮРө/РҝРҫРјРҫРіРё`
- **WellWishes**: `good luck/all the best`, `СғРҙР°СҮРё/РІСҒРөРіРҫ РҪР°РёР»СғСҮСҲРөРіРҫ`

### Safety guard for Latin roots

FST phonology is tuned for Kazakh Cyrillic. Feeding `"John"` into `synthesise_noun(..., Case::Instrumental)` would produce garbled `"JohnРјР°РҪ"`. The realiser now detects non-Cyrillic roots and falls back to plain substitution вҖ” no suffix attached. Output: `"John СӮР°РҪСӢСҒТӣР°РҪСӢРјР° ТӣСғР°РҪСӢСҲСӮСӢРјСӢРҪ"` rather than hallucinated morphology.

### Ordering change

`StatementOfName` is now checked BEFORE `Greeting` in `interpret_text`. This prevents `"hi i am John"` from misfiring as a bare Casual greeting. All StatementOfName patterns (Р°СӮСӢРј/РөСҒС–РјС–Рј/Р·РҫРІСғСӮ/my name is/call me/[greet] i am X) are explicit enough to rule out false positives.

### Tests

73 dialog end-to-end pairs (up from 56), 17 new:
- 10 recogniser triggers (greetings Г— 3, farewell, affirmation, negation, thanks, apology, ask-how-are-you, ask-name)
- 4 self-introduction patterns (Russian `Р·РҫРІСғСӮ`, English `my name is` / `call me` / `hi i am`)
- 2 output-is-Kazakh invariants (Russian input вҶ’ Cyrillic-only output; Latin name вҶ’ no FST suffix)
- 1 multilingual multi-turn conversation flow

Workspace: **245 passing**, 4 ignored, 0 failing. Foundation CI green.

### Known v0.9.6 limitations

- Recogniser catches the common phrasings. Edge cases (British contractions "init", ru-ua mix, Kazakh with Latin transliteration "salem") are not handled.
- Latin names stay un-inflected in templates requesting `{name|features}`. Transliteration to Cyrillic (e.g. `John` вҶ’ `Р”Р¶РҫРҪ`) would let the FST synthesise properly вҖ” possible future work.
- No output-language switching: Russian / English input still gets Kazakh output by design.

## [0.9.5] вҖ” 2026-04-19

FST-backed slot expansion. Templates can now emit `{slot|features}` atoms; the realiser synthesises grammatical forms via `adam_kernel_fst::morphotactics::synthesise_noun` instead of plain text substitution. Cross-slot templates (using multiple slots in one response) drop in naturally because of the v0.8.5 template-fillability filter.

### New slot syntax

```text
{slot}                    вҖ” plain: substitute slot value verbatim
{slot|feat1+feat2+...}    вҖ” FST: synthesise via morphotactics
```

Feature tokens (case-insensitive, `+`-separated, unknown tokens ignored):

| token | вҶ’ field |
|---|---|
| `nominative / nom` | `case = Nominative` |
| `genitive / gen` | `case = Genitive` |
| `dative / dat` | `case = Dative` |
| `accusative / acc` | `case = Accusative` |
| `locative / loc` | `case = Locative` |
| `ablative / abl` | `case = Ablative` |
| `instrumental / inst` | `case = Instrumental` |
| `singular / sg` | `number = Singular` |
| `plural / pl` | `number = Plural` |

### Examples of what now works

| template | filled | rendered |
|---|---|---|
| `{city\|locative} СӮТұСҖР°СҒСӢР· РұР°` | city=РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢ | РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢРҙР° СӮТұСҖР°СҒСӢР· РұР° |
| `{city\|ablative} С…Р°РұР°СҖ Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢ РјР°` | city=РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢ | РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢРҙР°РҪ С…Р°РұР°СҖ Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢ РјР° |
| `{name\|instrumental} СӮР°РҪСӢСҒТӣР°РҪСӢРјР° ТӣСғР°РҪСӢСҲСӮСӢРјСӢРҪ` | name=Р”УҷСғР»РөСӮ | Р”УҷСғР»РөСӮРҝРөРҪ СӮР°РҪСӢСҒТӣР°РҪСӢРјР° ТӣСғР°РҪСӢСҲСӮСӢРјСӢРҪ |
| `{occupation\|plural} вҖ” ТӣР°Р¶РөСӮСӮС– РјР°РјР°РҪРҙСӢТӣ` | occupation=РјТұТ“Р°Р»С–Рј | РјТұТ“Р°Р»С–РјРҙРөСҖ вҖ” ТӣР°Р¶РөСӮСӮС– РјР°РјР°РҪРҙСӢТӣ |
| `СҒУҷР»РөРј {name}, {city\|ablative} С…Р°РұР°СҖ Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢ РјР°` | name=Р”УҷСғР»РөСӮ, city=РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢ | СҒУҷР»РөРј Р”УҷСғР»РөСӮ, РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢРҙР°РҪ С…Р°РұР°СҖ Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢ РјР° |

The last one is a cross-slot template: the planner only considers it when BOTH `name` and `city` are in session.

### Public API additions

- `adam_dialog::slot_syntax::{parse_placeholder, parse_noun_features}`

### TOML changes (v1.toml version вҶ’ 0.9.5)

- `greeting.casual`, `greeting.polite` each get a cross-slot `{name}+{city|abl/loc}` variant.
- `statement_of_name` gets `{name|instrumental}` variants.
- `statement_of_location` gets 3 FST-backed variants: locative / ablative / dative.
- `statement_of_occupation` gets plural + dative variants.

### Tests

56 dialog end-to-end pairs (up from 52), 4 new covering every FST-backed expansion path + the cross-slot greeting. 7 slot-syntax unit tests + 1 additional planner unit test. 1 doc-test.

Workspace: **229 passing**, 4 ignored, 0 failing.

### Known v0.9.5 limitations

- Feature parser covers noun `case + number` only. Derivation and possessive are v1.0.0 additions.
- Occupation recogniser still uses the fixed 6-form table; generic 1sg-copula stripping via FST lookup is queued for v0.9.8.
- No verb slot expansion вҖ” `{root|verb_features}` would need a different synthesiser dispatch.

## [0.9.0] вҖ” 2026-04-19

Full entity absorption: every social-topic statement now contributes an extractable entity to session state. Age is parsed from Kazakh numerals (1вҖ“99), city from ablative/locative case stripping, occupation from 1sg-copula stripping.

### Intent payload changes (breaking)

- `StatementOfAge` вҶ’ `StatementOfAge { years: Option<u32> }`
- `StatementOfLocation` вҶ’ `StatementOfLocation { city: Option<String> }`
- `StatementOfOccupation` вҶ’ `StatementOfOccupation { occupation: Option<String> }`

`None` means the intent matched on keywords but the entity wasn't parseable ("Р¶Р°СҒСӢРј Р¶Р°СҒСӢСҖСӢРҪ").

### Numeral parser

`semantics::parse_kazakh_age` handles:

- Bare tens: РҫРҪ (10), Р¶РёСӢСҖРјР° (20), РҫСӮСӢР· (30), ТӣСӢСҖСӢТӣ (40), РөР»Сғ (50), Р°Р»РҝСӢСҒ (60), Р¶РөСӮРҝС–СҒ (70), СҒРөРәСҒРөРҪ (80), СӮРҫТӣСҒР°РҪ (90)
- Bare units: РұС–СҖ (1) вҖҰ СӮРҫТ“СӢР· (9)
- Compound forms: "РҫСӮСӢР· РұРөСҒ" (35), "Р¶РёСӢСҖРјР° РөРәС–" (22)
- Literal digit strings: "30"

### Entity extraction

- `StatementOfLocation`: strips ablative+copula (`-РҙР°РҪРјСӢРҪ/-РҙРөРҪРјС–РҪ/-СӮР°РҪРјСӢРҪ/-СӮРөРҪРјС–РҪ`) or locative (`-РҙР°/-РҙРө/-СӮР°/-СӮРө`) to recover the city root. Preserves original casing: "РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢРҙР°РҪРјСӢРҪ" вҶ’ "РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢ"; "Р°СҒСӮР°РҪР°РҙР° СӮТұСҖР°РјСӢРҪ" вҶ’ "Р°СҒСӮР°РҪР°".
- `StatementOfOccupation`: matches a fixed table of 1sg-copula forms and emits the stripped noun root: "РјТұТ“Р°Р»С–РјРјС–РҪ" вҶ’ "РјТұТ“Р°Р»С–Рј".

### Session wiring

- `Conversation::absorb_entities` and `planner::extract_slots` both consume the new fields and populate `{age}`, `{city}`, `{occupation}` slots (in addition to `{name}` from v0.8.5).
- Once absorbed, the entities persist across turns just like `{name}` does.

### Templates (TOML v0.9.0)

New personalised variants in `statement_of_age`, `statement_of_location`, `statement_of_occupation`:

- `statement_of_age`: `"{age} Р¶Р°СҒ вҖ” СӮР°РјР°СҲР° РәРөР·РөТЈ"`, `"Р¶Р°СҒСӢТЈСӢР· {age} РөРәРөРҪ"`
- `statement_of_location`: `"{city} вҖ” УҷРҙРөРјС– ТӣР°Р»Р°"`, `"{city} СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РәУ©Рҝ РөСҒСӮС–РҙС–Рј"`
- `statement_of_occupation`: `"{occupation} вҖ” ТӣТұСҖРјРөСӮСӮС– РәУҷСҒС–Рҝ"`, `"СҒС–Р· {occupation} РөРәРөРҪСҒС–Р·"`

Only eligible when the slot can be filled; untouched by templates stay canonical for utterances without extractable entities.

### Tests

52 dialog end-to-end pairs (up from 44), 8 new:

- 3 intent tests covering age numeral parsing (bare, compound, none)
- 2 location extraction tests (ablative + locative)
- 1 occupation extraction test
- 1 multi-turn absorption test (age+city+occupation into session)
- 1 multi-turn personalisation test (numeral appears in response)

Workspace: **215 passing**, 4 ignored, 0 failing.

### Known v0.9.0 limitations

- Occupation extraction uses a fixed 6-form table. Regular 1sg-copula stripping via FST lookup lands in v0.9.5 together with `{root|features}` slot expansion.
- Location extraction is surface-pattern only вҖ” no FST lookup yet, so misspelt or inflected cities ("ТҡСӢР·СӢР»РҫСҖРҙР°РҙР°РҪРјСӢРҪ") get a raw root rather than normalised lexicon lemma.
- No cross-slot templates yet (`"{name}, СҒС–Р· {age} Р¶Р°СҒСӮР°СҒСӢР· РұР°?"` вҖ” possible but unwritten).

## [0.8.5] вҖ” 2026-04-19

First session state in the dialog layer. The new [`Conversation`] struct accumulates entities across turns, so a user who introduces themselves once gets greeted by name on every subsequent turn.

- `Conversation { session: HashMap<String, String> }` with `new()`, `turn(input, lex, repo, seed) -> String`, `reset()`.
- `planner::plan_response_with_session(intent, seed, repo, session)` merges per-turn slots with session slots (per-turn wins on collision) and filters candidate templates down to those whose every `{slot}` reference is satisfiable. If filtering empties the pool, falls back to the full pool (visible `{slot}` is better than a crash).
- `plan_response_with_repo` is now defined in terms of `plan_response_with_session(вҖҰ, &HashMap::new())` вҖ” no behaviour change for existing callers.
- Greeting families get `{name}` variants:
  - `greeting.casual`: СҒУҷР»РөРј / СҒУҷР»РөРј РҙРҫСҒСӮСӢРј / **СҒУҷР»РөРј {name}**
  - `greeting.polite`: СҒУҷР»РөРјРөСӮСҒС–Р· РұРө / Р°СҖРјСӢСҒСӢР· / **СҒУҷР»РөРјРөСӮСҒС–Р· РұРө {name}**
  - `greeting.morning` / `.day` / `.evening` all get a corresponding `{name}` variant.
- `adam_chat` CLI now holds a single `Conversation` for the whole REPL session; `--trace` mode dumps the live session map.
- Ordering: `Conversation::turn` absorbs entities BEFORE planning, so the SAME turn that says "РјРөРҪС–ТЈ Р°СӮСӢРј X" can already receive a response containing `{name}` substituted to X.

Tests: 44 dialog end-to-end pairs (+3 session tests covering persistence, non-persistence when no name said, and `reset()`). 3 planner unit tests for `template_is_fillable`. Workspace: **204 passing**, 4 ignored, 0 failing.

Known v0.8.5 limitations:

- Only `name` is persisted across turns вҖ” `age`, `location`, `occupation`, `family` recognition exists but their entities aren't yet extracted into session. That lands in v0.9.0 together with numeric extraction.
- No context-aware responses: the model doesn't say "РјРөРҪ СҒС–Р·РҙС– Р”УҷСғР»РөСӮ РҙРөРҝ Р°СӮР°РҙСӢРј, РёУҷ?" to confirm, or disambiguate "Р”УҷСғР»РөСӮ" the name from "РҙУҷСғР»РөСӮ" the concept.

## [0.8.0] вҖ” 2026-04-19

Dialog layer widened from 10 to **25 intents**. First entity extraction lands: the user's name is pulled out of self-introduction patterns and substituted into the response template.

New intents (+15, recognisers in `semantics.rs`):

- `StatementOfName { name }` вҖ” "РјРөРҪС–ТЈ Р°СӮСӢРј X" / "РјРөРҪС– X РҙРөРҝ Р°СӮР°Р№РҙСӢ" / "РөСҒС–РјС–Рј X"
- `AskAge` / `StatementOfAge` вҖ” Р¶Р°СҒСӢТЈ РҪРөСҲРө / РјРөРҪС–ТЈ Р¶Р°СҒСӢРј РҫСӮСӢР·
- `AskLocation` / `StatementOfLocation` вҖ” ТӣР°Р№РҙР° СӮТұСҖР°СҒСӢР· / РјРөРҪ РҗР»РјР°СӮСӢРҙР°РҪРјСӢРҪ
- `AskOccupation` / `StatementOfOccupation` вҖ” РҪРөРјРөРҪ Р°Р№РҪР°Р»СӢСҒР°СҒСӢТЈ / РјРөРҪ РјТұТ“Р°Р»С–РјРјС–РҪ
- `AskFamily` / `StatementOfFamily` вҖ” РұР°Р»Р°Р»Р°СҖСӢТЈ РұР°СҖ РјР° / РјРөРҪС–ТЈ РұР°Р»Р°Рј РұР°СҖ
- `AskWeather` / `StatementOfWeather` вҖ” Р°СғР° СҖР°Р№СӢ ТӣР°Р»Р°Р№ / РұТҜРіС–РҪ СҒСғСӢТӣ
- `AskTime` вҖ” СҒР°Т“Р°СӮ РҪРөСҲРө
- `Compliment` вҖ” Р¶Р°СҖР°Р№СҒСӢТЈ / РәРөСҖРөРјРөСӮ / СӮР°РјР°СҲР°
- `Request` вҖ” У©СӮС–РҪРөРјС–РҪ / РәУ©РјРөРәСӮРөСҒС–ТЈС–Р·СҲС–
- `WellWishes` вҖ” СҒУҷСӮСӮС–Р»С–Рә / Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢ РәТҜРҪ СӮС–Р»РөР№РјС–РҪ

Entity extraction + slot expansion:

- `semantics::detect_statement_of_name` extracts the PersonName from three surface patterns (Р°СӮСӢРј / РјРөРҪС– X РҙРөРҝ Р°СӮР°Р№РҙСӢ / РөСҒС–РјС–Рј) with case-preserving capitalisation.
- `ResponsePlan` gains a `slots: HashMap<String, String>` field populated by the planner from the Intent.
- `realiser::realise` substitutes `{slot}` placeholders in the chosen template; templates like `"ТӣРҫСҲ РәРөР»РҙС–ТЈС–Р· {name}"` now personalise.

Ordering subtlety: Statement-of-X is checked BEFORE Ask-of-X in every topic pair вҖ” a 1st-person marker ("РәРөР»РҙС–Рј", "СӮТұСҖР°РјСӢРҪ", "Р¶Р°СҒСӢРј") unambiguously identifies the user as stating, not asking. Without this, "ТӣР°Р№РҙР°РҪ РәРөР»РҙС–Рј" would hit `AskLocation` first (because of "ТӣР°Р№РҙР°РҪ").

TOML repository: +15 families вҶ’ 29 families total, version = "0.8.0".

Tests: 41 dialog end-to-end pairs (up from 23), 18 new covering recognition, slot substitution, and planner coverage for every new intent. Workspace: **201 passing**, 4 ignored, 0 failing.

Known v0.8.0 limitations (by design, not bugs):

- No session state: the model doesn't remember the user's name across turns. Adding a `Conversation` struct lands in v0.8.5.
- Numeric extraction (age, time) is a v0.9.0 concern; StatementOfAge templates acknowledge generically.
- Templates are still literal phrases with optional `{slot}` text replacement. FST-backed `{root|features}` atoms land in v0.9.0.
- Templates have not been native-speaker reviewed вҖ” stiffness is expected; v0.9.0 tightens phrasing.

## [0.7.5] вҖ” 2026-04-19

Dialog layer widened from 5 to **10 intents** and templates externalised to TOML.

New intents (+recognisers in `semantics.rs`):

- `Thanks` вҖ” СҖР°С…РјРөСӮ / РәУ©Рҝ СҖР°С…РјРөСӮ / СҖР°ТӣРјРөСӮ вҶ’ РҫТӣР°СҒСӢ Р¶РҫТӣ, РөСҲСӮРөТЈРө РөРјРөСҒ, СҖРёР·Р°РјСӢРҪ
- `Apology` вҖ” РәРөСҲС–СҖС–ТЈС–Р· / Т“Р°С„Сғ РөСӮ вҶ’ РөСҲСӮРөТЈРө РөРјРөСҒ, РјРөР№Р»С–, СӮТҜРә РөСӮРҝРөР№РҙС–
- `AskHowAreYou` вҖ” ТӣР°Р»Р°Р№СҒСӢТЈ / ТӣР°Р»Р°Р№СҒСӢР· / Р¶Р°Т“РҙР°Р№СӢТЈСӢР· ТӣР°Р»Р°Р№ вҶ’ Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢРјСӢРҪ СҖР°С…РјРөСӮ, Р¶Р°РјР°РҪ РөРјРөСҒРҝС–РҪ, Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢ Р°Р» СҒС–Р· ТӣР°Р»Р°Р№СҒСӢР·
- `StatementOfWellbeing` вҖ” Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢРјСӢРҪ / Р¶Р°РјР°РҪ РөРјРөСҒ вҶ’ Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢ РөРәРөРҪ, ТӣСғР°РҪР°РјСӢРҪ, Р°Р» СҒС–Р· ТӣР°Р»Р°Р№СҒСӢР·
- `AskName` вҖ” Р°СӮСӢТЈ РәС–Рј / РөСҒС–РјС–ТЈС–Р· ТӣР°Р»Р°Р№ вҶ’ РјРөРҪС–ТЈ Р°СӮСӢРј Р°РҙР°Рј, РјРөРҪС– Р°РҙР°Рј РҙРөРҝ Р°СӮР°Р№РҙСӢ

Templates are now loaded from `data/dialog/templates/v1.toml` (14 families, one per intent-key), not hardcoded in `planner.rs`. `TemplateRepository::load_default()` auto-discovers the TOML file; `hardcoded_fallback()` preserves MVP guarantees when the file is missing.

Public API additions:

- `adam_dialog::TemplateRepository` + `TemplateError`
- `adam_dialog::respond_with_repo(input, lex, repo, seed)` вҖ” explicit-repo variant of `respond`
- `adam_dialog::plan_response_with_repo(intent, seed, repo)` + `intent_key(intent)`

`adam_chat` REPL now loads the TOML repo at startup (falls back to hardcoded if missing) and prints family count on stderr.

Ordering subtlety in the semantic dispatcher: `Thanks`/`Apology` are checked BEFORE `Affirmation` so "СҖР°С…РјРөСӮ" (thanks) can't accidentally fall into affirmation if later extended.

Tests: 23 dialog end-to-end pairs (up from 15), 8 new covering all 5 new intents. Workspace totals: **183 passing**, 4 ignored, 0 failing.

Known v0.7.5 limitations (by design, not bugs):

- Templates are still literal phrases; slotted templates with `(root, features)` atoms land in v0.8.0.
- No entity extraction (own name from "РјРөРҪС–ТЈ Р°СӮСӢРј X" вҶ’ greeting back by name).
- 10 intents cover greetings + basic social politeness; v0.8.0 widens to 25.
- No multi-turn state.

## [0.7.0] вҖ” 2026-04-19

First iteration of the predictable dialog layer. New crate `adam-dialog` implements a 5-layer pipeline (FST parser вҶ’ semantics вҶ’ planner вҶ’ realiser вҶ’ FST synthesiser) against the architectural spec in `docs/kazakh_grammar/07_dialog_architecture.md`.

Recognises 5 intents from raw Kazakh input:
- `Greeting` with kind `Casual` / `Polite` / `TimeOfDay(Morning|Day|Evening)`
- `Farewell`
- `Affirmation`
- `Negation`
- `Unknown` (fallback)

Each intent has 2вҖ“4 hand-written response variants; planner picks one by seeded PRNG mod count. The entire output space is enumerable per input вҖ” no free generation.

New binary `adam_chat` with three modes:
- `--once "<input>"` вҖ” single-shot stdout response
- default вҖ” interactive REPL over stdin
- `--trace` вҖ” dump each layer's state (parses, intent, trace lines, output)

Tests: 15 end-to-end pairs cover the full pipeline. Workspace totals: 175 passing, 4 ignored, 0 failing.

Known v0.7.0 limitations (by design, not bugs):
- Only 5 social intents; ~150 templates needed for v1.0.0 MVP.
- Templates are hardcoded in `planner.rs`, not data-driven TOML (v0.7.5).
- No morphological info used for intent classification yet (v0.7.5+).
- No multi-turn state.

## [0.6.0] вҖ” 2026-04-19

Derivational morphology вҖ” the "word-formation layer" the user flagged as a v1.0.0-path requirement. The FST now transforms a root into a new root via a derivational suffix before applying inflection. Eleven derivation types covered:

- `Agent` `-СҲСӢ/-СҲС–` (Р¶Р°Р·Сғ вҶ’ Р¶Р°Р·СғСҲСӢ)
- `Abstract` `-Р»СӢТӣ/-Р»С–Рә` (Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢ вҶ’ Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢР»СӢТӣ)
- `Privative` `-СҒСӢР·/-СҒС–Р·` (СӮТұР· вҶ’ СӮТұР·СҒСӢР·)
- `Endowed` `-Р»СӢ/-Р»С–` (РәТҜСҲ вҶ’ РәТҜСҲСӮС–)
- `Similative` `-РҙР°Р№/-РҙРөР№` (СӮР°Сғ вҶ’ СӮР°СғРҙР°Р№)
- `Comparative` `-СҖР°Тӣ/-СҖРөРә` (Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢ вҶ’ Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢСҖР°Тӣ)
- `VerbalNoun` `-Сғ` (Р¶Р°Р· вҶ’ Р¶Р°Р·Сғ)
- `ActionNoun` `-СӢРј/-С–Рј` (Р°Р№СӮ вҶ’ Р°Р№СӮСӢРј)
- `Diminutive` `-СҲСӢТӣ/-СҲС–Рә` (ТҜР№ вҶ’ ТҜР№СҲС–Рә)
- `Ordinal` `-РҪСҲСӢ/-РҪСҲС–` (РұС–СҖ вҶ’ РұС–СҖС–РҪСҲС–)
- `Collective` `-РөСғ/-Р°Сғ` (РұС–СҖ вҶ’ РұС–СҖРөСғ)

`NounFeatures` gains a `derivation: Option<Derivation>` field; `synthesise_noun` applies the derivation BEFORE inflection so the two pipelines chain correctly (Р¶Р°Р·Сғ вҶ’ Agent вҶ’ Р¶Р°Р·СғСҲСӢ вҶ’ Dative вҶ’ Р¶Р°Р·СғСҲСӢТ“Р°).

Tests added: 10. `adam-kernel-fst` lib now at **78 passing**. Workspace at **160 passing**, 4 ignored, 0 failing.

No other code changes.

## [0.5.5] вҖ” 2026-04-19

Pure Kazakh lexicon milestone. Enforces the "no loanwords" directive at the lexicon level and augments coverage from classical 19th-century sources.

Pipeline:

1. **Purity audit** (`lexicon_purity_audit` binary) вҖ” classified all 16,373 entries from v0.4.0 curated + v0.4.5 Apertium-imported against strict pre-modern-Kazakh criteria (Russian-only letters, loanword suffixes, no Kazakh-specific letter).
2. **Pure Kazakh build** (`build_pure_kazakh_lexicon`) вҖ” filtered out 1,500 contaminated entries (824 Russian letters, 128 loanword suffixes, 681 no-Kazakh-signal). Retained 13,606.
3. **Abai gap analysis** (`extract_abai_gap`) вҖ” identified 715 unique root candidates missing from the lexicon but present as word forms in Abai's corpus.
4. **Augmentation** (`augment_lexicon_from_abai`) вҖ” automatically classified the top 500 gap candidates (393 nouns + 107 verbs) with POS, vowel harmony, and final sound class. Output: `data/lexicon_v1/abai_augmented_roots.json`.

Result:

| metric | v0.5.0 | v0.5.5 |
|---|---|---|
| Lexicon entries (pure) | n/a | 14,106 |
| Loanwords dropped | 0 | 1,500 |
| Abai vocabulary coverage | 88.8% | **97.8%** (+9 pp) |

Missing-vocabulary examples added (each backed by corpus frequency):
- `СҒУ©Р·` (word, speech вҖ” 123Г— in Abai)
- `РұРҫР№`, `ТӣР°РҪ`, `ТӣРҫР»`, `ТӣР°СҖ`, `Р¶Р°Сғ`, `Р¶Р°СӮ`, `Р¶РөСӮ`, `СӮТҜСҒ`, `ТӣСӢСҒ`, `Р¶Р°Р№`
- `РҪР°РҙР°РҪ` (ignorant вҖ” Abai's key philosophical concept)

These are fundamental proto-Kazakh vocabulary items the Apertium import had zero entries for.

No changes to the FST code, phonology, or morphotactics modules. The augmented lexicon file lives alongside the v0.4.5 imports and can be unioned into the active lexicon at load time.

Workspace totals: 150 tests passing, 4 ignored, 0 failing.

## [0.5.0] вҖ” 2026-04-19

Expands the v0.4.5 FST to cover Kazakh non-finite verb forms.

- **Vowel-final-stem aorist coalescence** (Apertium rules 17, 18, 19, 20, 30). Stem-final `СӢ/С–` merge with the aorist `{A}` to produce `Рё` (e.g. `РҫТӣСӢ` + PRES + 3 вҶ’ `РҫТӣРёРҙСӢ`, not the previous `*РҫТӣСӢР°`). Stems ending in other vowels take a `Р№`-glide (`СҒУ©Р№Р»Рө` вҶ’ `СҒУ©Р№Р»РөР№РҙС–`). Past tense on vowel-final stems (`РҫТӣСӢ` + PAST + 1SG вҶ’ `РҫТӣСӢРҙСӢРј`) continues to work without coalescence.
- **Participles** вҖ” three new `Tense` variants:
  - `ParticiplePast` вҖ” `-{G}{A}РҪ` (`Р¶Р°Р·Т“Р°РҪ`, `РұРөСҖРіРөРҪ`, `ТӣР°Р»Т“Р°РҪ`).
  - `ParticipleHabitual` вҖ” `-{A}СӮСӢРҪ` (`Р¶Р°Р·Р°СӮСӢРҪ`, `РәРөР»РөСӮС–РҪ`).
  - `ParticipleFuture` вҖ” `-{A}СҖ` (`Р¶Р°Р·Р°СҖ`, `РәРөР»РөСҖ`).
- **Converbs** вҖ” two new `Tense` variants:
  - `ConverbPerfect` вҖ” `-{Y}Рҝ` (`Р¶Р°Р·СӢРҝ`, `РұРөСҖС–Рҝ`).
  - `ConverbImperfect` вҖ” `-{A}` (`Р¶Р°Р·Р°` without personal ending).

Tests: **68 unit tests passing** in `adam-kernel-fst` (up from 55 in v0.4.5). Workspace totals: 150 passing, 4 ignored, 0 failing.

No changes to v0.4.0 transformer baseline or v0.4.5 FST core code.

## [0.4.5] вҖ” 2026-04-19

Introduces **adam-kernel-fst**, a pure-Rust deterministic finite-state transducer for Kazakh morphology. This is Phase 1 of the architecture pivot from stochastic transformers to deterministic morphology + small LM (v1.0.0 track). v0.4.0 transformer stack stays untouched; v0.4.5 adds the new FST layer alongside.

Highlights:

- **New crate `adam-kernel-fst`** вҖ” phonology module (12 archiphonemes, 20+ of 54 Apertium twol rules implemented), morphotactics module (25 suffix templates covering noun plural/possessive/case and verb tense/voice/negation/person), parser module (`analyse(surface) вҶ’ Vec<(root, features)>`), lexicon loader (union of 4,454 curated + 11,919 Apertium-imported entries).
- **55 unit tests + 1 smoke test + 4 ignored slow roundtrips**. Slow tests (manual: `cargo test --test roundtrip -- --ignored`) roundtrip the full 14k lexicon on 4 feature combinations: **36,238 / 36,238 = 100.0 %** success.
- **CLI binary `adam_fst`** вҖ” `synth`, `analyse`, `stats` subcommands. Hand-rolled arg parsing (no CLI-framework dep).
- **Apertium-kaz import pipeline** (`import_apertium_lexicon` binary) with POS mapping, loanword filter, and prefix-conflict guard.
- **Grammar study notes**: `docs/kazakh_grammar/00_architecture_v1.md`, `01_phonology.md`, `02_morphology.md`, `03_syntax.md`, `04_lexicon_sources.md`, `05_work_plan.md`, `06_apertium_twol_catalogue.md`.

Not yet:

- Vowel-final-stem edge cases (rule 17 coalescence, semivowel Сғ).
- Participles, converbs, infinitive.
- LM over root + feature-bundle sequences (v0.5+ target).
- Replacement of v0.4.0 pipeline (deliberately left untouched).

Workspace totals: 137 tests passing, 4 ignored, 0 failing. CI green.

## [0.4.0] вҖ” 2026-04-17

Corpus and infrastructure maturity release. Adds the first classical-literature source (Abai Qunanbayuly via Wikisource, 146 works, 2,253 samples), the first web-crawl source (CC-100 Kazakh, 50,000 samples filtered for Cyrillic-ratio and repetition), and fixes a data-composition bug in the synthetic generator (1- and 2-word outputs dominated the corpus, teaching the model early EOS). BPE retrained at vocab **8,192** with **3.27Г— compression** on a 12.5M-token pretokenized corpus. Model rolled back from the v0.4.0-failed experiment (27.3M, H=512 L=6) to **24.2M params** (H=512 L=5) after confirming that the L=6 scale-up was undertrained at 3.9M tokens.

Training: 20,000 steps, batch 8, seq 128, 3e-4 peak lr with cosine decay, 8h on M2 Metal at 0.64вҖ“0.70 steps/s throughput. First-class reliability: `train_baseline` now writes a periodic checkpoint every 2000 steps after a reboot lost 13k uncheckpointed steps mid-run.

Validation (honest):
- 12,101 held-out samples (larger/harder distribution vs v0.3.0's 1,939)
- mean_ce: 7.43, **perplexity: 1691.89**
- bits/char: **3.28** (v0.4.0-failed: 3.26; v0.3.0: 3.49 вҖ” val sets not directly comparable)

Qualitative:
- Complete grammatical Kazakh sentences now appear in `temp=0.8` and nucleus samples (6 of 30 showcase outputs): `Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢ Р°РҙР°Рј РјР°Т“СӢРҪР° РұРөСҖРөРҙС–`, `РҫР» Р¶Р°Р·Р°РҙСӢ`, `РҫР»Р°СҖ Р¶ТҜСҖРөРҙС–`, `ТҜР»РәРөРҪ Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢ Р°РҙР°Рј РҫТӣСӢР№РҙСӢ`, `РјРөРәСӮРөРҝ СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ РјУҷСҖСӮРөРұРө РҪР°ТӣСӮСӢР»Р°Р№РҙСӢ`.
- Greedy still terminates early вҖ” expected for a capacity-bound model (24M params Г— 4M training tokens is ~25Г— below Chinchilla-optimal data).

v0.5.0 will address the data bottleneck: curriculum-style FSM expansion (L1/L2/L3 difficulty), larger CC-100 sample (50k вҶ’ 500k), classical-literature expansion (РҗСғСҚР·РҫРІ, РқСғСҖРҝРөРёСҒРҫРІ, Р‘У©РәРөР№ locally), and SFT on translated Alpaca for the first instruction-following pass.

## [0.3.0] вҖ” 2026-04-15

First capacity scale-up. `ModelConfig::tiny` grows from 4.28M вҶ’ **20.0M params** (hidden 224вҶ’512, layers 4вҶ’5, ffn 896вҶ’2048, head_dim 28вҶ’64). 15,000 training steps on the 39k unified corpus, 3h 45m on MacBook Air M2 Metal. Validation perplexity drops from **1112.31 вҶ’ 871.30 (вҲ’21.7%)** вҖ” first meaningful delta since Phase 6a real-text onset. The 4.28M envelope was saturated at Phase 6d; Wikipedia + Common Voice distribution breadth required more model capacity. Peak RSS ~2.5 GB of 8 GB unified memory вҖ” headroom confirmed.

## [0.2.0] вҖ” 2026-04-15

First minor release after v0.1.0. Full retrain on the 39k unified corpus assembled across Phases 6aвҖ“6d. BPE retrained (3,336 merges, 2.80Г— compression, **0 unknowns, 100.00% roundtrip**). 4.28M model, 15,000 steps, 1h 48m wall time on M2 Metal. Val PPL 1078.68 вҶ’ 1112.31 (flat; val set is larger and harder вҖ” capacity bottleneck now visible).

## [0.1.6] вҖ” 2026-04-15

**Phase 6d вҖ” Common Voice KK.** Mozilla Common Voice Kazakh sentence-collector integrated (6,108 accepted, CC0-1.0 text only). Unified corpus grows 32,986 вҶ’ 39,058 unique (+6,072; 4,282 duplicates dedup'd at assembly). Also fixes `scripts/bump_foundation_version.sh`: Cargo.lock is no longer perl-replaced (corrupted transitive deps under naive substring replace); regenerated by `cargo build` after Cargo.toml bump.

## [0.1.5] вҖ” 2026-04-15

**Phase 7.1 вҖ” Wikipedia-augmented retrain.** 4.28M baseline retrained on the corpus enlarged with Wikipedia KZ. 14,000 steps, ~2h on M2 Metal. Val PPL 626.81 вҶ’ 1078.68, reflecting a broader, harder val set (Wikipedia sentences are lexically richer than Tatoeba) вҖ” honest baseline on the enlarged distribution, not a regression.

## [0.1.4] вҖ” 2026-04-14

**Phase 6c вҖ” Kazakh Wikipedia.** Plain-text extracted from the kk.wikipedia.org XML dump (~296k articles вҶ’ 15,000 clean samples after filter; CC-BY-SA 4.0). Unified corpus 17,986 вҶ’ 32,986 unique. Infrastructure: `scripts/fetch_wikipedia_kz.sh` (bzcat + perl streaming with UTF-8 fix), `process_wikipedia_kz` binary.

## [0.1.3] вҖ” 2026-04-14

**Phase 7 вҖ” first real-text baseline.** 4.28M model trained on the unified corpus including authentic Kazakh (Tatoeba): 7,000 steps, 61m on M2 Metal, dropout 0.05, grad clipping max-norm 1.0. Explicit `loss.backward() вҶ’ clip вҶ’ opt.step` replaces `opt.backward_step`. First honest perplexity on real text: **626.81** (vs 129.49 on pure synthetic вҖ” tells us real Kazakh is harder).

## [0.1.2] вҖ” 2026-04-14

BPE vocab size bumped 1390 вҶ’ **4096**. Char-level fallback + Tatoeba real text saturate the larger target.

## [0.1.1] вҖ” 2026-04-14

**Phase 6a/6b вҖ” first authentic Kazakh source.** Tatoeba Kazakh integrated (4,058 sentences, CC-BY 2.0 FR) via `fetch_tatoeba_kazakh.sh` + `process_tatoeba_kazakh`. Tokenizer adds char-level fallback for FSM-unknown words and leading-punct в–Ғ marker placement вҖ” brings roundtrip to 100% on mixed real/synthetic text.

## [0.1.0] вҖ” 2026-04-14

First minor release. The foundation works end-to-end: a Kazakh-first 3.06M-parameter transformer language model trained from scratch on a self-generated, FSM-validated synthetic corpus, evaluated against held-out perplexity, and serving inference with morpheme-aware BPE encode/decode.

### Brand
- Logo `assets/shanraq.svg` integrated into README header.
- README rewritten with centered title, badges, quickstart, and stats.
- `AUTHORS` file added.

### Quality (cumulative since v0.0.85)
- Validation perplexity: **129.49** on a 699-sample held-out set (mean cross-entropy 4.86 over 2532 tokens).
- All 464 segmentation eval examples match at 10000 bps.
- Foundation validation green across 11 layers.

---

## Phase 5 вҖ” Training and inference (v0.0.81 вҶ’ v0.0.92)

### [0.0.92] вҖ” Phase 5i: Generation showcase report
- New `generation_showcase` binary: 20 prompts Г— 3 sampling configs = 60 generations.
- Report artifact `data/training/generation_showcase_report.json`.
- Foundation validation now requires showcase + perplexity reports.

### [0.0.91] вҖ” Phase 5h: Top-p + repetition penalty
- `generate` gains nucleus (top-p) sampling and GPT-2-style repetition penalty.
- Backwards-compatible CLI; defaults are no-ops.

### [0.0.90] вҖ” Phase 5g: Hyperparameter tuning
- Dropout 0.10 вҶ’ 0.05 reduces over-regularization on small corpus.
- Gradient clipping (max-norm 1.0) added to `train_baseline`.
- Training extended to 7000 steps with 300-step warmup.
- **Perplexity: 165.98 вҶ’ 129.49 (вҲ’22%).**

### [0.0.89] вҖ” Phase 5f: Model scaling + dropout
- ModelConfig::tiny() bumped: hidden 192 вҶ’ 224, heads 6 вҶ’ 8, ffn 768 вҶ’ 896, +dropout=0.1.
- 2.33M вҶ’ 3.06M params.
- `forward(ids, train: bool)` added to gate dropout on/off.

### [0.0.88] вҖ” Phase 5e: Held-out eval + perplexity
- `encode_corpus` extended with deterministic train/val split (FNV hash of sample id).
- New `eval_perplexity` binary writes structured `validation_perplexity_report.json`.
- First baseline: **165.98 perplexity**.

### [0.0.85] вҖ” Phase 5d: Inference binary
- `generate` binary: load checkpoint, autoregressive sampling (greedy/temperature/top-k).
- First sentence generated by the model: "Р¶Р°ТӣСҒСӢ Р°РҙР°Рј Р°Р· РәУ©СҖСҒРөСӮРөРҙС–."

### [0.0.84] вҖ” Phase 5c: Training loop
- `train_baseline` binary: AdamW + linear-warmup + cosine-decay LR + safetensors checkpointing.
- First trained checkpoint, training loss 7.94 вҶ’ 3.39 in 7m on Metal.

### [0.0.83] вҖ” Phase 5b: Data loader
- `DataLoader` reads ids pack, produces shifted (input, target) batches on device.
- End-to-end smoke test: forward + cross-entropy loss.

### [0.0.81] вҖ” Phase 5a: Candle integration
- Added candle (HuggingFace Rust ML) with Metal backend on macOS, CPU elsewhere.
- `AdamBaseline` decoder-only transformer (initial 2.21M params).
- M2 Metal smoke test passes.

---

## Phase 4 вҖ” Tokenizer (v0.0.78 вҶ’ v0.0.80)

### [0.0.87] вҖ” Phase 4d+4e: Lexicon-seeded BPE
- BPE vocab now seeded with all 211 lexicon roots + all 422 rule forms before counting pairs.
- 0% `<unk>` on any FSM-parseable Kazakh word.

### [0.0.80] вҖ” Phase 4c: BPE encoder/decoder
- `bpe::BpeTokenizer` module: load vocab+merges, encode text вҶ’ ids, decode ids вҶ’ text.
- `encode_corpus` binary writes a training-ready ids pack.
- 100% round-trip on 7,737 samples.

### [0.0.79] вҖ” Phase 4b: BPE trainer
- `train_bpe` binary: iterative most-frequent-pair merging over morpheme stream.
- Skips merges across word boundary (right token starts with в–Ғ).
- 567 merges learned from corpus statistics; 2.12Г— compression.

### [0.0.78] вҖ” Phase 4a: Pre-tokenizer
- `pretokenize(text, lexicon, rules)`: morpheme-aware splitting via FSM.
- SentencePiece-style в–Ғ marker on word-start morphemes.
- Handles standalone punctuation and whole-word fallback.

---

## Phase 3 вҖ” Corpus (v0.0.74 вҶ’ v0.0.77)

### [0.0.86] вҖ” Phase 3e: Full POS coverage
- 15 вҶ’ 30 templates exercising every POS (adverbs, particles, modals, РҫР»/РҫР»Р°СҖ, conjunctions).
- Synthetic corpus 10,000 вҶ’ 18,000 samples.
- Unified corpus 7,737 вҶ’ 13,929 unique samples.

### [0.0.77] вҖ” Phase 3d: Kazakh proverbs
- Added 80 classical РјР°ТӣР°Р»-РјУҷСӮРөР»РҙРөСҖ across 23 themes.
- Proverbs bypass FSM-validation policy (archaic morphology); Cyrillic-only check.

### [0.0.76] вҖ” Phase 3c: Unified corpus
- `assemble_unified_corpus` binary: dedup + renumber across packs.
- 7,657 unique samples from 10,094 inputs.

### [0.0.75] вҖ” Phase 3b: Rich templates
- Generator templates 6 вҶ’ 15: pronouns with matched person, conjunctions, multi-argument, etc.
- 10,000 sentences (95% yield).

### [0.0.74] вҖ” Phase 3a: Synthetic generator
- `synth_sentences` binary: combines FSM lexicon and rules to produce grammatically valid Kazakh sentences.
- Self-validation: every generated word verified by `deterministic_segment_token`.
- FSM fix: removed vowel from `verb_tense_a/e_from_stem` allowed finals (linguistically correct вҖ” `Р№` handles vowel-final aorist).

---

## Phase 2 вҖ” Grammatical foundation (v0.0.66 вҶ’ v0.0.73)

### [0.0.73] вҖ” Phase 2h: Modals
- New `Modal` POS, 6 roots: РәРөСҖРөРә, РјТҜРјРәС–РҪ, СӮРёС–СҒ, СҲСӢТ“Р°СҖ, ТӣР°Р¶РөСӮ, Р»Р°Р№СӢТӣ.

### [0.0.72] вҖ” Phase 2g: Nominal predicate
- 16 predicative personal suffix rules: -РјСӢРҪ/РјС–РҪ, -СҒСӢТЈ/СҒС–ТЈ, -СҒСӢР·/СҒС–Р·, -РјСӢР·/РјС–Р· on noun + adjective.
- 3 copula bare lexemes as Particle: РөРҙС–, РөРәРөРҪ, РөРјРөСҒ.

### [0.0.71] вҖ” Phase 2f: Adverbs
- New `Adverb` POS, 19 roots: ТӣР°Р·С–СҖ, РұТҜРіС–РҪ, РөСҖСӮРөТЈ, РәРөСҲРө, СӮРөР·, РұР°СҸСғ, Р¶РҫТӣ, РёУҷ, etc.

### [0.0.70] вҖ” Phase 2e: Numerals
- New `Numeral` POS, 20 cardinals: РұС–СҖвҖ“Р¶ТҜР·, РјСӢТЈ.
- 4 ordinal suffix rules: -РҪСҲСӢ/РҪСҲС–/-СӢРҪСҲСӢ/С–РҪСҲС–.

### [0.0.69] вҖ” Phase 2d: Conjunctions + Particles
- New `Conjunction` POS, 9 roots: Р¶УҷРҪРө, РұС–СҖР°Тӣ, СҒРөРұРөРұС–, У©Р№СӮРәРөРҪС–, etc.
- New `Particle` POS, 12 roots: РјР°/РјРө, РұР°/РұРө, РҝР°/РҝРө, Т“РҫР№, РҙР°/РҙРө, СӮРөРә, ТӣР°РҪР°, У©СӮРө.

### [0.0.68] вҖ” Phase 2c: Roots + 3sg aorist
- 29 nouns, 13 verbs, 5 adjectives added.
- Critical FSM fix: `tense вҶ’ person_3sg` was missing for aorist forms (e.g. РұРөСҖРөРҙС– = РұРөСҖ+Рө+РҙС–). Added rules for both future and negative_future predecessors.
- "Р№" connector rule for vowel-final verb stems (Р¶Р°СҒР°Р№РҙСӢ).
- Coverage 19.79% вҶ’ **73.77%** on educational corpus.

### [0.0.67] вҖ” Phase 2b: Postpositions
- New `Postposition` POS, 15 roots: Р°СҖТӣСӢР»СӢ, ТҜСҲС–РҪ, СӮСғСҖР°Р»СӢ, РәРөР№С–РҪ, etc.

### [0.0.66] вҖ” Phase 2a: Adjectives
- New `Adjective` POS, 25 roots, 57 inflection rules (mirror of noun rules).
- Coverage 4.56% вҶ’ 17.93%.

---

- `coverage_report` strips trailing punctuation before FSM matching.

- adam-core merged into adam-kernel.
- New `coverage_report` binary measures FSM coverage on real Kazakh text.
